title: SELECT N (N != 0) in cluster mode silently switches DB; upstream rejects with 'SELECT is not allowed in cluster mode'
description: Upstream db.c::selectCommand:756-770 rejects SELECT N when cluster_enabled is true and N != 0 with 'ERR SELECT is not allowed in cluster mode' (cluster mode only uses DB 0). fr's handle_select_command in fr-runtime/src/lib.rs:10359 and select() in fr-command/src/lib.rs:11980 both skip this check and unconditionally accept any in-range DB index — exposing a parity gap when cluster_enabled is true. Add the check in both paths after argument parsing and before the db_index assignment, mirroring upstream's wording.

title: Lua tonumber('-N', base) should wrap via strtoul, not preserve signed value
description:
Edge case discovered during frankenredis-s99a4 verification. Lua 5.1's
luaB_tonumber dispatches to strtoul(s1, _, base) when base is explicit;
strtoul handles a leading '-' by parsing the absolute digits and then
applying unsigned negation (wrap-around) to the result. Lua pushes the
unsigned-long as a lua_Number (f64), and Redis casts f64→i64 for the
reply, which saturates to INT64_MIN for the very large positive value.

  EVAL "return tonumber('-5', 16)" 0
    vendored: -9223372036854775808     (i.e. INT64_MIN from saturation)
    fr:       -5                       (signed parse, more sensible but
                                       not bit-compatible)

This is a vendored quirk — fr's behavior is arguably more sensible.
Pinning parity requires reproducing the unsigned wrap-around: parse
the digits as u64, wrapping_neg, cast to f64. Then the lua_to_resp
boundary will saturate to INT64_MIN.

Fix locus: crates/fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs — the 'tonumber' arm,
LuaValue::Str path inside the `if let Some(base)` branch. The handling
currently does i64::from_str_radix(stripped, base) which faithfully
parses signed ints; replace with a u64-and-wrap variant when a leading
'-' was consumed.

Test plan: pin tonumber("-5", 16) → INT64_MIN as a number. Confirm
that tonumber("-0xFF") (no explicit base) keeps the existing signed
luaO_str2d fallback.

Priority 4 — extreme edge case, unlikely to bite real scripts.
close_reason: fixed

title: ZSET listpack encodes integer-valued scores as ASCII strings instead of compact listpack int encoding
description:
Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4:
ZADD z3 1 a 2 b
DEBUG OBJECT z3 → serializedlength: ven=18, fr=20

DUMP z3 hex bytes:
ven: 11 11 11 00 00 00 04 00 81 61 02 01 01 81 62 02 02 01 ff ...
fr:  11 13 13 00 00 00 04 00 81 61 02 81 31 02 81 62 02 81 32 02 ff ...

Both use RDB_TYPE_ZSET_2 (0x11). The listpack body diverges: vendored encodes integer scores 1 and 2 as compact listpack int entries (0x01 0x01 / 0x02 0x01 = uint7+backlen), while fr encodes them as listpack STRING entries (0x81 '1' 0x02 / 0x81 '2' 0x02 = string with 1-byte payload). Each integer score wastes 1 byte.

Per upstream listpack.c::lpInsert + lpEncodeGetType, when storing a score that round-trips through long long (i.e. the float is integer-valued), the listpack writer picks the compact uint7/uint13/sint16/etc. encoding instead of the string-with-int-content encoding.

fr-store/fr-persist's listpack writer for zsets needs to detect integer-valued scores and use the compact int encoding. Affects: DEBUG OBJECT serializedlength, MEMORY USAGE for zsets, RDB DUMP wire shape (related to project_rdb_stream_incompatibility memory note).

Acceptance: ZADD z 1 a 2 b → DEBUG OBJECT serializedlength byte-equal vendored.
close_reason: fr-store::encode_listpack_entry now mirrors upstream listpack.c lpEncodeGetType — integer-looking strings round-trip through parse_listpack_integer and emit the compact uint7/uint13/sint16/sint24/sint32/sint64 encoding instead of the 0x80|len string form. ZADD z 1 a 2 b → DEBUG OBJECT serializedlength now reports 18 (== vendored), DUMP bytes are byte-identical to vendored 7.2.4. Same fix lights up integer-looking HASH values and LIST elements (verified via DUMP h1/l1 byte-identical). Pin: zset_listpack_encodes_integer_scores_as_compact_int_per_upstream.

title: fr-command INFO Memory section hardcodes number_of_cached_scripts/functions/libraries as 0 — real counters exist in Store
description:
REVIEW MODE Tick 14 finding via /mock-code-finder skill. fr's INFO command emits 0 for three counters that have real data sources, leaving observability tools blind to script/function/library state.

LOCATION (crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs lines 11920-11922):

  info.push_str('number_of_cached_scripts:0\r\n');
  info.push_str('number_of_functions:0\r\n');
  info.push_str('number_of_libraries:0\r\n');

SOURCES THAT EXIST:
  - Store::function_stats() returns (lib_count, func_count) — used correctly by FUNCTION STATS but NOT by INFO.
  - Store::script_cache: HashMap<String, Vec<u8>> — has .len() but no public accessor.
  - SCRIPT LOAD inserts into script_cache; SCRIPT FLUSH clears it; SCRIPT EXISTS uses it.
  - FUNCTION LOAD populates function_libraries; FUNCTION STATS reports correctly.

UPSTREAM (legacy_redis_code/redis/src/server.c info section):
  number_of_cached_scripts:N    where N = dictSize(server.lua_scripts)
  number_of_functions:N          where N = functionsLibCount() (sum across libs)
  number_of_libraries:N          where N = engineLibrariesNum()

Reproducer:
  redis-cli> SCRIPT LOAD 'return 1'
  '<sha1>'
  redis-cli> FUNCTION LOAD '#!lua name=lib1\nredis.register_function("f1", function() return 1 end)'
  'lib1'
  redis-cli> INFO memory | grep number_of
  upstream:
    number_of_cached_scripts:1
    number_of_functions:1
    number_of_libraries:1
  fr (current):
    number_of_cached_scripts:0
    number_of_functions:0
    number_of_libraries:0

Severity: [LOW]. Observability divergence on hot-path INFO. Affects monitoring tools that scrape these counters (RedisInsight, datadog-redis-integration, prometheus_redis_exporter, redis-exporter).

Fix:
1. Add Store::script_count() -> usize.
2. Wire all 3 INFO lines to real values using script_count() + function_stats().
3. Add regression test via fr-command lib tests.

Discovered via /mock-code-finder skill — grep for 'placeholder|fake|stub' in production code paths, narrowed to the INFO emit which had a comment explicitly noting the placeholder ('Allocator-derived stats (placeholders since fr uses the Rust system allocator)') and inferred the script/function counters were lumped in even though they have real backing data.
close_reason: fr-command INFO memory now reports real values for number_of_cached_scripts (Store::script_count() = script_cache.len()), number_of_functions and number_of_libraries (Store::function_stats() = (lib_count, func_count)). New helper Store::script_count() exposes the existing private script_cache HashMap. Wired the three INFO emit lines from hardcoded ':0' to format!() with live values. New test info_memory_reports_real_script_function_library_counts pins both the empty-store baseline (all 0) and the post-SCRIPT LOAD + FUNCTION LOAD live state (1, 2, 1 for a single script + a library with 2 functions). Discovered via /mock-code-finder skill (grep for placeholder/stub/fake/0-stub patterns in production code paths). All fr-command lib tests pass; clippy clean. The 4 unrelated pre-existing failures (xreadgroup/xinfo stream/migrate-vendored) reproduce on a clean stash and are untouched.

title: Lua string.gmatch iterator loses state when called outside for-in loop
description:
Lua 5.1's string.gmatch returns an iterator function whose internal
closure carries the source string, pattern, and position. When called
with no args, it advances and returns the next match. fr's gmatch
implementation returns a (RustFunction, state_table, nil) triple
where the iterator function reads state from its first arg — which
works for the `for ... in gmatch(s, p) do` form (the for-in loop
passes state explicitly) but breaks for direct calls:

  EVAL "return string.gmatch('abc def', '%a+')()" 0
    vendored: abc
    fr:       (nil)

  EVAL "local g=string.gmatch('a b c', '%a'); return g()..g()..g()" 0
    vendored: abc
    fr:       ERR attempt to concatenate a nil value

The fix requires giving the iterator stateful closure semantics. Options:

1. Add a LuaValue variant for "rust closure with captured args" or
   reuse a Function with captured upvalues. Heavyweight.
2. Stash the gmatch state on LuaState keyed by an opaque cookie (a
   string id), have __gmatch_iter look up state from current
   invocation. Risk: leaks state across scripts unless cleared.
3. Use a LuaValue::Table with an embedded callable metatable (__call)
   that delegates to __gmatch_iter with the table as state. Lighter
   weight but introduces a new __call dispatch pattern.

Option 3 is probably the cleanest. The for-in path continues to work
(state arg is the table); the no-arg call goes through __call which
prepends the table as the first arg.

Test plan: pin gmatch(s, p)() returns first match, three successive
calls return 1st/2nd/3rd matches then nil, and the existing for-in
regression continues to work.
close_reason: completed

title: Lua 'attempt to call a nil/thread/X value' errors lack 'user_script:1:' prefix
description: fr's call_function emits 'attempt to call a nil value' (and similar for thread/non-callable types) without the standard 'user_script:1: ' source-location prefix that upstream Lua 5.1 wraps around every runtime error. Live differential probe: 'redis.register_function' inside EVAL (where it's nil) gives 'ERR attempt to call a nil value' in fr but 'ERR user_script:1: attempt to call field "register_function" (a nil value)' in vendored. Two divergences: missing prefix AND missing 'field NAME' context. The field-name context requires plumbing through the call site -- larger refactor. The prefix is the cheap win. Fix: prepend 'user_script:1: ' to the three call_function error literals at fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs:2973-2975.
close_reason: Added the 'user_script:1: ' source-location prefix to the three call_function 'attempt to call' errors (nil/thread/other-type) at fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs:2973+. Live differential probe: 'local x = nil x()' and 'local x = 42 x()' now carry the prefix matching vendored. Remaining 'local NAME (a TYPE value)' context detail (vendored emits 'attempt to call local x (a nil value)') requires plumbing the call-site name through to call_function -- filed as follow-up.

title: INFO commandstats collapses container subcommands — fr emits cmdstat_memory not cmdstat_memory|usage
description:
Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4 INFO commandstats:

ven emits per-subcommand entries (server.c::genRedisInfoStringCommandStats keys by cmd->fullname which is 'parent|sub' for containers):
  cmdstat_memory|usage:calls=12,...
  cmdstat_memory|help:calls=1,...
  cmdstat_object|encoding:calls=4,...
  cmdstat_object|refcount:calls=3,...
  cmdstat_pubsub|channels:calls=1,...
  cmdstat_client|info:calls=1,...
  cmdstat_command|info:calls=23,...
  cmdstat_function|stats:calls=2,...

fr collapses every container call into the parent:
  cmdstat_client:calls=1,...
  cmdstat_memory:calls=12,...
  cmdstat_object:calls=6,...
  cmdstat_pubsub:calls=2,...

Per fr-runtime/src/lib.rs:2484 the histogram key is just argv[0].to_ascii_lowercase(). The container/subcommand namespacing already exists for the subscribe-context error wording (Self::pubsub_blocked_command_name at line 3985). Extract that into a shared helper and reuse it in record_command_histogram_outcome + record_command_rejected so the histogram key matches cmd->fullname.

Acceptance: INFO commandstats emits cmdstat_memory|usage / cmdstat_object|encoding / cmdstat_client|info etc. for container subcommands, while non-container commands (GET, SET, EVAL) still key as bare parent.
close_reason: Extracted canonical_command_fullname helper from pubsub_blocked_command_name and reused it in record_command_histogram_outcome + record_command_rejected so commandstats/latencystats/latency-histogram bucket container subcommands as parent|sub. Pinned via info_commandstats_namespaces_container_subcommands_per_upstream.

title: CONFIG GET <pattern> <pattern>... returns duplicate entries when patterns overlap; upstream dedupes
description: Upstream config.c::configGetCommand uses a 'matches' dict to dedupe entries across multiple patterns: each name is only added once even if multiple patterns (or repeats of the same pattern) match it. fr's handle_config_get at crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs:6945 calls collect_config_entries once per pattern and just appends to a flat Vec, so 'CONFIG GET maxmemory maxmemory' returns the maxmemory pair twice, and 'CONFIG GET maxmemory maxmemory*' returns maxmemory in both passes. Dedupe entries by key (case-insensitive) before constructing the RESP2 alternating-array / RESP3 Map reply.
close_reason: fixed

title: redis parity: Lua redis.call('CONFIG', 'GET', ...) response shape
description: redis.call('CONFIG', 'GET') returns a dictionary/map in newer Redis rather than a flat array. Align the Lua evaluator.
close_reason: Completed

title: Downgrade FEATURE_PARITY.md replication status to honest assessment
description:
# Honest Replication Status

## What
Change FEATURE_PARITY.md "Replication baseline | parity_green" to "parity_gap" or "in_progress" until cross-process replication actually works.

## Why
The current "parity_green" rating for replication is dishonest. The state machine logic works in isolation but no bytes flow between processes. Honesty about project status is a prerequisite for good engineering.

## Implementation
1. Update FEATURE_PARITY.md: change replication row status to "in_progress"
2. Update notes to honestly describe what works (FSM logic, PSYNC decision math, offset tracking) and what does not (actual TCP replication)
3. After replication epic is complete, upgrade back to parity_green with evidence

## No Dependencies
This should be done immediately, before any replication implementation work.
close_reason: Updated FEATURE_PARITY.md replication row from parity_green to in_progress with honest assessment of what works (TCP replication, PSYNC, FULLRESYNC) and what does not (runtime REPLICAOF command, replica-of-replica chains, FR-to-FR E2E, diskless replication)

title: Lua lexer 'escape sequence too large' missing 'near <token>' suffix
description:
After frankenredis-whyor, fr correctly rejects \d+ escapes >255 but emits just "escape sequence too large". Vendored Lua 5.1.5's luaX_lexerror appends " near '<token>'" where <token> is the open/close quote rendering.

Probe:
  EVAL "return string.byte('\\256')" 0
    vendored: "escape sequence too large near '''"
    fr:       "escape sequence too large"

Same near-suffix pattern as the existing 'unfinished string near <eof>' in fr; just extend to escape-too-large. Probe sweep #122.
close_reason: completed

title: Implement real CONFIG GET responses instead of empty array stub
close_reason: CONFIG GET is already fully implemented in fr-runtime::handle_config_get with 80+ parameters — the fr-command stub is dead code

title: Align XREAD BLOCK timeout validation with Redis integer-ms semantics
description: Redis requires XREAD/XREADGROUP BLOCK to be an integer millisecond timeout and rejects out-of-range values. FrankenRedis currently validates BLOCK as a float in fr-command and uses saturating_add in fr-server, which can accept fractional or oversized inputs incorrectly.
close_reason: Completed

title: [HIGH] r73v sub-task (a-ext): byte-identical re-emit (compact-type encoder selection)
description: Stretch acceptance for br-frankenredis-r73v: 're-emit byte-identical on save'. The 9c460ed live_redis_rdb_reemit_loadrdb_matches_upstream gate proves STATE-equivalence cross-impl, but the bytes differ because fr-persist::encode_rdb always emits canonical type tags (RDB_TYPE_LIST=1, RDB_TYPE_SET=2, RDB_TYPE_HASH=4, RDB_TYPE_ZSET_2=5) where upstream picks the COMPACT form (LIST_QUICKLIST_2=18, SET_INTSET=11, SET_LISTPACK=20, HASH_LISTPACK=16, ZSET_LISTPACK=17) for shapes that fit its size thresholds. Wire fr-persist::encode_rdb to mirror upstream's compact-type selection (re-using the encoder helpers that already exist in fr-store::dump_key for DUMP/RESTORE). Acceptance: encode_rdb(decode_rdb(upstream_dump)) == upstream_dump for all shapes within compact thresholds.
close_reason: Implemented compact RDB default re-emit with Redis zset ordering and conformance coverage

title: Lua __index chain depth-exhaustion returns nil instead of 'loop in gettable' error
description:
Upstream Lua 5.1 lvm.c::luaV_gettable detects __index chain cycles
via MAXTAGLOOP (2000) iterations; when exhausted it raises
'loop in gettable'. fr's table_lookup_with_index_meta caps at 16
hops and silently returns nil on exhaustion, masking what should
be a runtime error.

Repro:
  EVAL "local t = {}; setmetatable(t, {__index=t}); return tostring(t.x)" 0
    vendored: (error) ERR user_script:1: loop in gettable ...
    fr:       nil

The 16-hop cap is a fr-only safety limit; vendored uses 2000. For
parity, fr should:
  1. Raise 'loop in gettable' (with the user_script:1: prefix from
     the runtime, dropped under pcall/anonymous-C) when the limit
     is hit.
  2. Mirror the same behavior in get_with_index_depth (raw table
     lookup) if any callers go through that path.

Fix at fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs:4429 — change the depth-exhausted
arm from `Ok(LuaValue::Nil)` to
`Err("user_script:1: loop in gettable".to_string())`.
close_reason: completed

title: SETBIT promotes encoding to raw
description: Upstream bitops.c::setbitCommand calls dbUnshareStringValue (or dbAdd for new keys) which always uses raw encoding regardless of length. fr was leaving int-encoded values as int (when SETBIT didn't extend) or as embstr (when length stayed ≤44). Now SETBIT sets force_raw_encoding on both the existing-entry mutation path and the new-entry creation path

title: redis parity: fix WAITAOF without appendonly parity
description: WAITAOF returns 1 0 instead of 0 0 when appendonly is disabled and min replicas are unmet.
close_reason: Completed

title: COMMAND INFO for container commands omits subcommand metadata
description:
Vendored Redis 7.2.4 emits subcommand metadata as the tenth field of every COMMAND INFO entry whose command is a container. fr currently emits the subcommand entries for only four containers (command, object, xgroup, xinfo) and an empty array for the rest, even though SUBCOMMAND_TABLE has rows for all the subcommands.

Line counts (vendored vs fr):
  acl      : 194 vs  10
  client   : 262 vs  10
  cluster  : 357 vs  10
  config   :  83 vs  10
  function : 128 vs  10
  latency  : 121 vs  10
  memory   :  95 vs  10
  module   :  75 vs  10
  pubsub   :  85 vs  10
  script   :  82 vs  10
  slowlog  :  66 vs  10
  debug    :  15 vs  15  (vendored intentionally omits — admin-flagged subcommands)
  command  :  95 vs  95  (already correct)
  object   : 137 vs 137  (already correct)
  xgroup   : 173 vs 173  (already correct)
  xinfo    : 111 vs 111  (already correct)

Impact: client tooling that parses COMMAND INFO to enumerate available subcommands (RedisInsight, monitoring agents, library autoconfig) sees container commands as opaque. ACL category attribution, fast/slow classification, write/read attribution all derived from per-subcommand metadata become unavailable.

Likely fix: extend `SUBCOMMAND_PARENTS_WITH_INFO` in crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs (line ~15563) from the current four entries to include acl, client, cluster, config, function, latency, memory, module, pubsub, script, slowlog. Excluding `debug` mirrors vendored's behavior (it suppresses debug subcommand fan-out, likely because the admin-flagged subcommands shouldn't be enumerated in COMMAND INFO).

Test plan:
  - Pin the line counts above in a fr-command integration test (or rely on byte-for-byte fixture comparison against legacy_redis_code outputs).
  - Verify SUBCOMMAND_TABLE entries match vendored for each affected subcommand's flags/arity/keyspec by spot-checking client|kill, cluster|getkeysinslot, memory|usage.
  - Add fr-command unit test: COMMAND INFO CLUSTER returns at least the canonical cluster|count-failure-reports entry with arity=3, admin/stale flags, @admin/@slow/@dangerous categories.

title: string.format accepts width/precision >=100 (vendored caps at 99 via scanformat)
description:
Upstream Lua 5.1.5 lstrlib.c::scanformat hardcodes a 2-digit cap
on both width and precision; a third digit raises 'invalid format
(width or precision too long)'. fr's format spec parser accepts
arbitrary-length numeric runs, producing huge formatted output for
specs like '%.999d' or '%999d' that vendored rejects up front.

Repro:
  EVAL "local ok,e = pcall(string.format, '%.100d', 1); return tostring(ok)..':'..tostring(e):sub(1,80)" 0
    vendored: false:invalid format (width or precision too long)
    fr:       true:000000...000 (100 zeros)

  EVAL "local ok,e = pcall(string.format, '%100d', 1); ..."
    same divergence

  Boundary:
    precision=99 → both succeed
    precision=100 → vendored errors, fr succeeds
    width=99 → both succeed
    width=100 → vendored errors, fr succeeds

Fix: at the format spec parser (crates/fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs
near the %s/%d/%g handling), reject any width or precision whose
decimal value exceeds 99 with the upstream wording. The simplest
place is wherever the format spec is extracted before dispatch on
conv type.
close_reason: completed

title: coroutine.create/wrap/resume/status hardcode user_script:1: and 'name' in errors
description:
Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4:

  pcall(coroutine.create)        v=bad argument #1 to '?' (Lua function expected)
                                  fr=user_script:1: bad argument #1 to 'create' (Lua function expected)
  pcall(coroutine.resume)        v=bad argument #1 to '?' (coroutine expected)
                                  fr=user_script:1: bad argument #1 to 'resume' (coroutine expected)
  pcall(coroutine.status,1)      v=bad argument #1 to '?' (coroutine expected)
                                  fr=user_script:1: bad argument #1 to 'status' (coroutine expected)
  pcall(coroutine.wrap,'x')      v=bad argument #1 to '?' (Lua function expected)
                                  fr=user_script:1: bad argument #1 to 'wrap' (Lua function expected)

Four coroutine.* handlers at fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs lines ~5864-5901 hardcode the named/prefixed wording. Route through lua_format_argerror with the per-function name so pcall(coroutine.X,…) renders the anonymous shape (`'?'` name, no user_script:1: prefix).

NOTE: existing test lua_coroutine_arg_validation_rejects_non_function_creation pins the direct-call shape ("user_script:1: bad argument #1 to 'create' ..."). That stays the same; only the pcall path changes.
close_reason: Routed all 4 coroutine.* argerror sites (create/wrap/resume/status) through lua_format_argerror. Regression test coroutine_argerror_pcall_shape_96j2u pins 9 pcall cases + 4 direct-call regressions. Existing 11 coroutine tests still pass. Live-diff vs vendored 16380: 7/7 scripted cases match.

title: Reject CONFIG SET databases as immutable
description: Redis marks databases as IMMUTABLE_CONFIG in legacy_redis_code/redis/src/config.c. FrankenRedis currently accepts CONFIG SET databases via static CONFIG fallback; freeze parity by rejecting runtime mutation while preserving CONFIG GET visibility.
close_reason: Completed

Remove the dead helper or wire it back into the harness so fr-conformance --all-targets passes -D warnings again, then verify with rch check/clippy/tests.

title: Conformance: MIGRATE rejects non-canonical integer args
close_reason: Added MIGRATE non-canonical integer conformance cases and enforced strict port parsing

title: COMMAND LIST omits 128 'parent|sub' namespaced subcommand entries that vendored 7.2.4 emits (acl|cat, client|kill, cluster|nodes, etc.)
description:
Differential probe vs vendored 16380:
  COMMAND LIST | wc -l
    vendored: 370 entries
    fr      : 242 entries (missing 128)
  COMMAND INFO 'client|kill'
    vendored: client|kill, -3, admin noscript loading stale, 0, 0, 0, @admin
    fr      : nil

The 128 missing entries are the 'parent|sub' namespaced rows for every container command. Examples:
  acl|cat, acl|deluser, acl|dryrun, acl|genpass, acl|getuser, acl|help,
  acl|list, acl|load, acl|log, acl|save, acl|setuser, acl|users, acl|whoami,
  client|caching, client|getname, client|getredir, client|help, client|id,
  client|info, client|kill, client|list, client|no-evict, client|no-touch,
  client|pause, client|reply, client|setinfo, client|setname, client|tracking,
  client|trackinginfo, client|unblock, client|unpause,
  cluster|addslots ... (all 25 cluster|* subcommands),
  command|count, command|docs, command|getkeys, command|getkeysandflags,
  command|help, command|info, command|list,
  config|get, config|help, config|resetstat, config|rewrite, config|set,
  function|delete, function|dump, function|flush, function|help, function|kill,
  function|list, function|load, function|restore, function|stats,
  latency|*, memory|*, module|*, object|*, pubsub|*, script|*, slowlog|*,
  xgroup|*, xinfo|*

Upstream emits these from server.c::commandCommand by walking each container command's subcommand_dict and synthesising a 'parent|sub' name with the subcommand's arity / flags / categories.

Fix: expand fr-command's COMMAND LIST emitter to walk each container's subcommand table and emit a synthetic 'parent|sub' entry per subcommand, matching upstream's name|arity|flags|categories for each. COMMAND INFO 'client|kill' must dispatch to the same metadata.

Note COMMAND COUNT (241 vendored, 242 fr) is asymmetric here: COUNT returns top-level command count only (excluding the parent|sub entries), so the missing-subcommands defect doesn't affect COUNT — see separate bead bx2i8 for the spurious sentinel entry.
close_reason:
Added SUBCOMMAND_TABLE constant with 129 namespaced parent|sub rows in fr-command/src/lib.rs, sourced by programmatic sweep of vendored Redis 7.2.4 COMMAND INFO output. Updated COMMAND (no arg), COMMAND LIST, COMMAND LIST FILTERBY PATTERN, and COMMAND INFO to walk both COMMAND_TABLE and SUBCOMMAND_TABLE. COMMAND COUNT remains top-level only per upstream commandCountCommand. Pinned via command_list_includes_namespaced_subcommands_per_upstream covering: total namespaced count, 15 representative spot-checks across all 15 containers, COMMAND COUNT staying at top-level, COMMAND INFO 'client|kill' returning per-sub metadata, and FILTERBY PATTERN 'client|*' returning all 18 client subs.

Verified byte-for-byte parity vs vendored 7.2.4 (16380): COMMAND LIST count 370/370, namespaced 129/129, COMMAND INFO 'client|kill' identical bytes including ACL categories @admin/@slow/@dangerous/@connection (derived by command_info_entry from name+flags). Diff sweep of all 129 subs against vendored COMMAND INFO shows zero divergences. COMMAND COUNT stays at 241/241.

Out of scope (deferred): COMMAND DOCS for namespaced subs (still emits top-level only). DOCS requires summary/since/group strings per sub which would mean a much larger metadata table; filed separately if needed. Per-sub key_specs sub-array fidelity also deferred — current entries emit empty key_specs with first/last/step matching upstream.

879/879 fr-command lib tests pass (excluding 4 pre-existing stream-related failures unrelated to this bead). 358/358 fr-runtime lib tests + all 138 integration tests pass. Workspace clippy clean under -D warnings.

title: CONFIG SET shutdown-on-sigterm/sigint accepts the conflicting 'save nosave' combo — vendored 7.2.4 rejects with 'shutdown options SAVE and NOSAVE can't be used simultaneously'
description:
Differential probe vs vendored 16380:
  CONFIG SET shutdown-on-sigterm 'save now nosave'
    vendored: ERR CONFIG SET failed (possibly related to argument 'shutdown-on-sigterm') - shutdown options SAVE and NOSAVE can't be used simultaneously
    fr      : OK

Upstream config.c:2378-2385 (validateShutdownOnSigFlags) explicitly checks the SAVE+NOSAVE combination and rejects it with that exact wording. Each individual token (default, save, nosave, now, force) is valid; the conflict only fires when both 'save' and 'nosave' appear in the same combination.

fr's handler at crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs:8739-8765 validates the per-token allowlist but doesn't check the mutually-exclusive pair.

Fix: after the per-token allowlist check, also verify that the parsed token set doesn't include both 'save' and 'nosave'.
close_reason: Added the SAVE+NOSAVE conflict check to fr-runtime's shutdown-on-sigterm/sigint handler at fr-runtime/src/lib.rs:8739-8765, mirroring upstream config.c::validateShutdownOnSigFlags (lines 2378-2385). After the existing per-token allowlist passes, the lowercased value is split and checked for both 'save' and 'nosave' tokens; the conflict is surfaced via config_set_failed with the exact upstream wording 'shutdown options SAVE and NOSAVE can't be used simultaneously'. All other combinations (default, save+now, save+force, nosave+now+force, etc.) still flow through as OK. Pinned via config_set_shutdown_on_sig_rejects_save_nosave_combination_per_upstream which exercises 4 conflict orderings (save nosave, nosave save, save now nosave, nosave force save) for both shutdown-on-sigterm and shutdown-on-sigint, plus 7 valid non-conflicting combinations. Live differential probe vs vendored 16380 confirms identical conflict-error wording for both subkeys and identical 'OK' for valid combos. Workspace clippy clean under -D warnings.

title: DEBUG missing subcommands: STRINGMATCH-LEN, JMAP, LISTPACK-ENTRIES
description: fr rejects three DEBUG subcommands that vendored Redis 7.2.4 supports (when enable-debug-command=local is set): STRINGMATCH-LEN <pattern> <string>, JMAP (jemalloc memory map), LISTPACK-ENTRIES (toggle aggressive listpack-entry threshold). fr returns 'ERR unknown subcommand or wrong number of arguments for X. Try DEBUG HELP.' Upstream debug.c (search for stringmatchLen, jemalloc_mallctl, listpack-entries) defines these. They are diagnostic/testing helpers used by upstream's test suite (notably tests/unit/type/list-3.tcl, tests/integration/debug.tcl). Implementation effort is low: STRINGMATCH-LEN is a thin wrapper over the existing stringmatch logic to count input bytes consumed; LISTPACK-ENTRIES toggles a global ceiling; JMAP requires jemalloc which fr does not vendor and would emit a 'jemalloc not enabled' error in its absence. Recommended: implement STRINGMATCH-LEN (low effort), implement LISTPACK-ENTRIES as a no-op accepting yes/no/auto + reading state via DEBUG OBJECT, stub JMAP with 'jemalloc not enabled' error matching upstream's --without-jemalloc build behavior. Reproduce: redis-cli -p 16480 DEBUG STRINGMATCH-LEN 'a*' abc -> ERR unknown subcommand. Surfaced during REVIEW PHASE differential-probe sweep.
close_reason: Not a bug. Verified against upstream debug.c in legacy_redis_code/redis/src/debug.c: STRINGMATCH-LEN, JMAP, and LISTPACK-ENTRIES are NOT real DEBUG subcommands in Redis 7.2.4. Upstream only has STRINGMATCH-TEST (already implemented in fr at crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:20517). The earlier probe appeared to show divergence because vendored rejects EVERY unknown DEBUG subcommand with the umbrella 'DEBUG command not allowed' error (enable-debug-command=no by default), whereas fr (started with enable-debug-command=yes in our probe env) correctly returned 'ERR unknown subcommand or wrong number of arguments for X. Try DEBUG HELP.' which IS the upstream wording for unknown subcommands. If vendored had been started with --enable-debug-command=yes, it would emit the same 'unknown subcommand' reply. No source code change required.

title: Lua attempt-to-X errors omit local/field/upvalue/global label for index/arith/length/unary-neg
description:
Lua 5.1's runtime errors include the accessor that resolved to the bad
value: "attempt to index local 'mylocal' (a nil value)",
"attempt to perform arithmetic on field 'count' (a string value)", etc.
fr already includes the label for the call and concatenate paths but
omits it for index, arithmetic (binary and unary -), and length (#).

Probed against vendored Redis 7.2.4 on :16380 / fr on :16480:

  local t = nil; return t.field
    vendored: attempt to index local 't' (a nil value)
    fr:       attempt to index a nil value

  local t = {a=nil}; return t.a.b
    vendored: attempt to index field 'a' (a nil value)
    fr:       attempt to index a nil value

  local x = nil; return x + 1
    vendored: attempt to perform arithmetic on local 'x' (a nil value)
    fr:       attempt to perform arithmetic on a nil value

  local s = nil; return #s
    vendored: attempt to get length of local 's' (a nil value)
    fr:       attempt to get length of a nil value

  local b = true; return b.f
    vendored: attempt to index local 'b' (a boolean value)
    fr:       attempt to index a boolean value

  local nm = 5; return nm.f
    vendored: attempt to index local 'nm' (a number value)
    fr:       attempt to index a number value

Upstream (lvm.c::luaG_typeerror): walks the call stack at runtime to
recover the syntactic name of the offending operand from the bytecode's
debug info. fr's tree-walker has direct access to the AST expression so
it can synthesize the same label without bytecode debug info.

The infrastructure already exists in fr — `callee_label(expr, env)`
returns "local 'X'" / "field 'X'" / "upvalue 'X'" / "global 'X'" / "?"
and is used by the call and concatenate paths. Wire it into:

- Expr::Index (line ~3206) and Expr::Field (~3221) — for "attempt to index"
- Expr::UnaryOp(Neg, …) (line ~3170) — for "attempt to perform arithmetic"
- Expr::UnaryOp(Len, …) (line ~3184) — for "attempt to get length of"
- Expr::BinOp arithmetic arms — for "attempt to perform arithmetic"

Test plan: pin each of the 6 cases above plus regressions where the
label is empty (e.g. arithmetic on a function-call result).
close_reason: Added type_error_with_label helper in crates/fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs that builds the upstream-shaped wording 'attempt to <op> <label> (a TYPE value)' using the existing callee_label helper. Wired into four error sites: Expr::Index and Expr::Field (now name the indexed local/field/upvalue/global), UnaryOp::Neg and UnaryOp::Len (now label the operand), and binary arithmetic operators (Add/Sub/Mul/Div/Mod/Pow) after metamethod fallback. Regression test lua_attempt_to_X_errors_include_accessor_label_9ckvq pins 15 cases including the unlabeled fallback when the operand has no syntactic name. Live diff vs vendored Redis 7.2.4 on :16380 clean across 17 probes covering nil/bool/number/table/string operands and field/local accessors.

title: Guard BLMPOP/BZMPOP numeric conversions
close_reason: Guarded count/numkeys conversions (BLMPOP/BZMPOP + related limits)

title: Lua math.deg/rad/sinh/cosh/tanh missing + nan vs -nan emission
description: Several math library functions Redis 7.2.4's Lua 5.1 exposes are not registered in fr's globals: math.deg, math.rad, math.sinh, math.cosh, math.tanh. Scripts using them get 'attempt to call a nil value'. (math.atan2, math.ldexp, math.log/log10, math.pow/^ already work.) Additionally tostring(0/0) emits 'nan' in fr but '-nan' in vendored -- C printf %g of a quiet NaN with a negative sign bit (which IEEE 754 0/0 produces on x86-64) prints '-nan'. Fix: (1) add the five trig helpers to the math globals registration alongside floor/ceil/abs (lib.rs ~line 1829); (2) wire dispatch handlers in call_builtin's math.* arm using Rust's stdlib (f64::to_degrees, to_radians, sinh, cosh, tanh); (3) update lua_number_to_string's NaN branch to emit '-nan' instead of 'nan' to match C %.14g on x86-64.
close_reason: Five missing Lua math helpers added + NaN-sign format. (1) Added math.deg/math.rad/math.sinh/math.cosh/math.tanh to the math globals registration in fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs:1850 and the call_builtin dispatch arm at line 3662 -- Rust's f64 stdlib supplies each directly (to_degrees, to_radians, sinh, cosh, tanh). (2) Updated lua_number_to_string's NaN special case to emit '-nan' when n.is_sign_negative() (which IEEE 754 0/0 produces on x86-64), matching C printf %g via Lua's %.14g. Live differential probe: math.deg(pi)=180, math.rad(180)=pi, math.sinh(0)=0, math.cosh(0)=1, math.tanh(0)=0, tostring(0/0)='-nan' all byte-exact with vendored. Tests: fr-command 910 pass.

title: Lua __newindex metamethod (table assignment hook)
description: Sub-bead of frankenredis-00gjf metamethods epic. Lua 5.1 invokes __newindex(table, key, value) when ASSIGNING to a missing/nil key on a table whose metatable has __newindex. Probe: 'local stored={}; local t=setmetatable({},{__newindex=function(_,k,v) stored[k]=v end}); t.x=1; return stored.x' → vendored 1, fr nil. Also __newindex as TABLE delegates writes there. Implementation: at every table-assignment site (Stmt::Assign with Expr::Index/Expr::Field LHS), check the metatable __newindex; rawset bypasses (already correct). Touch sites: assign_to_field/assign_to_index logic in exec_stmt Assign branch (search for 'Expr::Field' inside Assign). Filed during 00gjf decomposition.
close_reason: Added table_assign_with_newindex() routing all table writes through Lua 5.1's __newindex chain. Existing keys bypass the metamethod and write directly; missing keys with __newindex=function invoke (table, key, value); __newindex=table cascades the write (recursively, up to 16 hops); __newindex=non-nil-non-callable emits 'attempt to index a TYPE value' (matching vendored which tries to index the handler value); rawset() still bypasses entirely since it goes through LuaTable::set directly. Wired into table_set_by_expr so all Stmt::Assign LHS Expr::Field and Expr::Index sites honor it. Verified live diff vs vendored on port 16380: 10/10 cases match including function/table handlers, existing-key bypass, rawset bypass, bracket-indexing, nil-value writes, chained-table cascade, recursive __newindex (depth=3 cap), and the non-callable error path. Pinned by lua_newindex_metamethod_intercepts_table_assignment covering 7 sub-cases. All 919 fr-command lib tests pass. Parent bead frankenredis-00gjf remains open for __concat, arithmetic ops, comparison ops, __len, __tostring.

title: fr-command: RESP3 map-typed replies emit as Array instead of Map (HGETALL, CONFIG GET, XINFO STREAM, CLUSTER SHARDS, CLIENT INFO, etc.)
description:
Multiple commands return map-typed replies in RESP3 mode upstream (Redis 7.2 networking.c::addReplyMap*) but fr's command implementations always emit RespFrame::Array regardless of session.resp_protocol_version. Affected commands include:

  - HGETALL h
  - CONFIG GET <param> (returns key/value map)
  - HRANDFIELD h N WITHVALUES
  - CLIENT INFO (single client info as RESP3 map)
  - XINFO STREAM/GROUPS/CONSUMERS

When redis-cli connects with -3, fr replies with array form (display: 'k\nv\nk\nv\n...'); upstream replies with map form (display: 'k v\nk v\n...').

Acceptance: when session.resp_protocol_version == 3, the listed commands emit RespFrame::Map(Some(pairs)) where each pair is (key, value). For fr-command this means routing the protocol_version through the dispatch path or providing a render-time override. fr-protocol already has the Map variant (crates/fr-protocol/src/lib.rs:13).

Reference: ad-hoc differential probe of fr (port 16700) vs vendored redis 7.2.4 (port 16701) with -3 flag.

title: v8s5 part a: document upstream DUMP/RESTORE payload format (fr-store reference)
description:
Preparation sub-bead for br-frankenredis-v8s5. Extract the upstream DUMP payload layout from legacy_redis_code/redis/src/cluster.c::dumpCommand + rdb.c::rdbSaveObject + restoreCommand + rdb.c::rdbLoadObject and write it up as crates/fr-store/docs/dump-format.md.

Scope:
  * Overall payload: type_byte + rdbSaveObject + version (u16 LE) + CRC64 (u64 LE).
  * Version field semantics (currently upstream is version 11 for 7.2.x, 12 for 7.4.x).
  * Per-type rdbSaveObject layout for STRING/LIST_QUICKLIST_2/SET_INTSET/SET_LISTPACK/HASH_LISTPACK/HASH_METADATA/ZSET_LISTPACK/STREAM_LISTPACKS_3.
  * CRC64 polynomial (Redis CRC64 with the Jones polynomial, already implemented at crates/fr-persist/src/lib.rs:848).
  * A comparison table: current frankenredis-internal layout vs upstream expectation, field-by-field.

No code changes.
close_reason: Reference doc crates/fr-store/docs/dump-format.md already exists (260 lines) covering envelope/footer, RDB length + string encodings, all upstream type tags (0/2/4/5/11/16/17/18/20/21 + Redis 7.4 24/25), per-type body layouts, fr-store delta vs upstream, and decoder/encoder checklist. No further work needed for the docs-only sub-bead.

title: CONFIG GET missing hidden parameters: use-exit-on-panic, aof-disable-auto-gc, rdb-key-save-delay, key-load-delay, watchdog-period, cluster-ping-interval, loading-process-events-interval-bytes
description:
Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4:

  127.0.0.1:16380> CONFIG GET aof-disable-auto-gc
  1) "aof-disable-auto-gc"
  2) "no"
  127.0.0.1:16480> CONFIG GET aof-disable-auto-gc
  (empty array)

Same divergence for: use-exit-on-panic (no), aof-disable-auto-gc (no), rdb-key-save-delay (0), key-load-delay (0), watchdog-period (0), cluster-ping-interval (0), loading-process-events-interval-bytes (2097152).

Per upstream config.c lines 3091, 3096, 3122, 3177, 3178, 3183, 3192, 3203, 3232 — these are HIDDEN_CONFIG entries (don't appear in CONFIG GET '*' wildcard sweep but DO respond when explicitly named). Defaults from createBoolConfig/createIntConfig/createOffTConfig third-from-last argument.

Root cause: CONFIG_STATIC_PARAMS in fr-runtime/src/lib.rs (line 351) does not list these 7 hidden parameters. canonical_static_config_param returns None and CONFIG GET resolves to empty array.

Fix: add the 7 entries to CONFIG_STATIC_PARAMS with their upstream default values. They should NOT be exposed via CONFIG GET '*' wildcard (already hidden via the existing 'don't list' machinery if any; otherwise add a hidden tag), but should respond to direct CONFIG GET <name>.

Acceptance: CONFIG GET <name> for each of the 7 returns the upstream default value.
close_reason: Added CONFIG_STATIC_HIDDEN_PARAMS table (use-exit-on-panic, aof-disable-auto-gc, rdb-key-save-delay, key-load-delay, watchdog-period, cluster-ping-interval, loading-process-events-interval-bytes) with upstream defaults from config.c lines 3091/3096/3177/3178/3183/3203/3232. handle_config_get only consults this table for literal (non-glob) patterns, mirroring upstream config.c::configGetCommand line 998 'if (config->flags & HIDDEN_CONFIG) continue;' which skips hidden configs in wildcard sweeps. canonical_static_config_param now chains both tables. Verified via differential probe: all 7 names respond to exact CONFIG GET (matches vendored), zero appear in CONFIG GET '*' (also matches vendored). Pin: config_get_returns_hidden_params_only_for_exact_name_per_upstream.

title: Canonicalize zero in sorted-set BTree ordering
close_reason: Completed

title: Lua cjson.decode error message wording differs from upstream
description:
Vendored Redis 7.2.4 uses the bundled cjson decoder which produces precise error messages like:

  user_script:1: Expected value but found invalid token at character 6

fr emits a generic wrapper instead:

  invalid JSON: [1,2,not_json

Reproduction:
  EVAL "return cjson.decode('[1,2,not_json')" 0
    vendored: ERR user_script:1: Expected value but found invalid token at character 6 script: <sha>, on @user_script:1.
    fr:       ERR invalid JSON: [1,2,not_json script: <sha>, on @user_script:1.

Impact: low — error message wording only. Real applications generally pcall around cjson.decode and inspect the result, not the message text, so this is mainly a parity-pinning issue.

Implementation: fr's cjson.decode handler (likely in crates/fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs) needs to produce upstream-shaped messages keyed by the character offset and the kind of parse failure. Vendored cjson messages include:
  - Expected value but found invalid token at character N
  - Expected colon but found T at character N
  - Expected comma or end of array but found T at character N
  - Unterminated string at character N
  - Invalid string character at character N
  - etc.

Test plan: pin three or four canonical messages (invalid token, unterminated string, expected colon) under EVAL and run them against both servers.

title: fr-command has 6 pre-existing clippy errors blocking '--all-targets -D warnings' CI
description:
`cargo clippy -p fr-command --lib -- -D warnings` fails with 6 pre-existing errors that block any swarm agent's clean-clippy step:

  16644:31 fn wait_cmd unused variable `store`
  19659:4  fn bitfield_clamp is never used (dead code)
  3882:58  manual char comparison `|c| c == 'p' || c == 'P'` -> `['p','P']`
  3905:20  redundant guard `Some(s) if s.is_empty()`
  9961:24  collapsible `if let ... if ident == 'callback'`
  lua_eval.rs:5127  collapsible `if let LuaValue::Table` + `if let LuaValue::Str`

All mechanical lint cleanups. The bitfield_clamp dead code requires a judgment call (delete vs prefix _ vs wire it up); rest are pure refactors with no behavior change.
close_reason: Removed unused store binding (wait_cmd → _store), deleted dead bitfield_clamp wrapper, replaced manual char comparison with array, replaced redundant guard with literal pattern, collapsed two if-let nests, removed duplicated #[test] attribute on swapdb_via_dispatch_rejected_in_cluster_mode (was causing double registration). fr-command --all-targets clippy now green under -D warnings. fr-command 892 / fr-runtime 348 / fr-store 332 lib tests pass.

title: EPIC: Harden security beyond RESP parsing — persistence integrity, connection auth, and adversarial resilience
description:
# Security Hardening Epic

## Why
AGENTS.md Security Doctrine requires: "Defend against malformed protocol frames, replay/order attacks, and persistence tampering." The reality check found:
- RESP parser correctly rejects malformed frames ✓
- CRC64 checksums on RDB files ✓  
- No evidence of replay attack defense beyond basic auth
- No evidence of persistence tampering detection beyond CRC64
- No adversarial fuzzing of the RESP parser or command handlers
- No rate limiting or connection throttling

## What Success Looks Like
- Fuzz test for RESP parser (catches crashes on malformed input)
- Fuzz test for RDB decoder (catches crashes on corrupted files)
- AOF integrity verification (detect truncation/corruption on load)
- Documentation of threat model per AGENTS.md requirements
close_reason: All sub-tasks complete: RESP parser fuzz (29c7, 40K inputs), RDB/AOF decoder fuzz (9zk5, 30K inputs), threat model documentation (fap1, 6 subsystems). Zero panics found in 70K total random inputs.

title: fr-command: 4 xgroup_* inline tests broken on main since peer a6af191 (XINFO FULL group lag now Integer(1) instead of BulkString(None))
description:
On main since commit a6af191 ("fix: compute XINFO FULL group lag [frankenredis-mut9]"), four #[test]s in crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs panic at the `lag` field assertion because they pin BulkString(None) while the new (correct, upstream-aligned) implementation returns Integer(N) for groups with a backlog:

  tests::xgroup_create_and_xinfo_groups_report_created_group       L26468
  tests::xgroup_validation_missing_mkstream_wrongtype_and_syntax   L26569
  tests::xgroup_setid_updates_group_cursor_and_supports_dollar     L26849
  tests::xgroup_createconsumer_and_delconsumer_update_group_consumer_counts  L27093

Failure shape (one example):

  left:  ... last-delivered-id 0-0 entries-read None lag Integer(1)  (actual, correct)
  right: ... last-delivered-id 0-0 entries-read None lag BulkString(None)  (stale)

a6af191's behavior matches upstream Redis 7.2 t_stream.c::xinfoReplyWithStreamInfo which always emits the integer lag value when the group has a positive offset behind the stream tail — BulkString(None) was the pre-implementation placeholder.

Fix: align the four tests with the new lag emission. No production code change.

Acceptance: cargo test -p fr-command -- xgroup passes 8 / 8.

Reference: /testing-golden-artifacts cleanup of stale XINFO GROUPS pin on 2026-05-04.
notes: Aligned 5 lag-pin sites across 4 inline tests with the post-mut9 lag emission (Integer(N) where N = entries between group cursor and stream tail; was BulkString(None) placeholder). Sites: xgroup_create_and_xinfo_groups_report_created_group lag=0, xgroup_validation_missing_mkstream_wrongtype_and_syntax lag=0, xgroup_setid_updates_group_cursor_and_supports_dollar lag=1 then lag=0 after SETID $, xgroup_createconsumer_and_delconsumer_update_group_consumer_counts lag=1 (stays 1 after delconsumer). cargo test -p fr-command -- xgroup now 8/8; clippy clean. No production code change.

title: Add metamorphic tests for Redis Streams in fr-store
Add RDB decoder fuzz test for crash resilience on corrupted files
close_reason: Added 3 proptest fuzz tests to fr-persist: decode_rdb_never_panics, decode_aof_stream_never_panics, decode_rdb_with_valid_header_never_panics. Each runs 10,000 random cases. All pass — zero panics found. Added proptest dev-dependency.

title: DEBUG OBJECT serializedlength still off for LZF-compressible strings, hash, zset (smaller divergences after xp6mu fix)
description:
After frankenredis-xp6mu landed (DEBUG OBJECT now uses dump_key payload bytes instead of memory_usage_for_key), the fr ↔ vendored divergence dropped by orders of magnitude. Three smaller gaps remain:

  type=str x*100 (100 bytes):  fr=102  vendored=13  (fr-store's encode_rdb_string doesn't apply LZF compression — fr-persist's rdb_encode_string at fr-persist/src/lib.rs:936 does)
  type=hash 2 fields (4 bytes each):  fr=24  vendored=13  (different listpack encoding or threshold)
  type=zset 3 members:  fr=26  vendored=23  (different zset listpack encoding, off by 3)

For STRING, the fix is to mirror fr-persist's rdb_encode_string LZF path into fr-store's encode_rdb_string (or refactor both to share one implementation).

For HASH/ZSET listpack divergences, need to compare fr-store's encode_listpack_strings byte layout vs upstream listpack.c — likely a count or backlen byte that differs.

Filed P3 as a follow-up since the orders-of-magnitude bug is resolved; these are byte-precision parity issues that mostly matter for tools that diff RDB sizes.
close_reason:
Added LZF compression to fr-store's encode_rdb_string by exposing fr-persist::lzf_compress and mirroring rdbSaveLzfStringObject's policy: try LZF for inputs > 20 bytes, emit the 0xC3 [comp_len][orig_len][payload] special encoding when the compressed wire form is strictly smaller than the raw form. Closes the largest residual divergence after xp6mu — DEBUG OBJECT serializedlength for repetitive 100-byte 'x' strings dropped from 102 → 9 bytes (vendored: 13). The remaining 4-byte underrun vs vendored is because fr's LZF implementation is slightly more aggressive than upstream on highly-repetitive input; both produce valid 0xC3-encoded LZF streams.

Verified vs vendored 7.2.4 (16480 vs 16380):
  len=25   fr=8   ven=12
  len=50   fr=8   ven=12
  len=100  fr=9   ven=13
  len=200  fr=9   ven=13
  len=500  fr=12  ven=16
  non-compressible 25b: fr=32  ven=32 (raw path, identical)

Pinned via debug_object_serializedlength_compresses_repetitive_strings_via_lzf_per_upstream covering both the LZF path (100x compresses to <30 bytes) and the raw fallback (25-byte non-compressible string emits exactly 26 bytes = 1 length-prefix + 25 raw).

Out of scope (deferred): hash listpack (fr=24 vs vendored=13) and zset listpack (fr=26 vs vendored=23) divergences require deeper changes to fr-store's encode_listpack_strings — separate beads if pursued.

885/885 fr-command lib + 332/332 fr-store lib pass. Workspace clippy clean under -D warnings.

title: [HIGH] HINCRBYFLOAT Infinity error wording + HRANDFIELD arity error drift
description: Surfaced by live_redis_core_hash_matches_runtime: 5 HINCRBYFLOAT inf/-inf/inf-minus-inf cases emit 'ERR increment would produce NaN or Infinity' where upstream emits 'ERR value is NaN or Infinity'. Plus hrandfield_too_many_args returns 'ERR wrong number of arguments' where upstream returns 'ERR syntax error'. Fix in fr-command HINCRBYFLOAT + HRANDFIELD.
close_reason: Specific error wording cases listed in bead are no longer failing in live_redis_core_strings_matches_runtime / live_redis_core_hash_matches_runtime under STRICT mode (remaining strings failures are absolute-time TTL, tracked on 7rp6).

title: Generate real run_id and report real process_id in INFO server
close_reason: INFO server now reports real run_id (40-char hex from LCG), process_id (std::process::id), and tcp_port (from CLI args)

title: Lua table.concat accepts non-string separator and emits garbage
description:
fr's table.concat handler accepts ANY LuaValue as the separator (arg #2) and routes it through to_display_string, producing garbage output like "1table:0x...2table:0x...3" when a table is passed as the separator. Upstream Redis 7.2.4 (ltablib.c::tconcat) uses luaL_optlstring, which requires the separator to be a string or number (or absent/nil), and otherwise raises:

  bad argument #2 to 'concat' (string expected, got <type>)

Reproduction (port 16380 vendored vs 16480 fr):
  EVAL "return table.concat({1,2,3}, {})" 0
    vendored: ERR user_script:1: bad argument #2 to 'concat' (string expected, got table)
    fr:       1table:0x006238999006f02table:0x006238999006f03

Impact: a script that passes a non-string separator (often via a bug or attacker-controlled value) produces meaningless output on fr but errors on vendored. Detection-as-an-error semantics matter.

Fix location: crates/fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs line ~5729 — the sep extraction block of "table.concat". Reject non-string/non-number separators with the upstream wording (with the user_script:1: prefix per luaL_argerror).

Test plan:
  - Pin EVAL "return table.concat({1,2,3}, {})" -> error with vendored wording.
  - Pin EVAL "return table.concat({1,2,3}, function() end)" -> error.
  - Pin EVAL "return table.concat({1,2,3}, true)" -> error.
  - Pin EVAL "return table.concat({1,2,3}, 5)" -> "1525253" (number coerces to its string repr).
  - Pin EVAL "return table.concat({1,2,3}, ', ')" -> "1, 2, 3".

title: Implement LATENCY sample tracking infrastructure in Store
description:
# Implement LATENCY sample tracking infrastructure

## What
Redis LATENCY monitoring tracks execution time samples per named event type. When a command exceeds the latency-monitor-threshold (configurable via CONFIG SET), a sample is recorded. LATENCY LATEST/HISTORY/RESET/GRAPH/DOCTOR then query these samples.

## Design
Add a LatencyTracker struct to Store (or a new pub field):

```rust
pub struct LatencySample {
    pub timestamp_sec: u64,
    pub duration_ms: u64,
}

pub struct LatencyTracker {
    /// Per-event-type sample ring buffers. Max 160 samples per event (Redis default).
    events: HashMap<String, VecDeque<LatencySample>>,
    /// Threshold in milliseconds. 0 = disabled.
    pub threshold_ms: u64,
}
```

Event types in Redis: "command", "fast-command", "fork", "expire-cycle", "eviction-cycle", "aof-write", etc. For FrankenRedis, start with "command" and "fast-command".

## Implementation plan
1. Add LatencyTracker to Store
2. Add pub fn record_latency_sample(&mut self, event: &str, duration_ms: u64, now_sec: u64)
3. Add pub fn latency_latest(&self) -> Vec<(String, LatencySample)>
4. Add pub fn latency_history(&self, event: &str) -> &[LatencySample]
5. Add pub fn latency_reset(&mut self, events: &[&str]) -> usize
6. Wire CONFIG SET latency-monitor-threshold to update tracker.threshold_ms
7. In fr-runtime's command execution path, after measuring duration, call record_latency_sample if duration > threshold

## Why in Store (not Runtime)
So that both the runtime's command handler AND fr-command's latency_cmd can access the data. The runtime records samples; fr-command reads them for LATENCY LATEST/HISTORY.

## Key details
- Ring buffer: max 160 samples per event (matches Redis)
- LATENCY RESET with no args resets ALL events; with args resets named events
- LATENCY GRAPH: generates ASCII art timeline (see Redis source for format)
- LATENCY DOCTOR: generates human-readable analysis text
- threshold_ms=0 means disabled (no samples collected) — this is the default

## Testing
- Unit test: record samples, verify LATEST returns most recent per event
- Unit test: verify ring buffer overflow (161st sample evicts 1st)
- Conformance: CONFIG SET latency-monitor-threshold 1, run slow commands, verify LATENCY LATEST non-empty

## Files to modify
- crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs (LatencyTracker struct + Store field)
- crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs (latency_cmd reads from store)
- crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs (record samples after command execution)
close_reason: Completed

title: Track real total_error_replies in INFO stats
description:
# Track real total_error_replies in INFO stats

## Why
`INFO stats` still hardcodes `total_error_replies:0` even though the runtime already knows when it emits RESP errors. This is a real-data-available stub under the open remediation epic.

## Scope
- Add a store-owned counter for total error replies.
- Increment it when the final client-visible reply is `RespFrame::Error`.
- Wire `INFO stats` to emit the real counter.
- Cover both runtime-managed and delegated command errors with tests.

## Files

- `crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs`

## Validation
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p fr-command info_stats_reports_expire_and_evict_counters -- --exact --nocapture`
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p fr-runtime --lib total_error_replies_counts_runtime_and_delegated_errors -- --exact --nocapture`

close_reason: Implemented store-backed total_error_replies counter, INFO stats wiring, runtime counting, and focused tests

title: Lua math.min/math.max wrong-type wording
description:
fr's math.min and math.max emit a generic 'bad argument to math.min' / 'bad argument to math.max' when an argument is not coercible to a number. Upstream lmathlib.c::math_min/math_max use luaL_checknumber on each arg starting at index 1 and emit the upstream template with the source-location prefix and arg index.

Reproduction:
  EVAL "return math.min(1, 'a')" 0
    vendored: ERR user_script:1: bad argument #2 to 'min' (number expected, got string)
    fr:       ERR bad argument to 'math.min'

Affected handlers: crates/fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs lines ~5046 and ~5062.

Fix: iterate with enumerate(), call lua_check_number(args, idx, "min") to surface upstream wording with prefix and (idx+1)-arg-position.

Test plan:
  - Pin math.min(1, 'a') / math.max(1, 'a') with arg #2 wording.
  - Pin math.min({}, 1) / math.max(nil, 1) with arg #1 wording.
  - Pin math.min() / math.max() unchanged (already correctly emits "got no value").

title: Add live Redis conformance tests for generic key commands
notes: core_generic_live_redis_matches_runtime exists

title: Fix CONFIG GET stub in fr-command to return real store-level values
close_reason: CONFIG GET in dispatch_argv now returns real values for store-level params (encoding thresholds, maxmemory-policy, ziplist aliases). Fixes Lua redis.call('CONFIG','GET',...) returning empty arrays. 3 Lua conformance cases added to verify.

title: MethodCall on non-table receiver wrongly emits 'attempt to call method' instead of 'attempt to index'
description:
Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4:

  local x = 1; x:foo()
    vendored: user_script:1: attempt to index local 'x' (a number value)
    fr:       user_script:1: attempt to call method 'foo' (a nil value)

  local x = nil; x:foo()
    vendored: user_script:1: attempt to index local 'x' (a nil value)
    fr:       user_script:1: attempt to call method 'foo' (a nil value)

Upstream Lua 5.1 grammar: `x:foo(...)` is sugar for `x.foo(x, ...)`. The first step is indexing x with "foo" — if x is not a table (and has no __index meta), indexing fails BEFORE the call is attempted, so the runtime emits the indexing error with accessor context.

fr has two MethodCall sites:
1. Expr::MethodCall direct handler at lua_eval.rs ~line 3638: hits `_ =>` at 3657 emitting bare "attempt to index a TYPE value" (no accessor).
2. eval_call_args last-arg MethodCall handler at ~line 3814: hits `_ =>` at 3828 silently returning LuaValue::Nil — which then bubbles into call_function_with_callee and emits "attempt to call method 'foo' (a nil value)" with the WRONG semantics.

Fix both sites to route through type_error_with_label(obj_expr, &obj, env, "index") for accessor-aware "attempt to index local/field/etc. (a TYPE value)" wording.

NOTE: Table receivers with missing method key continue to emit "attempt to call method 'm' (a nil value)" — that's the correct path because indexing the table succeeds (returns nil), and the call step is what fails. Existing test lua_attempt_to_call_errors_carry_accessor_context covers that case.
close_reason: Fixed both MethodCall handlers to route non-table receivers through type_error_with_label with accessor context: (1) Expr::MethodCall direct handler at lua_eval.rs ~line 3657, and (2) eval_call_args last-arg MethodCall handler at ~line 3828 (which previously silently returned LuaValue::Nil → wrong 'attempt to call method' error). Regression test method_call_non_table_receiver_errors_at_index_step_aaudb pins 4 invalid-receiver cases + 1 table-receiver-with-missing-key sanity (unchanged) + 1 string-receiver sanity. Full release test suite (1013 tests) still passes. Live-diff vs vendored 16380: 6/6 cases match byte-for-byte.

title: Reject CONFIG SET rdbchecksum as immutable
description: Redis marks rdbchecksum as IMMUTABLE_CONFIG in legacy_redis_code/redis/src/config.c. FrankenRedis currently exposes rdbchecksum as a static CONFIG parameter and accepts CONFIG SET through the static fallback. Add runtime and fixture coverage that rejects mutation while preserving CONFIG GET visibility.
close_reason: Completed

title: fr-command BITPOS argc==6 parses end before BIT|BYTE check, inverts upstream precedence
close_reason:
Reordered bitpos() argc==6 trailing-arg parse to start → unit → end, mirroring upstream bitops.c lines 905-927.

CHANGE (crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs):
The unit (BIT|BYTE) check now runs immediately after start is parsed, BEFORE end is parsed. Previously fr parsed start → end → unit, which meant a malformed end would surface 'ERR value is not an integer or out of range' even when the unit token was also bogus — upstream surfaces the syntax error on the bad unit token.

Regression test bitpos_argc6_unit_check_beats_end_parse pins the new precedence: BITPOS bm 1 0 NOTANINT BADUNIT now returns SyntaxError.

Verification:
- cargo test -p fr-command --lib bitpos → 3/3 pass.
- cargo clippy -p fr-command --lib --no-deps -- -D warnings → clean.

[LOW] severity. Wire-format conformance gap, only visible when both end and unit are malformed.

title: ACL DRYRUN 'Command not found' uppercases input; upstream preserves caller's case
description: Differential probe vs vendored Redis 7.2.4: `ACL DRYRUN default unknownCmd` returns `ERR Command 'unknownCmd' not found`. Upstream acl.c::aclCommand DRYRUN passes `c->argv[3]->ptr` verbatim into the `Command '%s' not found` format string, so the reply mirrors whatever case the caller supplied. fr's handle_acl DRYRUN was calling `cmd_name.to_ascii_uppercase()` before formatting, so the same input echoed back as `'UNKNOWNCMD'`.
acceptance_criteria: ACL DRYRUN's command-not-found reply must echo argv[3] verbatim, preserving the caller's exact input case.
close_reason: Removed the to_ascii_uppercase() cast in fr-runtime/src/lib.rs::handle_acl_command DRYRUN branch and pinned with acl_dryrun_command_not_found_preserves_input_case covering mixed-case inputs (`unknownCmd`, `BoGuS`, `abcDEF`).

title: fr-runtime: ACL LOG -1 emits 'value is not an integer or out of range' instead of upstream silent clamp to 0
description: Upstream acl.c::aclCommand 'log' branch parses count via getLongFromObjectOrReply (NULL msg → standard 'value is not an integer or out of range') and silently clamps negative counts to 0. fr-runtime's handle_acl_log rejected negative counts as InvalidInteger. Switch to the silent clamp.
close_reason: Fixed

title: fr-runtime: ACL SETUSER #hash and +/-cmd modifiers accept invalid input silently or use generic 'Syntax error'
description:
Two ACL SETUSER modifier wording/validation gaps:

1. #hash modifier: upstream emits 'The password hash must be exactly 64 characters and contain only lowercase hexadecimal characters' for malformed hashes. fr emitted the generic 'Syntax error'.

2. +cmd / -cmd modifiers: upstream rejects unknown command names with 'Unknown command or category name in ACL'. fr silently accepted any command name (e.g. '+ALL', '-nosuchcmd') as a fictional rule.

Fix:
- Rewrite the #/! hash error to the upstream verbose form.
- Reject unknown command names (via fr_command::is_known_command) in +cmd / -cmd parsers.

Differential probe vs vendored redis 7.2.4 byte-matches all four cases after fix.
close_reason: Fixed

title: Adding any password-protected user globally locks out fresh connections; default-nopass auto-auth ignored once any user has a password
description:
Upstream src/networking.c::createClient assigns DefaultUser to every new client and sets c->authenticated = 1 only when DefaultUser carries USER_FLAG_NOPASS. So a fresh connection auto-authenticates iff the *default* user is nopass — adding other password-protected users (alice, bob, ...) does NOT change the default's auth requirement.

fr's auth_required was instead returning true if ANY ACL user had a password set:
  self.requirepass.is_some() || self.acl_users.values().any(|u| !u.passwords.is_empty())
so `ACL SETUSER alice on '>secret'` would lock out every subsequent fresh connection — even though default remained nopass — until alice was deleted again.

This is a P1-severity defect because:
  - Every operator who adds a per-user ACL with a password (the documented Redis pattern for least-privilege accounts) silently breaks all existing default-nopass clients.
  - The break is total: every command including PING returns NOAUTH on fresh connections.
  - Recovery requires identifying and deleting the password-protected user, since AUTH default '' on the new connection appears to succeed but the next connection still NOAUTHs.

Repro vs vendored 7.2.4 (server with default = on nopass +@all):
  > ACL SETUSER alice on '>secret'
  > PING (fresh connection)
    16380> PONG
    16381 (pre-fix)> NOAUTH Authentication required.
  > ACL SETUSER bob on '#<sha256-hash>'
  > PING (fresh connection)
    16380> PONG
    16381 (pre-fix)> NOAUTH Authentication required.
  > ACL SETUSER charlie on nopass
  > PING
    Both: PONG (the bug only fires on password-protected adds)

Fix: rewrite auth_required to short-circuit on requirepass and then look at the default user's nopass flag. Defensive fallback if default is missing returns true. Four pre-existing fr-runtime tests that pinned the BUG behavior were updated to use set_requirepass (which legitimately deauths default by setting default.nopass=false) for the gate cases.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Fresh default-nopass session auto-authenticates without AUTH. 2. Adding a non-default password-protected user does NOT deauth existing or new sessions — PING / GET still succeed. 3. Adding multiple password-protected users (alice, bob, ...) keeps default auto-auth. 4. set_requirepass(Some(...)) DOES gate (default.nopass becomes false). 5. set_requirepass(None) restores nopass on default. 6. Adding a 'on nopass' user (charlie) doesn't trigger the gate (already correct, no regression).
