title: BITFIELD SET FAIL writes the wrap value instead of returning nil; SAT clamps unsigned-negative to MIN instead of MAX
description:
Two upstream-divergent paths in BITFIELD SET, both caught by differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4 with `BITFIELD bf OVERFLOW <mode> SET u8 0 <input>`.

1. **SAT SET u8 0 -1** was clamping to 0 (MIN). Upstream bitops.c::bitfieldCommand SET path calls `checkUnsignedBitfieldOverflow(newval, 0, bits, owtype, &wrapped)` where `newval` (int64_t) is read as uint64_t, so a negative i64 becomes UINT64_MAX which exceeds max → triggers the positive-overflow path with `*limit = max`. Vendored byte 0 = 0xFF; fr byte 0 = 0x00.

2. **FAIL SET on overflow** was writing the wrap value and returning Integer(0). Upstream's SET path is gated by `if (!(overflow && owtype == BFOVERFLOW_FAIL)) { addReplyLongLong; setUnsignedBitfield; }`, so FAIL-on-overflow returns nil and does NOT write. fr's SET path had a misleading comment 'SET always succeeds per Redis behavior' and was always writing the clamped/wrapped value. This affected ALL FAIL-overflow shapes: unsigned-negative, unsigned-positive (e.g., SET u8 0 1000), and signed under/overflow (e.g., SET i8 0 -200).

Repro:
  > BITFIELD bf OVERFLOW SAT  SET u8 0 -1   then GET bf | xxd
    16380> 0xff (clamped to MAX) ; reply 0
    16381 (pre-fix)> 0x00 ; reply 0
  > BITFIELD bf OVERFLOW FAIL SET u8 0 -1   then GET bf | xxd
    16380> (nil) ; 0x00 (no write)
    16381 (pre-fix)> 0    ; 0xff (writes wrap)
  > BITFIELD bf OVERFLOW FAIL SET u8 0 1000
    16380> (nil)
    16381 (pre-fix)> 0
  > BITFIELD bf OVERFLOW FAIL SET i8 0 -200
    16380> (nil)
    16381 (pre-fix)> 0

Fix: switch the SET branch from bitfield_clamp to bitfield_clamp_with_overflow, signaling positive-overflow via `trigger_pos_overflow = !signed && value < 0` so the unsigned-SAT branch falls through to `(max as i64, true)`. Use the returned `overflowed` flag to short-circuit FAIL by pushing RespFrame::BulkString(None) and skipping the store write. The `value < 0 → (0, true)` arm in the clamp function is preserved unchanged for INCRBY's true-underflow path.
acceptance_criteria: 1. BITFIELD bf OVERFLOW SAT SET u8 0 -1 sets byte 0 to 0xff (clamps to 255). 2. BITFIELD bf OVERFLOW FAIL SET u8 0 -1 returns nil and leaves byte 0 unchanged. 3. BITFIELD bf OVERFLOW FAIL SET u8 0 1000 also returns nil and leaves byte 0 unchanged (positive-overflow FAIL). 4. BITFIELD bf OVERFLOW FAIL SET i8 0 -200 also returns nil (signed underflow FAIL). 5. BITFIELD bf OVERFLOW WRAP SET u8 0 -1 still wraps to 255 (regression check). 6. BITFIELD bf OVERFLOW SAT INCRBY u8 0 -1 from 0 still clamps to 0 (true underflow path unchanged). 7. The signed SET paths (SAT/WRAP/FAIL with i8 negative/positive overflow) keep their existing correct clamps.

title: fr-command: BITFIELD invalid bitfield type / invalid OVERFLOW type wordings lack test pins
description: Source-level fixes already in place at lib.rs:18512/18538/18574/18622/18659/18692 emitting upstream's 'ERR Invalid bitfield type. Use something like i16 u8. Note that u64 is not supported but i64 is.' (for u200/u0/i65/etc.) and 'ERR Invalid OVERFLOW type specified' (for OVERFLOW <bogus>). The bitfield_overflow_{wrap,sat,fail} tests at lib.rs:41052+ pin overflow semantics but no test asserts the rejection wordings. Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4 confirmed both wordings; just need a test pin to lock them.

title: fr-command: BITCOUNT/BITPOS with both BIT and BYTE keywords lacks dedicated test pin
description: BITCOUNT/BITPOS accept either BIT or BYTE as the trailing index-mode flag. Upstream emits 'ERR syntax error' when both are specified together. Source-level guard already enforces this in fr (parser sees the duplicate keyword as unknown trailing arg → SyntaxError). Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4 confirmed wording. Need a regression guard.

title: COMMAND GETKEYSANDFLAGS emits generic [RW, access, update] for 13 commands instead of upstream keyspec flags
description:
Cluster of GETKEYSANDFLAGS divergences against vendored Redis 7.2.4 — fr's generic derived_flags fallback emits [RW, access, update] for any write command that lacks an exact_flags override, but upstream commands.def declares narrower per-keyspec flag sets for store-style writes, in-place rewrites, and inserts.

Affected commands (per upstream commands/<cmd>.json key_specs):

  | command         | dst keyspec           | src keyspec     |
  |-----------------|-----------------------|-----------------|
  | ZINTERSTORE     | OW, UPDATE            | RO, ACCESS      |
  | ZUNIONSTORE     | OW, UPDATE            | RO, ACCESS      |
  | ZDIFFSTORE      | OW, UPDATE            | RO, ACCESS      |
  | SUNIONSTORE     | OW, UPDATE            | RO, ACCESS      |
  | SINTERSTORE     | RW, UPDATE            | RO, ACCESS      |
  | SDIFFSTORE      | OW, UPDATE            | RO, ACCESS      |
  | GEOSEARCHSTORE  | OW, UPDATE            | RO, ACCESS      |
  | ZRANGESTORE     | OW, UPDATE            | RO, ACCESS      |
  | SMOVE           | src: RW, ACCESS, DELETE; dst: RW, INSERT |
  | LSET            | RW, UPDATE            | (single key)    |
  | LTRIM           | RW, DELETE            | (single key)    |
  | LINSERT         | RW, INSERT            | (single key)    |
  | PFMERGE         | dst: RW, ACCESS, INSERT; src: RO, ACCESS |

Add per-command arms in command_key_references_with_exact_flags() at
fr-command/src/lib.rs (the same function btf8o just extended for EVAL).
Source keys for the *STORE variants need the standard numkeys parsing
(numkeys at argv[2] for Z*STORE/ZRANGESTORE; SUNIONSTORE/SINTERSTORE/
SDIFFSTORE/PFMERGE are variadic with no numkeys).

Discovered iter 140 probe sweep.
close_reason: completed

title: Add metamorphic property tests for core Redis command semantics
notes: fr-store/tests/metamorphic.rs has comprehensive property tests for Redis command semantics

title: INFO cpu uses /proc/self/stat (10ms precision) instead of getrusage (us precision)
description: fr's read_cpu_times() reads /proc/self/stat which has CLOCK_TICKS=100Hz (10ms) precision, so used_cpu_sys/used_cpu_user emit values like 241.860000 (only 2 meaningful digits after the decimal). Upstream Redis uses getrusage(RUSAGE_SELF, &self_ru) (server.c::getrusage call) which returns timeval with microsecond precision, e.g. 77.569422. Fix: replace /proc/self/stat parsing with libc::getrusage(RUSAGE_SELF). Then ru_utime / ru_stime each have tv_sec + tv_usec; combine as tv_sec + tv_usec/1_000_000.0. Source: crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:18681 read_cpu_times. Source: crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:18614 parse_linux_proc_self_stat_cpu_times. Note: For RUSAGE_CHILDREN, also need a separate getrusage call to emit used_cpu_sys_children/used_cpu_user_children correctly (currently hardcoded to 0). Also used_cpu_sys_main_thread/used_cpu_user_main_thread should be the per-thread main thread value from getrusage(RUSAGE_THREAD).
close_reason: Wontfix — fr-command's crate-level attribute is #![forbid(unsafe_code)], which blocks direct libc::getrusage usage. All sibling fr-* crates likewise forbid or deny unsafe (fr-runtime uses deny; fr-expire/fr-server/fr-sentinel/fr-eventloop/fr-repl/fr-protocol/fr-config/fr-persist/fr-conformance all forbid). The kernel exposes microsecond-precision per-process CPU split (user vs sys) only via the getrusage syscall — /proc/self/stat is 100Hz (10ms) precision; /proc/self/schedstat is nanosecond but doesn't split user vs sys; clock_gettime(CLOCK_PROCESS_CPUTIME_ID) is also unsafe. rustix 1.1.4 (already in lockfile) does not expose getrusage. Achieving byte-equivalent INFO cpu precision would require either: (a) loosening the no-unsafe policy in fr-command, (b) adding a new fr-syscalls helper crate that locally allows unsafe, or (c) adding the nix crate as a workspace dep. None of these is a small change and the cosmetic precision gap on used_cpu_* INFO fields does not justify the architectural cost — vendored's 6-digit precision vs fr's 3-digit precision (e.g. 241.860000 vs 241.864217) is observable only by clients parsing INFO cpu output, and the trailing-zero format  is already correct. Documenting as a known cosmetic limitation.

FrankenRedis exposes daemonize as a static CONFIG parameter and currently accepts CONFIG SET through the static fallback. title: Implement keyspace notifications (notify-keyspace-events)

title: Align blocking timeout conversion with Redis ceil semantics
description: Redis rounds blocking command timeouts up to the next millisecond via ceill(seconds * 1000) and errors on out-of-range values. FrankenRedis currently truncates in fr-server parse_blocking_deadline and only validates finite/nonnegative syntax in fr-command, which can shorten positive fractional waits and accept oversized finite inputs.

title: COMMAND DOCS containers missing subcommands fan-out
description: fr's COMMAND DOCS for container commands (CLUSTER, CONFIG, CLIENT, COMMAND, MEMORY, OBJECT, SCRIPT, FUNCTION, ACL, LATENCY, SLOWLOG, MODULE, PUBSUB, XGROUP, XINFO) emits a basic entry (summary/since/group/complexity/doc_flags) but NO subcommands field. Vendored emits a 'subcommands' array containing one full COMMAND DOCS entry per subcommand. Implementation: extend command_docs_entry to emit 'subcommands' between arguments and the closing entry, populated by iterating SUBCOMMAND_TABLE for entries matching name|*. command_info_subcommands at lib.rs:15015 has a similar SUBCOMMAND_PARENTS_WITH_INFO list and pattern. Discovered while working frankenredis-f7670.
close_reason: Added command_docs_subcommands() fan-out wired into command_docs_entry. SUBCOMMAND_PARENTS_WITH_DOCS covers 16 containers; iterates SUBCOMMAND_TABLE for matching parent|* rows. Also fixed command_group_for_docs to inherit parent group for subcommand fullnames via split_once('|') recursion. Subcommand counts match vendored across all 15 populated containers (CLUSTER 28, CLIENT 18, ACL 13, etc.). DEBUG correctly has no subcommands (vendored handles internally). Two divergences remain for byte-equivalent diff (filed as followups): (a) subcommand iteration order — vendored uses Redis siphash hashtable order, fr uses alphabetical; (b) per-subcommand arg layouts still hit fallback arg1/arg2 placeholders. Pinned with 4 new tests covering counts/structure/negative cases. Workspace clippy clean, fr-command 910/910 + new pins green.

title: Track real total_reads_processed and total_writes_processed in INFO stats
description:
# Track real total_reads_processed and total_writes_processed in INFO stats

## Why
`INFO stats` still hardcodes `total_reads_processed:0` and `total_writes_processed:0` even though command metadata already distinguishes read and write surfaces.

## Scope
- Add store-owned counters for client-visible read and write commands.
- Use command flags from `fr_command::get_command_flags` to classify completed commands in runtime.
- Wire `INFO stats` to emit the real counters.
- Reset them in `CONFIG RESETSTAT`.
- Add focused tests for one readonly command and one write command, plus INFO visibility.

## Files
- `crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs`

## Validation
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p fr-command info_stats_reports_expire_and_evict_counters -- --exact --nocapture`
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p fr-runtime --lib total_reads_and_writes_processed_counts_classified_commands -- --exact --nocapture`
- `rch exec -- cargo check -p fr-runtime --lib`
close_reason: Implemented store-backed read/write counters, INFO stats wiring, resetstat coverage, and focused tests

title: Strict CONFIG SET integer parsing for store thresholds
description: CONFIG SET for list/hash/set/zset listpack thresholds accepted +1/01 and returned generic integer errors. Redis rejects non-canonical ints and uses specific parse/range messages. Use strict parsing and emit CONFIG SET failed messages.
close_reason: Strict CONFIG SET parsing + Redis-style errors + tests

title: COMMAND GETKEYSANDFLAGS emits only [access] for EVAL/EVALSHA/FCALL keys instead of [RW, access, update]
description:
COMMAND GETKEYSANDFLAGS EVAL emits only the 'access' flag per key, missing the 'RW' and 'update' flags vendored emits for script keys.

Repro:
  COMMAND GETKEYSANDFLAGS EVAL "return 1" 2 k1 k2 a b
  vendored: k1 -> [RW, access, update], k2 -> [RW, access, update]
  fr:       k1 -> [access],              k2 -> [access]

EVAL/EVALSHA/FCALL falls through fr-command/src/lib.rs:782's command_key_references_with_exact_flags() (no special-case) and lands in the generic derived_flags path at line 786-794. The COMMAND_TABLE entry for 'eval' (line 14142) has flags 'noscript stale skip_monitor no_mandatory_keys movablekeys' — no 'readonly' or 'write' — so command_getkeysandflags_flags() returns the default &['access'].

Upstream commands.def for EVAL/EVALSHA/FCALL declares keySpec flags = ['RW', 'ACCESS', 'UPDATE'] (the script can mutate the keys). The _RO variants declare ['RO', 'ACCESS']. fr already has this taxonomy documented at fr-command/src/lib.rs:15398-15410 for the COMMAND DOCS notes path (bead 5t2pb), but that override doesn't reach the GETKEYSANDFLAGS code path.

Fix: add EVAL/EVALSHA/FCALL/EVAL_RO/EVALSHA_RO/FCALL_RO arms in command_key_references_with_exact_flags() that consume the numkeys integer from argv[2] (or numkeys is the first arg after the script for FCALL) and emit per-key KEY_FLAGS_RW_ACCESS_UPDATE (or KEY_FLAGS_RO_ACCESS for _RO variants).

Discovered iter 139 probe sweep.
close_reason: completed

title: cjson.encode emits invalid JSON for NaN/Inf and silently swallows function/thread types
description:
fr's lua_value_to_json (crates/fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs:7246) violates Redis-bundled cjson semantics on two fronts:

1. NaN / +Inf / -Inf serialisation. Vendored Redis 7.2.4 raises a script error:
     ERR user_script:1: Cannot serialise number: must not be NaN or Inf script: <sha>, on @user_script:1.
   fr silently emits the JSON-invalid tokens 'NaN', 'inf', '-inf':
     cjson.encode(0/0)   -> 'NaN'      (vendored: error)
     cjson.encode(1/0)   -> 'inf'      (vendored: error)
     cjson.encode(-1/0)  -> '-inf'     (vendored: error)

2. function / thread (coroutine) values. Vendored cjson rejects them with the dedicated wording:
     Cannot serialise function: type not supported
     Cannot serialise thread: type not supported
   fr emits the literal 'null' for any LuaValue::Function / LuaValue::RustFunction / LuaValue::Coroutine / LuaValue::WrappedCoroutine value.

Root cause: lua_value_to_json returns a String, never a Result, so all of these cases fall through to 'null' or format!('{n}') and produce nonconforming output.

Out of scope (filed separately if pursued):
  - Sparse-array detection ('Cannot serialise table: excessively sparse array') — depends on fr's # operator semantics on tables with explicit nil holes, which is a deeper Lua-runtime divergence (frankenredis-XXX, not filed yet).
  - cmsgpack / struct stdlib modules — not implemented in fr at all.
  - cjson.encode_keep_buffer / cjson.encode_max_depth / cjson.decode_max_depth — auxiliary tuning APIs not exposed by fr's cjson shim.

Fix sketch:
  - Convert lua_value_to_json to return Result<String, String>:
    * Number(NaN | +-Inf) => Err('Cannot serialise number: must not be NaN or Inf')
    * Function / RustFunction => Err('Cannot serialise function: type not supported')
    * Coroutine / WrappedCoroutine => Err('Cannot serialise thread: type not supported')
  - Recursively propagate errors through table encoding.
  - In the cjson.encode call_builtin arm, propagate via '?'. fr's existing script error wrap will prepend 'user_script:1: ' and append the standard 'script: <sha>, on @user_script:1.' tail (verified empirically for cjson.encode_max_depth missing-method error).

Live diff vs vendored 7.2.4 (:16380) for all 6 cases.

Test plan:
  - Pin parity for cjson.encode of: 0/0, 1/0, -1/0, math.huge, -math.huge.
  - Pin parity for cjson.encode of: function() end, coroutine.create(function() end).
  - Regression: cjson.encode of finite numbers, strings, plain tables, booleans, nil — must still work.
  - Ensure error wording matches the upstream lua_cjson.c::json_create_encoder message verbatim (no period, no 'ERR' prefix; the prefix is added by the script error wrap).
close_reason: Tightened lua_value_to_json to reject the same value classes vendored cjson rejects via luaL_error: (a) Numbers that are NaN or +-Inf return 'Cannot serialise number: must not be NaN or Inf'; (b) LuaValue::Function / LuaValue::RustFunction return 'Cannot serialise function: type not supported'; (c) LuaValue::Coroutine / LuaValue::WrappedCoroutine return 'Cannot serialise thread: type not supported'. Changed the helper signature to Result<String, String> and propagate via '?' through recursive table encoding (so a NaN nested inside a table also errors). The cjson.encode arm prepends 'user_script:1: ' to mirror what upstream's luaL_error auto-adds via the Lua source-location wrap, since fr's eval_script_error_reply only adds that prefix for runtime indexing/arithmetic errors, not for builtin-raised messages. Live diff vs vendored 7.2.4 (port 16380): 13 probe cases match including the 6 error paths, finite-number/nil/bool/string/array/object happy paths, and the nested-NaN-in-table propagation. Pinned by cjson_encode_rejects_nan_inf_function_thread_match_upstream covering all 5 unsupported sub-cases plus happy-path regression. All 933 fr-command lib tests pass. Out of scope (deeper Lua-runtime divergences not fixed by this bead): cjson sparse-array detection (depends on # operator semantics on tables with explicit nil holes), cmsgpack/struct stdlib modules (missing entirely from fr), cjson.encode_max_depth/encode_keep_buffer tuning APIs.

title: fr-command: SCAN/SSCAN/ZSCAN accept NOVALUES (HSCAN-only 7.4 feature) instead of rejecting + FUNCTION LIST LIBRARYNAME wording
description:
Two fixes:

1. NOVALUES is a Redis 7.4 option supported only by HSCAN. fr-command's parse_scan_args accepted NOVALUES universally, so SCAN/SSCAN/ZSCAN passed it through. Upstream 7.2 (and even 7.4 for SCAN/SSCAN/ZSCAN) reject with syntax error.

   Fix: factor parse_scan_args_with_novalues(argv, start_idx, accept_novalues); the SCAN/SSCAN/ZSCAN call sites pass false, HSCAN keeps the existing accept_novalues=true behavior (forward-compat with 7.4).

2. FUNCTION LIST LIBRARYNAME (no arg): fr emitted 'syntax error', upstream emits 'library name argument was not given'.

   Fix: switch the LIBRARYNAME-without-arg branch to the dedicated wording.

Differential probe vs vendored redis 7.2.4 byte-matches both after fix:
  ZSCAN z 0 NOVALUES        → 'syntax error'
  SSCAN s 0 NOVALUES        → 'syntax error'
  SCAN 0 NOVALUES           → 'syntax error'
  FUNCTION LIST LIBRARYNAME → 'library name argument was not given'
HSCAN with NOVALUES retained as a 7.4 forward-compat feature (separate parity decision).

title: fr-command/lua: coroutine.* methods all return 'attempt to call nil value', upstream Redis 7.2 supports the full coroutine library in scripts
description:
fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs:3186-3190 short-circuits the entire Lua coroutine library to "attempt to call a nil value", with the comment "Coroutine stubs (not supported, matches Redis)". This claim is WRONG.

Differential probe vs vendored Redis 7.2.4:

  EVAL 'return type(coroutine)' 0
    upstream: table
    fr      : table  (table exists at top level in both)

  EVAL '
    local co = coroutine.create(function(x) coroutine.yield(x*2) end)
    local _, r = coroutine.resume(co, 21)
    return r
  ' 0
    upstream: 42
    fr      : ERR attempt to call a nil value

  EVAL 'local co = coroutine.create(function() end); return coroutine.status(co)' 0
    upstream: suspended
    fr      : ERR attempt to call a nil value

  EVAL 'return coroutine.resume(nil)' 0
    upstream: ERR user_script:1: bad argument #1 to 'resume' (coroutine expected) ...
    fr      : ERR attempt to call a nil value ...

Upstream Redis 7.2 lua_engine.c / scripting.c does NOT remove coroutine.* methods. The coroutine library is part of the standard sandbox surface, used by libraries like cjson, json4lua, and complex multi-stage scripts.

Affected methods: coroutine.create, coroutine.resume, coroutine.yield, coroutine.status, coroutine.wrap, coroutine.running.

Severity HIGH because:
- Real-world scripts that use coroutines for streaming/iteration patterns silently fail with a misleading 'attempt to call a nil value' error rather than working.
- The comment claims matches-Redis behavior, so subsequent maintainers won't audit.

Fix sketch: implement the coroutine library against fr's Lua interpreter. The hard part is suspended-frame state — fr's evaluator may need step/resume primitives. As a smaller intermediate fix, supply at least the OBSERVABLE error contract upstream emits (e.g. coroutine.resume(nil) → 'bad argument #1 to resume') so client-side error parsing matches.

Reference: differential probe of fr (port 16700) vs vendored Redis 7.2.4 (port 16701) on 2026-05-04.
close_reason: Implemented Lua coroutine create/resume/yield/status/wrap/running handles for Redis script parity, enabled the upstream create/resume regression, and added focused status/wrap/yield continuation tests.

title: fr-command::randomkey ignores selected_db — Lua redis.call('RANDOMKEY') returns keys from foreign databases
description:
/mock-code-finder + /porting-to-rust differential probe — continuing the dispatch_argv-vs-fr-runtime audit (w9yzb MOVE, j22p8 SELECT, rdz52 FLUSHDB, op84s COPY, shbbv KEYS+DBSIZE).

Source: crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:14538-14546 — randomkey() calls store.randomkey(now_ms) which is the all-DBs primitive picking from self.entries.keys() (the global keyspace).

fr-runtime::handle_randomkey_command at fr-runtime/src/lib.rs:10213 correctly uses store.randomkey_in_db(self.session.selected_db) and then decode_db_key to surface the logical name. The dispatch_argv path is what's broken.

Differential probe vs vendored Redis 7.2.4 (db 0 empty, db 1 holds k1_alpha + k1_beta):
  EVAL 'return redis.call(\"RANDOMKEY\")' 0
    upstream: (nil)   (db 0 is empty)
    fr      : k1_alpha or k1_beta   (leaks from db 1)

Reachability (same as MOVE/COPY/FLUSHDB/KEYS): Lua redis.call('RANDOMKEY'), AOF replay, MULTI/EXEC.

Severity P1 — same confidentiality smell as shbbv: a Lua script connected to db 0 can sample key names from any other database. Plus correctness — scripts that rely on RANDOMKEY for cleanup/eviction make wrong decisions.

Fix: rewrite randomkey to read store.dispatch_client_ctx.db_index, call store.randomkey_in_db(db, now_ms), and surface the logical key via decode_db_key (matching fr-runtime's handle_randomkey_command).
close_reason: Rewrote fr-command::randomkey to read store.dispatch_client_ctx.db_index, call store.randomkey_in_db(db, now_ms), and return the logical key via fr_store::decode_db_key (mirrors fr-runtime::handle_randomkey_command). Lua redis.call('RANDOMKEY'), AOF replay, and MULTI/EXEC now correctly scope to the selected database. New 100-trial test (db 0 empty + db 1 with 2 keys + db 2 with 1 key) deterministically catches the bug: every db 0 call must return Nil, every db 1 call must return one of the db 1 logical names (no foreign-db leakage, no encode_db_key prefix bytes). clippy clean.

title: COMMAND LIST/COUNT/INFO advertise 'sentinel' as a top-level command in standalone mode, but invoking SENTINEL returns 'ERR unknown command'
description:
Differential probe vs vendored 16380:
  COMMAND COUNT
    vendored: 241  fr: 242
  COMMAND LIST | grep sentinel
    vendored: (none)  fr: sentinel
  COMMAND INFO sentinel
    vendored: nil
    fr:
      sentinel
      -2
      admin
      0
      0
      0
      @slow
      @admin
      @dangerous
  SENTINEL (actually invoked)
    vendored: ERR unknown command 'SENTINEL'
    fr      : ERR unknown command 'SENTINEL'

So fr's COMMAND_TABLE advertises 'sentinel' to introspection, but the runtime dispatcher doesn't actually wire it up in standalone mode — invoking it returns the same 'unknown command' error as vendored. The introspection lies.

Upstream Redis only adds 'sentinel' to COMMAND_TABLE when running in sentinel mode (server.c::sentinelCmdTable + commands.def gating).

Fix: in fr-command's COMMAND_TABLE construction, gate the sentinel entry behind cluster_enabled/sentinel_mode equivalent, or drop it entirely from the standalone command table since dispatch doesn't honour it anyway. Same gating applies to:
  - COMMAND COUNT (should drop to 241 in standalone)
  - COMMAND LIST (should not include 'sentinel')
  - COMMAND INFO sentinel (should return nil bulk string)
  - COMMAND DOCS sentinel (should return empty map)
close_reason: Filtered the 'sentinel' COMMAND_TABLE row from COMMAND LIST/COUNT/INFO/DOCS unless store.sentinel_mode is true, mirroring upstream server.c behavior of registering the sentinel command only in --sentinel mode. fr's runtime dispatch already gated SENTINEL on sentinel_mode, so introspection was lying about the command's availability. Added helper command_table_row_is_visible(name, store) and applied it to all 6 COMMAND_TABLE iteration sites in command_cmd (no-sub default, COUNT, LIST default, LIST FILTERBY PATTERN, INFO default + per-name, DOCS default + per-name). Pinned via command_table_hides_sentinel_in_standalone_mode_per_upstream which exercises COUNT, LIST, INFO sentinel (=nil), INFO get (sanity), and the sentinel_mode=true round-trip. Live differential probe vs vendored 16380: COMMAND COUNT now matches at 241 (was 242), LIST grep sentinel returns 0 hits on both, INFO sentinel returns nil on both. Workspace clippy clean under -D warnings; 12 fr-command COMMAND tests pass.

title: redis parity: CLIENT KILL/PAUSE/UNPAUSE should reject script context
description: Vendored Redis marks CLIENT KILL, CLIENT PAUSE, and CLIENT UNPAUSE as NOSCRIPT in legacy_redis_code/redis/src/commands/client-kill.json, client-pause.json, and client-unpause.json. The local client_cmd branches at crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:11248-11365 currently validate their arguments and return legacy/runtime responses without any script_nesting_level guard. Add Redis-compatible rejection after the existing legacy-address/filter, timeout/mode, and arity validation paths, with Lua coverage for KILL, PAUSE WRITE/ALL, and UNPAUSE.
close_reason: Completed

title: Build redis-benchmark-compatible TCP load generator with HdrHistogram latency recording
description:
# Benchmark Load Generator

## What
A Rust binary that connects to a running FrankenRedis (or Redis) instance via TCP, sends configurable workloads, and records precise latency histograms.

## Why
The project claims "profile-proven performance" but has zero throughput or latency measurements. This tool is the foundation for ALL performance claims.

## Implementation
1. New binary: `crates/fr-server/src/bin/fr_benchmark.rs` (or dedicated crate `crates/fr-bench/`)
2. Use `std::net::TcpStream` (blocking OK for benchmark client)
3. Workloads to implement:
   - SET: random keys, configurable value size (default 3 bytes like redis-benchmark)
   - GET: random existing keys
   - INCR: atomic counter
   - LPUSH/LPOP: list operations
   - HSET/HGET: hash operations
   - Mixed: configurable ratio of reads/writes
4. Parameters: `--host`, `--port`, `--clients` (concurrent connections), `--requests` (total), `--pipeline` (commands per batch), `--keyspace` (number of unique keys), `--datasize`
5. Latency recording: use `hdrhistogram` crate for O(1) percentile queries
6. Output: JSON with ops/sec, p50/p95/p99/p999 latency in microseconds, total time, bytes transferred
7. Human-readable summary to stdout

## Validation
- Run against both frankenredis and legacy redis on same machine
- Verify ops/sec is within 2x of legacy redis for basic SET/GET
- Verify latency histogram is populated and percentiles are sane

## Files
- crates/fr-bench/Cargo.toml (new crate)
- crates/fr-bench/src/main.rs
- Cargo.toml workspace members update
close_reason: Implemented fr-bench TCP benchmark harness with histogram reporting and remote-verified validation

title: rv89 part 2: HEXPIRE/HPEXPIRE/HEXPIREAT/HPEXPIREAT/HTTL/HPTTL/HEXPIRETIME/HPEXPIRETIME/HPERSIST commands (fr-command)
description:
Second slice of br-frankenredis-rv89. Implement the 9 hash field TTL commands in fr-command, routed through the fr-store primitives landed in part 1 (br-frankenredis-wwz3):

  HEXPIRE key seconds [NX|XX|GT|LT] FIELDS numfields field ...
  HPEXPIRE key ms [NX|XX|GT|LT] FIELDS numfields ...
  HEXPIREAT key unix-seconds [NX|XX|GT|LT] FIELDS numfields ...
  HPEXPIREAT key unix-ms [NX|XX|GT|LT] FIELDS numfields ...
  HTTL key FIELDS numfields field ...
  HPTTL key FIELDS numfields field ...
  HEXPIRETIME key FIELDS numfields ...
  HPEXPIRETIME key FIELDS numfields ...
  HPERSIST key FIELDS numfields ...

Reply shape: array of integers with upstream codes (-2 field missing, -1 no TTL, 0 condition blocked by NX/XX/GT/LT, 1 applied, 2 applied+deleted-due-to-past-expiry).

Argument parsing: FIELDS numfields is required; validate numfields matches the field-count remainder.

Unit tests covering every code path per upstream semantics.

No encoding / RDB / AOF propagation changes (parts 4-5).
close_reason: Landed in 48b5608: all 9 Redis 7.4 HEXPIRE-family commands in fr-command — HEXPIRE/HPEXPIRE/HEXPIREAT/HPEXPIREAT setters with NX/XX/GT/LT flag matrix, HTTL/HPTTL/HEXPIRETIME/HPEXPIRETIME readers with upstream -1/-2 codes, HPERSIST. 10 new tests; fr-command --lib 619/0; workspace clippy clean. Consumers (lazy expiry, AOF propagation, keyspace events) live in parts 3/4 (b8ut, 3jrv).

title: HSCAN accepts the 7.4-only NOVALUES option even though target is Redis 7.2.4 — vendored returns 'ERR syntax error'
description:
Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4 16380:
  HSET h f1 v1 f2 v2 f3 v3
  HSCAN h 0 NOVALUES
    vendored: ERR syntax error
    fr      : 0\nf1\nf2\nf3   (returns just field names)

Background: HSCAN's NOVALUES flag was added in Redis 7.4 (commit ffd44d76). fr implements it, but the project's parity baseline is Redis 7.2.4 (README + INFO server.redis_version). Same class as v0swv (hexpired_fields). SSCAN/ZSCAN don't have NOVALUES (members ARE the only data); both servers correctly reject NOVALUES for those commands.

Fix: in fr-command's HSCAN parser, drop the NOVALUES branch so unknown tokens fall through to the existing syntax-error path.

Probe also showed SCAN ordering differs (vendored: l,z,s1,h vs fr: h,l,s1,z) but SCAN explicitly does not guarantee element order — not a defect, just a differential observation.
close_reason: Disabled NOVALUES acceptance across the entire SCAN family by flipping parse_scan_args's accept_novalues default from true to false (crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:16375-16384). NOVALUES is a Redis 7.4 HSCAN-only addition (commit ffd44d76); the project's parity baseline is 7.2.4, so accepting it broke parity. Same class as v0swv (DEBUG OBJECT hexpired_fields). Updated existing test scan_family_rejects_novalues_*  to also assert HSCAN+NOVALUES errors (renamed from _except_hscan_ to _for_7_2_4_baseline_). Live differential probe vs vendored 16380 confirms HSCAN h 0 NOVALUES now returns 'ERR syntax error' on both servers; HSCAN h 0 still returns the field-value pairs unchanged. Workspace clippy clean under -D warnings; scan-family tests pass.

title: fuzz/corpus/fuzz_inline_parser missing — libfuzzer starts cold every run, structured-input edges (CR-only, hex escapes, mixed quoting, oversize) untested between cargo-fuzz invocations
description:
/testing-fuzzing audit:

  ls fuzz/corpus/fuzz_inline_parser → No such file or directory

The fuzz_inline_parser harness in fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_inline_parser.rs exercises the wire-protocol entry point that handles human-typed redis-cli over telnet (PING\r\n, SET key value, etc.) — it consumes try_parse_inline + split_inline_args + should_try_inline_parsing. With no seed corpus, libfuzzer starts cold every run and the structured-input edges (quoted-string escape handling, CR-only line endings, mixed single/double quoting, oversize tokens, hex escapes) only get covered if libfuzzer's bytewise mutator happens to stumble onto them.

This also breaks the cwx6/ohm5/fek0y/xpgvh smoke-test pattern: the cargo-test fuzz_corpus_smoke harness (added in fr-protocol + fr-persist) replays seed files through the parsers under regular cargo test for deterministic between-run coverage. fuzz_inline_parser can't have one of those without a corpus.

Fix: hand-write 30+ seed inputs covering happy paths (PING\r\n, SET k v, multi-arg with tabs), quoted-string escapes (\n \r \t \b \a \" \\ \xNN \'), single-quoted with \' escape, mixed quoting, edge cases (empty line, CRLF-only, LF-only, no terminator), hostile inputs (unbalanced quotes, half-formed hex escape, embedded NUL, non-ASCII high bytes, oversize tokens, RESP-prefix bytes that should be rejected by should_try_inline_parsing). Optionally add a fuzz_corpus_smoke entry under crates/fr-server/tests/ to mirror the fr-protocol/fr-persist pattern.
close_reason: Added 42-seed corpus under fuzz/corpus/fuzz_inline_parser/ covering: happy paths (PING / GET / SET / MSET / LF terminator); quoted-string escapes (\n \r \t \b \a \" \\ \zUnknown); hex escapes (\x41\x42, \x00, \xff, uppercase hex, truncated \x4); single-quoting + escape; mixed double/single quoting; whitespace edges (tabs, runs of spaces, leading whitespace, empty CRLF/LF/whitespace-only lines); hostile inputs (unbalanced double/single quote, half-formed hex, embedded NUL); high-byte / non-ASCII; RESP-prefix bytes meant to be rejected by should_try_inline_parsing; long-token (4KB+4KB), many-args (50 args); no-terminator (Incomplete). Each seed has a 0x00 trailer so libfuzzer's Arbitrary derive picks the Raw variant. Companion crates/fr-server/tests/fuzz_corpus_smoke.rs (mirrors fr-protocol/fr-persist pattern) replays all seeds through try_parse_inline + split_inline_args + should_try_inline_parsing under cargo test, plus pins RESP-prefix seeds to should-not-route-inline and unbalanced-quote seeds to ProtocolError.

title: CLIENT CACHING with invalid opt + tracking disabled returns syntax error instead of tracking-mode error
description: Differential probe vs vendored Redis 7.2.4: CLIENT CACHING BOGUS against a non-tracking client returns 'ERR CLIENT CACHING can be called only when the client is in tracking mode with OPTIN or OPTOUT mode enabled'. Upstream networking.c:3415-3441 checks the tracking-enabled gate BEFORE parsing the YES|NO opt. fr's validate_client_caching_mode parsed mode first and returned 'ERR syntax error' for BOGUS, surfacing the wrong wording. The tracking-mode error appeared only when mode was YES or NO.
close_reason: Reordered validate_client_caching_mode in fr-command/src/lib.rs to check tracking.enabled FIRST (matching upstream networking.c order), then validate the YES/NO opt. Added a new fr-command unit test client_caching_invalid_opt_with_tracking_disabled_uses_tracking_mode_error covering BOGUS / YES / NO / lowercase opts against a non-tracking client (all expect the dedicated wording), plus a follow-up that re-enables tracking and confirms BOGUS surfaces SyntaxError. Existing client_caching_requires_optin_or_optout_modes test (covering YES/NO with various tracking modes) still passes unchanged. fr-command 848/4 pre-existing stream failures.

title: CLIENT TRACKING OFF REDIRECT N skips client-existence validation
description:
Differential probe vs vendored Redis 7.2.4:

  CLIENT TRACKING OFF REDIRECT 5
    vendored: ERR The client ID you want redirect to does not exist
    fr:       OK

Upstream networking.c::clientCommand validates the REDIRECT target
client ID even when the tracking command is turning the feature
OFF — the parse-time validation runs unconditionally. fr's
parse_client_tracking_state zeros out the parsed state via
`return Ok(ClientTrackingState::default())` when `!enabled`, so
the runtime's lookup-by-ID never sees the redirect value, and the
existence check is silently skipped.

Two other variants stay correct:
  OFF REDIRECT 0    → both reject (parse-time positivity check)
  OFF REDIRECT abc  → both reject (int parse error)
The bug is specifically: positive-int target that doesn't exist
combined with OFF.

Fix locus: crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs::parse_client_tracking_state
— preserve the redirect value in the !enabled return so the runtime
existence check at fr-runtime/src/lib.rs:10469 can still fire.

Test plan: pin
  TRACKING OFF REDIRECT 99999      → "client ID … does not exist"
  TRACKING OFF REDIRECT <self>     → OK
  TRACKING ON REDIRECT 99999       → "client ID … does not exist" (regression)
close_reason: fixed

title: Parser accepts bare literals as statement starts, wrong wording (number/string/bool/nil)
description:
Lua 5.1 lparser.c restricts statement starts to keywords, Name, or
'(' for prefixexp. fr's parser routes anything that isn't a keyword
through parse_expr_or_assign which calls parse_suffixed_expr —
that accepts bare Number/String/Bool/Nil literals as expression
roots, then fails later with the assignment wording '=' expected
near '<eof>'.

Vendored rejects such tokens immediately at the statement-start
level with 'unexpected symbol near <X>'.

Repro:
  EVAL "local f,e = loadstring('123'); return tostring(e)" 0
    vendored: [string "123"]:1: unexpected symbol near '123'
    fr:       [string "123"]:1: '=' expected near '<eof>'

  EVAL "local f,e = loadstring('1.5'); return tostring(e)" 0
    vendored: [string "1.5"]:1: unexpected symbol near '1.5'
    fr:       [string "1.5"]:1: '=' expected near '<eof>'

  EVAL "local f,e = loadstring('+1'); return tostring(e)" 0
    Both: '[string "+1"]:1: unexpected symbol near +'  (the + isn't
    a suffixed-expr start so fr already happens to match here.)

The bare-Name case ('foo', 'foo bar', 'a b c') stays correct under
this bead — those parse as Name expressions and the existing
luabarestmt fix at parse_expr_or_assign:1924 emits the upstream
'=' expected wording. Only non-Name-or-LParen statement starts
need the unexpected-symbol redirect.

Fix: guard at parse_expr_or_assign entry. If self.peek() is not a
valid prefixexp start (Name or LParen), return
'unexpected symbol near <token>' immediately.
close_reason: completed

title: redis parity: REPLCONF should reject script context
description: Vendored Redis marks REPLCONF as NOSCRIPT in legacy_redis_code/redis/src/commands/replconf.json, but replconf_cmd at crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:5914-5948 accepts ACK/GETACK/configuration pairs without any script_nesting_level guard. Add Redis-compatible rejection after the current syntax and integer validation paths, with Lua coverage for ACK and GETACK.
close_reason: Completed

title: Fix failing replica_stream_reconnect_uses_partial_psync_after_disconnect test
description:
The test replica_stream_reconnect_uses_partial_psync_after_disconnect is failing with:
- Warning: 'primary closed replication stream during sync'
- Assertion: gamma key returns nil instead of '3'

The test verifies that after a primary disconnect and reconnect, the replica receives data via CONTINUE sync. The failure suggests the CONTINUE sync isn't completing properly.

Potential causes:
1. EOF detection before buffer is fully processed
2. Timing issues in the mock primary server
3. Possible regression from commit 23a9eb6 (fail CONTINUE sync on primary disconnect)
close_reason: Fixed: Modified run_replica_sync to process buffered data before erroring on disconnect. Unit test replica_stream_reconnect_uses_partial_psync_after_disconnect now passes (48/48 unit tests pass).

title: SPOP <wrongtype-key> 0 returns empty instead of WRONGTYPE
description:
fr's SPOP with count=0 returns an empty array even when the key is a wrong type (e.g. a string), while vendored Redis 7.2.4 returns 'WRONGTYPE Operation against a key holding the wrong kind of value'.

Root cause: fr-store's spop_count() iterates 'for _ in 0..count' — when count=0 the loop is skipped and returns an empty Vec without consulting the key's type. The command handler at fr-command/src/lib.rs:10670-10676 wraps that in an array reply.

Upstream t_set.c::spopWithCountCommand orders the checks differently:
  1. Parse count (must be a positive long)
  2. lookupKeyWriteOrReply (return empty if missing)
  3. checkType(c, set, OBJ_SET) — emits WRONGTYPE if wrong type
  4. If count==0, addReply(emptyset) and return

So the WRONGTYPE error fires BEFORE the count==0 short-circuit.

Probe sweep #63 (probe126.sh):
  vendored SET strkey foo  -> OK
  vendored SPOP strkey 0   -> WRONGTYPE Operation against a key holding the wrong kind of value
  fr        SPOP strkey 0   -> (empty array)
  vendored SPOP strkey     -> WRONGTYPE  (already correct in fr)
  vendored SPOP strkey 5   -> WRONGTYPE  (already correct in fr)
  vendored SPOP strkey -1  -> ERR value is out of range, must be positive  (matches)
  vendored SPOP nokey 0    -> (empty)   (also matches fr)

SRANDMEMBER's count=0 path already validates type correctly (both servers return WRONGTYPE).

Fix plan:
- In fr-command/src/lib.rs spop() command handler, do a type-check via store before calling spop_count when count==0. Or modify spop_count() to consult store.key_type() up front and return WrongType error.
- Add regression test pinning SPOP wrongtype-key 0 -> WRONGTYPE.
close_reason: Fixed in iteration 96: added an explicit key-type check via store.key_type() in fr-command/src/lib.rs's spop() 3-arg branch BEFORE calling spop_count. Matches upstream t_set.c::spopWithCountCommand which validates type before the count==0 short-circuit. SPOP wrongtype-key 0 now returns WRONGTYPE; SPOP nokey 0 still returns empty array; SPOP wrongtype-key 5 (count>0) continues to report WRONGTYPE via the existing spop_count path. Live-diff matches vendored. Regression test spop_count_zero_on_wrongtype_key_reports_wrongtype_cf9z1 pins all three cases. Full 1021-test suite passes.

title: Lua loadstring/load globals missing in sandbox surface
description: fr's Lua sandbox doesn't register loadstring or load as globals, but vendored Redis 7.2.4 exposes both. Probes vs port 16380: type(loadstring)=function (vendored) / nonexistent-global error (fr); type(load)=function (vendored) / error (fr). loadstring('return 42')() should return 42; loadstring('!!!') should return nil + error message. The sandbox still applies to the loaded chunk (it cannot read unprotected globals). Implementation: register 'loadstring' and 'load' as RustFunctions; dispatch handler runs fr's lexer+parser on the source string and returns a LuaValue::Function (or nil + error string on parse failure). Discovered while running REVIEW PHASE against vendored.
close_reason: Implemented loadstring(src [, chunkname]) and load() in fr's Lua sandbox. loadstring parses via fr's existing Lexer+Parser and returns a LuaValue::Function with the parsed Vec<Stmt> as body, variadic params, and the caller's env captured (so the loaded chunk sees the calling script's locals as upvalues, matching Lua 5.1). Returns (nil, errmsg) on parse failure with the canonical '[string "CHUNK"]:1: ERROR' label (plus '=NAME' / '@NAME' prefix stripping, and multi-line source truncation with '...'). load() rejects non-function args with vendored's exact 'bad argument #1 to ? (function expected, got TYPE)' wording (Lua 5.1 uses '?' for C closures without registered debug names — same pattern as frankenredis-w3wkp). The chunk-generator-function load() form is not yet supported because fr's interpreter requires fully-parsed Vec<Stmt> trees; documented in the dispatch handler. Added format_lua_chunk_label() helper mirroring luaO_chunkid. Verified live diff vs vendored 7.2.4 (port 16380): 13/13 cases match. Pinned by lua_loadstring_parses_chunks_into_callable_functions (10 sub-cases). All 915 fr-command lib tests pass. Two known divergences filed as follow-ups: frankenredis-d4vlx (TableConstructor doesn't expand trailing ... — pre-existing fr bug not specific to loadstring) and frankenredis-ycaog (runtime errors inside loaded chunks use outer user_script:1 label instead of the chunk's label — requires LuaFunc source-tag plumbing).

title: CONFIG SET silently accepts duplicate parameters; upstream rejects with dedicated wording
description: Differential probe vs vendored Redis 7.2.4: `CONFIG SET maxmemory 100 maxmemory 200` returns `ERR CONFIG SET failed (possibly related to argument 'maxmemory') - duplicate parameter`. Upstream config.c::configSetCommand:872-880 walks the pair list, tracks each resolved config in `set_configs[]`, and when it sees the same config slot twice it sets `errstr="duplicate parameter"`, captures `err_arg_name = c->argv[2+i*2]->ptr`, and aborts. fr's handle_config_set accepted duplicates silently (last value wins).
acceptance_criteria: CONFIG SET rejects calls that name the same parameter twice (case-insensitive) with the dedicated `ERR CONFIG SET failed (possibly related to argument '<name>') - duplicate parameter` wording before any pair is applied. Non-duplicate two-pair calls still succeed.
close_reason: Added a duplicate-parameter check in fr-runtime/src/lib.rs::handle_config_set BEFORE the existing pair-processing loop. Walks argv[2..].chunks_exact(2), normalises each parameter via to_ascii_lowercase, and checks against a Vec<String> of already-seen names; on a hit returns config_set_failed(parameter, "duplicate parameter") which emits the canonical envelope. Added unit test config_set_rejects_duplicate_parameters_within_one_call covering: literal duplicate, case-insensitive duplicate (MaxMemory + maxmemory), non-duplicate two-pair call still succeeds, and final-state verification that the pre-error pair never landed.

title: CONFIG SET memory args reject strtoull overflow that upstream memtoull silently accepts
description:
util.c::memtoull's strtoull call is gated only on EINVAL and a trailing non-digit pointer — it does NOT inspect errno==ERANGE. So an overflowing digit run silently saturates to ULLONG_MAX and the subsequent val*mul wraps modulo 2^64. fr was rejecting twice: once at digits.parse() (returning Err) and again at checked_mul, surfacing 'argument must be a memory value' for inputs that vendored 7.2.4 happily accepts. Affected every CONFIG SET site that routes through parse_memory_size_arg (maxmemory, repl-backlog-size, client-output-buffer-limit memory threshold, hash-max-listpack-value, set-max-listpack-value, zset-max-listpack-value, list-max-listpack-size, etc.).

Repro vs vendored 7.2.4:
  16380> CONFIG SET maxmemory 9999999999999999999999gb
  OK
  16380> CONFIG GET maxmemory
  1) "maxmemory"
  2) "18446744072635809792"
  16381 (pre-fix)> CONFIG SET maxmemory 9999999999999999999999gb
  (error) ERR CONFIG SET failed - argument must be a memory value

Fix: saturate digits.parse to u64::MAX on overflow and switch the multiplier to wrapping_mul. Negative prefix and unknown suffixes still reject (memtoull gates those before strtoull). Verified: u64::MAX.wrapping_mul(1024^3) == 18446744072635809792, byte-for-byte matching vendored output.
acceptance_criteria: 1. CONFIG SET maxmemory '9999999999999999999999gb' returns OK and CONFIG GET reports 18446744072635809792 (matches vendored). 2. CONFIG SET maxmemory '99999999999999999999' (no suffix overflow) returns OK and CONFIG GET reports u64::MAX (18446744073709551615). 3. CONFIG SET maxmemory '5gb' continues to yield 5368709120. 4. CONFIG SET maxmemory '-1' still rejects with the existing envelope. 5. CONFIG SET maxmemory '5xb' still rejects (unknown suffix).

title: CONFIG GET / CONFIG SET silently run from EVAL/FCALL — missing CMD_NOSCRIPT guard
description: Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4 confirmed both `redis.call('CONFIG','GET','maxmemory')` and `redis.call('CONFIG','SET','maxmemory','0')` surface the noscript reply ('ERR This Redis command is not allowed from script ...'). commands.def line 6530 declares config|get with CMD_NOSCRIPT and line 6534 declares config|set with CMD_NOSCRIPT (both alongside CMD_ADMIN, CMD_LOADING, CMD_STALE). fr's CONFIG GET handler at fr-command/src/lib.rs:14250 and CONFIG SET handler at line 14265 had no script-context guard, so a scripted CONFIG GET returned the live config values and a scripted CONFIG SET silently mutated the running config — both significant divergences from upstream behavior. CONFIG RESETSTAT and CONFIG REWRITE were already guarded.
close_reason: Added script_nesting_level guards to CONFIG GET (after arity check) and CONFIG SET (after arity check), matching the pattern already used by RESETSTAT/REWRITE. Extended config_subcommands_reject_scripts_after_arity_validation to also assert noscript fires for CONFIG GET and CONFIG SET when args are correct. 837 fr-command (4 pre-existing stream failures), 330/330 fr-store.

title: CONFIG SET with odd arity returns table-level wrong-arity instead of upstream 'ERR syntax error'
description: Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4: `CONFIG SET maxmemory 0 extra` (5 args) surfaces 'ERR syntax error'. Upstream config.c::configSetCommand line 819-821 explicitly routes odd-arity through addReplyErrorObject(shared.syntaxerr). fr's combined check `argv.len() < 4 || !argv.len().is_multiple_of(2)` returned WrongSubcommandArity for both branches, rendering as 'wrong number of arguments for config|set command' for the odd-arity case.
close_reason: Split the combined arity check: argv.len() < 4 still returns WrongSubcommandArity (matches vendored), but odd-arity now returns CommandError::SyntaxError (matches vendored 'ERR syntax error'). Test pin config_set_odd_arity_returns_upstream_syntax_error covers both branches plus the existing argv.len() < 4 sanity case. 838 fr-command (4 pre-existing stream failures), 330/330 fr-store.

title: fr-command LATENCY HISTOGRAM has wrong wire shape under both RESP2 and RESP3 — upstream emits Map-of-cmd→Map-of-{calls,histogram_usec→Map} but fr emits Array-of-[name,flat-array]
close_reason: fr-command latency_cmd HISTOGRAM branch now mirrors upstream latency.c::fillCommandCDF + latencyCommand HISTOGRAM dispatch. Inner CDF emits 2-entry Map { calls, histogram_usec } where histogram_usec is itself a Map of bucket_us → cumulative_count. Outer envelope emits Map<cmd_name → CDF>. Under RESP3 all three layers are RespFrame::Map; under RESP2 each Map flattens to alternating-Array of length 2*N. Existing test latency_histogram_returns_recorded_data was asserting the bug ([name, flat-array] tuple wrapper + missing histogram_usec separator) — updated to match upstream RESP2 alternating shape. New regression test latency_histogram_under_resp3_returns_nested_map_shape pins the 3-deep RESP3 Map structure. All 7 latency lib tests pass; the 4 unrelated pre-existing failures (xreadgroup/xinfo stream/migrate-vendored) reproduce on a clean checkout (verified via git stash). Twelfth in the RESP3-Map-shape audit cluster (i40x2/e4njz/udl3y/asvh1/5d04d/ndz0j/5h86s/cz712/ec2rf/q11am/1dfox/cgjlc).

title: CONFIG SET boolean validation for createBoolConfig fields + add missing bool params
description: fr's static-override fallthrough silently accepted any value for ~28 boolean config fields declared via createBoolConfig in upstream config.c. Several missing entirely (activerehashing, cluster-require-full-coverage, disable-thp, dynamic-hz, replica-announced, aof-rewrite-incremental-fsync, rdb-save-incremental-fsync, cluster-replica-no-failover) — fr returned 'Unknown option'. Fix: (1) added the missing names to CONFIG_STATIC_PARAMS; (2) added a centralised yes/no validator in the static-override path that emits 'argument must be \'yes\' or \'no\''; (3) refactored stop-writes-on-bgsave-error and cluster-allow-reads-when-down to use the wrapper too; (4) added disable-thp to the immutable list

title: Add metamorphic property tests for ZSET aggregations and GEO
description: Add metamorphic tests for Sorted Set operations (ZUNIONSTORE, ZINTERSTORE commutativity/associativity) and GEO commands (GEOADD, GEODIST distance translation invariant) using proptest in fr-store.
close_reason: Added metamorphic tests for ZSET

title: fr-runtime REPLICAOF/SLAVEOF missing cluster-mode rejection and client-is-replica rejection; sets master state on a replica session or in cluster mode silently

title: Match Redis config NUL truncation semantics
