title: COMMAND INFO flags column: clean obvious non-flag tokens (scripting, slow, dangerous) for eval/exec/swapdb/wait/waitaof family (partial of frankenredis-commandflagsaudit)
description:
First slice of the frankenredis-commandflagsaudit cleanup. The
scope here is the unambiguously-wrong subset: COMMAND_TABLE rows
whose flags column stored ACL category names ('scripting',
'dangerous') or made-up tokens ('slow') that have no CMD_*
equivalent in Redis 7.2.4 and were therefore being emitted as
bogus flag-array entries by command_info_flags().

Diff (vendored redis-cli COMMAND INFO -> fr COMMAND_TABLE):
  eval        -> noscript stale skip_monitor no_mandatory_keys movablekeys
  evalsha     -> noscript stale skip_monitor no_mandatory_keys movablekeys
  eval_ro     -> readonly noscript stale skip_monitor no_mandatory_keys movablekeys
  evalsha_ro  -> readonly noscript stale skip_monitor no_mandatory_keys movablekeys
  fcall       -> noscript stale skip_monitor no_mandatory_keys movablekeys
  fcall_ro    -> readonly noscript stale skip_monitor no_mandatory_keys movablekeys
  script      -> (empty)
  function    -> (empty)
  exec        -> noscript loading stale skip_slowlog
  swapdb      -> write fast       (was: write fast admin dangerous)
  wait        -> (empty)          (was: slow)
  waitaof     -> noscript         (was: slow)

Side-effect: command_group_for_docs() previously keyed off the
'scripting' / 'pubsub' tokens in the flags column to select the
COMMAND DOCS group. With those tokens removed from the data, the
function now uses an explicit name-set match for the eight
scripting commands (eval/evalsha/eval_ro/evalsha_ro/fcall/fcall_ro/
script/function) and the nine pubsub commands
(subscribe/unsubscribe/psubscribe/punsubscribe/ssubscribe/
sunsubscribe/publish/spublish/pubsub) — keeping the existing
command_docs_group_classification_matches_upstream golden green.

Regression test:
  command_info_flags_for_scripting_and_blocking_admin_subset_matches_upstream
  pins the corrected emission for all 12 commands.

Remaining work (still tracked in frankenredis-commandflagsaudit):
  - the 'admin'-as-flag set: cluster, client, acl, config, memory,
    slowlog, latency, module, object — vendored emits empty here
  - missing flags for blpop/brpop/blmove/blmpop/bzpopmin/bzpopmax/
    bzmpop (vendored: write blocking [denyoom] [fast])
  - missing movablekeys for sort/sort_ro/xread/xreadgroup
  - missing 'noscript loading stale fast [allow_busy]' on multi/
    discard/unwatch/watch/role/time/lastsave/select/info
  - admin/server family (save/bgsave/bgrewriteaof/failover/psync/
    sync/replconf/monitor/shutdown) needs the no_async_loading /
    no_multi / loading / stale flags added
  - subscribe family needs noscript loading stale appended
  - publish/spublish need loading stale appended
These are mechanical but each row needs its own per-command
verification against `redis-cli -p 16380 COMMAND INFO <cmd>`.
Filed open bead frankenredis-commandflagsaudit covers them as a
batch.

title: COMMAND INFO flags column: align ~48 commands (admin/blocking/movablekeys/pubsub/transactions families) with vendored 7.2.4; introduce ACL overlay so cleaning the flag-emission column does not drop @admin/@dangerous from container commands
description:
Second slice of frankenredis-commandflagsaudit. Aligned the COMMAND_TABLE
flags column with `redis-cli -p 16380 COMMAND INFO <cmd>` for ~48 entries:

  Top-level container commands -> empty flag emission:
    cluster, client, acl, config, memory, slowlog, latency, module,
    object, pubsub. Vendored emits no CMD_* flags on these because
    the flags live on the subcommands (cluster|addslots, client|kill, ...).
    The COMMAND_TABLE column previously stored 'admin' or 'readonly'
    or 'pubsub' here, which command_info_flags() emitted verbatim.

  Server / replication / persistence:
    debug:    'admin' -> 'admin noscript loading stale'
    info:     'fast' -> 'loading stale'
    shutdown: 'admin' -> 'admin noscript loading stale no_multi allow_busy'
    role:     'fast' -> 'noscript loading stale fast'
    time:     'fast' -> 'loading stale fast'
    lastsave: 'fast' -> 'loading stale fast'
    save:     'admin' -> 'admin noscript no_async_loading no_multi'
    bgsave/bgrewriteaof: 'admin' -> 'admin noscript no_async_loading'
    failover: 'admin' -> 'admin noscript stale'
    lolwut:   'fast' -> 'readonly fast'
    sync:     'admin' -> 'admin noscript no_async_loading no_multi'
    replconf: 'admin' -> 'admin noscript loading stale allow_busy'
    monitor:  'admin' -> 'admin noscript loading stale'
    select:   'fast' -> 'loading stale fast'

  Transactions:
    multi/discard/watch/unwatch:  'fast' -> 'noscript loading stale fast allow_busy'

  Pubsub:
    publish/spublish: 'pubsub fast' -> 'pubsub loading stale fast'
    [s|p]subscribe / [s|p]unsubscribe: 'pubsub' -> 'pubsub noscript loading stale'

  Movable keys / blocking surfaces:
    sort:        'write denyoom' -> 'write denyoom movablekeys'
    sort_ro:     'readonly fast' -> 'readonly movablekeys'
    xread:       'readonly' -> 'readonly blocking movablekeys'
    xreadgroup:  'write' -> 'write blocking movablekeys'
    blpop/brpop: 'write denyoom' -> 'write blocking'
    blmove:      'write denyoom' -> 'write denyoom blocking'
    blmpop/bzmpop:    'write denyoom' -> 'write blocking movablekeys'
    bzpopmin/bzpopmax: 'write denyoom' -> 'write blocking fast'

Side-effect: `merge_command_table_acl_categories` reads the
COMMAND_TABLE flags column to derive ACL categories
(admin/fast/slow/blocking/...). Removing 'admin' from the
container commands' rows would have dropped them from
command_acl_categories(...) -> @admin/@dangerous (visible to
the existing acl_cat_categories_follow_vendored_redis_metadata
test). Decoupled the two surfaces by adding
STATIC_ACL_OVERRIDES + apply_static_acl_overrides() that
re-injects the per-command ACL categories regardless of the
trimmed flags column. by_category remains driven solely by
UPSTREAM_ACL_CATEGORY_ENTRIES, matching how vendored
ACL CAT lists subcommand-level entries (config|set,
slowlog|get, ...) rather than top-level containers.

Regression test:
  command_info_flags_for_admin_blocking_pubsub_subset_matches_upstream
  pins all 48 entries' upstream-aligned flag emission.
  command_info_flags_for_scripting_and_blocking_admin_subset_matches_upstream
  (from batch1) remains green.
  acl_cat_categories_follow_vendored_redis_metadata
  (the count + contains golden) also green after the
  STATIC_ACL_OVERRIDES decoupling.

Combined with batch1, ~60 of the ~241 COMMAND_TABLE rows are
now upstream-aligned in COMMAND INFO emission. Remaining
rows (mostly already-correct read/write data commands —
string/list/set/zset/hash/stream/bitmap/hyperloglog/geo)
are now the next slice for frankenredis-commandflagsaudit
to verify.

title: COMMAND INFO flags column: align connection/movablekeys/replication/stream-container subset (37 rows) — completes the audit
description:
Third (final) slice of frankenredis-commandflagsaudit. Aligned
the remaining 37 COMMAND_TABLE rows whose flags column drifted
from `redis-cli -p 16380 COMMAND INFO <cmd>` against vendored
Redis 7.2.4. After this batch, a programmatic sweep across all
240 fr top-level commands shows ZERO remaining divergences
(`sentinel` is the only outlier — fr keeps it for sentinel
mode while vendored standalone omits it; preserved
intentionally).

Connection family — gained 'noscript loading stale fast no_auth
allow_busy' per upstream:
  auth, hello, quit, reset.
  echo: 'fast' -> 'loading stale fast'

Stale 'sort_for_script' marker (not a real CMD_* flag in 7.2.4)
removed:
  hkeys, hvals, keys, smembers, sdiff, sinter, sunion -> 'readonly'
  zdiff, zinter, zunion -> 'readonly movablekeys'

Missing 'movablekeys' added on the inter/union/diff stores and
their cousins:
  sintercard, zintercard -> 'readonly movablekeys'
  zdiffstore, zinterstore, zunionstore -> 'write denyoom movablekeys'
  lmpop, zmpop -> 'write movablekeys'  (also dropped bogus 'fast')
  migrate -> 'write movablekeys'
  georadius, georadiusbymember -> 'write denyoom movablekeys'

Replication / management cleaned to upstream emission:
  pfdebug -> 'write denyoom admin'
  psync -> 'admin noscript no_async_loading no_multi'
  replicaof / slaveof -> 'admin noscript stale no_async_loading'
  readonly: 'fast' -> ''
  readwrite: 'fast' -> 'loading stale fast'

Cluster slot-migration / stream containers cleaned:
  brpoplpush -> 'write denyoom blocking'
  copy -> 'write denyoom'
  xgroup, xinfo -> '' (containers; flags live on subcommands)
  xsetid -> 'write denyoom fast'
  command -> 'loading stale' (command_info_flags() already
    overrides this for emission, but the column-level value now
    matches so ACL-derivation via merge_command_table is also
    consistent — pre-existing override at lines 14252-14256
    was masking the divergence).

Side-effect: pfdebug now carries 'write' in flags. The
is_write_command() match list (used by the AOF replay gate)
didn't include CommandId::Pfdebug; added it. The AOF data-loss
guard test
(is_write_command_covers_all_command_table_write_flags) is
now green for pfdebug as well.

Regression test:
  command_info_flags_for_connection_movablekeys_subset_matches_upstream
  pins all 37 rows.
Existing flag/ACL/AOF goldens stay green.
Three batches (frankenredis-flagsbatch1/2/3) close out
frankenredis-commandflagsaudit.

title: COMMAND GETKEYS/GETKEYSANDFLAGS SPUBLISH should emit 'no key arguments' error
description:
SPUBLISH ch msg should return 'ERR The command has no key arguments' on vendored, but fr returns an empty array. Upstream commands/spublish.json has no key_specs; commandHasKeys() returns 0 so getKeysFromCommandWithSpecs reports the upstream no-key-arguments error.

Repro:
  COMMAND GETKEYSANDFLAGS SPUBLISH ch msg
  vendored: ERR The command has no key arguments
  fr:       (empty array)

PUBLISH is handled correctly (returns the same error). Likely the SPUBLISH entry in command_has_keys() lookup is misclassified.

Discovered iter 140 probe sweep.
close_reason: completed

title: string.format errors don't route via current_invocation_name (direct vs pcall shape)
description:
Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4 — pcall(string.format, ...) emits the anonymous-C-function form:

  pcall(string.format,'%d','hi')
    vendored: bad argument #2 to '?' (number expected, got string)
    fr:       user_script:1: bad argument #2 to 'format' (number expected, got string)

  pcall(string.format,'%s')
    vendored: bad argument #2 to '?' (no value)
    fr:       user_script:1: bad argument #2 to 'format' (no value)

  pcall(string.format,nil)
    vendored: bad argument #1 to '?' (string expected, got nil)
    fr:       user_script:1: bad argument #1 to 'format' (string expected, got nil)

  pcall(string.format,'%K',5)
    vendored: invalid option '%K' to 'format'    (luaL_error: 'format' name preserved, prefix gone)
    fr:       user_script:1: invalid option '%K' to 'format'

Direct calls (string.format(...) without pcall) and Lua-wrapped pcalls already match — only the direct-pcall(C-builtin) shape diverges.

This is the same dual-shape pattern as `load()` (frankenredis-i18ug) — fr's `lua_format_argerror` already handles luaL_argerror correctly via `current_invocation_name`, but `lua_string_format` hardcodes 'user_script:1: bad argument #N to format ...' wording. Two fixes needed:

1. Thread inv_name: Option<&str> through `lua_string_format` and replace hardcoded errors with `lua_format_argerror(inv_name, "format", idx, reason)`.

2. The "invalid option '%K' to 'format'" error (luaL_error, not luaL_argerror) keeps the 'format' name in both shapes but loses the prefix when invoked via pcall. Render conditionally on inv_name.

Also affects the arg-1 fmt-string validation at line ~6017 in the `"string.format"` branch which hardcodes 'user_script:1: bad argument #1 to format'.
close_reason: Routed string.format errors through lua_format_argerror by threading current_invocation_name (Option<&str>) into lua_string_format. The luaL_error 'invalid option' branch handled separately via invalid_option closure (preserves 'format' name in both shapes but drops user_script:1: prefix when invoked via pcall). Regression test string_format_pcall_shape_drops_prefix_and_uses_anonymous_name_fllxr pins all 10 pcall shapes + 2 direct-call regressions + 1 lua-wrapped-pcall regression. Live-diff vs vendored 16380: 10/10 scripted pcall cases match.

title: Add fuzz target for runtime execute_bytes RESP ingress
close_reason: Completed

title: FUNCTION LOAD silently accepts table-form register_function with no callback field
description: Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4 (function_lua.c::luaRegisterFunction): a table-form invocation like `register_function{function_name='nocb'}` should be rejected with 'ERR Error registering functions: ERR redis.register_function must get a callback argument'. fr's textual parser only checked for `function_name=`, so an incomplete table-form was silently registered as an empty FunctionEntry, leaving FUNCTION LIST/FCALL in an inconsistent state.
close_reason: Added has_table_key helper in fr-store/src/lib.rs:12668 (presence check independent of value shape) and a callback-presence guard in function_load before extract_function_metadata runs. Test pin function_load_table_form_rejects_missing_callback covers the wording and the success-path sanity case. 322/322 fr-store tests pass.

title: fr-store: FUNCTION LOAD 'Engine X not found' wording uppercases engine name instead of preserving original case
Add XPENDING IDLE option support (Redis 6.2+)
description:
## Problem

fr-command/src/lib.rs line 4629: XPENDING extended form parser requires exactly 6 or 7 args. Redis 6.2+ allows: XPENDING key group [IDLE min-idle-time] start end count [consumer]

With IDLE option: 8 args (no consumer) or 9 args (with consumer). Currently returns WrongArity.

## Implementation Plan

1. Check if argv[3] is \"IDLE\" (case-insensitive)
2. If IDLE present: parse min_idle_time from argv[4], shift start/end/count to argv[5..7], optional consumer at argv[8]
3. If IDLE not present: existing parsing at argv[3..5] for start/end/count, optional consumer at argv[6]
4. Pass min_idle_time to xpending_entries as a filter
5. In Store::xpending_entries, filter entries where idle_ms < min_idle_time

## Files to Modify

- crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs: XPENDING argument parsing
- crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs: xpending_entries add min_idle_time filter parameter
close_reason: XPENDING extended form now supports IDLE min-idle-time option (Redis 6.2+), with min_idle_ms filter parameter in xpending_entries

title: FUNCTION LOAD silently accepts duplicate function names within one library
description: Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4 (functions.c::functionLibCreateFunction): a library that registers the same function name twice should be rejected with 'ERR Error registering functions: ERR Function already exists in the library'. fr's function_load loop pushed a duplicate FunctionEntry, so FUNCTION LIST would emit the function twice and FCALL silently dispatched to whichever appeared first in the wrapper script.
close_reason: Fixed in fr-store/src/lib.rs:10403 (functions.iter().any duplicate-name check) with the upstream-prefixed wording. Test pin function_load_rejects_duplicate_function_name_within_library added. 321/321 fr-store tests pass.

title: FUNCTION LOAD ignores flags field validation in table-form register_function
description: Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4 (function_lua.c::luaRegisterFunction): the flags field in a table-form register_function call must (1) be a table, and (2) contain only registered flag names — accepted set in 7.2.4 is no-writes / allow-oom / allow-stale / no-cluster / allow-cross-slot-keys. fr's textual parser ignored the flags field entirely, so `flags='x'`, `flags=42`, or `flags={'bogus'}` all silently loaded the library. Upstream wordings: 'flags argument to redis.register_function must be a table representing function flags' and 'unknown flag given' (both prefixed with 'ERR Error registering functions:').
close_reason: Added extract_table_value_raw and extract_flag_strings helpers in fr-store/src/lib.rs:12695-12762; wired flags validation into function_load (must-be-table check first, then per-entry flag-name check). Test pin function_load_table_form_validates_flags covers scalar/integer flags, bogus-flag, mixed valid+bogus, all five valid flags, and empty flags table. 325/325 fr-store tests pass.

title: FUNCTION LOAD discards table-form flags, breaking FCALL_RO no-writes dispatch and FUNCTION LIST flags output
description: function_load validated table-form flags (after frankenredis-fnflags) but always pushed `flags: Vec::new()` onto FunctionEntry, so the registered flag set was discarded. Two consequences: (1) FCALL_RO's no-writes lookup at fr-command/src/lib.rs:9476 always returned false, so a function correctly declared with flags={'no-writes'} could never be dispatched via FCALL_RO — every read-only call returned 'ERR Can not execute a script with write flag using *_ro command.'. (2) FUNCTION LIST surfaced an empty flags array for every function, hiding the registered flag set from clients. Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4 confirmed both issues.
close_reason: function_load now captures flags via extract_table_value_raw + extract_flag_strings at the FunctionEntry push site (fr-store/src/lib.rs:10500-10520). Test pin function_load_table_form_captures_flags_into_function_entry covers single-flag, multi-flag (preserving order), and flagless cases. 326/326 fr-store tests pass.

title: Lua setmetatable/getmetatable ignore __metatable protection
close_reason: setmetatable/getmetatable in crates/fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs now honor the __metatable protection field: (1) getmetatable returns the __metatable field value (masking the real metatable) when present, falling back to the metatable otherwise; (2) setmetatable raises 'cannot change a protected metatable' (prefix toggled by current_invocation_name to match where(1) behavior) when the target's existing metatable has a non-nil __metatable; (3) setmetatable with only one arg now raises 'bad argument #2 (nil or table expected)' — previously fr silently coerced missing → nil and cleared the metatable. Regression test lua_metatable_protection_and_setmetatable_arg_check_fnh42 pins 7 cases including direct-vs-wrapped pcall variants. Live diff vs vendored Redis 7.2.4 on :16380 clean for all 13 probed inputs.

title: FUNCTION LOAD header validation gaps: no-newline payload + duplicate name= silently accepted
description:
Two related header-validation gaps confirmed by differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4 (functions.c::functionExtractLibMetaData):

1. NO-NEWLINE PAYLOAD: A single-line payload like '#!lua name=l1' (no '\n') should surface 'ERR Invalid library metadata' upstream because the shebang is unterminated. fr previously fell through to the function-extraction loop and surfaced 'ERR No functions registered'.

2. DUPLICATE name=: A header with two name= tokens should surface 'ERR Invalid metadata value, name argument was given multiple times' upstream. fr previously silently overwrote and accepted the second value.

Upstream behavior verified at /data/projects/frankenredis/legacy_redis_code/redis/src/functions.c:904-907 and 923-927.
close_reason: Fixed in fr-store/src/lib.rs:10271 (no-newline check via find('\n')) and 10286-10295 (duplicate name= rejection via name_seen flag). Two test pins added in fr-store/src/lib.rs (function_load_no_newline_payload_returns_invalid_library_metadata + function_load_rejects_duplicate_name_argument). 320/320 fr-store tests pass.

title: fr-store: FUNCTION LOAD silently ignores unknown shebang meta tokens instead of upstream 'Invalid metadata value given' rejection
description:
fr-store::function_load at lib.rs:10282-10288 only recognizes the engine (first token) and 'name=X' tokens; any other token in the shebang line is silently ignored. Upstream functions.c rejects with 'ERR Invalid metadata value given: <token>'. Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4:
  '#!lua name=lib extra_token\nbody'   -> upstream 'Invalid metadata value given: extra_token', fr loads silently
  '#!lua badkey=value name=lib\nbody' -> upstream 'Invalid metadata value given: badkey=value', fr loads silently

Fix: in the meta-token loop, after recognizing index 0 (engine) and 'name=' prefix, reject any unknown token with the upstream wording. The check fires before the LUA gate to match upstream ordering.

title: FUNCTION LOAD silently accepts table-form register_function with no function_name field
description: Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4 (function_lua.c::luaRegisterFunction): a table-form invocation like `register_function{callback=fn}` or `register_function{}` should fail with 'ERR Error registering functions: ERR redis.register_function must get a function name argument'. Upstream validates function_name BEFORE callback. fr's textual parser previously fell through to extract_function_metadata, returned None, and surfaced the wrong 'No functions registered' wording when no other registrations existed in the library.
close_reason: Reordered the table-form presence checks in fr-store/src/lib.rs:10384-10412 so function_name fires first (matching upstream), then callback. Added test pin function_load_table_form_rejects_missing_function_name covering callback-only, empty-table, and description-only variants. 323/323 fr-store tests pass.

title: fr-command: FUNCTION LOAD silently accepts unknown options + empty-body libraries instead of upstream rejection
description:
Two related defects in FUNCTION LOAD parsing surfaced by differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4:

(1) FUNCTION LOAD <BANANA> <code> (non-REPLACE first arg with code following) is silently mis-parsed: fr's parser at lib.rs:9020-9028 sets code_idx=2 when arg2 != REPLACE, treats BANANA as the code body, and the subsequent function_load('BANANA') fails with 'ERR Missing library metadata'. Upstream emits 'ERR Unknown option given: BANANA' before parsing the code.

(2) FUNCTION LOAD '#!lua name=lib\n' (well-formed shebang, no register_function calls) is accepted by fr, returning the library name. Upstream rejects with 'ERR No functions registered'.

Both fixes are localized: (1) reject any non-REPLACE token in the option slot with the dedicated wording before passing to function_load; (2) inside store::function_load, after the function-extraction loop, verify functions.is_empty() and return the upstream wording.

title: FUNCTION LOAD positional form silently accepts a third argument
description: Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4 (function_lua.c::luaRegisterFunction): a positional call with three or more arguments like `register_function('f', function() return 1 end, 'extra')` should fail with 'ERR Error registering functions: ERR wrong number of arguments to redis.register_function'. fr previously walked past the function expression and silently ignored trailing arg(s), pushing a FunctionEntry as if the call were well-formed.
close_reason: Added positional_call_has_extra_arg_after_function helper that walks past function(params) body end via paren-balanced match for params and the keyword-block depth counter for body, then checks for a trailing comma. Wired into the positional-form validation chain after the second-arg type check. Test pin function_load_positional_form_rejects_third_argument covers the failure path plus a two-arg sanity case. 330/330 fr-store tests pass.

title: FUNCTION LOAD positional form silently accepts zero-arg and single-string-arg invocations
description:
Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4 (function_lua.c::luaRegisterFunction) surfaced two arity gaps in fr's positional-form parser:
  (1) `register_function()` (zero args) → upstream 'ERR wrong number of arguments to redis.register_function'
  (2) `register_function('f')` (single string arg) → upstream 'ERR calling redis.register_function with a single argument is only applicable to Lua table (representing named arguments).'
fr fell through to extract_function_metadata in both cases, surfacing the wrong 'No functions registered' wording (zero-arg) or silently loading a body-less function entry (single-arg) that would later fail at FCALL with 'attempt to call a nil value'.
close_reason: Added zero-arg and single-string-arg arity guards in the positional-form arm of function_load (fr-store/src/lib.rs:10515 and 10550), each surfacing the upstream-prefixed wording. Test pin function_load_positional_form_arity_wordings covers both cases. 329/329 fr-store tests pass.

title: FUNCTION LOAD positional form silently accepts non-string first arg and non-function callback
description:
Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4 (function_lua.c::luaRegisterFunction) surfaced two silent-acceptance gaps in fr's positional-form parser:
  (1) `register_function('f', 'not_fn')` (callback as string/number/table/nil) → upstream 'ERR second argument to redis.register_function must be a function'
  (2) `register_function(true, function() ... end)` (non-string first arg) → upstream 'ERR first argument to redis.register_function must be a string'
fr's textual parser only used extract_quoted_string on the first arg; non-quoted first args returned None (skipping the line, surfacing 'No functions registered') and string callbacks were silently accepted, pushing a FunctionEntry whose body was missing from the wrapper script.
close_reason: Added positional-form arm in function_load alongside the table-form one: when register_function is followed by `(`, parse the first arg (must be a quoted string), then check the second arg starts with `function`. Test pin function_load_positional_form_type_checks_callback_and_first_arg covers string/number callbacks, non-string first arg, and the success-path sanity case. 328/328 fr-store tests pass.

title: FUNCTION LOAD: per-function register_function name-validation error missing 'ERR Error registering functions:' prefix
description: When a register_function call inside a library uses an invalid name (e.g. 'bad-name'), upstream Redis 7.2.4 returns 'ERR Error registering functions: ERR Library names ...'. fr drops the prefix, conflating per-function and header (name=) name-validation paths. Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4 confirmed divergence.
close_reason: Fixed in fr-store/src/lib.rs:10359 — per-function name-validation now emits the upstream 'ERR Error registering functions: ' prefix; existing test pin at fr-store/src/lib.rs:18108 updated.

title: Fix remaining live ZSET parity gaps in ZMPOP and ZADD conflicts
close_reason: Fixed ZMPOP/BZMPOP tuple nesting and Redis-exact ZADD conflict wording

title: FCALL/FCALL_RO fails for table-form register_function libraries — wrapper script transformation only handles positional form
description: fr-command::transform_register_function only matched the positional form `register_function('name', function(...) ... end)`, returning None for table-form `register_function{function_name='n', callback=function(...) ... end}`. The dispatch loop then SKIPPED the line entirely (continue without push), so the function body was lost from the wrapper script. FCALL/FCALL_RO on a table-form library always failed with 'ERR Error running function: attempt to call a nil value'. Discovered while validating the fnflagstore fix end-to-end via dispatch_argv.
close_reason: Extended transform_register_function to detect a `{` after the call site and delegate to a new transform_register_function_table_form helper that extracts function_name (any quote style) and callback function body using brace-depth tracking and the same coarse keyword-block depth counter used in fr-store. Test pin fcall_ro_succeeds_for_table_form_function_with_no_writes_flag exercises the full LOAD → FCALL_RO path. 837 passed (4 pre-existing stream failures only), 326/326 fr-store.

title: FUNCTION LOAD silently accepts wrong-typed values for function_name/description/callback in table form
description:
Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4 (function_lua.c::luaRegisterFunction) surfaced three silent-acceptance gaps in fr's table-form validation:
  (1) function_name={'a'} → upstream 'ERR function_name argument given to redis.register_function must be a string'
  (2) description={'x'} → upstream 'ERR description argument given to redis.register_function must be a string'
  (3) callback='not_fn' → upstream 'ERR callback argument given to redis.register_function must be a function'
fr's textual parser only checked PRESENCE of these keys, not the value shape; non-conforming values would either skip metadata extraction silently or load a broken library where the callback is never callable.
close_reason: Added head-byte type checks in function_load before the unknown-field scan: a `{` head for function_name/description triggers the must-be-a-string wordings; a value not starting with `function` for callback triggers the must-be-a-function wording. All three wordings are upstream-prefixed with 'ERR Error registering functions:'. Test pin function_load_table_form_type_checks_string_and_callback_fields covers all three. 327/327 fr-store tests pass.

title: FUNCTION LOAD silently accepts table-form register_function with unknown fields
description: Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4 (function_lua.c::luaRegisterFunction): a table-form invocation like `register_function{function_name='f', callback=fn, bogus='x'}` should be rejected with 'ERR Error registering functions: ERR unknown argument given to redis.register_function'. The four valid fields are function_name, callback, description, flags. fr's textual parser previously ignored unknown fields silently — even typos like `function_nme` or `flgs` would slip through.
close_reason: Added find_unknown_table_key in fr-store/src/lib.rs:12695 — walks the table body skipping strings, nested braces, and `function ... end` blocks (coarse keyword-block depth counter), then flags any top-level <ident>= whose ident isn't in the allowed set. Wired into function_load. Test pin function_load_table_form_rejects_unknown_field covers AFTER-callback, BEFORE-callback, and all-four-valid-fields cases. 324/324 fr-store tests pass.

title: fr-command/lua: EVAL error wording divergences for unknown command + script compile errors
description:
Several Lua-script error wording divergences from upstream Redis 7.2 script_lua.c:

- EVAL 'return redis.call("NOPE")' 0:
    fr 'ERR Error running script: ERR unknown command NOPE, with args beginning with: '
    redis 'ERR Unknown Redis command called from script script: <sha1>, on @user_script:1.'
- EVAL 'return redis.pcall("NOPE")' 0 (in-table form):
    fr 'ERR unknown command NOPE...'
    redis 'ERR Unknown Redis command called from script'
- EVAL '@@@' 0 (script compile error):
    fr 'ERR Error running script: unexpected character ...'
    redis 'ERR Error compiling script (new function): user_script:1: unexpected symbol near ...'

Upstream uses a fixed prefix 'Error compiling script (new function):' for compile errors and 'Unknown Redis command called from script' for unknown-command paths, plus appends script SHA1 + source location for the redis.call (not pcall) variant.

All cosmetic but visible.

title: EPIC: Eliminate all stubs, placeholders, and hardcoded zeros from production code
description:
# Stub/Placeholder Remediation Epic

## Motivation
A comprehensive audit identified 28+ stubs, placeholders, hardcoded zeros, and incomplete implementations across the FrankenRedis codebase. While many are in fr-command (the dispatch_argv path used by Lua scripts and AOF replay), some affect the runtime directly (ACL SAVE/LOAD, LATENCY tracking). Eliminating these is critical for:

1. **Lua script compatibility**: redis.call() from Lua goes through dispatch_argv, hitting fr-command stubs instead of runtime implementations for SLOWLOG, CLIENT, etc.
2. **Monitoring tool compatibility**: INFO stats with hardcoded zeros break Redis monitoring dashboards (redis-stat, RedisInsight, Grafana).
3. **Drop-in replacement fidelity**: The project's #1 goal is full behavioral parity with legacy Redis.

## Architecture Approach
Rather than duplicating runtime logic into fr-command, we use a **shared state pattern**:
- Add tracking counters and data structures to Store (which both fr-command and fr-runtime can access)
- For runtime-only state (blocked clients, maxmemory), pass context through new Store fields that the server populates each tick
- For LATENCY, add a LatencyTracker to Store that records samples from both dispatch paths

## Execution Layers (in dependency order)
1. **Foundation**: Add stat counters and tracking structures to Store
2. **LATENCY**: Build latency sample infrastructure + wire into command execution
3. **ACL persistence**: Implement real ACL SAVE/LOAD file I/O
4. **INFO accuracy**: Wire real counters into all INFO sections
5. **Stream metrics**: Track XINFO radix-tree and deletion metadata
6. **RDB LZF**: Implement LZF decompression for full RDB compatibility
7. **DEBUG commands**: Implement real behavior for DEBUG subcommands
8. **Lua-path stubs**: Forward SLOWLOG/CLIENT from fr-command to shared state

## Success Criteria
- Zero hardcoded zeros in INFO output where real data is available
- LATENCY LATEST/HISTORY returns real samples after threshold crossings
- ACL SAVE/LOAD round-trips to disk
- redis.call("SLOWLOG", "GET") from Lua returns real slow log entries
- RDB files with LZF-compressed strings can be decoded
- All existing conformance tests continue to pass
- New conformance tests validate each fix
close_reason: done

title: INFO Memory: maxmemory/maxmemory_human/maxmemory_policy emitted in wrong position — vendored emits after used_memory_scripts_human, fr emits after used_memory_dataset_perc
description:
Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4 INFO memory:

ven order:
  used_memory_dataset_perc:..
  allocator_allocated:..
  allocator_active:..
  ...
  used_memory_scripts:..
  used_memory_scripts_human:..
  maxmemory:..               <-- here
  maxmemory_human:..
  maxmemory_policy:..
  allocator_frag_ratio:..

fr order:
  used_memory_dataset_perc:..
  maxmemory:..               <-- WRONG: emitted way too early
  maxmemory_human:..
  maxmemory_policy:..
  allocator_allocated:..
  ...

Per upstream server.c::genRedisInfoString memory section, the maxmemory triple is bunched with the maxmemory-related fragmentation/eviction lines later in the section, not in the middle of the dataset/allocator block. Move it from line ~12715 (right after used_memory_dataset_perc) to right after used_memory_scripts_human at line ~12774.
close_reason: Moved maxmemory/maxmemory_human/maxmemory_policy emit point from after used_memory_dataset_perc to after used_memory_scripts_human, matching upstream server.c::genRedisInfoString. Pinned via info_memory_emits_maxmemory_triple_after_scripts_human_per_upstream.

title: Tighten LATENCY admin command semantics
close_reason: Completed

title: fr-command: XSETID accepts last-id smaller than current stream top, missing upstream rejection
description:
Upstream t_stream.c::xsetidCommand validates that the provided last-id is >= the current stream top entry id. If smaller, it replies 'ERR The ID specified in XSETID is smaller than the target stream top item'.

fr-command's xsetid_cmd silently accepted any last-id, allowing the stream's last_id to regress below the top entry. This breaks the invariant that XADD '*' produces monotonically increasing IDs — after a regression, subsequent XADDs would generate IDs that conflict with existing entries.

Differential probe vs vendored redis 7.2.4:
  XADD st 100-0 f v
  XSETID st 50-0 → fr OK, redis 'smaller than the target stream top item'

Add the top-id check after option parsing.

title: AUTH user/pass does not switch session.authenticated_user when default has nopass (requirepass empty)
description:
When requirepass is empty (no global password) and the default user has nopass, vendored Redis still honours an explicit `AUTH <user> <pass>` and switches the session to that user. fr's handle_auth_command returns OK but the session reverts to default — verified empirically.

Reproduction:
  ACL SETUSER alice on '>pw' '~k*' '+get'
  (on a fresh connection)
  AUTH alice pw      -> OK  (both)
  ACL WHOAMI         -> vendored: NOPERM ('acl|whoami' denied for alice)
                     -> fr:       'default'  (session reverted)

  With CONFIG SET requirepass <some>:
  AUTH alice pw      -> OK  (both)
  ACL WHOAMI         -> NOPERM (both)  ✓

So fr's switch only sticks when auth_required() is true. Likely cause: a downstream refresh_authentication_for_server call between frames silently resets authenticated_user back to default when auth_state.auth_required() is false.

Impact:
  ACL command/key/channel enforcement (frankenredis-7ymk5) only kicks in for non-default users. So without requirepass, ACL restrictions effectively cannot be applied in fr.

Out of scope: this bead is the follow-up to frankenredis-7ymk5 — that bead removed the script_nesting_level gate from the ACL enforcement path; this bead fixes the session-bridge logic that prevents AUTH from switching users in the first place.

Test plan:
  - Connect with default credentials (no requirepass).
  - Run ACL SETUSER alice on '>pw' '+get'.
  - On a fresh connection: AUTH alice pw, then ACL WHOAMI must return 'alice' (or NOPERM on vendored, depending on alice's ACL).
  - SET k1 v on alice's session must return NOPERM.
  - On the same default-user session that ran SETUSER: ACL WHOAMI must remain 'default'.

title: COMMAND INFO emits subcommands in alphabetical order; vendored uses declaration order
description:
COMMAND INFO for parent commands with subcommands (OBJECT, FUNCTION, ACL, CLIENT, CLUSTER, etc.) emits the subcommand list in alphabetical order. Vendored Redis 7.2.4 emits them in source-declaration order (the order they appear in commands/*.json).

Reproduction:
  COMMAND INFO object
    vendored order: freq, refcount, idletime, encoding, help
    fr order:       encoding, freq, help, idletime, refcount   (alphabetical)

  COMMAND INFO function
    vendored order: list, load, flush, dump, kill, delete, restore, help, stats
    fr order:       delete, dump, flush, help, kill, list, load, restore, stats

The subcommand metadata itself (arity, flags, key specs) matches between fr and vendored — only the iteration order differs.

Scope: low-impact (cosmetic), but a real parity gap for clients that parse COMMAND INFO output positionally. Fix would require preserving each parent's subcommand declaration order in COMMAND_TABLE or a side table.

Probe sweep #117 (COMMAND INFO object/function, inline).
close_reason: completed

title: Update FEATURE_PARITY.md Current State section to match code reality
description:

title: [MOCK] Lua nested global assignment still relies on unwrap invariants
description:
Evidence: crates/fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs:2033-2037 uses names.last().unwrap(), path.last_mut().unwrap(), and path.pop().unwrap() while rebuilding nested table assignments.

Current gap: this path still depends on internal panic-on-invariant shortcuts instead of explicit Lua errors, so malformed or future-expanded assignment callers can crash rather than returning a script error.

Real impl path: harden the table-path rebuild helper in crates/fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs to validate empty-path / empty-field cases and return structured Lua errors instead of unwrap panics.
close_reason: Completed

title: redis parity: fix WrongSubcommandArity formatting for config and client
description: Revert config and client to use 'command' instead of 'subcommand' in their wrong arity strings.
close_reason: Completed

title: Implement Lua coroutine stubs that return proper Redis-compatible errors
description:
# Lua Coroutine Stubs

## What
The hand-written Lua interpreter (4,750 lines) correctly implements most of Lua 5.1 but is missing coroutines (create/resume/yield/status). While rarely used in Redis Lua scripts, calling them currently produces confusing errors.

## Implementation
Add stub functions that return clear error messages:
- coroutine.create → error "coroutines are not supported in FrankenRedis Lua"
- coroutine.resume → same
- coroutine.yield → same
- coroutine.status → same

This matches Redis behavior (Redis also does not support coroutines in EVAL).

## Files
- crates/fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs (add coroutine table stubs)
close_reason: Added coroutine table with stubs for create/resume/yield/status/wrap/running. All return 'attempt to call a nil value' error, matching Redis behavior. Added 2 tests: coroutine_stubs_return_error and coroutine_table_is_accessible.

FrankenRedis exposes logfile as a static CONFIG parameter and currently accepts CONFIG SET through the static fallback. Add runtime and core_config conformance coverage that rejects mutation while preserving CONFIG GET visibility.

title: Lua string.format %s tolerates booleans (should error)
description: After xse81's tightening, string.format('%s', true)/false returns 'true'/'false' but Lua 5.1's luaO_pushvfstring rejects booleans for %s (only string and number are accepted). Live differential probe: fr returns 'true' but vendored errors with 'user_script:1: bad argument #2 to format (string expected, got boolean)'. Fix: drop the LuaValue::Bool arm from the %s match in fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs so it falls through to the type-error path.
close_reason: Dropped the LuaValue::Bool arm from the %s match so it falls through to the standard type-error path. Live differential probe: string.format('%s', true/false) now byte-exact error with vendored ('user_script:1: bad argument #2 to format (string expected, got boolean)'); regression-clean for 'hi', 42, 3.14 (still pass), nil (still errors). Tests: fr-command 910 pass.

title: redis parity: transaction-control commands should reject script context
description: Vendored Redis marks MULTI, EXEC, DISCARD, WATCH, and UNWATCH as NOSCRIPT in legacy_redis_code/redis/src/commands/multi.json, exec.json, discard.json, watch.json, and unwatch.json. Lua redis.call() currently routes through crates/fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs:3556 into fr-command::dispatch_argv, but the fr-command layer has no transaction-control handlers, so scripts currently fall through to unknown-command behavior instead of Redis-style NOSCRIPT for this family. Add a script-path rejection that preserves normal client/runtime handling outside Lua, and cover the transaction-control surface from scripts.

Use the existing RDB serializer/loader as the Rust implementation surface and add targeted unit/adversarial tests with reason-code-compatible expectations for persist.rdb.opcode_contract_violation / persist.rdb.aux_handling_invalid follow-on runtime wiring.

FrankenRedis exposes set-proc-title as a static CONFIG parameter and currently accepts CONFIG SET through the static fallback. Add runtime and fixture coverage that rejects mutation while preserving CONFIG GET visibility.

title: fr-command APPEND error wording diverges from upstream: '(512MB)' should be '(proto-max-bulk-len)'
description:
REVIEW MODE Tick 14 finding via /testing-conformance-harnesses sweep on string-size error wording.

UPSTREAM (t_string.c::checkStringLength line 48):
  addReplyError(c,"string exceeds maximum allowed size (proto-max-bulk-len)");

This wording is shared by APPEND (line 715) and SETRANGE (lines 464, 484). The trailing parenthetical names the *config knob* a user would tune, not a fixed byte size — proto_max_bulk_len defaults to 512 MiB but is reconfigurable.

FR INCONSISTENCY (crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs):
  Line 2700 (APPEND): 'ERR string exceeds maximum allowed size (512MB)'
  Line 9422 (SETRANGE): 'ERR string exceeds maximum allowed size (proto-max-bulk-len)'  ← matches upstream

APPEND alone uses '(512MB)', diverging from both upstream and SETRANGE.

REPRO:
  SET k <huge string near 512MB>
  APPEND k <small> → fr emits 'ERR string exceeds maximum allowed size (512MB)'
                     upstream: 'ERR string exceeds maximum allowed size (proto-max-bulk-len)'

Severity: [LOW]. Wire-format conformance gap. A test suite scraping for the upstream wording would fail; a user trying to relax the limit would not see the config knob name they need to bump.

FIX:
  Replace '(512MB)' with '(proto-max-bulk-len)' in append() to match upstream and SETRANGE.
close_reason:
Replaced '(512MB)' with '(proto-max-bulk-len)' to match upstream t_string.c::checkStringLength wording.

Two call sites carried the old wording:
1. crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs::append (line 2700) — fired by APPEND when current_len + added_len > 512 MiB.
2. crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs (line 2738) — fired by BITFIELD when needed_bytes > 512 MiB.

Both now emit 'ERR string exceeds maximum allowed size (proto-max-bulk-len)' — the same wording fr already used in SETRANGE (fr-command line 9425) and that upstream emits at t_string.c line 48 / bitops.c paths.

No regression test added because triggering the size check requires an actual ≥512 MiB allocation in the test runner — impractical. The change is a one-line wording update in two locations; visual review and the existing test suites are sufficient.

Verification:
- cargo test -p fr-command --lib append → 2/2 pass.
- cargo test -p fr-store --lib → 318/318 pass.
- cargo clippy -p fr-command --lib + cargo clippy -p fr-store --lib → both clean.
- grep across fr-* crates confirms '(512MB)' is gone from active code (only artifact files retain it).

[LOW] severity. Wire-format conformance gap; a client suite scraping for the upstream wording would otherwise mis-categorize the error.

Add a focused regression proving self-generated multi-library FUNCTION DUMP payloads restore back to the same sorted snapshot and re-encode byte-identically regardless of insertion order.

title: Add fixed empty-policy FUNCTION RESTORE collision regression
description: fr-store already pins explicit APPEND collision atomicity and empty-policy success on disjoint payloads, but it does not freeze the empty-policy collision path. Add a focused regression proving an empty FUNCTION RESTORE policy inherits APPEND semantics, rejects colliding payloads, and leaves the pre-existing library snapshot byte-stable.
close_reason: Completed

title: Add fixed empty-policy empty FUNCTION RESTORE regression
description: fr-store already treats an empty FUNCTION RESTORE policy as APPEND and already pins explicit APPEND behavior on empty dumps, but it does not freeze the default-policy plus empty-dump path directly. Add a focused regression proving an empty policy with an empty FUNCTION DUMP payload is a byte-stable no-op over an existing multi-library snapshot.
close_reason: Completed

title: Add metamorphic empty-policy empty FUNCTION RESTORE regression
description: fr-store now has a fixed regression for empty-policy FUNCTION RESTORE against the empty dump sentinel, but the adjacent metamorphic suite still skips that default-policy path. Add a generated-library property proving an empty policy with an empty FUNCTION DUMP payload is a snapshot-preserving, byte-stable no-op across randomized library sets.
close_reason: Completed

title: Add fuzz target for PSYNC reply parser
close_reason: Completed

title: Subscribe-context error message uses parent command name instead of 'parent|sub' for CLIENT and other containers
description:
Differential probe vs vendored Redis 7.2.4 (16380):

  Connection in SUBSCRIBE state issues 'CLIENT INFO':
    fr  : ERR Can't execute 'client': only (P|S)SUBSCRIBE / (P|S)UNSUBSCRIBE / PING / QUIT / RESET are allowed in this context
    ven : ERR Can't execute 'client|info': only ...

Same divergence for CLIENT LIST and likely other CLIENT subcommands. Upstream server.c uses c->cmd->fullname which expands to 'parent|sub' for subcommands.

Fix: extend pubsub_blocked_command_name() in crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs:3985 to namespace CLIENT (and all other container commands) the same way it already does for PUBSUB. Currently only PUBSUB subcommands get the 'pubsub|sub' treatment; the helper falls through to the bare command name for everything else.

Affected commands (any container subcommand emitted in subscribe context will be named without its sub-qualifier):
  CLIENT INFO/LIST/KILL/PAUSE/REPLY/SETINFO/SETNAME/...
  Likely also: CONFIG/DEBUG/OBJECT/MEMORY/SLOWLOG/LATENCY/XINFO/XGROUP/FUNCTION/MODULE/SCRIPT

CONFIG/DEBUG/etc. need verification — they may be allowed in subscribe context (admin-class) but the error wording matters for any that aren't.
close_reason:
Extended pubsub_blocked_command_name() in fr-runtime + check_subscription_mode_gate() in fr-server to namespace all 16 container commands (acl, client, cluster, command, config, debug, function, latency, memory, module, object, pubsub, script, slowlog, xgroup, xinfo) to 'parent|sub' instead of just the bare parent — matches upstream server.c which uses c->cmd->fullname. Both runtime-level dispatch and TCP-server-level gate now produce the same per-upstream wording.

Pinned via pubsub_context_error_namespaces_container_subcommands_per_upstream covering 17 representative subcommands across all 16 containers. Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4 (16380): zero divergences across CLIENT/CONFIG/DEBUG/OBJECT/MEMORY/SLOWLOG/LATENCY/COMMAND/FUNCTION/MODULE/SCRIPT/XGROUP/XINFO/ACL/CLUSTER subcommands.

359/359 fr-runtime lib + 883/883 fr-command lib + 34/34 fr-server lib tests pass. Workspace clippy clean under -D warnings (re-fixed clippy::manual_contains hint in CONTAINERS lookup).

title: fr-runtime: CLUSTER SLAVES (deprecated alias) returns 'unknown subcommand' instead of upstream's behavior
description:
fr-runtime's classify_cluster_subcommand at crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs:1522 doesn't recognize SLAVES (the deprecated alias for REPLICAS), so it falls through to ClusterSubcommand::Unknown which returns 'ERR unknown subcommand SLAVES. Try CLUSTER HELP.' Upstream Redis 7.2 still accepts SLAVES (per legacy_redis_code/redis/src/commands/cluster-slaves.json) and reports 'ERR This instance has cluster support disabled' (when cluster is off) or the actual replica array (when cluster is on).

Acceptance: 'CLUSTER SLAVES <node-id>' against fr returns 'ERR This instance has cluster support disabled' on a non-cluster instance, matching upstream byte-for-byte. (When cluster mode is enabled it should behave identically to CLUSTER REPLICAS.)

Reference: fr port 16700 vs vendored redis port 16701, 'CLUSTER SLAVES abc'. fr: 'ERR unknown subcommand SLAVES. Try CLUSTER HELP.'. redis: 'ERR This instance has cluster support disabled'. Caught by ad-hoc differential probe.

Fix is straightforward: add SLAVES to the cluster subcommand classifier in fr-runtime, and add a SLAVES branch in fr-command::cluster_cmd that mirrors the REPLICAS branch (since SLAVES is just the legacy alias).

title: Align live CLIENT handler with fail-closed unsupported tracking semantics
description: fr-runtime still accepts unsupported CLIENT TRACKING/CACHING and has drifted from fr-command/runtime intent; fix live semantics and add regressions for server-visible mismatches.
close_reason: Live CLIENT TRACKING/CACHING now fail closed again

title: fr-command/lua: coroutine.resume(co, args) drops args on subsequent resumes — yield's return value is always nil
description:
REVIEW MODE finding (continued cross-review of bw15 and follow-ups):

  local co = coroutine.create(function()
      local v = coroutine.yield()
      return v
  end)
  coroutine.resume(co)                              -- runs body up to yield
  local _, r = coroutine.resume(co, 'second-args')  -- upstream: r = 'second-args'
  return tostring(r)

  upstream Lua: 'second-args'
  fr:           'nil'

Two interlocking issues:

1. resume_coroutine (fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs:2566-2572) only uses
   the args param on FIRST resume (when inner.env is None). On
   subsequent resumes, the saved env is reused and args are dropped.

2. The yield call's Err(SENTINEL) abort happens inside eval_expr,
   before the binding step. So `local v = coroutine.yield()` never
   reaches the assignment — the LocalAssign stmt is consumed by
   exec_coroutine_stmts' next_pc + 1 advance, and on resume the
   stmt is skipped entirely. v is never bound; later 'return v'
   evaluates to nil.

Either issue alone would make yield-with-return-value broken; both
together confirm bw15 only safely supports yield when:
  - yield is at the body's outer-stmt level (ztawj already enforces),
  - yield is the WHOLE stmt (no surrounding LocalAssign / Assign /
    return / function-arg context), AND
  - the caller doesn't expect a return value from yield itself.

Proper fix is multi-day: resume needs to (a) capture the resume
args, (b) substitute them as the yield call's return value, and
(c) re-enter the same outer stmt at the post-yield point — none of
which bw15's offset-based PC tracking supports.

Defensive interim: detect yield-as-non-bare-statement (e.g. yield
inside LocalAssign / Assign / Return / function-arg evaluation) and
return the upstream Lua 5.1 boundary error instead of silently
producing nil. Track via a 'currently_in_bare_expression_stmt' flag
that exec_stmt sets only for Stmt::Expression; yield rejects when
the flag is false (and adds to the existing depth + current_
coroutine gates).

Filed P2 because the silent-wrong-result is similar in severity to
ztawj's silent-iteration-drop, but the fix requires more careful
threading of the bare-stmt flag through call_function paths.
close_reason: Added inside_bare_expression_stmt flag on LuaState. exec_stmt's Stmt::Expression arm save/restores it around eval_expr (true on entry, original on exit) — covers nested function calls without leaking the flag. resume_coroutine resets it to false at the coroutine boundary alongside nested_exec_stmts_depth so the body's outer stmts run with flag=false until each Stmt::Expression sets it. coroutine.yield's gate now also rejects when !inside_bare_expression_stmt, returning the upstream Lua 5.1 wording 'attempt to yield across metamethod/C-call boundary'. Two new pin tests: yield-in-LocalAssign rejected, yield-in-Return rejected. Existing 9 coroutine tests still pass; clippy clean.

title: Add MONITOR/MODULE/FAILOVER/SENTINEL conformance fixtures
close_reason: 27 conformance cases added in core_module_sentinel.json covering MONITOR, MODULE (LIST/HELP/LOAD/LOADEX/UNLOAD), FAILOVER, SENTINEL (HELP/MYID/MASTERS/MASTER/REPLICAS/GET-MASTER-ADDR-BY-NAME/CKQUORUM/FAILOVER/RESET/REMOVE)

title: fr-command: Lua-context ACL GENPASS conflates parse vs range errors, fr-runtime correctly splits them
description:
fr-runtime/handle_acl_genpass parses GENPASS bits via parse_i64_arg; non-integer surfaces 'value is not an integer or out of range' (matches upstream's getLongFromObjectOrReply with NULL msg) while out-of-range (<=0 or >4096) uses the dedicated 'ACL GENPASS argument must be the number of bits...' wording.

fr-command/lua_eval.rs::acl_script_result conflated the two under the GENPASS-specific wording, so redis.call('ACL','GENPASS','abc') from a script returned the range error for any non-integer arg.

Upstream processCommand fires CMD_NOSCRIPT first regardless of args, but fr's deliberate pre-noscript validation should still match its own non-script wording. Fix splits the two arms in lua_eval.rs to mirror fr-runtime, and adds lua_redis_call_acl_genpass_splits_parse_vs_range_errors to pin the parity.

title: fr-command: GEORADIUS/GEOSEARCH ANY without COUNT silently accepted instead of upstream rejection
description: Upstream geo.c::geoSearchGeneric replies 'ERR the ANY argument requires COUNT argument' when ANY is given without COUNT. fr-command's parse_geo_search_flags at lib.rs:4906 sets any=true unconditionally and never validates the COUNT pairing, so GEORADIUS/GEORADIUSBYMEMBER/GEOSEARCH/GEOSEARCHSTORE silently accept ANY without COUNT and execute as if ANY weren't specified. Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4 confirmed.

title: parse_geo_f64_with_msg silently coerces overflow-to-infinity; upstream rejects via string2d ERANGE
description: Differential probe vs vendored Redis 7.2.4 found that GEOADD/GEOSEARCH/GEOSEARCHSTORE/GEORADIUS treat overflow-to-infinity inputs (e.g. `1e500`, `1e1000`) as silent matches: `GEOSEARCH g FROMMEMBER a BYRADIUS 1e500 m` returns the entire member list instead of `ERR need numeric radius`. Upstream util.c::string2d:574-586 rejects exactly the ERANGE case (`errno==ERANGE && (*dp==HUGE_VAL || *dp==-HUGE_VAL || fpclassify(*dp)==FP_ZERO)`) without rejecting the explicit `inf`/`infinity` literals, since strtod doesn't set ERANGE for those tokens. fr's parse_geo_f64_with_msg only rejected NaN, so any token Rust's parse::<f64> saturated to f64::INFINITY slipped through. fr-command's parse_f64_arg already implements the correct dichotomy for ZADD/ZINCRBY (frankenredis-uxwm); GEO surfaces just need the same logic.
acceptance_criteria: GEO commands routed through parse_geo_f64_with_msg must reject `1e500`, `-1e500`, `1e1000`, `+1e500` with the caller-supplied wording (e.g. `ERR need numeric radius`, `ERR need numeric width`, `ERR value is not a valid float`). Explicit `inf`/`infinity` literals (any case, optional sign) must still be accepted.
close_reason: Mirrored fr-command::parse_f64_arg's overflow-vs-explicit-inf dichotomy in parse_geo_f64_with_msg: post-parse infinities are rejected with the caller's err() unless the source token was an inf/infinity literal. Pinned with geosearch_radius_rejects_overflow_to_infinity_but_accepts_explicit_inf covering BYRADIUS/BYBOX overflow rejection plus an explicit-inf accept case.

title: GEORADIUS / GEORADIUSBYMEMBER STORE/STOREDIST silently drop missing key, _RO variants accept STORE, radius wording differs from upstream
description:
Three tightly-coupled defects in the GEORADIUS / GEORADIUSBYMEMBER trailing-options parser: (1) trailing STORE/STOREDIST with no destination key was silently dropped — upstream gates the branches on (i+1) < remaining and falls through to shared.syntaxerr; (2) GEORADIUS_RO / GEORADIUSBYMEMBER_RO accepted STORE/STOREDIST even though upstream sets RADIUS_NOSTORE for those variants, so both should reject with shared.syntaxerr regardless of whether a key follows; (3) non-numeric radius surfaced the generic 'value is not a valid float' wording instead of upstream's literal 'need numeric radius' from extractDistanceOrReply.

Repros vs vendored 7.2.4:
  GEORADIUS g 13 41 abc km
    16380> ERR need numeric radius
    16381 (pre-fix)> ERR value is not a valid float
  GEORADIUS g 13 41 500 km STORE
    16380> ERR syntax error
    16381 (pre-fix)> 1) "a"  2) "b"   <- silently ignored STORE without key
  GEORADIUS_RO g 13 41 500 km STORE dst
    16380> ERR syntax error
    16381 (pre-fix)> (integer) 2  <- silently accepted writes via _RO variant

Fix: add validate_geo_store_args + is_geo_ro_variant helpers, call the validator in both georadius() and georadiusbymember() before parse_geo_search_flags, and route the radius parse through parse_geo_f64_with_msg("need numeric radius").
acceptance_criteria: 1. GEORADIUS / GEORADIUSBYMEMBER with trailing STORE or STOREDIST (no destination key) returns ERR syntax error. 2. GEORADIUS_RO / GEORADIUSBYMEMBER_RO with STORE or STOREDIST (with or without key) returns ERR syntax error. 3. Non-numeric radius for legacy GEORADIUS / GEORADIUSBYMEMBER surfaces ERR need numeric radius. 4. STORE / STOREDIST with valid destination key continues to write to the destination and reply with the integer count (no regression on the happy path). 5. Non-numeric longitude/latitude continues to surface the generic 'value is not a valid float' wording (not the radius-specific message).

title: Record initial performance baselines and check into baselines/ directory
description:
# Initial Baselines

## What
Run the benchmark harness against both FrankenRedis and legacy Redis, record the results, and check them into baselines/.

## Why
The baselines/ directory is empty. Without baselines, there is no way to detect performance regressions or prove improvements. The README claims "profile-proven performance" which requires actual numbers.

## Implementation
1. Build frankenredis in release mode: `cargo build --release -p fr-server`
2. Start frankenredis on port 6399
3. Run fr_benchmark against it with standard workloads:
   - SET only: 100k requests, 50 clients, pipeline 1
   - GET only: 100k requests, 50 clients, pipeline 1 (pre-populate keys first)
   - Mixed 50/50 SET/GET: 100k requests, 50 clients
   - Pipeline depth 16: 100k requests, 50 clients
   - INCR: 100k requests, 50 clients
4. Stop frankenredis, start legacy redis-server on same port
5. Run identical workloads against legacy Redis
6. Record all results as JSON in baselines/:
   - baselines/frankenredis_v0.1.0_set.json
   - baselines/frankenredis_v0.1.0_get.json
   - baselines/frankenredis_v0.1.0_mixed.json
   - baselines/frankenredis_v0.1.0_pipeline16.json
   - baselines/frankenredis_v0.1.0_incr.json
   - baselines/redis_7.2_set.json (comparison anchor)
   - baselines/redis_7.2_get.json
   - etc.
7. Create scripts/record_baselines.sh that automates steps 1-6
8. Create scripts/compare_baselines.py that reads two baseline files and reports deltas

## Validation
- baselines/ contains at least 10 JSON files (5 workloads x 2 servers)
- Each JSON has: ops_sec, p50_us, p95_us, p99_us, p999_us, total_time_sec, total_requests
- FrankenRedis throughput is at least 50% of legacy Redis for basic SET/GET (single-threaded, so parity is expected)
- If FrankenRedis is slower than 50% of Redis, create a follow-up bead to investigate

## Depends On
- Benchmark harness (bxlo) — CLOSED, already implemented

## Files
- baselines/*.json (new, many files)
- scripts/record_baselines.sh (new)
- scripts/compare_baselines.py (new)
close_reason: Recorded initial baselines, added automation scripts, and filed frankenredis-zjii for severe perf gap

title: GETEX rejects duplicate same-type expiry options that vendored Redis 7.2.4 accepts (PERSIST PERSIST, EX 5 EX 10, PXAT 1 PXAT 2)
description:
Vendored Redis 7.2.4 t_string.c::parseExtendedStringArgumentsOrReply allows the same expiry-kind to be repeated (last value wins) — only mixed types conflict via the per-flag !OBJ_X/!OBJ_Y guards. fr-command::getex(...) at crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:11994 hardcodes a strict argc check ("if argv.len() != 4" for EX/PX/EXAT/PXAT, "if argv.len() != 3" for PERSIST) and rejects duplicates with "ERR syntax error".

Differential probe (fr-port 16380 vs frankenredis):
  GETEX k PERSIST PERSIST  -> vendored: gv (success);  fr: ERR syntax error
  GETEX k EX 5 EX 10        -> vendored: gv + TTL=10;  fr: ERR syntax error
  GETEX k PXAT 1 PXAT 2     -> vendored: gv (success); fr: ERR syntax error
  GETEX k EX 5 EXAT 100     -> vendored: ERR syntax error;  fr: ERR syntax error (correct)
  GETEX k PERSIST EX 5      -> vendored: ERR syntax error;  fr: ERR syntax error (correct, but for the wrong reason)

Mirror the SET handler's flag-based ExpiryMode parser (lines 2470-2570): track an ExpiryMode enum and only reject mixed kinds, allowing duplicate same-kind options to overwrite (last wins).

title: fr-command: COMMAND GETKEYS for no-key commands evaluates arity before key-spec count, surfacing 'Invalid number of arguments' instead of upstream's 'no key arguments'
description:
Upstream getKeysFromCommandWithSpecs (commands.def) inspects the wrapped command's key spec count first; commands with zero key specs (PUBLISH, SUBSCRIBE, UNSUBSCRIBE, WAIT, PING, ...) report 'ERR The command has no key arguments' regardless of how many trailing args followed. fr-command's command_key_references at lib.rs:618-622 evaluated check_command_arity *before* command_has_keys, so under-arity calls of no-key commands surfaced 'ERR Invalid number of arguments specified for command' instead.

Differential probe vs vendored redis 7.2.4:
  COMMAND GETKEYS PUBLISH       fr: 'Invalid number of arguments...' / vendored: 'no key arguments'
  COMMAND GETKEYS SUBSCRIBE     fr: 'Invalid number of arguments...' / vendored: 'no key arguments'
  COMMAND GETKEYS WAIT          fr: 'Invalid number of arguments...' / vendored: 'no key arguments'
  COMMAND GETKEYSANDFLAGS PUBLISH same divergence

Fix: swap the two checks in command_key_references — command_has_keys first, arity second. Verified pin-test (command_getkeys_no_key_commands_report_no_keys_before_arity) covers PUBLISH/SUBSCRIBE/UNSUBSCRIBE/WAIT/PING and a SET under-arity sanity case.

title: redis parity: WAITAOF appendonly-disabled behavior
description: WAITAOF returns 1 0 instead of 0 0 when appendonly is disabled and min-replicas are unmet.
close_reason: Implemented WAITAOF appendonly-disabled behavior

title: Port upstream COMMAND GETKEYS/GETKEYSANDFLAGS key-spec parity
description: Bring FrankenRedis COMMAND GETKEYS and COMMAND GETKEYSANDFLAGS to Redis C-reference parity. Match upstream error strings and arity validation for invalid/no-key commands, use real key-spec extraction instead of coarse first/last/step for special commands, and return per-key access flags that match Redis semantics. Add conformance coverage in core_server and targeted runtime tests.
close_reason: Implemented upstream COMMAND GETKEYS/GETKEYSANDFLAGS parity and verified against live Redis

title: COMMAND INFO subcommand flag mismatches (no_async_loading etc.)
description: After 94zpo extended container set, byte-for-byte diff vs vendored shows minor flag-string drift in SUBCOMMAND_TABLE entries. Example: cluster|addslots — fr emits 'no_async_loading' that vendored does not. Realign against legacy_redis_code/redis/src/commands/*.json.
close_reason: completed

title: Convert NUM_DATABASES fixed arrays to Vec for runtime database count
description:
## Problem

Store uses `[usize; NUM_DATABASES]` fixed arrays for db_key_counts and db_expires_counts. NUM_DATABASES=16 is a compile-time const. Redis allows configuring database count at startup.

## Implementation Plan

1. Remove `pub const NUM_DATABASES: usize = 16` from fr-store
2. Add `database_count: usize` field to Store, defaulting to 16
3. Change db_key_counts and db_expires_counts to Vec<usize>
4. Initialize both Vecs in Store::new() with `vec![0; 16]`
5. Update all array access to use bounds-checked Vec indexing
6. In SELECT command: validate db < store.database_count (currently checks < NUM_DATABASES)
7. In encode_db_key/decode_db_key: validate db < database_count
8. In SWAPDB: validate both db indices
9. Accept database count as constructor parameter for Store

## Files to Modify

- crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs: Major — all array references
- crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs: SELECT validation, Store construction
- crates/fr-server/src/main.rs: May need to pass database_count at startup

## Testing

- Verify all existing tests pass (should be transparent since default stays 16)
- Add test: Store with 32 databases, SELECT 31 works, SELECT 32 fails
- Add test: CONFIG GET databases returns configured count
close_reason: db_key_counts/db_expires_counts converted from fixed [usize;16] arrays to Vec<usize>. database_count field added to Store. All references updated to use self.database_count.

title: Move aspirational methodology items to clearly labeled Roadmap section in README
description:
# README Honesty Fix

## What
The README "Methodological DNA" section lists four disciplines as if they are current project characteristics:
1. alien-artifact-coding — no evidence of formal decision theory or confidence calibration
2. extreme-software-optimization — no workload benchmarks exist
3. RaptorQ-everywhere — zero implementation, no crate dependency
4. frankenlibc/frankenfs compatibility thinking — this one IS implemented (strict/hardened modes)

## Implementation
1. Keep #4 (compatibility thinking) in Methodological DNA — it is real
2. Move #1-3 to a new "## Roadmap" section with honest status
3. Change "profile-proven performance" language to "performance-focused design" until benchmarks exist
4. Update "Current State" section to match what the code actually does today
5. Keep DLRC language but add caveat that latency tracking infrastructure is implemented but workload benchmarks are pending

## Validation
- Every claim in README can be verified by examining the codebase
- No claim requires future work to be true
close_reason: Moved aspirational methodology items (alien-artifact-coding, extreme-software-optimization, RaptorQ-everywhere) to Roadmap section with honest status. Kept compatibility-security thinking in Methodological DNA (it's implemented). Changed 'profile-proven performance' to 'performance-focused design'. Every README claim is now verifiable from the codebase.

title: fr-command DEBUG OBJECT emits BulkString; upstream emits SimpleString via addReplyStatusFormat
description:
REVIEW MODE Tick 14 finding via /modes-of-reasoning-project-analysis. Deferred follow-up from az4t9 (DEBUG OBJECT refcount fix). Same code path emits the wrong frame type.

LOCATION (crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs lines 16893-16894):
  let debug_info = format!('Value at:0x0 refcount:{refcount} ...');
  Ok(RespFrame::BulkString(Some(debug_info.into_bytes())))

UPSTREAM (legacy_redis_code/redis/src/debug.c::debugCommand line 648):
  addReplyStatusFormat(c,
      'Value at:%p refcount:%d encoding:%s ...',
      ...);

addReplyStatusFormat emits a SimpleString frame ('+...\r\n'), not BulkString ('$len\r\n...\r\n'). Same divergence pattern as the HELP cluster (0o9vo / vtege / kuthf / s574p / tnscz / 7ysld) which all converted hello_bulk → hello_simple.

Severity: [LOW]. Wire-format divergence on a debug introspection command. RESP parsers treat SimpleString and BulkString as distinct types; clients that switch on frame type before consuming the body (lettuce, jedis, redis-py, etc.) get the wrong path.

Reproducer:
  redis-cli> SET k v
  redis-cli> DEBUG OBJECT k
  upstream wire: +Value at:0x... refcount:1 encoding:embstr ...\r\n
  fr     wire:   $N\r\nValue at:0x0 refcount:1 encoding:embstr ...\r\n

Fix:
1. Replace RespFrame::BulkString with RespFrame::SimpleString.
2. Update existing debug_object_reports_live_metadata test to assert SimpleString instead of BulkString.
3. Update az4t9's debug_object_reports_int_max_refcount_for_shared_integer_value too.
4. Update debug_object_reports_hexpired_fields_counter likewise (line 22126).
close_reason:
fr-command DEBUG OBJECT now emits SimpleString frame matching upstream addReplyStatusFormat (debug.c::debugCommand line 648). Wire format flips from $len
...
 (BulkString) to +...
 (SimpleString). Same divergence pattern as the HELP cluster (0o9vo / vtege / kuthf / s574p / tnscz / 7ysld). Updated all 3 DEBUG OBJECT tests to assert RespFrame::SimpleString instead of BulkString: debug_object_reports_live_metadata, debug_object_reports_int_max_refcount_for_shared_integer_value (from az4t9), debug_object_reports_hexpired_fields_counter. RESP parsers treat SimpleString and BulkString as distinct types; clients (lettuce, jedis, redis-py, ioredis) that switch on frame type get the correct path now. All fr-command lib tests pass; clippy clean. Thirtieth concrete fix this session, immediate follow-up to az4t9.

title: Reject CONFIG SET always-show-logo as immutable
description: Redis marks always-show-logo as IMMUTABLE_CONFIG in legacy_redis_code/redis/src/config.c. FrankenRedis exposes always-show-logo as a static CONFIG parameter and currently accepts CONFIG SET through the static fallback. Add runtime and core_config conformance coverage that rejects mutation while preserving CONFIG GET visibility.

title: fr-runtime: PUBSUB HELP extra args returns 'ERR syntax error', upstream returns 'ERR wrong number of arguments for pubsub|help command'
description:
Differential probe vs vendored Redis 7.2.4 (port 16701):

  redis-cli -p 16701 PUBSUB HELP extra
  → ERR wrong number of arguments for 'pubsub|help' command

  redis-cli -p 16700 PUBSUB HELP extra
  → ERR syntax error

Source: crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs:9906-9909 returns CommandError::SyntaxError when argv.len() != 2. Upstream commands.def declares pubsub|help with arity = 2 (exact), so extra args trigger the table-level WrongSubcommandArity wording, mirroring how PUBSUB NUMPAT already does at lines 9990-9996.

Test at lines 13644-13647 currently pins the buggy 'ERR syntax error' wording; needs to flip to the upstream phrasing.

Fix: replace SyntaxError with CommandError::WrongSubcommandArity { command: "PUBSUB", subcommand: "HELP".to_string() }.to_resp(), update the test.
close_reason: Fixed in fr-runtime::handle_pubsub_command: PUBSUB HELP with extra args now emits CommandError::WrongSubcommandArity matching upstream's 'ERR wrong number of arguments for pubsub|help command' wording, mirroring the NUMPAT pattern. Test live_pubsub_help_and_arity_follow_redis_syntax updated to assert the new wording. Differential probe vs port 16701 confirms parity.

title: INFO Stats field ordering: total_eviction_exceeded_time + current_eviction_exceeded_time emitted at end vs upstream right after evicted_clients
description:
Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4 INFO Stats:

ven order (right after evicted_clients):
  evicted_keys:0
  evicted_clients:0
  total_eviction_exceeded_time:0
  current_eviction_exceeded_time:0
  keyspace_hits:...

fr order (at end of section):
  evicted_clients:0
  keyspace_hits:...
  ...
  acl_access_denied_channel:0
  current_eviction_exceeded_time:0
  total_eviction_exceeded_time:0

Per upstream server.c::genRedisInfoString around the evicted_keys/_clients lines, the eviction-time pair is emitted contiguously with the eviction-keys/clients block. fr-runtime appends them at the end of the Stats section (likely a recent addition). Reorder so they sit between evicted_clients and keyspace_hits to match vendored byte-for-byte.

Acceptance: INFO stats field order matches vendored under the canonical INFO field name list.
close_reason: Reordered total_eviction_exceeded_time + current_eviction_exceeded_time to emit contiguously after evicted_clients per upstream server.c::genRedisInfoString. Pinned via info_stats_emits_eviction_exceeded_time_right_after_evicted_clients_per_upstream.

title: DEBUG OBJECT for shared integer values (refcount = INT_MAX) emits regular lru clock; vendored emits lru:0 with elapsed idle
description:
Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4:

  127.0.0.1:16380> SET shared 5
  OK
  127.0.0.1:16380> DEBUG OBJECT shared
  Value at:0x... refcount:2147483647 encoding:int serializedlength:2 lru:0 lru_seconds_idle:40297
  127.0.0.1:16480> DEBUG OBJECT shared
  Value at:0x0 refcount:2147483647 encoding:int serializedlength:2 lru:40297 lru_seconds_idle:0

Per upstream object.c::createObject, shared integer objects (those in shared.integers[0..10000) with refcount = OBJ_SHARED_REFCOUNT = INT_MAX = 2147483647) have their lru field set to 0 at creation. Subsequent lookups don't update it. estimateObjectIdleTime then returns (lruclock - 0) = lruclock (the entire wall-clock seconds of the LRU clock) as the idle time. This is the upstream signal that 'this object's LRU is meaningless because it's shared' — its lru:0 is sentinel-like.

fr applies the regular 'lru = last_access_ms / 1000' rule even for shared integers, so the lru clock comes out non-zero and lru_seconds_idle stays at 0.

Fix in fr-command/src/lib.rs:18736: when refcount == INT_MAX (i.e. shared integer), emit lru:0 and lru_seconds_idle = the LRU clock value, mirroring upstream's sentinel behaviour. Other paths that read lru_clock are unaffected.

Acceptance: DEBUG OBJECT on a shared small integer (any value 0..=9999) emits lru:0 with idle = LRU_CLOCK seconds, matching vendored.
close_reason: On further investigation the original divergence (ven lru:0 idle:40297, fr lru:N idle:0) was a timing/state artifact, not a real semantic divergence. Per upstream object.c::createSharedObjects + initObjectLRUOrLFU (line 53-64), shared integer objects are created at server startup with lru = LRU_CLOCK() at that moment (not 0); initObjectLRUOrLFU is a no-op for shared objects so their lru never gets updated by lookupKey, but createObject sets it once at boot. After re-probing with a fresh state (FLUSHALL + SET shared 5), vendored returns lru:40510 idle:39 and fr returns matching lru:40510 idle:0 — the only remaining diff is the idle (39s of accumulated idle in vendored vs 0s for fr's freshly-created entry), which is environment timing, not a parity bug. Reverted the speculative force-lru:0-for-shared patch.

title: Lua lexer doesn't support long-string/long-comment level markers ([=[...]=], --[==[...]==])
description:
Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4:

  return [=[abc]=]            v=abc                            fr=unexpected symbol near '['
  return [==[ab]=cd]==]       v=ab]=cd                         fr=unexpected symbol near '['
  return [===[deep]===]       v=deep                           fr=unexpected symbol near '['
  --[=[ level 1 ]=] return 2  v=2                              fr=(no output - parses '[=[' as comment open?)
  --[[ unterminated           v=unfinished long comment near '<eof>'  fr='=' expected near '<eof>'
  --[=[ unterminated          v=unfinished long comment near '<eof>'  fr=(empty)

Upstream Lua 5.1's llex.c::skip_sep() counts a run of `=` between opening `[` and inner `[`, then closes only on `]<n equal signs>]`. fr's lexer hardcodes `[[`/`]]` and `--[[`/`]]`. Two sites:

1. skip_whitespace_and_comments (~line 939): long-comment scanner.
2. next_token's `[` branch (~line 1148) + read_long_string (~line 1016): long-string scanner.

Both need a `level` counter (number of `=` between brackets) and matching close logic. Also:
- Unterminated long comment should emit `"unfinished long comment near '<eof>'"`.
- Long strings drop a leading newline (skip first \n if present) — already done for `[[`, must preserve for level >= 1.

Deferred separately (related but different scope):
- Suffix `[expr]` accepted on number/string/bool literals at parse time (vendored rejects: `'<eof>' expected near '['` for `return 1[2]`).
- `function a.b.c()` declaration on a non-existent global emits "attempt to index a nil value" instead of "Script attempted to access nonexistent global variable 'a'".
close_reason: Implemented Lua 5.1 long-bracket level markers via new try_long_bracket_open helper that detects [=*[ openers and returns the level count. read_long_string takes a level parameter and matches the corresponding ]=*] closer; skip_whitespace_and_comments uses the same helper for long-comment scanning. Unterminated long comment now emits the upstream wording 'unfinished long comment near <eof>'. Signature change to skip_whitespace_and_comments (now returns Result) plumbed through next_token. Regression test lua_long_string_and_comment_level_brackets_h1vbd pins 5 long-string cases + 3 long-comment cases + 2 unterminated cases. Full release test suite (1010 tests) still passes. Live-diff vs vendored 16380: 10/10 cases match byte-for-byte.
