title: fr-command SLOWLOG GET emits wrong error wording for non-integer count (uses generic InvalidInteger; upstream emits 'count should be greater than or equal to -1')
description:
## Symptom
\`SLOWLOG GET abc\` via dispatch_argv (fr-command/src/lib.rs:15493-15500) returns 'ERR value is not an integer or out of range' (CommandError::InvalidInteger). Upstream returns 'ERR count should be greater than or equal to -1' for both parse failure AND out-of-range.

## Differential probe
Upstream slowlog.c::slowlogCommand at legacy_redis_code/redis/src/slowlog.c:172-174:
\`\`\`
if (getRangeLongFromObjectOrReply(c, c->argv[2], -1,
        LONG_MAX, &count, \"count should be greater than or equal to -1\") != C_OK)
    return;
\`\`\`

The msg argument is passed through getRangeLongFromObjectOrReply → getLongFromObjectOrReply → getLongLongFromObjectOrReply (object.c:895-907), and is used for BOTH parse failure AND out-of-range. So 'abc', 'inf', '99999999999999999999', and -2 all yield the same canonical wording.

fr-runtime/src/lib.rs:9633-9640 already handles this correctly via a bad_count closure that catches both branches via match. fr-command's slowlog_cmd at lib.rs:15493-15500 does not — it uses \`parse_i64_arg(count_arg)?\` which propagates CommandError::InvalidInteger before the bounds check fires.

## Fix
Replace the 'parse_i64_arg(count_arg)?' + 'if parsed < -1' block in fr-command/src/lib.rs:15493-15506 with the same closure-mapping shape used in fr-runtime: any parse error or count < -1 maps to CommandError::Custom('ERR count should be greater than or equal to -1').

## Test
Extend slowlog_get_accepts_minus_one_and_rejects_invalid_shapes (line 39111) with a non-integer count case asserting the canonical wording.

## Out of scope
SLOWLOG GET reply uses empty BulkString peerid/cname (since fr doesn't track per-client slowlog); upstream uses real client peer/name. fr's empty bytes are functionally fine for now.
close_reason: fr-command SLOWLOG GET now mirrors upstream's single canonical 'count should be greater than or equal to -1' wording for both parse failure (non-integer, empty, float) and out-of-range (< -1). Matches the bad_count closure pattern already used in fr-runtime's handle_slowlog_command. Test slowlog_get_accepts_minus_one_and_rejects_invalid_shapes extended to cover the parse-failure branch with three inputs (abc, empty, 1.5).

title: [parity-gap] XSETID ENTRIESADDED is ignored by XINFO group lag
description:
Review of recent stream/XINFO work found that XSETID accepts ENTRIESADDED but fr-store does not model the stream entries_added counter, so explicit ENTRIESREAD lag is computed from current stream length instead of Redis' entries-added total.

Repro against vendored Redis 7.2.4:
1. Start legacy Redis: legacy_redis_code/redis/src/redis-server --port 63988 --save "" --appendonly no
2. Run:
   XADD s 1-1 a 1
   XADD s 2-1 b 2
   XGROUP CREATE s g 0 ENTRIESREAD 0
   XSETID s 2-1 ENTRIESADDED 10
   XINFO GROUPS s
3. Upstream reports entries-read 0 and lag 10. FrankenRedis accepts ENTRIESADDED but currently comments that it is not modeled, so XINFO group lag uses live length and would report 2 after the ENTRIESREAD fix.

Acceptance: model per-stream entries_added, update it on XADD/XSETID/RDB restore, preserve through persistence paths, and make XINFO GROUPS/FULL lag use entries_added - entries_read when entries_read is valid and no deleted-entry ambiguity invalidates the lag.
close_reason: Implemented XSETID ENTRIESADDED stream counter parity with XINFO GROUPS/FULL conformance coverage

title: Lua string.byte silently accepts non-integer index args
description:
Vendored Redis 7.2.4's string.byte applies luaL_checknumber to the optional start/end index args and errors with 'bad argument #N to byte (number expected, got string)' for non-numeric values. fr silently ignores the bad arg and uses defaults.

Probe:
  EVAL "return string.byte('a', 'b')" 0
    vendored: ERR ... bad argument #2 to 'byte' (number expected, got string)
    fr:       97

Fix: validate args[1] (i) and args[2] (j) as numeric in fr's string.byte handler. Same pattern as table.insert/table.remove position arg validation.

Probe sweep #130.
close_reason: completed

title: Lua comparison metamethods __eq/__lt/__le
description: Sub-bead of frankenredis-00gjf. Lua 5.1 dispatches comparison ops (==, ~=, <, >, <=, >=) to __eq/__lt/__le when operand types match (both tables or both userdata). Probe: 'local a=setmetatable({},{__eq=function() return true end}); local b=setmetatable({},getmetatable(a)); return a==b' → vendored true, fr false. __lt: 'local a=setmetatable({v=1},{__lt=function(x,y) return x.v<y.v end}); local b=setmetatable({v=2},getmetatable(a)); return a<b' → vendored true. __le: similar; > and >= invoke __lt/__le with swapped args. Implementation: reuse lookup_binop_metamethod() in eval_binop's Eq/Ne/Lt/Gt/Le/Ge paths. Note: __eq is ONLY consulted when raw equality is false AND both operands are the same metatable-bearing type. Filed during 00gjf decomposition.
close_reason: Implemented __eq/__lt/__le metamethod dispatch in eval_expr's BinOp branch. __eq is consulted only when both operands are tables (matching Lua 5.1's type-match rule), raw-equal is false, and they share a __eq metamethod — fr detects 'shared' via either same-metatable-Rc OR raw-equal of the __eq values (LuaValue::Function lacks identity in fr, so two syntactically-identical-but-separate function literals are treated as distinct, matching upstream). ~= inverts the __eq result. </>/<=/>= dispatch to __lt/__le with arg-swapping for > (lt swapped) and >= (le swapped); <= and >= fall back to  when __le is missing. Non-callable handlers naturally produce call-errors via call_function. Cross-type comparison (table vs number) still errors with the existing wording. Verified live diff vs vendored 7.2.4 (port 16380): 14/14 cases match including shared-mt __eq fire, different-mt no-fire, raw-equal bypass, ~= inversion, __lt direct, > swap, <= __le, <= __lt fallback, cross-type error, non-callable error, and plain-number compare. Pinned by lua_comparison_metamethods_dispatch_on_matching_table_types covering 11 sub-cases. All 922 fr-command lib tests pass. Parent bead frankenredis-00gjf remains open for __len and __tostring.

title: RDB stream decode should fail on truncated group header
close_reason: Decode now enforces stream group length; added truncated-stream regression test

title: COMMAND INFO multi-keyspec commands diverge from upstream (mset/copy/lmove/*store/etc.)
description:
After frankenredis-zbq1t fixed single-keyspec divergences, ~17 commands still diverge because they require multiple keyspec entries with per-position OW/RO flags:

- mset: ven=[OW,update]                  fr=[RW,access]
- msetnx: ven=[OW,insert]                fr=[RW,access]
- pfmerge: ven=[RW,access,insert,RO,access]  fr=[RW,access] (single)
- rename: ven=[RW,access,delete,OW,update]   fr=[RW,access]
- renamenx: ven=[RW,access,delete,OW,insert] fr=[RW,access]
- copy: ven=[RO,access,OW,update]        fr=[RW,access]
- sort: ven=[RO,access,RO,access,OW,update]  fr=[RW,access]
- sort_ro: ven=[RO,access,RO,access]     fr=[RO,access] (only one spec)
- lmove/rpoplpush: ven=[RW,access,delete,RW,insert]  fr=[RW,access]
- bitop, zdiffstore, zinterstore, zunionstore, zrangestore, sunionstore, sdiffstore, geosearchstore: ven=[OW,update,RO,access] (or [RW,update,RO,access] for sinterstore)
- sinterstore: ven=[RW,update,RO,access] fr=[RW,access]

Each needs a custom command_info_key_specs return path that emits multiple key_spec arrays with the right begin_search/find_keys structures per position.
close_reason: Added command_info_multi_keyspec(name) returning Option<Vec<RespFrame>> for 15 multi-keyspec commands (copy/rename/renamenx/lmove/rpoplpush/pfmerge/bitop/sinterstore/sunionstore/sdiffstore/zinterstore/zunionstore/zdiffstore/zrangestore/geosearchstore) plus added mset/msetnx to the single-keyspec override. Two helpers (make_keyspec_index_range, make_keyspec_index_keynum) centralize frame construction. Pin test covers all 17. Byte-level COMMAND INFO output between fr and ven matches. Sort/sort_ro and movablekeys-empty cases tracked in b0wme. Commit df54bf8.

title: fuzz/corpus/fuzz_store_bitops missing — bitops model-check fuzzer starts cold every run, BITFIELD width permutations + BITOP cross-key shapes uncovered
description:
/testing-fuzzing audit:

  ls fuzz/corpus/fuzz_store_bitops → No such file or directory

The fuzz_store_bitops harness in fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_store_bitops.rs is a model-checking fuzzer that runs SETBIT/GETBIT/BITFIELD GET/BITFIELD SET/BITOP AND|OR|XOR|NOT against fr-store and asserts the result matches a shadow byte-model. It also rotates through DUMP/RESTORE round-trips, wrong-type sentinel keys, and missing-key edge cases. Without a seed corpus, libfuzzer starts cold every run and the structured-input edges (BITFIELD widths 1/2/4/5/8/12/16/24/32/48/64; BITOP source counts 0/1/2/3 incl. WrongType + Missing source refs; round-trip cycles) only get covered if the bytewise mutator happens to stumble onto them.

Seeds 8-10 small raw byte streams that exercise distinct shapes — Arbitrary parses them via Unstructured::new(data) into FuzzInput::ops sequences. The seeds don't need to be hand-decoded — diversity in bytes produces diversity in op variants.

Companion smoke test (mirrors cwx6/ohm5/fek0y/c85oq pattern): replay every corpus file through the fuzz_target driver under regular cargo test so corpus shrinkage / decoder regressions get caught between cargo-fuzz runs.
close_reason: Seeded fuzz/corpus/fuzz_store_bitops/ with 11 hand-crafted inputs covering distinct shapes: empty, 1-byte, 2-byte, 8-byte op discriminants, 32-byte small mix, 64-byte deterministic moderate, SetBit burst (20 ops at varied offsets/values), BitfieldSet burst (15 ops × all 11 width variants), BitOp combinations (12 ops × {AND,OR,XOR,NOT} × 4 source counts), WrongType sentinel burst (8 × 4 ops), RoundTrip burst (32 cycles). Each seed exercises a distinct ops sequence via Arbitrary's Unstructured derive, so libfuzzer's bytewise mutator gets immediate diversity instead of cold-starting. Smoke test deferred (requires re-deriving FuzzInput under fr-store/tests; arch issue separate from corpus seeding).

title: fr-command: INCR/DECR/INCRBY/DECRBY overflow boundaries lack dedicated test pins
description: fr-command's INCR/DECR/INCRBY/DECRBY emit upstream's 'ERR increment or decrement would overflow' wording (lib.rs:1048) for i64 overflow but no test pins assert this specific wording at the boundary. INCR on i64::MAX, DECR on i64::MIN, INCRBY/DECRBY pushing past the i64 limits all need locked-in coverage to prevent regression. Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4 confirmed wording byte-exact. Source already correct; just need the regression guard.

title: INCRBYFLOAT parse-order divergence: rejects invalid delta before type-checking the key, leaking value-not-a-valid-float on WRONGTYPE keys
description:
Vendored Redis 7.2.4 t_string.c::incrbyfloatCommand ordering:
  1. lookupKeyWrite(key)
  2. checkType(o, OBJ_STRING)  -> WRONGTYPE if not string
  3. parse current value as long double
  4. parse delta as long double  <- only reached after 1-3 pass
  5. add, check NaN/Inf

fr-command::incrbyfloat parses the delta FIRST (line 10284), so a probe like
  INCRBYFLOAT <list-key> abc
returns "ERR value is not a valid float" while vendored returns
  WRONGTYPE Operation against a key holding the wrong kind of value.

Same divergence applies to INCRBYFLOAT <hash-key> abc, etc.

Fix: pre-flight a type check (fr_store::Store::key_type) before parse_f64_arg, mirroring upstream order.

title: INFO is missing Commandstats and Latencystats sections (vendored has 13 sections, fr has 11)
description:
Differential probe vs vendored Redis 7.2.4: vendored emits 13
INFO sections under ALL/EVERYTHING:
  Server, Clients, Memory, Persistence, Stats, Commandstats,
  Latencystats, Replication, CPU, Modules, Errorstats, Cluster,
  Keyspace.

fr (lib.rs:12881-13416) emits 11 sections — missing Commandstats
and Latencystats — and uses the same set for INFO DEFAULT and
INFO ALL (vendored differs: DEFAULT excludes Commandstats and
Latencystats, ALL/EVERYTHING includes them).

Vendored format:
  # Commandstats
  cmdstat_<cmd>:calls=N,usec=N,usec_per_call=N.NN,rejected_calls=N,failed_calls=N
  ...
  # Latencystats
  latency_percentiles_usec_<cmd>:p50=N,p99=N,p99.9=N
  ...

Both sections are derived from per-command histograms.
fr-store/src/lib.rs:1908 already exposes
all_command_histograms() (used by LATENCY HISTOGRAM at
fr-command/src/lib.rs:19150-19265). The plumbing for
command-level latency tracking exists; what's missing is the
INFO emission path that turns it into the upstream-style
cmdstat_/latency_percentiles_usec_ lines.

Implementation scope:
  1. Add a 'Commandstats' branch (section_requested
     gate) inside the INFO handler. Format matches upstream
     server.c::genRedisInfoString:5818-5841 — total calls,
     total usec, derived usec_per_call (with %.2f), plus
     rejected_calls and failed_calls. fr probably needs to
     start tracking rejected/failed counters separately
     (currently only total calls + duration).
  2. Add a 'Latencystats' branch. Format mirrors
     latency.c::fillCommandCDF emission — for each command
     bucket, compute p50/p99/p99.9 from the histogram bucket
     CDF. Gated on `latency-tracking yes` (default yes per
     upstream).
  3. Update INFO DEFAULT vs ALL handling: DEFAULT excludes
     Commandstats and Latencystats; ALL/EVERYTHING includes
     them. Currently fr's `is_all` boolean conflates both.
  4. Pin a regression test asserting both `# Commandstats`
     and `# Latencystats` headers appear under INFO ALL but
     not under INFO DEFAULT. Surface a single cmdstat_/
     latency_percentiles_usec_ line in each section to lock
     the format.

Filed open at P3: telemetry surface only, no correctness
impact — clients that scrape these sections (Prometheus
exporters, redis-cli --stat) will miss data. Estimated
effort 90-120 min once rejected/failed counter wiring is
scoped.
close_reason: Wire real total_usec/rejected/failed counters into CommandHistogram; INFO Commandstats now reports calls/usec/usec_per_call/rejected_calls/failed_calls from live data instead of zero stubs; pubsub commands no longer mis-extracted as ACL keys

title: Add bitfield and BITOP fuzz target for fr-store binary string mutations
description:
Evidence: there is no direct fuzz target for fr-store bit mutation surfaces even though they perform offset arithmetic and dynamic buffer growth. The uncovered entry points are crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs:2290 (bitfield_get), 2320 (bitfield_set), 8000 (bitop), plus internal helpers 10206 (bitfield_read) and 10235 (bitfield_write). Existing fuzz_runtime_sequence does not drive these paths.

Need: add a structure-aware fuzz target that mutates offsets, signed/unsigned widths, BITOP opcodes, and source/destination key combinations, then checks invariants against a small shadow bit-vector model: reads after writes agree, no-op writes preserve digest, BITOP output length matches Redis rules, and hostile offsets never panic or corrupt unrelated keys.

Real impl path: fuzz/Cargo.toml plus new fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_store_bitops.rs calling the fr-store bitfield/bitop APIs directly.
close_reason: Completed

title: fr-store: stream XADD/XRANGE/XDEL lacks metamorphic invariant coverage (range-monotonicity, len-after-add/del, distinct-ids monoid)
description:
crates/fr-store/tests/ has metamorphic suites for bit, bitop, hash variants, string, set, sortedset variants but NOT for streams. Stream operations have several upstream-aligned metamorphic invariants worth pinning to catch refactor drift in the rax/listpack stream backbone:

  MR-XLEN-AFTER-XADD-DISTINCT   xlen(s) == count of distinct IDs added
                                via XADD with no overwrites
  MR-XRANGE-MONOTONIC           xrange(s, -, +) entries ordered by
                                non-decreasing StreamId (key invariant
                                of the rax cursor walk)
  MR-XADD-OVERWRITE-IDEMPOTENT  XADD with an ID that already exists is
                                idempotent for the entry count (the
                                second insert returns false; len stays)
  MR-XLEN-AFTER-XDEL            xlen(s) == initial_count - len(unique
                                deleted IDs that were present)
  MR-XDEL-MISSING-IDS-NOOP      Deleting IDs that don't exist returns
                                count of actually-deleted IDs and
                                doesn't change xlen for present-but-
                                already-deleted re-runs

These are existence properties, not byte-for-byte parity — they catch
regressions in the rax/btree iteration order, the high-watermark
counter (stream_last_ids), and the entries-added counter without
needing a live oracle.

Acceptance: a new tests/metamorphic_stream.rs that pins all five
invariants. Use deterministic seeded inputs (no proptest needed; quick
loops over crafted ID sequences).

Reference: /testing-metamorphic gap audit on 2026-05-04.
notes: Added crates/fr-store/tests/metamorphic_stream.rs with 6 invariants: mr_xlen_after_xadd_distinct_ids_equals_count, mr_xrange_returns_entries_in_nondecreasing_id_order, mr_xadd_overwrite_with_existing_id_does_not_grow_xlen, mr_xlen_after_xdel_decreases_by_count_of_present_ids, mr_xdel_repeated_call_is_no_op_for_already_deleted_ids, mr_xrange_includes_xadd_immediately. All 6 pass; clippy clean. Catches refactor drift in the rax cursor walk, the high-watermark counter, and the entries-added bookkeeping without needing a live oracle.

title: EPIC: End-to-end integration test suite proving FrankenRedis works as a real Redis drop-in
description:
# Integration Testing Epic

## Why
Unit tests (1,076) and conformance fixtures (4,055 cases) test internal logic. TCP e2e tests (5) prove basic connectivity. But there is no test that:
- Runs a real workload through frankenredis and verifies all results
- Compares frankenredis output against legacy Redis output for the same workload
- Tests multi-client concurrent access
- Tests persistence round-trip (start → write → stop → restart → verify)
- Tests pub/sub across multiple TCP connections

These integration tests are the proof that FrankenRedis is a "drop-in replacement."

## What Success Looks Like
- A test suite that starts frankenredis, connects via redis-cli or TCP, runs realistic workloads, and verifies all responses
- A differential test that runs the same commands against both frankenredis and legacy redis, comparing outputs
- Tests cover: concurrent clients, pub/sub cross-client, persistence restart, blocking commands with timeout, Lua scripting via TCP
close_reason: All sub-tasks complete: persistence restart (e1e9), pub/sub cross-client (v7zc), multi-client concurrency (06pn), WATCH/EXEC transactions (6axy), redis-cli smoke test (42df). 25+ E2E tests now prove FrankenRedis works as a real Redis drop-in.

title: fr-command: INFO server section missing atomicvar_api, process_supervised, server_time_usec, io_threads_active, listener0 fields
description: INFO server in fr emits 16 fields; upstream Redis 7.2.4 emits 21. Missing: atomicvar_api, process_supervised, server_time_usec, io_threads_active, listener0. Tools that grep on these field names will see fields go missing. Caught by ad-hoc differential probe.

title: Normalize sorted-set zero scores to avoid -0 regressions
close_reason: Completed

title: Implement replication backlog buffer that stores command bytes for partial resync
description:
# Replication Backlog Buffer

## What
The existing `BacklogWindow` in fr-repl tracks replication offsets (replid, start offset, size) but does NOT store actual command bytes. For partial resync (PSYNC CONTINUE) to work, the primary must buffer recent write commands so that a reconnecting replica can receive just the delta instead of a full resync.

## Current State
- `BacklogWindow` struct exists with replid, start_offset, size fields
- `decide_psync()` checks if requested offset falls within the backlog window
- But no actual byte buffer exists — when CONTINUE is decided, there are no bytes to send

## Implementation
1. Add a `backlog_bytes: VecDeque<u8>` field to the replication state (either in ServerState or a new BacklogBuffer struct)
2. Configure max size via `repl-backlog-size` (already a config knob, default 1 MiB)
3. After each write command is propagated, append its RESP-serialized bytes to the backlog
4. When backlog exceeds max size, drain from the front (oldest bytes)
5. Track the byte offset corresponding to the front of the buffer
6. On PSYNC CONTINUE: calculate the byte range the replica needs, send those bytes
7. On PSYNC FULLRESYNC: reset backlog after snapshot send

## Unit Tests
- Test 1: Append 100 commands, verify backlog size stays within limit
- Test 2: Partial resync — append commands, request offset in range, verify correct bytes returned
- Test 3: Offset out of range — verify FULLRESYNC fallback
- Test 4: Backlog wraps — oldest bytes evicted when limit reached
- Test 5: Reset on FULLRESYNC — verify backlog clears

## Depends On
- REPLICAOF TCP client (1v8b) — need connection before backlog matters
- Must be done BEFORE command propagation (1ng7) — propagation writes to this buffer

## Files
- crates/fr-repl/src/lib.rs (backlog buffer struct + methods)
- crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs (wire backlog into server state)
close_reason: Already implemented: aof_records Vec<AofRecord> in ServerState stores command bytes, encoded_aof_stream_from_offset() extracts delta for partial resync, PSYNC CONTINUE handler at main.rs:1642-1644 sends backlog bytes to reconnecting replicas.

title: string.find / string.match silently default init when non-numeric (vendored errors)
description:
string.find and string.match's third arg ('init', the starting
position) is required by upstream luaB_str_find_aux to be a number
or numeric string. Anything else (table, boolean, non-numeric
string) errors with 'bad argument #3 to <name> (number expected,
got TYPE)'. fr silently defaults to 1 using
`args.get(2).and_then(|v| v.to_number()).unwrap_or(1.0)` — so
bogus init args are accepted and the match runs from position 1.

Repro:
  EVAL "local ok,e = pcall(string.match,'h','h','bad'); return tostring(ok)..':'..tostring(e)" 0
    vendored: false:bad argument #3 to '?' (number expected, got string)
    fr:       true:h

  EVAL "local ok,e = pcall(string.find,'h','h',{}); return tostring(ok)..':'..tostring(e)" 0
    vendored: false:bad argument #3 to '?' (number expected, got table)
    fr:       true:1

Numeric and numeric-string inits still work on both
(string.match('h','l',2), string.match('h','l','2')).

Fix at fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs:6637 (string.find) and 6707
(string.match): replace the silent default with
lua_optional_integer_arg(inv, 3, args.get(2), 1)? — that helper
already routes through lua_bad_number_arg producing the upstream
wording (with inv_name routing the user_script:1: prefix away for
pcall callers).
close_reason: completed

title: Lua sandbox missing strict-globals enforcement (read undefined → nil, write to global → silent)
description:
fr's custom Lua interpreter at crates/fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs does not enforce the upstream Redis Lua sandbox's strict-globals policy:

```
ven> EVAL 'return UNDEFINED' 0
(error) ERR user_script:1: Script attempted to access nonexistent global variable 'UNDEFINED' script: …

ven> EVAL 'X = 5; return X' 0
(error) ERR user_script:1: Attempt to modify a readonly table script: …

fr> EVAL 'return UNDEFINED' 0
(nil)

fr> EVAL 'X = 5; return X' 0
5
```

Upstream sets two metatables on the global table (script_lua.c::luaSetErrorMetatable + luaSetTableProtectionRecursively):
- __index returns ERR for any non-allowlisted name
- __newindex allows only names in an allow list (KEYS, ARGV, redis, etc.); everything else raises 'Attempt to modify a readonly table'

The error wording is exact: 'Script attempted to access nonexistent global variable '%s'' and 'Attempt to modify a readonly table'.

fr should:
1. Track which names are allowed (std lib names + KEYS, ARGV, redis, etc.)
2. On global READ: if name not in allow list and not present, raise the upstream error.
3. On global WRITE: raise 'Attempt to modify a readonly table' unless the name is on the upstream allow list (which is small — most user-script writes go to local vars).
close_reason: Added globals_locked flag to LuaState, set true at start of execute(). Read of undefined global raises upstream error wording; write to global (incl. function decl) raises readonly-table error. FCALL register_function transform now uses `local function` to avoid tripping the gate. Pin test covers 5 scenarios + KEYS/ARGV access. Wording matches upstream byte-for-byte except missing user_script:LINE: prefix (broader formatting issue). Commit f33b94a.

title: CONFIG GET literal-pattern echoes user-typed casing
description: fr's handle_config_get canonicalised every pattern to lowercase before matching, so 'CONFIG GET MaxMemory' replied 'maxmemory'/value pairs. Upstream config.c::configGetCommand inserts the user-typed pattern (c->argv[i]->ptr) into the matches dict for literal (non-glob) lookups and the canonical key for glob matches; the reply uses the dict key. Now we rewrite newly-added entry-keys to the user-typed pattern only when raw_pattern has no '*?[' glob characters

title: fr-command::select is a stub — accepts only db=0, rejects all others, doesn't mutate dispatch_client_ctx.db_index
close_reason: Implemented dispatch_argv SELECT state mutation against Store database_count and pinned SELECT+MOVE metamorphic coverage.

title: fr-runtime: gate DEBUG command on enable-debug-command config (matches upstream Redis 7.2 default-deny)
description: fr-runtime exposes enable-debug-command as a static CONFIG GET value defaulted to 'no', matching upstream's CLI/config-file knob. But the DEBUG command is dispatched unconditionally in fr-command — no gate is actually enforced. Upstream Redis 7.2 rejects DEBUG with the canonical error 'ERR DEBUG command not allowed. If the enable-debug-command option is set to local, you can run it from a local connection, otherwise you need to set this option in the configuration file, and then restart the server.' when enable-debug-command=no (the default) and the connection isn't a special debug peer. fr accepts arbitrary DEBUG subcommands instead, surfaced by an end-to-end smoke comparing 30 commands against vendored redis 7.2.4. Acceptance: in default config, DEBUG OBJECT returns the upstream-shape lockout error; with enable-debug-command=yes (set via config file at startup, not CONFIG SET — that one is immutable), DEBUG runs as today. Gate must be in fr-runtime so command-table tests still pass; tests should set the CLI flag.
close_reason: fr-runtime now intercepts DEBUG via RuntimeSpecialCommand::Debug + handle_debug_command_gate, returning the canonical upstream Redis 7.2 lockout error when enable_debug_command is 'no' (default). New Runtime::set_enable_debug_command setter; --enable-debug-command CLI + config-file directive plumbing; CONFIG GET emits the live value. 11 fr-runtime tests opt-in to DEBUG via the setter; new debug_default_denies_with_upstream_lockout_error pins the default-deny wording. Conformance fixtures opt in via configure_runtime_for_fixture. E2E verified.

title: Add live Redis conformance tests for SET commands
notes: core_set_live_redis_matches_runtime exists

title: HLL is always dense (16389 bytes); vendored uses sparse for low cardinality
description:
fr's HyperLogLog representation is always dense (16389 bytes per HLL), regardless of cardinality. Vendored Redis 7.2.4 uses a sparse RLE-encoded format for low cardinalities, then promotes to dense at ~3000 bytes (configurable via hll-sparse-max-bytes).

Reproduction (STRLEN h after N inserts):
  N=1     vendored= 21 bytes  fr=16389 bytes   (780x bloat)
  N=10    vendored= 47        fr=16389         (350x bloat)
  N=100   vendored=277        fr=16389         (60x)
  N=1000  vendored=1912       fr=16389         (8x)
  N=2000  vendored=12304      fr=16389         (1.3x — vendored promoted to dense)

PFCOUNT estimates match within ±0.1% so the counting algorithm is correct.
PFDEBUG ENCODING reports 'sparse' on fr below the cardinality threshold but the actual byte size is always dense — the label and storage diverge.

Root cause: fr-store/fr-command's PFADD / PFMERGE always allocate the full dense 16384-byte register array (plus 5-byte header = 16389). No sparse RLE encoding exists.

Sparse format spec (vendored hyperloglog.c):
- HLL_DENSE / HLL_SPARSE distinguishable via header byte 0x4 (encoding flag)
- Sparse opcodes:
  - XZERO: 2 bytes, `01xxxxxx xxxxxxxx` — long run of zeros (1..16384 zeros, 14-bit count - 1)
  - ZERO:  1 byte,  `00xxxxxx` — short run of zeros (1..64 zeros, 6-bit count - 1)
  - VAL:   1 byte,  `1vvvvvxx` — encode value v (1..32, 5 bits) with run length 1..4 (2 bits)
- Promotion to dense when:
  - A VAL opcode would need to encode value >= 32 (HLL_SPARSE_VAL_MAX_VALUE)
  - Total sparse byte size exceeds hll-sparse-max-bytes (default 3000)
  - PFMERGE always promotes to dense

Fix plan (substantial):
1. Add HLL header parser distinguishing sparse vs dense.
2. Implement sparse opcode encoder/decoder.
3. Implement PFADD on sparse representation (locate register, possibly split run, possibly promote).
4. Implement sparse-to-dense promotion path.
5. PFMERGE: always promote to dense (matches upstream).
6. PFDEBUG TODENSE, PFDEBUG GETREG should both work on sparse.
7. Add CONFIG GET / SET for hll-sparse-max-bytes (default 3000).
8. Pin parity tests for STRLEN at several cardinalities and the sparse-to-dense promotion threshold.

Scope: substantial — likely 500+ LOC across fr-store/fr-command. Not scoped for this iteration; filed as P2 so it doesn't get lost.

Probe sweep #115 (PFADD/STRLEN at varying N, inline).

title: Replication ack monotonicity should allow negative ACKs and preserve prior offset
close_reason: Behavior already correct: negative ACKs are rejected with error per conformance test replconf_ack_negative_rejected. Monotonicity is also enforced - lower offsets don't update state.

title: XRANGE/XREVRANGE missing exclusive '(N' prefix (Redis 6.2+)
description:
Vendored Redis 7.2.4 supports the exclusive open-paren prefix in XRANGE/XREVRANGE start/end arguments per the docs (added in Redis 6.2): '(N' means 'everything strictly greater than N' for the start bound, or 'everything strictly less than N' for the end bound. fr's parse_stream_range_bound at crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:5561 rejects any '(' prefix as 'ERR Invalid stream ID specified as stream command argument'.

Live diff vs vendored 7.2.4 (port 16380) using XRANGE xex '(1' '+' on a stream populated with IDs 1-0, 2-0, 2-1, 3-0:
  vendored: returns entries 2-0, 2-1, 3-0
  fr:       ERR Invalid stream ID specified as stream command argument

Same divergence for: 'XRANGE xex (2-0 +', 'XRANGE xex - (2-1', 'XREVRANGE xex + (1', 'XREVRANGE xex (3 -'.

Edge cases that should still be rejected (vendored behaviour confirmed):
  - '(+' / '(-' / '(' alone — ERR Invalid stream ID

Scope: ONLY XRANGE/XREVRANGE. Vendored explicitly rejects '(' prefix on XREAD STREAMS id, XTRIM MINID, and XADD MINID — those code paths should NOT change.

Fix sketch:
  In parse_stream_range_bound (crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:5561):
    1. Detect leading '(' byte; if present, strip it and parse the remainder.
    2. After parsing the inner StreamId, adjust ±1:
       - start (exclusive): next id after inner. If inner.seq == u64::MAX → (inner.ms+1, 0); else (inner.ms, inner.seq+1). Saturate at (u64::MAX, u64::MAX).
       - end (exclusive): prev id before inner. If inner.seq == 0 → if inner.ms == 0 → reject (nothing less than 0-0); else (inner.ms-1, u64::MAX). Else (inner.ms, inner.seq-1).
    3. Reject '(', '(+', '(-' as invalid.
    4. Partial ID semantics for the inclusive form remain: '(1' means '(1-MAX)' for start (so first hit > 1-MAX = 2-0) and '(1-0)' for end interpretation needs reading vendored — based on probe, '(1' on start jumped past 1-* entirely, suggesting partial id resolves to ms-MAX for start-exclusive. Verify via differential probe.

Test plan:
  - Pin parity for inclusive XRANGE/XREVRANGE across:
    * '(N', '(N-S' on both start and end
    * Partial-id resolution of '(N' for start vs end
    * Edge: '(0-0' as end → matches vendored (likely error since no id < 0-0)
    * '(' / '(+' / '(-' continue to return ERR Invalid stream ID
  - Ensure XREAD/XTRIM/XADD MINID still reject '(' (do not regress)
close_reason: Added '(N' exclusive prefix support to XRANGE/XREVRANGE/XPENDING/XAUTOCLAIM bound parsing, matching upstream streamParseIntervalIDOrReply. Helpers stream_incr_id/stream_decr_id mirror upstream's t_stream.c:streamIncrID/streamDecrID with the same overflow semantics (returning 'invalid start/end ID for the interval' on (0-0 end and (MAX-MAX start). Routed XAUTOCLAIM start ID through parse_stream_range_bound (previously parse_stream_id, which rejected -/+/(prefix). Hardened XGROUP CREATE/SETID and XREAD/XTRIM/XADD MINID to continue rejecting '(' explicitly. Live diff vs vendored 7.2.4 (port 16380): 20+ probe cases match including inclusive happy path, '(N'/'(N-S' partial-id completion, '(0-0' underflow, '((1'/'('/'(+'/'(-' rejection, and cross-command negative tests. Pinned by xrange_exclusive_open_paren_bounds_match_upstream and xautoclaim_start_accepts_sentinels_and_open_paren. All 932 fr-command lib tests pass.

title: Lua assert/tonumber/select still emit user_script:1: prefix when called via pcall
description:
Three follow-up divergences from the broader i18ug refactor:

1. assert (lbaselib.c::luaB_assert) raises via luaL_error which
   prepends the where(1) source-location. fr's handler always
   prepended user_script:1: regardless of caller frame.

   pcall(assert, false)
     vendored: assertion failed!
     fr (was): user_script:1: assertion failed!

2. tonumber (lbaselib.c::luaB_tonumber) raises via luaL_argerror.
   fr had hand-rolled "user_script:1: bad argument #1 to 'tonumber'"
   strings rather than routing through format_builtin_argerror.

   pcall(tonumber)
     vendored: bad argument #1 to '?' (value expected)
     fr (was): user_script:1: bad argument #1 to 'tonumber' (value expected)

3. select (lbaselib.c::luaB_select). When called with no args, the
   "(no value)" got-label should fire — fr was reporting "got nil".

   return select()
     vendored: user_script:1: bad argument #1 to 'select' (number expected, got no value)
     fr (was): user_script:1: bad argument #1 to 'select' (number expected, got nil)

Fix locus: crates/fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs.

- assert: replace unconditional `format!("user_script:1: {msg}")` with
  conditional on current_invocation_name (same pattern as setmetatable's
  protected-metatable error).
- tonumber: route all 4 error sites through format_builtin_argerror.
- select: pass `lua_arg_got_label(idx_opt)` (which returns "no value"
  for None) rather than `idx.type_name()` (which returns "nil" when
  the LuaValue::Nil fallback runs).

Test plan: pin all three vendored variants plus the AST-direct
counterparts (where the prefix is correctly emitted).
close_reason:
Three follow-up fixes in crates/fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs:
(1) assert now toggles the user_script:1 prefix on self.current_invocation_name (matching luaL_error's where(1) behavior) rather than always prepending.
(2) tonumber routes its four bad-arg sites through format_builtin_argerror so pcall(tonumber) reports '?' with no prefix.
(3) select's missing-arg case now reports 'got no value' via lua_arg_got_label rather than 'got nil' via type_name on the LuaValue::Nil fallback.
Regression test lua_assert_tonumber_select_indirect_pcall_j438k pins 8 cases including AST-direct regressions. Live diff vs vendored Redis 7.2.4 on :16380 clean across 12 of 13 probes; the 13th (tonumber(5, 3.7)) is a separate semantic bug filed as frankenredis-s99a4.

title: CRITICAL: FUNCTION STATS test expects Integer(0) but pinned impl + upstream return nil (BulkString(None))
description:
## Summary

`fr-command::tests::function_list_and_stats_match_redis_reply_shapes` fails on fresh HEAD (eb6b915). The test asserts that `FUNCTION STATS` emits `Integer(0)` in the `running_script` slot, but the implementation at `fr-command/src/lib.rs:6394` intentionally emits `RespFrame::BulkString(None)` with a `PINNED 2026-04-22` comment citing live redis:7.4.8 evidence.

A fresh probe against vendored `legacy_redis_code/redis/src/redis-server` (7.2.4) confirms the implementation is correct:

```
redis-cli --no-raw FUNCTION STATS
1) \"running_script\"
2) (nil)
3) \"engines\"
...
```

So the implementation matches upstream; the **test assertion** is the bug.

## How it got this way

`git show fd17b663` (titled "docs(command): pin FUNCTION LIST/STATS reply shape to live redis:7.4 behavior") is self-contradictory:

- Adds a long `PINNED 2026-04-22` comment at `lib.rs:6388-6393` asserting `running_script` must be `BulkString(None)` per live 7.4.8 evidence.
- In the same commit, flips the test assertion from `BulkString(None)` to `Integer(0)`:
  ```
  -                RespFrame::BulkString(None),
  +                RespFrame::Integer(0),
  ```
- Commit message says "No behavioural change — the three lines at 6317 / 6377 / 6380 already carried the correct values in the current tree. This commit only adds the pinning comments so the oscillation stops." — but the test diff contradicts the commit message.

The test diff at line 30431 was the mistake; the pin to `BulkString(None)` in the source is the canonical answer.

## Failure

```
thread 'tests::function_list_and_stats_match_redis_reply_shapes' panicked at crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:30427:9:
assertion `left == right` failed
  left: ... BulkString(None) ...   // actual from dispatch
  right: ... Integer(0) ...        // test expectation
```

## Suggested fix direction

Single-line revert of the test assertion:

```diff
-                RespFrame::Integer(0),
+                RespFrame::BulkString(None),
```

at `crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:30431`. No implementation change. Optional: add a block-comment on the test pointing back to the `PINNED 2026-04-22` evidence so the flip-flop doesn't resume.

## Related

Once this and frankenredis-v4x3 land, `cargo test --workspace` should be green again and the CI gate reopens.

## Labels

ci · tests · fr-command · conformance · function-stats · self-contradictory-pin
close_reason: Fixed in 243c8f4 — test assertion realigned to BulkString(None) matching the pinned impl and live redis:7.4.8 oracle probe.

description:

  HEXPIRE h 60 FIELDS 1 f1            vendored: ERR unknown command 'HEXPIRE'  fr: 1
  HEXPIREAT h 9999999999 FIELDS 1 f1  vendored: ERR unknown command            fr: 1
  HPEXPIRE h 60000 FIELDS 1 f1        vendored: ERR unknown command            fr: 1
  HPEXPIREAT h ... FIELDS 1 f1        vendored: ERR unknown command            fr: 1
  HEXPIRETIME h FIELDS 1 f1           vendored: ERR unknown command            fr: 9999999999
  HPEXPIRETIME h FIELDS 1 f1          vendored: ERR unknown command            fr: 9999999999000
  HTTL h FIELDS 1 f1                  vendored: ERR unknown command            fr: 8221640722
  HPTTL h FIELDS 1 f1                 vendored: ERR unknown command            fr: 8221640721855
  HPERSIST h FIELDS 1 f1              vendored: ERR unknown command            fr: 1
  HGETEX h FIELDS 1 f1                vendored: ERR unknown command            fr: v1
  HGETDEL h FIELDS 1 f1               vendored: ERR unknown command            fr: v1

Same class as v0swv (DEBUG OBJECT hexpired_fields) and c5746 (HSCAN NOVALUES) — both removed 7.4 features for 7.2.4 parity. Per project's parity baseline (README + INFO server.redis_version 7.2.x).

The implementation is in crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs starting around line 1521 (dispatch table) and 2682 (HEXPIRE family block). Removing the 11 dispatches makes them surface 'ERR unknown command' on the wire.

Suggested fix scope:
- Drop the 11 commands' COMMAND_TABLE rows.
- Drop the 11 dispatch arms (delegate handlers can stay as dead code or be deleted — leaving them keeps the test surface but adds dead-code warnings; deleting them is the cleaner choice).
- Update INFO commandstats / CLUSTER COUNTKEYSINSLOT references.
- Audit any test that pinned these commands as functional and either move them under #[cfg(feature='redis_7_4')] or delete.

This is a larger refactor than the prior 7.4-leak removals; ship as a single bead.
close_reason:
Removed all 11 Redis 7.4 hash field TTL commands (HEXPIRE/HPEXPIRE/HEXPIREAT/HPEXPIREAT/HEXPIRETIME/HPEXPIRETIME/HTTL/HPTTL/HPERSIST/HGETEX/HGETDEL) from fr-command's wire dispatch. Removed: 11 CommandId variants, the write-classification arm group, the dispatch arms (1281-1307), both lookup paths, the 449-line HEXPIRE family handler block (3164-3612), and the ~960-line in-tests block that exercised the wire commands (23489-24448). Kept: the fr-store::HashFieldTtl* primitives plus their unit/metamorphic tests, since RDB type 21 still round-trips per-field TTL data via the HashWithTtls variant. Updated the rdb_roundtrip_preserves_hash_field_ttls_via_hashwithttls_variant test to seed/verify via Store API directly. Deleted two AOF-roundtrip tests (aof_roundtrip_hexpire_family_reconstructs_per_field_ttls + aof_roundtrip_hpexpireat_past_deadline_replays_reaped_state) that exercised wire-level HEXPIRE; AOF mechanism remains covered by the rest of aof_roundtrip_test.rs. Updated the debug_object_omits_redis_7_4_hexpired_fields_suffix test to inline its hash setup. Added redis_7_4_hash_field_ttl_commands_return_unknown_command_per_7_2_4_parity pin asserting all 11 commands surface 'ERR unknown command' on the wire.

Tagalong fix: realigned debug_reload_requires_configured_persistence (now debug_reload_round_trips_in_memory_without_persistence_per_upstream) to assert the 8hzzv-correct in-memory round-trip behavior.

Verified byte-for-byte parity with vendored Redis 7.2.4 (16380): all 11 commands emit identical 'ERR unknown command <CMD>, with args beginning with: ...' replies. fr-runtime suite: 358/358 lib + 138/138 integration tests pass. Workspace clippy clean under -D warnings. Pre-existing failures (4 fr-command stream tests, 5 conformance live-oracle/network tests) confirmed present on main without my changes — filed as frankenredis-o3m0f and remain unrelated to ja8yu.

title: INCRBYFLOAT precision: large floats (1e308) emit '1' followed by 308 zeros instead of long-double mantissa expansion
description:
Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4:
SET k 1.5
INCRBYFLOAT k 1e308
ven: 999...878 (vendored emits the 17-digit-precision mantissa expansion of the IEEE long-double sum)
fr : 1 followed by 308 zeros (fr emits '1.0e308' expanded as if it were exactly representable)

Per object.c::ld2string with LD_STR_HUMAN mode, upstream stores the value as long double (80-bit on x86_64) and formats with %.17Lg-equivalent precision then strips trailing zeros. fr uses f64 + standard {value} formatting that doesn't preserve mantissa precision past 1 digit when scientific notation kicks in.

Fix: upgrade INCRBYFLOAT/HINCRBYFLOAT/ZINCRBY accumulators to long-double-equivalent precision (e.g., Float80 emulation via fp_macros) and switch the to_string path to a high-precision formatter (Grisu / Ryu fixed-mode at 17 digits) before stripping trailing zeros. Acceptance: SET k 1.5 + INCRBYFLOAT k 1e308 byte-equal vendored.
close_reason: Implemented pure-Rust long-double integer text fallback for large INCRBYFLOAT/HINCRBYFLOAT decimal operands; added vendored 1e308 store coverage.

title: Add expired/evicted key counters to Store
description:
# Add expired_keys and evicted_keys counters to Store

## What
Add four new pub u64 fields to Store:
- stat_expired_keys: total keys removed by expiration (lazy or active)
- stat_evicted_keys: total keys removed by maxmemory eviction
- stat_expired_stale_perc: percentage of expired keys found during sampling (for active-expire efficiency)
- stat_expire_cycle_cpu_milliseconds: cumulative CPU time spent in active-expire cycles

## Where to increment
- stat_expired_keys: in drop_if_expired() (lazy expiry) and in the active-expire cycle in fr-runtime
- stat_evicted_keys: in the eviction cycle in fr-runtime (handle_eviction)
- The fr-runtime eviction and active-expire code already has the logic — it just doesn't count

## Key implementation points
- Add fields to Store struct (fr-store/src/lib.rs near line 722)
- Initialize to 0 in Store::new()
- For lazy expiry: increment stat_expired_keys inside drop_if_expired() when a key is actually removed
- For active expiry: the runtime's active_expire_cycle already iterates and removes keys — add counting there
- For eviction: the runtime's eviction_cycle already removes keys — add counting there
- Reset in CONFIG RESETSTAT (already wired in runtime)

## Why this matters
- INFO stats reports expired_keys:0 and evicted_keys:0 — essential for monitoring memory pressure
- total_keys_expired and total_keys_evicted are aliases of the same counters
- Redis monitoring tools alert on eviction rate increases

## Testing
- Unit test: set key with TTL, advance time past expiry, verify stat_expired_keys incremented
- Conformance: INFO stats after expiry operations should show non-zero counts

## Files to modify
- crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs (Store struct + drop_if_expired)
- crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs (active_expire_cycle, eviction_cycle)
close_reason: Completed

title: Fix NOMKSTREAM behavior for existing empty streams
description:
## Problem

fr-command/src/lib.rs lines 4000-4007: XADD with NOMKSTREAM incorrectly returns nil for existing empty streams (created via XGROUP CREATE ... MKSTREAM).

The NOMKSTREAM check uses xlen==0 AND xlast_id==None to determine the stream doesn't exist. But an empty stream (created by MKSTREAM) has xlen==0 and may have xlast_id==None, causing NOMKSTREAM to return nil when it should proceed.

## What Redis Does

NOMKSTREAM only prevents creating a NEW stream key. If the key already exists as a stream (even empty), XADD proceeds normally.

## Fix

Check whether the key exists as a stream type, not whether the stream has entries:
  if nomkstream && !store.key_exists_as_type(key, \"stream\") {
      return Ok(RespFrame::BulkString(None));
  }

This requires either a new store method or checking the entry type directly.

## Files to Modify

- crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs: XADD NOMKSTREAM check
- crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs: May need a type-checking helper
close_reason: Fixed: NOMKSTREAM now uses store.exists() to check key existence, not xlen/xlast_id

New helper zrange_emit_with_resp threads dispatch_client_ctx.resp_protocol_version. Under RESP3 + WITHSCORES emits Array<[member, score]> (per-pair 2-element sub-Array, matching upstream's addReplyArrayLen(c,2) per row when c->resp > 2). Under RESP2 stays flat alternating Array. New regression test zrange_withscores_under_resp3_returns_array_of_pair_subarrays exercises four ingestion paths: ZRANGE rank, ZRANGEBYSCORE, ZRANGE BYSCORE REV, ZREVRANGE — plus a RESP2 flat-shape pin. All 19 zrange-named lib tests pass; 4 pre-existing unrelated failures (xreadgroup/xinfo stream/migrate-vendored/config-set-appendfsync) reproduce on git-stash and are unchanged. Thirteenth in the RESP3-shape audit cluster (i40x2/e4njz/udl3y/asvh1/5d04d/ndz0j/5h86s/cz712/ec2rf/q11am/1dfox/cgjlc/jnf53).

title: HIGH: expand fr-protocol golden harness — adversarial + boundary RESP2 fixtures (empty, limits, incomplete, truncated, negative, deep-nest)
description:
## Summary

`crates/fr-protocol/tests/golden.rs` currently covers exactly 10 happy-path cases: simple_string, error_string, integer, bulk_string, null_bulk_string, array, null_array, nested_array, invalid_prefix, invalid_bulk_length. The parser it exercises (`parse_frame`) has at least nine distinct error variants (`Incomplete`, `InvalidPrefix`, `UnsupportedResp3Type`, `InvalidInteger`, `InvalidBulkLength`, `InvalidMultibulkLength`, `InvalidUtf8`, `BulkLengthTooLarge`, `MultibulkLengthTooLarge`, `RecursionLimitExceeded`, `LineTooLong`) plus `ParseResult.consumed`-boundary arithmetic — none of those paths have frozen expectations.

Golden artifacts are the right gate here per `/testing-golden-artifacts`: pin the exact Debug-formatted parse result for every boundary, so any silent drift in error variants, `consumed` math, or bulk-length accounting trips the harness.

## New fixtures to add (≥15 cases)

Happy-path boundaries:
- `empty_simple_string` — `b\"+\\r\\n\"` → `SimpleString(\"\")`
- `empty_error_string` — `b\"-\\r\\n\"` → `Error(\"\")`
- `empty_bulk_string` — `b\"$0\\r\\n\\r\\n\"` → `BulkString(Some(vec![]))`
- `empty_array` — `b\"*0\\r\\n\"` → `Array(Some(vec![]))`
- `zero_integer` — `b\":0\\r\\n\"`
- `negative_integer` — `b\":-1234\\r\\n\"`
- `i64_max_integer` — `b\":9223372036854775807\\r\\n\"`
- `i64_min_integer` — `b\":-9223372036854775808\\r\\n\"`
- `bulk_string_with_binary` — `b\"$4\\r\\n\\x00\\xff\\r\\n\\r\\n\"` (embedded CRLF inside bulk body is legal)
- `pipelined_sequence` — `b\"+PING\\r\\n+OK\\r\\n:7\\r\\n\"` (one parse should take the first frame with `consumed=7`)

Incomplete / partial:
- `incomplete_simple_string` — `b\"+PING\"` (no CRLF) → `Err(Incomplete)`
- `incomplete_bulk_body` — `b\"$6\\r\\nfoo\"` (length says 6, only 3 delivered) → `Err(Incomplete)`
- `incomplete_bulk_terminator` — `b\"$3\\r\\nfoo\"` (body present, no trailing CRLF) → `Err(Incomplete)`
- `incomplete_array_child` — `b\"*2\\r\\n:1\\r\\n\"` (declared 2 children, delivered 1) → `Err(Incomplete)`

Adversarial error paths:
- `invalid_integer_payload` — `b\":abc\\r\\n\"` → `Err(InvalidInteger)`
- `integer_with_leading_plus_rejected` — `b\":+5\\r\\n\"` → Debug-pin whatever variant we emit today (likely `InvalidInteger`; lock behaviour regardless of which way the choice lands)
- `bulk_length_minus_two_invalid` — `b\"$-2\\r\\n\"` → `Err(InvalidBulkLength)`
- `array_length_minus_two_invalid` — `b\"*-2\\r\\n\"` → `Err(InvalidMultibulkLength)`
- `bulk_length_exceeds_limit` — `b\"$600000000\\r\\n...\"` with default `max_bulk_len=512 MiB` → `Err(BulkLengthTooLarge)` (use `parse_frame_with_config` if needed to keep the fixture small; document config in fixture comment)
- `recursion_limit_exceeded` — deeply nested `*1\\r\\n*1\\r\\n...` past 128 levels → `Err(RecursionLimitExceeded)`
- `unsupported_resp3_map_prefix` — `b\"%2\\r\\n...\"` → `Err(UnsupportedResp3Type('%'))`
- `unsupported_resp3_set_prefix` — `b\"~2\\r\\n...\"` → `Err(UnsupportedResp3Type('~'))`
- `unsupported_resp3_null_prefix` — `b\"_\\r\\n\"` → `Err(UnsupportedResp3Type('_'))`
- `unsupported_resp3_boolean_prefix` — `b\"#t\\r\\n\"` → `Err(UnsupportedResp3Type('#'))`

## Why golden, not inline asserts

- Each case pins ~5–40 lines of `{:#?}` output including nested enum variants and `consumed` — inline `assert_eq!` would be unreadable and noisy in review diffs. Golden files give a clean `git diff` when a change is intentional.
- Every RESP3 prefix fixture is the explicit XFAIL marker for `frankenredis-0zyf`'s RESP3 conformance gap — once RESP3 lands, regenerate those fixtures and track the intentional change in `git diff tests/golden/`.

## Scope guardrails

- Pure additive: new cases only, existing 10 cases unchanged.
- Does not touch `parse_frame` itself.
- Does not introduce new public API — only exercises existing `parse_frame` + `ParserConfig` surface.
- Zero dependency on `legacy_redis_code/redis` — fixtures are authored by hand and frozen as goldens.

## Acceptance

- `cargo test -p fr-protocol` on a fresh HEAD passes with all new goldens checked in.
- `UPDATE_GOLDENS=1 cargo test -p fr-protocol` regenerates without net-new diff (idempotent).
- `tests/golden/` gains ≥15 new files, each with the corresponding `#[test]` in `golden.rs`.
- Every covered `RespParseError` variant has at least one golden case.

## Labels

fr-protocol, testing-golden-artifacts, resp2, boundary-cases, parser-hardening
close_reason: Fixed in 70c6138 — expanded golden.rs from 10 to 38 fixtures covering every RespParseError variant plus RESP3 prefixes as XFAIL markers.

title: EVAL {map=...} returns RESP3 map frame to RESP2 clients (missing downconvert)
description:
Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4:

  EVAL 'return {map={a=1, b=2}}' 0 (RESP2 client):
    vendored: *4
$1
a
:1
$1
b
:2
  (flat 2N array)
    fr:       %2
$1
a
:1
$1
b
:2
  (RESP3 map)

Root cause: lua_to_resp in fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs emits RespFrame::Map for {map=...} hint tables, and fr-protocol's encoder writes '%N
' for Map unconditionally. Upstream src/script_lua.c::luaReplyToRedisReply emits Map/Set/Push types only when the calling connection is on RESP3; RESP2 clients receive the flat 2N array equivalent (auto-downconvert).

Affected: any Lua script returning {map=...}, {set=...}, or other RESP3-only shapes when the caller is on RESP2. Nested maps inside arrays/other maps must also downconvert.

Fix: after lua_to_resp in eval_script, check store.dispatch_client_ctx.resp_protocol_version. If < 3, walk the frame tree and rewrite Map(Some(entries)) → Array(Some(flat 2N)), Map(None) → Array(None), and Push → Array recursively.
close_reason: Added downconvert_lua_reply_to_resp2 in fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs that walks the frame tree after lua_to_resp and rewrites Map(Some) → flat 2N Array, Map(None) → Array(None), and Push → Array recursively when store.dispatch_client_ctx.resp_protocol_version != 3. Applied at the end of eval_script. Mirrors upstream src/script_lua.c::luaReplyToRedisReply auto-downconvert behavior. Regression tests eval_map_hint_flattens_to_array_for_resp2_jp7gs (covers RESP2 flat, RESP3 map preservation, nested-map downconversion, plain-array pass-through) and updated lua_to_resp_recognises_resp3_type_hint_tables to pin both shapes. Live diff vs vendored 7.2.4 matches on RESP2 (*4\r\n flat) and RESP3 (%2\r\n map) raw wire frames.

title: HIGH: WAIT / WAITAOF are stubs — always return 0, no replica ACK tracking
description:
## Summary

WAIT and WAITAOF are call-site present but behaviourally inert. See `crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:12072-12090`:

```rust
fn wait_cmd(argv: &[Vec<u8>]) -> Result<RespFrame, CommandError> {
    ...
    // WAIT numreplicas timeout - in standalone mode, return 0 replicas
    let _ = numreplicas;
    Ok(RespFrame::Integer(0))
}
```

`waitaof_cmd` at lib.rs:5772 follows the same pattern: argument validation only, then `Integer(0)` (or its array-of-zero analog). Existing project memory (`project_wait_commands_stub.md`) confirms: \"WAIT/WAITAOF always return 0, no blocking or replica ACK checking\".

## Why it matters

Deterministic Latency Replication Core (DLRC, AGENTS.md \"Crown-Jewel Innovation\") advertises recoverable persistence pipelines and replication-lag gating. Production clients that treat WAIT as a real barrier (quorum writes, post-write read-your-own-write semantics) will see writes that aren't durable — an invisible failure mode, not an error reply. Furthermore, our own test `tests::wait_clamps_negative_numreplicas_and_uses_timeout_specific_errors` only checks the argument-validation branch, giving false confidence.

Under test we already have a feedback rule: \"WAIT/WAITAOF must refresh replica ACK snapshots before evaluating\" — noted in `feedback_wait_refresh_snapshots.md`. That rule cannot be honoured while the command is a constant-return stub.

## Applying /testing-conformance-harnesses + /testing-fuzzing

**Conformance (Pattern 1 + Pattern 6):**
- Spawn upstream `legacy_redis_code/redis/src/redis-server` as primary + fr-server as replica and vice-versa.
- Send a WRITE followed by WAIT N timeout under three regimes: (a) no replicas connected, (b) one replica connected but slow, (c) replica caught up before timeout.
- Assert our reply count monotonically tracks upstream's within 10 ms of the same event ordering.
- For WAITAOF: drive `appendfsync always` / `everysec` / `no`, force flush lag, and differential against upstream's per-local-aof-offset accounting.

**Fuzzing (/testing-fuzzing, stateful):**
- New `fuzz_wait_replica_ordering.rs` in `fuzz/fuzz_targets/`: structure-aware harness that generates interleaved WRITE/WAIT/replica-ACK/timeout event sequences and asserts (i) WAIT never returns a count > total known replicas, (ii) returned count is monotonic within a single WAIT invocation, (iii) a completed WAIT N is observationally equivalent to N+1 replicas if an extra replica ACK arrives a microsecond before the return — i.e., no post-commit ACK lost.

## Concrete work items

1. Plumb ACK tracking into `fr-repl` so `Runtime::replica_ack_snapshot(offset)` returns `{count_at_or_past: usize}` — hook the existing replication stream's REPLCONF ACK path (see `HandshakeFsm` in fr-repl).
2. Convert WAIT/WAITAOF from return-constant to true session-blocking commands in `fr-runtime`'s blocking-command machinery (same plumbing as BLPOP/XREAD-BLOCK). Blocking must honour timeout-to-ms precision and must re-evaluate on every replica-ACK event (per the saved feedback rule).
3. Kill the `let _ = numreplicas;` at lib.rs:12088 once plumbing is in place.
4. Remove the stub test comment at lib.rs:28619 (`waitaof_standalone_reports_no_local_ack_without_appendonly`) and replace with a live-oracle diff.

## Acceptance

- With upstream Redis on port P1 as master, our server on port P2 as replica: send `SET k v` then `WAIT 1 500` to P1 — our replica's ACK is observable within the timeout, WAIT returns 1. Repeat with our server as master and upstream as replica: symmetric.
- WAITAOF under `appendfsync always` returns (localOk, syncedSlaves) that matches upstream within 10 ms wall-clock across 1000 synthetic writes.
- Fuzz target runs 1M iters without finding a monotonicity violation.
- `feedback_wait_refresh_snapshots.md` rule mechanised: a regression test asserts that the ACK snapshot is re-read on every blocking re-wake.

## Labels

fr-command, fr-repl, wait, waitaof, stub, conformance-gap
close_reason: Reviewed: fr-runtime already has real WAIT (handle_wait_command uses evaluate_wait over replica_ack_offsets) and WAITAOF (handle_waitaof_command uses evaluate_waitaof over local_fsync_offset + replica_fsync_offsets) implementations. The fr-command stubs I flagged (wait_cmd, waitaof_cmd at 12072/5776) are fallback dead code — RuntimeSpecialCommand::Wait/Waitaof pre-intercepts before fr-command dispatch. REPLCONF ACK/FACK records real per-replica ack_offset/fsync_offset/last_ack_timestamp_ms. WAIT correctly returns 0 in standalone mode because there ARE zero replicas. The tcp_waitaof_appendfsync_no test is flaky on legacy_redis_code/redis — its own probe returns ([0,0],[1,0]) or ([0,0],[0,0]) across runs — unrelated to franken impl. Core contract satisfied; full master-replica conformance matrix tracked under fr-conformance live_redis_fr_p2c_006_replication_journey. Closing as working-as-intended.

title: INFO clients: client_recent_max_input_buffer and client_recent_max_output_buffer hardcoded to 0
description: Follow-up to frankenredis-tepuj. After tepuj wired ClientSession.qbuf_bytes/output_buffer_bytes from fr-server's mio buffers, the per-client snapshot exists -- but fr-command/src/lib.rs:12816-12817 still hardcodes 'client_recent_max_input_buffer:0' and 'client_recent_max_output_buffer:0' in INFO clients output. Vendored 7.2.4 maintains these in server.stat_clients_max_input_buffer / stat_clients_max_output_buffer accumulators, updated by clientsCron via getRecentMaxClientInputBuffer / getRecentMaxClientOutputBuffer (server.c around the genRedisInfoString site). Implementation: (1) add two pub max-since-reset fields on Store (e.g. stat_clients_recent_max_input_buffer: usize and stat_clients_recent_max_output_buffer: usize), (2) in fr-runtime's record_client_session (already runs after each handle_readable + dispatch turn), update the accumulators with session.qbuf_bytes and session.output_buffer_bytes via store.observe_client_buffer_sizes(qbuf, obl); the store method takes the max with current values, (3) reset (or apply Redis's exponential decay) on a cron tick. Per upstream behavior the values reset periodically -- check what the exact decay/reset cadence is (CLIENT_TYPE_NORMAL bucket scan in clientsCronTrackExpansiveClients), then either implement the same decay or use the simpler 'reset on CONFIG RESETSTAT only' semantics that other fr counters already follow. (4) wire fr-command's INFO clients emitter to read from store.stat_clients_recent_max_input_buffer / max_output_buffer instead of the hardcoded 0. Acceptance: live differential probe vs vendored shows non-zero client_recent_max_input_buffer after at least one client has sent traffic; ideally byte-exact after the same workload. Surfaced during REVIEW PHASE differential probe right after tepuj close.
close_reason: INFO clients now emits real client_recent_max_input_buffer / max_output_buffer values from per-client buffer accumulators. Implementation: (1) Added stat_clients_recent_max_input_buffer / stat_clients_recent_max_output_buffer usize fields on Store (defaulted 0, reset by reset_info_stats so CONFIG RESETSTAT clears them along with other counters), (2) Added Store::observe_client_buffer_sizes(input, output) that takes the running max -- mirrors the contribution upstream's clientsCron makes to server.stat_clients_max_input_buffer, (3) Added Runtime::observe_client_buffer_sizes wrapper that fr-server calls from handle_readable BEFORE swapping the session into the runtime (capturing the pre-dispatch read_buf.len, the inbound bytes about to be parsed) and again after swap-back (capturing the post-dispatch write_buf.len before handle_writable drains it). (4) Wired fr-command's INFO clients emitter at lib.rs:12816 to read from store.stat_clients_recent_max_input_buffer / max_output_buffer instead of the hardcoded '0' strings. Live differential probe after restart + CONFIG RESETSTAT + SET bigkey of 500B + GET bigkey: fr reports 'client_recent_max_input_buffer:533 client_recent_max_output_buffer:508' vs vendored's 'client_recent_max_input_buffer:8 client_recent_max_output_buffer:0'. fr captures the strict-max-since-reset while vendored uses a decaying-max per clientsCron tick (its values are smaller because the 500B value's contribution decayed). Both are non-zero and informative; exact decay-window parity is a follow-up if needed. Tests: fr-store 336 + fr-command 910 + fr-runtime 372 (first run with clean /tmp state) all pass. The 2 pre-existing bgsave/rdb tests show flaky behavior dependent on /tmp dir state, reproduced both with and without these changes.

title: HIGH: CLUSTER admin subcommands + READONLY/READWRITE always return 'cluster support disabled' regardless of cluster_enabled
description:
## Summary

`crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:6007-6021` routes fifteen CLUSTER admin subcommands to the `cluster_disabled_error()` path unconditionally:

```rust
if sub.eq_ignore_ascii_case(\"RESET\")
    || sub.eq_ignore_ascii_case(\"MEET\")
    || sub.eq_ignore_ascii_case(\"FORGET\")
    || sub.eq_ignore_ascii_case(\"REPLICATE\")
    || sub.eq_ignore_ascii_case(\"REPLICAS\")
    || sub.eq_ignore_ascii_case(\"FAILOVER\")
    || sub.eq_ignore_ascii_case(\"ADDSLOTS\")
    || sub.eq_ignore_ascii_case(\"DELSLOTS\")
    || sub.eq_ignore_ascii_case(\"ADDSLOTSRANGE\")
    || sub.eq_ignore_ascii_case(\"DELSLOTSRANGE\")
    || sub.eq_ignore_ascii_case(\"FLUSHSLOTS\")
    || sub.eq_ignore_ascii_case(\"SAVECONFIG\")
    || sub.eq_ignore_ascii_case(\"SET-CONFIG-EPOCH\")
    || sub.eq_ignore_ascii_case(\"BUMPEPOCH\")
    || sub.eq_ignore_ascii_case(\"SLOTSTATE\")
{
    return Err(cluster_disabled_error());
}
```

The error message is \"ERR This instance has cluster support disabled\" — but the check does not consult `store.cluster_enabled`, so this error is returned even when the operator has set `cluster-enabled yes`.

READONLY and READWRITE at lines 6115-6131 are even blunter:

```rust
fn readonly_cmd(argv: &[Vec<u8>]) -> Result<RespFrame, CommandError> {
    if argv.len() != 1 { return Err(CommandError::WrongArity(\"READONLY\")); }
    Err(CommandError::Custom(
        \"ERR This instance has cluster support disabled\".to_string(),
    ))
}
fn readwrite_cmd(argv: &[Vec<u8>]) -> Result<RespFrame, CommandError> { /* same */ }
```

They never check `cluster_enabled` at all. When cluster is enabled (MYID/SHARDS/KEYSLOT all respond with real data), a client that says `READONLY` to route reads to a replica still gets this misleading error.

## Why it's a mock-code-finder catch

It fits the pattern's definition of a stub: short function, returns the same string regardless of input, callers (orchestrators, `redis-cli --cluster`) depend on real output but never get it. The stub takes the form of a *plausible error* rather than `todo!()`, which is exactly what made grep's first pass miss it — one has to trace through the conditional tree to find that the predicate never fires for real.

## Impact

- Any client that autodetects cluster mode via `CLUSTER INFO` + cluster_enabled_flag and then issues `CLUSTER MEET` to add a node sees a nonsensical \"disabled\" error when the server just told it cluster WAS enabled.
- `redis-cli --cluster create` cannot configure FrankenRedis nodes into a cluster.
- `redis-cli --cluster reshard` cannot move slots.
- Primary/replica replacement via `CLUSTER FAILOVER` is not available.
- Read-your-replica scale-out via `READONLY` is not available.

## Applying /testing-conformance-harnesses

Pattern 4 matrix of (cluster_enabled, subcommand, expected_error_class):

| cluster_enabled | subcommand | current reply | upstream reply | verdict |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| false | MEET host port | `ERR ... cluster support disabled` | same | PASS |
| true  | MEET host port | `ERR ... cluster support disabled` | attempts contact, returns OK or timeout | **FAIL** |
| true  | ADDSLOTS 0 1 2 | `ERR ... cluster support disabled` | `OK` or `ERR Slot X is already busy` | **FAIL** |
| true  | FAILOVER       | `ERR ... cluster support disabled` | `OK` (triggers election) or `ERR You should send CLUSTER FAILOVER to a replica` | **FAIL** |
| any   | READONLY       | `ERR ... cluster support disabled` | in cluster mode: `OK`; outside: `ERR` | **FAIL** on cluster_enabled=true |

## Fix direction

Two-pronged:

1. **Correctness layer.** Add a `cluster_enabled` guard to READONLY/READWRITE, and split the 15-subcommand short-circuit into a real dispatch. For every admin subcommand, return either the correct OK/response OR a specific, upstream-shaped error (e.g. `ERR CLUSTER SETSLOT slot X node Y failed: Unknown node ID`), not the blanket \"disabled\" string. Start with arity + syntax errors matching upstream wording; behavioural work lands later.
2. **Fuzzing layer.** Extend `fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_command_option_parsers.rs` (or a new `fuzz_cluster_admin_cmds.rs`) with structure-aware generation of all 15 admin commands × both cluster_enabled states; assert the reply category is never the blanket \"disabled\" string unless cluster_enabled=false.

## Related

- Depends on a real `fr-repl::ClusterState` with a slot map + node map (does not exist yet — separate bead to file if this grows).
- Builds on `frankenredis-s5su` (RDB for clustered state), `frankenredis-jqgv` (WAIT).
- Interacts with `frankenredis-1749` (differential fuzzer) — that fuzzer should opt-in cluster_enabled=true for half of its cases once this is fixed, otherwise it XFAILs every cluster-path divergence.

## Acceptance

- Matrix: every (cluster_enabled, subcommand) cell differential-matches upstream on at minimum: arity, syntax, unknown-subcommand wording.
- READONLY + READWRITE return OK when cluster_enabled and the client is not in a transaction.
- A shell loop of `redis-cli --cluster check 127.0.0.1:<our_port>` against a 3-node FrankenRedis cluster reports no unexpected errors for topology-read operations.

## Labels

fr-command, cluster, readonly, readwrite, misleading-error, conformance-gap, mock-finder
close_reason: Closed with be0d91c (+ earlier c122714 READONLY/READWRITE): first-pass implementation of 13 CLUSTER admin subcommands with upstream-matching arity, syntax, and cluster_enabled gating. 3 new tests + 609/0 in fr-command --lib. SET-CONFIG-EPOCH and SLOTSTATE still blanket-errored pending real fr-repl config-epoch + slot-state machinery (not a 60-min scope).

title: Conformance fixtures: core_scripting + core_server regressed (pre-existing, surfaced iter 128)
description:
Discovered during iter 128 workspace-test sweep: conformance_core_scripting and conformance_core_server both fail with multiple frame mismatches. Confirmed pre-existing (failures persist after stashing this iter's changes), so not caused by frankenredis-20i0i.

Sample regressions in core_scripting:

  - eval_table_concat_rejects_non_table_argument: expected 'ERR bad argument...', fr returns 'ERR user_script:1: bad argument...' (prefix added vs fixture's bare form)
  - eval_table_insert_rejects_out_of_bounds_position: expected error, fr returns BulkString(None) (table.insert position not validated)

Likely from one of the recent Lua-touching commits (whyor/2hgg1/yovmj/lwj8o) shifted lua_format_argerror prefix behavior in some paths. Fixture may need refresh OR the prefix toggle needs more care.

Pre-existing failures originally tracked as expiry/scan/fixture_core, now joined by these two. core_server failure unexplored.

title: Lua stdlib string.rep/table.concat/table.insert behavior diverges from vendored Lua 5.1
description:
Six Lua stdlib divergences uncovered during a follow-up audit after frankenredis-3osi6:

1. **string.rep on empty string with huge count**: 
   string.rep('', 2147483647) → vendored returns '' (since 0 * N = 0); fr returns 'ERR string length overflow' because the helper checks n_val > 512MB *before* the multiplication, so an empty source string still trips the guard. The check at crates/fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs:5170 fires unconditionally on huge n_val regardless of s.len(). Vendored's strchecker uses (s_len * n) > MAX so an empty source is fine.

2. **table.concat with nil separator**: 
   table.concat({1,2,3}, nil) → vendored treats nil sep as the default empty string and returns '123'; fr converts nil to the literal Lua-tostring 'nil' and returns '1nil2nil3'. luaL_optstring(L, 2, '') is the upstream contract — fr's code path uses to_display_string() which renders nil as 'nil'.

3. **table.concat with nil hole**: 
   table.concat({1,nil,3}) → vendored errors 'invalid value (nil) at index 2 in table for concat'; fr silently truncates at the first nil and returns '1'.
   table.concat({1,2,3,nil,5}) → vendored errors 'invalid value (nil) at index 4'; fr returns '123'.

4. **table.concat with out-of-range start/end**: 
   table.concat({1,2,3}, '', 0, 3) → vendored errors 'invalid value (nil) at index 0'; fr silently clamps to valid range and returns '123'.
   table.concat({1,2,3}, '', 1, 5) → vendored errors 'invalid value (nil) at index 4'; fr returns '123'.

5. **table.insert with no value arg**: 
   table.insert({}) → vendored errors 'wrong number of arguments to insert'; fr silently no-ops.

6. **table.insert with out-of-bounds pos**: 
   table.insert({1,2,3}, 10, 'x') → vendored ACCEPTS it (Lua 5.1 sparse-inserts at the requested pos, leaving nil holes); fr errors 'bad argument #2 to insert (position out of bounds)'.
   table.insert({1,2,3}, 0, 'x') → vendored ACCEPTS (same); fr errors.

   Upstream ltablib.c::tinsert just calls luaL_setn after a shift, no bounds check; pos==0 / pos > #t+1 are tolerated. fr's strict check at the insert site rejects what vendored quietly allows.

Out of scope (lower-priority wording-only fixes, file as a separate followup if pursued):
  - string.char(-1) / string.char(256) error wording missing 'user_script:1: ' prefix.
  - unpack({}, 1, 1000000) error missing the prefix.
  - string.gsub error has fr-only ', got nil' suffix instead of just '(string/function/table expected)'.

Live diff vs vendored 7.2.4 (:16380); each case is reproducible.

Test plan:
  - string.rep('', N) returns '' for any non-negative N (including 2^31-1).
  - table.concat({1,2,3}, nil) → '123'.
  - table.concat with a nil hole in the iteration range → 'invalid value (nil) at index <N>' with the upstream wording.
  - table.concat with out-of-range start/end indices → same 'invalid value (nil)' error.
  - table.insert({}) → 'wrong number of arguments to insert' error.
  - table.insert({1,2,3}, 0, 'x') and (..., 10, 'x') → accepted with sparse insertion; happy-path table.insert({1,2,3}, 2, 'x') still works.
  - No regressions on existing table.concat / table.insert tests.
close_reason:
Fixed 6 Lua stdlib behavior divergences:

1. string.rep('', N): The 512MB overflow guard now short-circuits when s.is_empty() — previously fr emitted 'string length overflow' for empty source with huge N even though 0 * N = 0. Vendored returns ''.

2. table.concat nil separator: nil/missing sep now maps to the empty string (luaL_optstring(L, 2, '') contract), not the literal 'nil' that to_display_string() produces. table.concat({1,2,3}, nil) now returns '123', not '1nil2nil3'.

3. table.concat nil hole inside range: now raises 'user_script:1: invalid value (nil) at index N in table for concat' matching upstream ltablib.c::tconcat. Out-of-range start/end and Mid-range nils both surface the same error.

4. table.concat non-string non-number entry: now raises 'invalid value (<type>) at index N in table for concat'.

5. table.insert 1-arg / 4+ arg call: now raises 'user_script:1: wrong number of arguments to insert' instead of silently no-op'ing, matching upstream's explicit luaL_error.

6. table.insert with out-of-bounds pos: vendored Redis ships a modified Lua 5.1 ltablib.c where the bounds check was replaced with 'if (pos > e) e = pos;' so pos > #t+1, pos <= 0 are accepted (sparse insertion and hash-part insertion respectively). fr previously errored on these. Vendored ltablib.c quoted in the bead description.

Live diff vs vendored 7.2.4 (port 16380): 16/16 probe cases match. Server stays alive after all error cases.

Pinned by three new regression tests (string_rep_huge_returns_empty_for_empty_source, table_concat_matches_upstream_lua_5_1 with 8 sub-cases, table_insert_wrong_arity_and_sparse_grow_match_upstream with 6 sub-cases). The old table_insert_rejects_out_of_bounds_position was renamed and inverted to verify the new permissive behavior; the old string_rep_huge_does_not_loop_forever was renamed and updated to verify empty-result instead of error.

All 939 fr-command lib tests pass.

Note: the deeper architectural divergence in fr's # operator on sparse tables (#{1,nil,3} = 1 vs vendored's 3) is unchanged and out of scope; the table.concat tests use explicit start/end indices to side-step that gap.

title: Centralize version string as single const across all crates
description:
## Problem

The Redis version string appears in multiple locations:
- fr-command/src/lib.rs: \"redis_version:7.2.0-frankenredis\" (INFO)
- fr-runtime/src/lib.rs: hello_bulk(\"7.2.0\") (HELLO response)
- fr-runtime/src/lib.rs: (\"redis-ver\", \"7.2.0\") (RDB aux field — was 7.0.0, fixed to 7.2.0 in f8c5a8a)

While these are now consistent (all 7.2.0), there's no single source of truth. If the version needs to change, all three locations must be found and updated.

## Fix

1. Define a const in fr-runtime (or a shared crate): pub const REDIS_COMPAT_VERSION: &str = \"7.2.0\";
2. Use this const in all locations
3. Consider also deriving the FrankenRedis version from Cargo.toml via env!(\"CARGO_PKG_VERSION\")

## Files to Modify

- crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs: Define const, use in RDB and HELLO
- crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs: Use const in INFO
close_reason: Centralized REDIS_COMPAT_VERSION const in fr-store, used by fr-command INFO and fr-runtime HELLO/RDB

title: hash-max-listpack-entries default 512 vs upstream 128 prevents encoding transition
description:
Differential probe vs vendored redis 7.2.4:

  CONFIG GET hash-max-listpack-entries → vendored: 128, fr: 512

Consequence: hashes with 129–512 fields stay as 'listpack' on fr
but transition to 'hashtable' on upstream. OBJECT ENCODING returns
divergent values across this range, breaking memory/encoding
assumptions for any client or test that observes encoding state.

Confirmed transition at n=129:
  vendored OBJECT ENCODING h (129 fields): hashtable
  fr        OBJECT ENCODING h (129 fields): listpack

Root cause: fr-store/src/lib.rs:1753 + fr-runtime/src/lib.rs:495 +
fr-persist/src/lib.rs:1028 all initialize hash_max_listpack_entries
to 512. Upstream Redis 7.2.4 default in config.c is 128.

Fix: change all three defaults to 128. Update any test that pinned
the value to the larger number (encoding_matrix.rs comment mentions
512). Verify the transition then fires at >128 to match upstream.

The other encoding-transition defaults already match upstream
(hash-max-listpack-value=64, set-max-listpack-entries=128,
zset-max-listpack-entries=128, set-max-intset-entries=512, etc.)
— only hash-max-listpack-entries is wrong.
close_reason: Updated default from 512 to 128 in fr-store, fr-runtime CONFIG defaults table, and fr-persist. Updated encoding_matrix test thresholds (128/129) and core_object.json/core_config.json fixtures. Verified hash listpack→hashtable transition now fires at n=129 matching vendored 7.2.4. The 4 pre-existing conformance failures (TTL/scan/scripting) are unrelated and were failing before this change.

title: s5su part a: document upstream RDB stream on-disk layout (fr-persist reference)
description:
Preparation sub-bead for br-frankenredis-s5su. Extract the upstream RDB_TYPE_STREAM_LISTPACKS (15), _LISTPACKS_2 (19), and _LISTPACKS_3 (21) on-disk layouts from legacy_redis_code/redis/src/rdb.c + t_stream.c and write them up as a reference doc at crates/fr-persist/docs/rdb-stream-format.md.

Scope:
  * Radix tree layout: (stream-id, listpack) pair count + per-pair listpack blob encoding.
  * Stream metadata fields: length, last_id, first_id, max_deleted_id, entries_added.
  * Per-group PEL layout: group-name length + name + last-delivered id + pel count + consumer slot.
  * Per-consumer fields: name + seen_time (v2+) + active_time (v3+) + per-consumer PEL ids.
  * Field-by-field u64/u32/length-encoded type list so the encoder sub-bead (s5su-d) can be written from this doc alone.

No code changes.
