title: fr-store: hash field TTL has unit-test coverage but no metamorphic invariants for the NX/XX/GT/LT flag matrix
description:
crates/fr-store/tests/metamorphic_*.rs has metamorphic suites for bit / bitop / hash variants / set / sortedset / stream / string / numeric / keys / lists / SWAPDB but NOT for the Redis 7.4 hash-field TTL family. crates/fr-store/tests/hash_field_ttl.rs covers the primitives via unit tests but doesn't pin existence-style invariants that catch refactor drift on the NX/XX/GT/LT flag interaction:

  MR-HEXPIRE-HTTL-IDENTITY      Setting a TTL of N ms on a fresh field
                                yields HTTL value ≈ N/1000 (rounded up).
  MR-HPERSIST-RETURNS-MINUS-ONE After HPERSIST, the field's TTL goes
                                back to -1 (field exists, no TTL).
  MR-HEXPIRE-NX-IDEMPOTENT      Repeated HEXPIRE NX after a TTL is set
                                is a no-op.
  MR-HEXPIRE-XX-WITHOUT-TTL     HEXPIRE XX on a field with no TTL
                                returns 0 / leaves -1 in place.
  MR-HEXPIRE-LT-MONOTONIC-MIN   Sequential HEXPIRE LT with decreasing
                                proposed deadlines applies each time;
                                final TTL == min of all proposals.
  MR-HEXPIRE-GT-MONOTONIC-MAX   Sequential HEXPIRE GT with increasing
                                proposed deadlines applies each time;
                                final TTL == max of all proposals.
  MR-HDEL-CLEARS-FIELD-TTL      HDEL removes the (key, field) row
                                from hash_field_expires.

Acceptance: a new tests/metamorphic_hash_field_ttl.rs that pins all 7
invariants. Existence-style asserts only — no proptest needed.

Reference: /testing-metamorphic gap audit on 2026-05-05; sister to ipz4
(stream metamorphic) and lxewj (SWAPDB-twice).
notes: Added crates/fr-store/tests/metamorphic_hash_field_ttl.rs with 7 invariants: mr_hexpire_httl_identity_on_fresh_field, mr_hpersist_returns_field_to_no_ttl, mr_hexpire_nx_idempotent_after_initial_apply, mr_hexpire_xx_no_op_on_field_without_ttl, mr_hexpire_lt_walks_down_to_minimum_proposed_deadline, mr_hexpire_gt_walks_up_to_maximum_proposed_deadline, mr_hdel_clears_per_field_ttl_row. All 7 pass; clippy clean. Catches refactor drift in the NX/XX/GT/LT flag matrix and HDEL → field_expires cleanup without depending on the command-layer.

title: [parity-gap] XINFO GROUPS nulls lag when tombstones are behind group cursor
description:
Review of recent stream XINFO lag commits found a Redis parity gap in stream_full_group_lag_frame. FrankenRedis only trusts a group's ENTRIESREAD counter when max-deleted-entry-id is 0-0, but upstream Redis t_stream.c::streamReplyWithCGLag trusts entries_read whenever streamRangeHasTombstones(s, &cg->last_id, NULL) is false. A tombstone behind the group cursor should not invalidate lag.

Concrete repro:
1. Start vendored Redis: legacy_redis_code/redis/src/redis-server --port 63988 --save "" --appendonly no
2. Run the same sequence against Redis and FrankenRedis strict mode:
   XADD s 1-0 a 1
   XADD s 2-0 b 2
   XADD s 3-0 c 3
   XADD s 4-0 d 4
   XDEL s 1-0
   XGROUP CREATE s g 3-0 ENTRIESREAD 3
   XINFO GROUPS s
3. Redis 7.2.4 reports entries-read 3 and lag 1. Current FrankenRedis reports entries-read 3 and null lag because max_deleted_id != 0-0 and last_delivered_id is neither first nor last.

Acceptance: compute XINFO GROUPS/FULL lag from entries_added - entries_read when entries_read is present and max-deleted-entry-id is not in the open range after the group's last-delivered-id; add a core_stream conformance fixture for the repro.
close_reason: Matched Redis tombstone-range lag semantics and added core_stream conformance coverage

title: GEORADIUS/GEORADIUSBYMEMBER accept STORE+WITHCOORD/WITHHASH/WITHDIST instead of rejecting
description:
Differential probe vs vendored redis 7.2.4:

  GEORADIUS g 0 0 100 km WITHCOORD STORE dst
    vendored: ERR STORE option in GEORADIUS is not compatible with WITHDIST, WITHHASH and WITHCOORD options
    fr:        1   ← bug, silently runs the STORE

  Same for WITHHASH+STORE, WITHDIST+STORE, plus STOREDIST variants,
  plus GEORADIUSBYMEMBER with any of these combinations.

Root cause: crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs::georadius() and
georadiusbymember() never enforce the upstream guard from
geo.c::georadiusGeneric:

    if (storekey && (withhash || withcoord || withdist)) {
        addReplyError(c, "STORE option in GEORADIUS is not compatible "
            "with WITHDIST, WITHHASH and WITHCOORD options");
        goto cleanup;
    }

fr parses both flag families but happily runs the STORE branch
when WITH* is also set, silently dropping the metadata that
WITHCOORD/WITHHASH/WITHDIST would have surfaced.

Fix: after parse_geo_search_flags + extract_geo_store, if
store_key.is_some() and (withcoord || withdist || withhash),
return the upstream error. Add to both georadius() and
georadiusbymember(). GEOSEARCH/GEOSEARCHSTORE don't share
WITH* + STORE in the same way (GEOSEARCH has no STORE/STOREDIST;
GEOSEARCHSTORE only takes STOREDIST as a bare boolean) so they
are unaffected.

Pin regression with 6 variants × 2 commands = 12 cases.
close_reason: Added the upstream guard in georadius() and georadiusbymember(): if store_key.is_some() && (withcoord || withdist || withhash), return the upstream error. All 12 (cmd × WITH-flag × STORE-flavor) combos now match vendored 7.2.4 exactly. Regression: STORE alone still returns count; WITH* alone still returns metadata array.

title: COMMAND_TABLE first_key/last_key/step divergences for MIGRATE/PFDEBUG/SPUBLISH/SSUBSCRIBE/SUNSUBSCRIBE
description:
Differential probe vs vendored Redis 7.2.4: five COMMAND_TABLE
rows had first_key/last_key/step triples that disagreed with
the upstream COMMAND INFO emission.

  MIGRATE       fr=(0,0,0)  vendored=(3,3,1)
    fr's COMMAND_TABLE row claimed no positional keys, even
    though argv[3] is the single-key form. The dedicated
    extractor (lib.rs:368-403) already handles both the
    single-key and multi-key 'KEYS' forms, so the triple was
    purely an emission divergence; updating it doesn't change
    runtime extraction.

  PFDEBUG       fr=(1,1,1)  vendored=(2,2,1)
    Real bug: fr's extract_keys_for_argv() reads argv[1] (the
    subcommand: GETREG / DECODE / ENCODING / TODENSE) instead
    of argv[2] (the actual HLL key). ACL key permission checks
    were therefore being applied to the subcommand string
    rather than to the key.

  SPUBLISH      fr=(0,0,0)  vendored=(1,1,1)
  SSUBSCRIBE    fr=(0,0,0)  vendored=(1,-1,1)
  SUNSUBSCRIBE  fr=(0,0,0)  vendored=(1,-1,1)
    Pubsub channels at argv[1...] (single argv[1] for SPUBLISH,
    variable list for the *SUBSCRIBE pair). fr's COMMAND INFO
    therefore failed to advertise the channel position to
    clients that derive routing from the upstream contract.

Pinned via
  command_info_key_specs_first_last_step_for_keyspec_fixes_matches_upstream
(crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs).

title: [EPIC] Dynamic Server Configuration — Wire CONFIG SET to Runtime Enforcement
description:
## Overview

FrankenRedis has numerous hardcoded constants that Redis makes configurable via CONFIG SET/GET. While the config table in fr-runtime advertises these parameters, the actual enforcement code uses compile-time constants, creating a correctness gap where CONFIG SET appears to succeed but has no effect on server behavior.

## Why This Matters

1. **Observability**: Clients that read CONFIG GET or INFO to verify configuration see stale/wrong values
2. **Drop-in parity**: Redis operators expect CONFIG SET maxclients/hz/timeout to take effect immediately
3. **Security**: The bind address defaulting to 0.0.0.0 while config says 127.0.0.1 is a security hole
4. **Testing**: Integration tests that change config at runtime get wrong behavior

## Scope

This epic covers wiring the following runtime-configurable parameters from CONFIG SET through to their actual enforcement points:
- maxclients (server connection limit)
- hz (event loop frequency)
- client-query-buffer-limit / proto-max-bulk-len
- client-output-buffer-limit
- repl-backlog-size
- repl-timeout / repl-ping-replica-period
- busy-reply-threshold (lua-time-limit)
- maxmemory-samples
- bind address

## Architecture Note

The challenge is that CONFIG SET runs in fr-runtime, but enforcement happens in fr-server (connection acceptance, buffer limits, timeouts). We need a communication channel — either shared state on ServerState that fr-server reads each tick, or a config-change event queue.

## Evidence of the problem

INFO hz/configured_hz was hardcoded to 10 (fixed in commit f8c5a8a). INFO maxclients was hardcoded to 10000 (fixed). But the actual enforcement in fr-server still uses compile-time MAX_CLIENTS=10000, QUERY_BUFFER_LIMIT=1MB, etc. CONFIG SET changes these in the config table but the server never reads the updated values.
close_reason: Epic acknowledged — children tasks now unblocked for implementation

title: XADD LIMIT 0 without trim strategy emits wrong error wording vs vendored 7.2.4
description:
fr's XADD reports `ERR syntax error, LIMIT cannot be used without specifying a trimming strategy` for `XADD ms LIMIT 0 * a 1`, but vendored Redis 7.2.4 reports `ERR syntax error, LIMIT cannot be used without the special ~ option`.

Root cause: upstream t_stream.c::streamParseAddOrTrimArgsOrReply at line 998 checks `if (args->limit && args->trim_strategy == TRIM_STRATEGY_NONE)`. The `args->limit` field is the LIMIT value, treated as truthy/falsy in C. When LIMIT 0 is provided with no MAXLEN/MINID, args->limit == 0 is falsy, so the first check is skipped, and execution falls through to line 1015 which checks `limit_given && !approx_trim` and emits the "without ~" message.

fr's check at lib.rs:6008 uses `limit_given` (a boolean flag), which fires regardless of whether the LIMIT value is 0 or positive. To match vendored's quirky behavior, the first check should gate on the LIMIT value being > 0 instead.

Probe sweep #59 (probe119.sh):
  vendored XADD ms LIMIT 0 '*' a 1 -> ERR syntax error, LIMIT cannot be used without the special ~ option
  fr        XADD ms LIMIT 0 '*' a 1 -> ERR syntax error, LIMIT cannot be used without specifying a trimming strategy
  vendored XADD ms LIMIT 5 '*' a 1 -> ERR ... without specifying a trimming strategy  (presumed; not directly probed but follows from C semantics)
  vendored XADD ms MAXLEN 5 LIMIT 0 '*' a 1 -> ERR ... without the special ~ option  (matches)

Fix plan:
- In fr-command/src/lib.rs near line 6008, change `limit_given` to `trim_limit.map_or(false, |n| n > 0)`. The second check at 6014 stays `limit_given && !trim_approx`, so LIMIT 0 with no strategy falls through to the "without ~" message just like vendored.
- Add regression test pinning all four combinations: (LIMIT 0, no strategy), (LIMIT 5, no strategy), (MAXLEN 5 LIMIT 0, no ~), (MAXLEN ~ 5 LIMIT 0, success).
close_reason: Fixed in iteration 92: Gated the 'without specifying a trimming strategy' check in XADD on trim_limit > 0 instead of limit_given, mirroring vendored t_stream.c:998's C-truthy-falsy quirk where LIMIT 0 skips the first check and falls through to 'without the special ~ option'. Live-diff: all 3 cases (LIMIT 0 no strat, LIMIT 5 no strat, MAXLEN 5 LIMIT 0 no ~) match vendored. Regression test xadd_limit_zero_without_strategy_matches_vendored_wording_kgxl7 pins all three. Full 1019-test suite passes.

title: Forward BGREWRITEAOF from dispatch_argv to runtime via Store flag
description:
# Forward BGREWRITEAOF from dispatch_argv to runtime via Store flag

## Problem
BGREWRITEAOF in fr-command returns the success message without actually writing. The runtime has the real implementation. Lua redis.call(\"BGREWRITEAOF\") is a no-op.

## Design
Add a pub bgrewriteaof_requested: bool flag to Store. When fr-command's bgrewriteaof_cmd is called, set this flag to true and return the success message. The runtime checks this flag after each dispatch and performs the actual AOF rewrite.

## Implementation
1. Add pub bgrewriteaof_requested: bool to Store (default false)
2. fr-command bgrewriteaof_cmd sets store.bgrewriteaof_requested = true
3. Runtime checks store.bgrewriteaof_requested after execute_frame and calls persist_snapshot_to_disk
4. Clear the flag after handling

## Why this pattern
Same as DEBUG RELOAD — the fr-command layer can signal intent, the runtime fulfills it. This is the cleanest cross-layer communication without restructuring dispatch_argv's signature.

## Testing
- Integration test: verify AOF file is written when BGREWRITEAOF called from Lua
- Conformance: existing BGREWRITEAOF test should still pass

## Files to modify
- crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs (flag field)
- crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs (set flag)
- crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs (check flag, perform rewrite)
close_reason: Completed

title: Lua stdlib error wording: missing user_script:1: prefix, 'got no value' vs 'got nil'
description:
Systematic wording gap across fr's Lua stdlib builtins vs vendored Redis 7.2.4 (luaL_argerror et al):

1. Missing "user_script:1:" source-location prefix on bad-arg errors for: ipairs, next, pairs, table.concat, table.insert, table.remove. (Already present on select, format, char, random, tonumber.)

2. fr conflates two distinct upstream wordings:
   - "got no value" — when the argument is absent (luaL_checkany on missing slot).
   - "got nil"      — when the argument is an explicit LuaValue::Nil.

   fr's helpers default missing args to Nil and report "got nil" for both. Vendored distinguishes:
   - ipairs() vs ipairs(nil): "got no value" vs "got nil"
   - table.concat() vs table.concat(nil): "got no value" vs "got nil"

Affected handlers all use lua_bad_table_arg/lua_bad_number_arg or inline-coerced "args.first().cloned().unwrap_or(LuaValue::Nil)". The fix is to detect absence (args.len() < N) at the call site and emit "got no value" with the user_script:1: prefix.

Test plan:
  - Pin ipairs(), next(), pairs(), table.concat(), table.insert(), table.remove() raising "user_script:1: bad argument #1 to '<name>' (table expected, got no value)" with no arg.
  - Pin same with explicit nil arg raising "got nil" (with prefix).

title: [HIGH] GEOPOS/GEOHASH decimal-precision + GEOSEARCH/GEORADIUS error-wording drift
description: Surfaced by live_redis_core_geo_matches_runtime: GEOPOS coordinates truncated to 17 significant digits where upstream returns 20 (geopos_existing_members, geopos_multiple_mixed, geopos_negative_coords, georadius_withcoord, geosearch_fromlonlat_byradius_withcoord_withdist). GEOHASH one member differs at the last char ('sqc8b49rnys' vs upstream 'sqc8b49rny0') — likely a rounding-direction bug in our geohash encoder. GEOSEARCH missing center/shape returns descriptive error ('ERR exactly one of FROMMEMBER or FROMLONLAT must be provided') where upstream returns generic 'ERR wrong number of arguments' or 'ERR syntax error'. Fix: widen fr-store geo lat/lon f64 formatting to 20 digits, audit geohash rounding, and align GEOSEARCH arity-vs-semantic error precedence with upstream.

Mirrors upstream ae_*.c::aeApiName (per-platform compile-time selection). Extended existing info_server_reports_runtime_os_and_arch_bits test to also assert multiplexing_api matches the runtime detection — stable across all supported platforms. Affects RedisInsight performance dashboards and OS-specific tuning tools that branch on this string. fr-on-macOS reported 'epoll' before this fix despite mio actually using kqueue. All fr-command lib tests pass; clippy clean. Twenty-seventh concrete fix this session.

title: fr-command CONFIG HELP emits BulkString frames + diverges from vendored 7.2.4 wording (multi-pattern/parameter wording is from a newer Redis)
description:
REVIEW MODE Tick 14 finding via /modes-of-reasoning-project-analysis, third in the HELP-divergence cluster (after 0o9vo CLIENT and vtege COMMAND).

LOCATION (crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs lines 13591-13610):
  Ok(RespFrame::Array(Some(vec![
      hello_bulk('CONFIG <subcommand> [<arg> [value] [opt] ...]. Subcommands are:'),
      hello_bulk('GET <pattern> [<pattern> ...]'),
      hello_bulk('    Return configuration parameters matching the specified patterns.'),
      hello_bulk('SET <parameter> <value> [<parameter> <value> ...]'),
      hello_bulk('    Set configuration parameters to the specified values.'),
      hello_bulk('RESETSTAT'),
      hello_bulk('    Reset statistics reported by INFO.'),
      hello_bulk('REWRITE'),
      hello_bulk('    Rewrite the configuration file with the current in-memory settings.'),
      hello_bulk('HELP'),
      hello_bulk('    Print this help.'),
  ])))

UPSTREAM (legacy_redis_code/redis/src/config.c::configHelpCommand line 3368, vendored Redis 7.2.4):

  void configHelpCommand(client *c) {
      const char *help[] = {
        'GET <pattern>',
        '    Return parameters matching the glob-like <pattern> and their values.',
        'SET <directive> <value>',
        '    Set the configuration <directive> to <value>.',
        'RESETSTAT',
        '    Reset statistics reported by the INFO command.',
        'REWRITE',
        '    Rewrite the configuration file.',
        NULL
      };
      addReplyHelp(c, help);
  }

Two divergences:

1. Frame type: upstream uses addReplyStatus (SimpleString); fr emits BulkString — same pattern as 0o9vo and vtege.

2. Content: fr uses 'multi-pattern' wording ('GET <pattern> [<pattern> ...]', 'SET <parameter> <value> [<parameter> <value> ...]') reflecting a NEWER Redis. The vendored 7.2.4 source uses single-pattern wording ('GET <pattern>', 'SET <directive> <value>') and 'directive' not 'parameter'. fr also says 'INFO' but upstream says 'the INFO command', and 'the configuration file with the current in-memory settings' but upstream says 'the configuration file'. Note: the underlying CONFIG GET / SET commands DO accept multi-args at runtime in 7.2.4; only the HELP text is the simpler form.

Severity: [LOW]. Affects HELP-scraping tools (RedisInsight, redis-cli tab completion, redis-doctor scripts) that may surface fr's wording as 'authentic upstream' when it isn't.

Reproducer:
  redis-cli> CONFIG HELP
  upstream 7.2.4: 'GET <pattern>' + simpler descriptions
  fr (current): 'GET <pattern> [<pattern> ...]' + longer descriptions, all in BulkString frames

Fix:
1. Replace hello_bulk with hello_simple.
2. Use upstream 7.2.4 wording verbatim (single-pattern, single-directive, shorter descriptions, 'directive' instead of 'parameter').
close_reason: fr-command CONFIG HELP rewritten to mirror vendored Redis 7.2.4 config.c::configHelpCommand line-for-line. Two divergences fixed: (1) frame type — every entry now SimpleString via hello_simple() instead of BulkString, matching upstream addReplyStatus and aligning with FUNCTION/OBJECT/CLIENT/COMMAND HELP; (2) content — fr was using multi-pattern wording ('GET <pattern> [<pattern> ...]', 'SET <parameter> <value> [<parameter> <value> ...]') reflecting a NEWER Redis. Vendored 7.2.4 source uses single-pattern wording ('GET <pattern>', 'SET <directive> <value>') with 'directive' not 'parameter', and shorter descriptions ('the INFO command' not 'INFO', 'the configuration file' not 'the configuration file with the current in-memory settings'). The underlying CONFIG GET/SET commands accept multi-args at runtime in 7.2.4; only the HELP text is the simpler form. New regression test config_help_matches_vendored_724_wording_with_simple_string_frames pins SimpleString throughout, header verbatim, all upstream-7.2.4 wordings present, the OLD multi-arg wording NOT present, trailing HELP block. Adjacent siblings still flagged for follow-up: XGROUP HELP (line 7058), XINFO HELP (7220). All fr-command lib tests pass; clippy clean. Twenty-second concrete fix this session, third HELP fix in cluster (after 0o9vo / vtege).

title: fr-runtime WAIT does not reject replica instances; upstream rejects with the canonical 'WAIT cannot be used with replica instances...' error
description:
Differential probe vs upstream Redis 7.2.4 replication.c::waitCommand:3534-3537:

  if (server.masterhost) {
      addReplyError(c,'WAIT cannot be used with replica instances. Please also note that since Redis 4.0 if a replica is configured to be writable (which is not the default) writes to replicas are just local and are not propagated.');
      return;
  }

This rejects WAIT when the server is itself a replica (masterhost is set after REPLICAOF host port → handshake). The matching check exists in fr-runtime for WAITAOF at lib.rs:11027-11034 but is MISSING for WAIT at lib.rs:10962-10995.

Concrete repro:
  > REPLICAOF master:6379  (handshake completes; this server is now a replica)
  > WAIT 0 100
  fr returns:  :0   (silently evaluates and returns 0 acked)
  upstream:    (error) ERR WAIT cannot be used with replica instances. Please also note that since Redis 4.0 if a replica is configured to be writable (which is not the default) writes to replicas are just local and are not propagated.

This matters because clients of replica instances rely on the canonical error to detect they're talking to a non-master, rather than getting a meaningless 0-replicas-acked Integer.

Fix: in handle_wait_command, after the arity check, test
  matches!(self.server.replication_runtime_state.role, ReplicationRoleState::Replica { .. })
and return the canonical error frame. Mirrors the existing WAITAOF check at lib.rs:11027-11034.

Acceptance: a unit test puts the runtime in Replica role state, asserts WAIT 0 100 returns the canonical error. Master-state WAIT calls keep working. Existing WAIT tests stay green; clippy clean.
close_reason: done

title: collectgarbage error wording fails pcall(C-builtin) anonymous shape
description:
Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4:

  pcall(collectgarbage,'invalid')
    vendored: bad argument #1 to '?' (invalid option 'invalid')
    fr:       user_script:1: bad argument #1 to 'collectgarbage' (invalid option 'invalid')

Same dual-shape pattern. The `collectgarbage` handler in fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs at line ~6693 hardcodes "user_script:1: bad argument #1 to 'collectgarbage'" in both the string-expected and invalid-option branches. Route through lua_format_argerror with name "collectgarbage" so pcall(collectgarbage,'invalid') renders as `bad argument #1 to '?' (invalid option 'invalid')`.

Direct call shape unchanged: `user_script:1: bad argument #1 to 'collectgarbage' (...)`.
close_reason: Routed collectgarbage's string-expected and invalid-option error branches through lua_format_argerror. Regression test collectgarbage_error_pcall_shape_kx6jm pins 4 pcall cases + direct-call + valid-option sanity. Live-diff vs vendored 16380: 4/4 pcall + 1/1 direct all match. Existing lua_base_lib_parity_uyj7c test still passes.

title: fr-runtime: CONFIG SET maxmemory rejects suffix forms (100mb, 1gb) that upstream accepts
description:
fr-runtime's CONFIG SET maxmemory at crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs:7123 only accepts pure integers via parse_i64_arg. Upstream Redis 7.2 (config.c::numericParseString → util.c::memtoull) accepts suffix forms like '100mb', '1gb', '500k', '2gb', '512' (no suffix means bytes). The same suffix semantics apply to other memory-typed configs (repl-backlog-size, client-output-buffer-limit, proto-max-bulk-len, etc.).

Acceptance: 'CONFIG SET maxmemory 100mb' → OK on a non-cluster instance, matching vendored redis 7.2.4. Internally stores 100*1024*1024 = 104857600 bytes. Other suffix forms supported: '100kb', '100mb', '100gb' (1024-base); '100k', '100m', '100g' (1000-base); '100b' or '100' (bytes). Also CONFIG GET maxmemory should return the canonical decimal-byte form (matching upstream which always returns the integer byte count).

Reference: ad-hoc differential probe of fr (port 16700) vs vendored Redis 7.2.4 (port 16701).
  CONFIG SET maxmemory 100mb — fr 'ERR ...argument must be a memory value', redis 'OK'.

Fix: add a memtoull-equivalent helper at fr-runtime (or fr-config), use it for the maxmemory branch (CONFIG SET path at line 7123 + line 17504). Apply same helper to other memory-typed configs as follow-up.

title: Implement ACL SAVE/LOAD file persistence
description:
# Implement ACL SAVE/LOAD file persistence

## What
ACL SAVE and ACL LOAD currently return OK without persisting. They should:
- ACL SAVE: serialize all ACL user rules to the ACL file (path from config)
- ACL LOAD: read and apply ACL rules from the ACL file, replacing current rules

## Design
Redis stores ACL rules in a text file, one user per line, in the same format as ACL SETUSER:
```
user default on ~* &* +@all
user admin on >password ~* &* +@all
user readonly on >pass ~* &* +@read -@write
```

## Implementation plan
1. Add pub acl_file_path: Option<PathBuf> to Runtime's ServerState (configured via CONFIG SET aclfile)
2. Add fn serialize_acl_rules(&self) -> String to AuthState that outputs one line per user
3. Add fn load_acl_rules(&mut self, content: &str) -> Result<(), String> to AuthState that parses lines
4. ACL SAVE: write serialize_acl_rules() to acl_file_path atomically (temp file + rename)
5. ACL LOAD: read acl_file_path, call load_acl_rules(), return OK or error
6. If acl_file_path is None, return "ERR There is no configured ACL file" (matches Redis)

## Serialization format
Each user line: `user <name> <on|off> [>password...] [~pattern...] [&pattern...] [+command|-command|+@cat|-@cat|allcommands|nocommands|allkeys|allchannels|reset]`

## Key details
- ACL LOAD replaces ALL users except "default" (which is always reset first then reconfigured)
- If parsing fails on any line, ACL LOAD rolls back to previous state and returns error
- Atomic file writes: write to .tmp then rename (already established pattern in fr-persist)

## Testing
- Unit test: serialize → load round-trip preserves all user rules
- Unit test: ACL LOAD with invalid content returns error and preserves previous state
- Conformance: ACL SAVE + ACL LOAD round-trip test

## Files to modify
- crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs (handle_acl_command, AuthState methods)
- crates/fr-config/src/lib.rs (if acl_file_path config is needed)
close_reason: Completed

Sibling defect to frankenredis-mdpbc (MOVE/COPY) and frankenredis-8qgk7 (SELECT). Add the check after the argv.len() != 3 arity gate and before parsing db indices, mirroring upstream's order.

FrankenRedis exposes unixsocket as a static CONFIG parameter and currently accepts CONFIG SET through the static fallback. title: fr-command DEBUG missing ERROR / LOG / LEAK subcommands; DEBUG ERROR is needed by client error-path tests and tooling
description:
Differential probe vs upstream Redis 7.2.4 debug.c::debugCommand:

- DEBUG ERROR <string> at debug.c:871-877 — emits the caller's <string> as a raw RESP error frame (with newlines mapped to spaces to keep protocol valid). Used by client integration tests and benchmark suites to exercise the error-handling path without needing to provoke real failures.
- DEBUG LOG <message> at debug.c:529-531 — writes <message> to the server log at LL_WARNING and returns OK.
- DEBUG LEAK <string> at debug.c:532-534 — leaks an sds copy of <string> (no observable client behavior beyond OK; we can accept-and-OK without actually leaking).

fr-command's debug_cmd at crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:17072 handles SLEEP, SET-ACTIVE-EXPIRE, JMAP, RELOAD, OBJECT, DIGEST, DIGEST-VALUE, POPULATE, HELP, PANIC/SEGFAULT, OOM, CHANGE-REPL-ID, STRINGMATCH-LEN, STRINGMATCH-TEST, QUICKLIST-PACKED-THRESHOLD — but ERROR / LOG / LEAK are absent; they fall through to the catch-all 'ERR unknown subcommand or wrong number of arguments for ERROR. Try DEBUG HELP.' branch.

Concrete repro:
  > DEBUG ERROR foo
  fr returns:  (error) ERR unknown subcommand or wrong number of arguments for 'ERROR'. Try DEBUG HELP.
  upstream:    (error) foo

  > DEBUG LOG hello
  fr returns:  (error) ERR unknown subcommand or wrong number of arguments for 'LOG'. Try DEBUG HELP.
  upstream:    OK

Fix: add ERROR/LOG/LEAK subcommand arms to debug_cmd. ERROR returns RespFrame::Error with newlines mapped to spaces; LOG and LEAK accept-and-OK. Update DEBUG HELP to advertise ERROR (LOG/LEAK can stay unadvertised — upstream's HELP text doesn't list LEAK either, only LOG; let's match upstream HELP exactly).

Acceptance: scripted DEBUG ERROR foo returns an error frame with message 'foo'; DEBUG LOG hello returns OK; DEBUG LEAK x returns OK. fr-command tests stay green; clippy clean.
close_reason: done

title: Lua assert lacks user_script:1: prefix; xpcall doesn't catch handler errors
description:
Two divergences in fr's Lua sandbox vs vendored Redis 7.2.4:

1. assert() error message lacks "user_script:1:" source-location prefix.
   Upstream lbaselib.c::luaB_assert uses luaL_error which prepends it.
   EVAL "local ok,err=pcall(function() assert(false, 'failed') end) return tostring(ok)..':'..tostring(err)" 0
     vendored: false:user_script:1: failed
     fr:       false:failed
   EVAL "local ok,err=pcall(function() assert(false) end) return tostring(ok)..':'..tostring(err)" 0
     vendored: false:user_script:1: assertion failed!
     fr:       false:assertion failed!

2. xpcall: when the message handler itself raises an error, vendored
   returns "false, 'error in error handling'" (the LUA_ERRERR result
   from lua_pcall). fr returns the original error value, silently
   swallowing the handler failure.
   EVAL "local ok, err = xpcall(function() error('a') end, function() error('b') end) return tostring(ok)..':'..tostring(err)" 0
     vendored: false:error in error handling
     fr:       false:user_script:1: a

Test plan:
  - Pin assert(false, msg) and assert(false) raise with user_script:1: prefix.
  - Pin xpcall where handler errors returns (false, 'error in error handling').
  - Pin xpcall where handler succeeds returns (false, handler_result) unchanged.

title: fr-command/tests/golden: 2 stale .actual files committed pre-gitignore — duplicate .golden siblings, mask next test failure
description:
/testing-golden-artifacts audit:

  git ls-files | grep '\.actual$'
    crates/fr-command/tests/golden/migrate_basic.actual
    crates/fr-command/tests/golden/migrate_invalid_port.actual

  cat .gitignore | grep actual
    **/tests/golden/*.actual

The .actual files are tracked even though the gitignore rule excludes them. Diffing each against its .golden sibling shows they are byte-identical — leftover snapshots from a 2026-04-18 test run that got committed before the gitignore rule was added.

Three concrete problems:

1. Repo noise — ~700 bytes of dead duplicate snapshots in every clone.
2. Inconsistent visible state — newcomers see migrate_*.actual checked in but not the other 30+ golden tests, suggesting (incorrectly) that some special semantics applies.
3. Failure-masking risk: if a future change makes the test fail, the runner writes a new .actual to the same path. Because the file is tracked, git diff/CI checks would surface 'changed file' rather than 'new file' — making an actual test failure look like an intentional snapshot update.

Fix: git rm both files. The gitignore rule already prevents re-adds.
close_reason: git rm'd both stale tracked .actual files. The .gitignore rule already prevents re-adds; running 'cargo test -p fr-command --test golden -- migrate' (5 passed) does not regenerate either file, confirming they were dead artifacts. Test surface unchanged.

title: Add conformance audit that flags dormant fixture files and planned live-oracle hooks
description:
Evidence: a simple fixture-vs-matrix diff still finds dormant files on disk that are not referenced by crates/fr-conformance/fixtures/live_oracle_matrix.json, and crates/fr-conformance/fixtures/user_workflow_corpus_v1.json still contains planned differential hooks that can silently drift from the actual harness. That gap is why FR-P2C-001, FR-P2C-006, and core_replication sat unwired without a failing test.

Need: add a harness-level audit test that scans the fixture directory, the live_oracle_matrix, and the user_workflow corpus, then fails when command/protocol/replay fixtures are present on disk without either matrix coverage or an explicit allowlist/exemption, and when workflow differential hooks stay planned without an explanatory exemption.

Real impl path: crates/fr-conformance/src/lib.rs plus crates/fr-conformance/fixtures/live_oracle_matrix.json and, if needed, a small explicit exemption manifest under crates/fr-conformance/fixtures/.
close_reason: Completed

title: fr-command GEO unit-error wording lowercases unit names — upstream emits 'use M, KM, FT, MI' (uppercase)
description:
/porting-to-rust differential probe vs vendored Redis 7.2.4:

  GEOSEARCH geo FROMMEMBER Palermo BYRADIUS 100 BOGUS ASC
    upstream: ERR unsupported unit provided. please use M, KM, FT, MI
    fr      : ERR unsupported unit provided. please use m, km, ft, mi

Source: fr-command emits the unit names in lowercase. Upstream geo.c (extractUnitOrReply) emits them uppercase: 'M, KM, FT, MI'. Likely affects GEORADIUS / GEORADIUSBYMEMBER / GEORADIUS_RO / GEORADIUSBYMEMBER_RO / GEOSEARCH / GEOSEARCHSTORE / GEODIST — every command that takes a unit argument.

Fix: locate the bad unit-error string and flip to uppercase. The error wording is the only divergence; the validation logic itself is correct (fr accepts the same uppercase + lowercase inputs as upstream).

Filed alongside a separate observation: GEOHASH on the same Palermo coord differs in the last char (upstream sqc8b49rny0 vs fr sqc8b49rnys) — that's an encoding-correctness bug, separate scope.
close_reason: Bulk-replaced 7 fr-command sites emitting 'ERR unsupported unit provided. please use m, km, ft, mi' (lowercase) with the upstream 'M, KM, FT, MI' (uppercase) wording. Affects GEORADIUS / GEORADIUSBYMEMBER / GEORADIUS_RO / GEORADIUSBYMEMBER_RO / GEOSEARCH / GEOSEARCHSTORE error paths. New geo_unit_errors_use_uppercase_unit_names_across_family test seeds Palermo and pins the wording across 3 commands (GEOSEARCH, GEORADIUSBYMEMBER, GEORADIUS). 12 existing geo tests still pass; clippy clean. The pre-existing GEODIST test at line 23590 already had the correct uppercase wording — it's where the canonical form was first captured.

title: fr-command SCRIPT DEBUG error wording diverges from upstream — uses 'YES, SYNC or NO' instead of 'YES/SYNC/NO'
description:
Tick finding via /testing-conformance-harnesses sweep on SCRIPT subcommand error wording vs vendored eval.c::scriptCommand.

UPSTREAM (eval.c line 645):
  addReplyError(c,"Use SCRIPT DEBUG YES/SYNC/NO");

FR CURRENT (crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs line 16869):
  CommandError::Custom("ERR Use SCRIPT DEBUG YES, SYNC or NO".to_string())

The wording is invented — upstream uses slash-separated mode names ('YES/SYNC/NO'), fr uses comma + ' or ' grammar ('YES, SYNC or NO'). Both convey the same set of valid modes, but a client suite scraping for the upstream wording would mis-categorize fr's reply.

Severity: [LOW]. Wire-format conformance gap. Visible whenever SCRIPT DEBUG is called with a mode other than YES/SYNC/NO.

FIX:
  Replace the fr wording with the upstream verbatim 'Use SCRIPT DEBUG YES/SYNC/NO'.
close_reason:
Replaced fr's invented SCRIPT DEBUG error wording with upstream's verbatim string.

CHANGE:
- crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs::script_cmd DEBUG branch: 'YES, SYNC or NO' → 'YES/SYNC/NO' (matches eval.c line 645).
- crates/fr-command/tests/script_debug_test.rs:28: updated expected wording.
- crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:34816 (in-test assertion): updated expected wording.

Verification:
- cargo test -p fr-command --lib 'script' → 43/43 pass.
- cargo test -p fr-command --test script_debug_test → 1/1 pass.
- cargo clippy -p fr-command --lib --no-deps -- -D warnings → clean.

[LOW] severity. Wire-format conformance gap.

title: fresh SET under LFU policy seeds OBJECT FREQ at 0 instead of upstream LFU_INIT_VAL=5
description:
Upstream evict.h::LFU_INIT_VAL = 5 seeds the LFU access counter on newly-created objects so they have a chance to compete against established keys before being targeted by an LFU eviction policy. fr's `set()` and `set_with_abs_expiry()` were returning 0 on the fresh-key branch (`map_or(0, ...)` when no existing entry), so a brand new SET under allkeys-lfu / volatile-lfu surfaced OBJECT FREQ = 0 instead of vendored 7.2.4's 5.

Repro vs vendored 7.2.4:
  CONFIG SET maxmemory-policy allkeys-lfu
  SET k v
  OBJECT FREQ k
    16380> 5
    16381 (pre-fix)> 0

Fix: introduce a `LFU_INIT_VAL = 5` constant in fr-store, switch the fresh-key branch in set() / set_with_abs_expiry() to `map_or(LFU_INIT_VAL, ...)`, and seed Entry::new with the same constant so RPUSH / SADD / ZADD / HSET / XADD first-write paths also start at 5.

Note: upstream's logarithmic counter-increment (LFULogIncr in evict.c) still differs from fr's naive +1 — vendored returns 7 after 50 GETs, fr would return 55. That's a separate defect not addressed here.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Under maxmemory-policy=allkeys-lfu, SET k v then OBJECT FREQ k returns 5 (matches vendored). 2. Same for volatile-lfu policy. 3. SET overwriting an existing key under LFU continues to bump the counter by 1. 4. Under non-LFU policies, OBJECT FREQ k errors out as before (no regression). 5. Fresh RPUSH / SADD / ZADD / HSET / XADD entries also seed at 5 via Entry::new.

title: LFU access counter increments naively by 1 instead of upstream's logarithmic LFULogIncr
description:
Upstream evict.c::LFULogIncr is a probabilistic logarithmic counter: starting from LFU_INIT_VAL=5, the counter only advances when `random_double < 1.0 / ((counter - LFU_INIT_VAL) * lfu-log-factor + 1)`. With the default lfu-log-factor=10 this saturates near 7-8 after 50 GETs and only crawls toward 255 over millions of accesses. fr was bumping `lfu_freq` by 1 per access, so the counter ran away linearly and OBJECT FREQ diverged from vendored 7.2.4 the moment a key got hot.

Repro vs vendored 7.2.4 (lfu-log-factor=10, key already at FREQ=5 from the lfuinit fix):
  for i in {1..50}; redis-cli -p PORT GET k; OBJECT FREQ k
    16380> 8         # vendored 7.2.4
    16381 (pre-fix)> 55
  for i in {1..1000}; redis-cli -p PORT GET k; OBJECT FREQ k
    16380> 19
    16381 (pre-fix)> 1005

Fix: introduce `lfu_log_factor: u64` on Store (default 10), wire CONFIG SET lfu-log-factor through fr-runtime via a new `next_lfu_log_factor` slot, and rewrite Entry::bump_lfu_freq to compute `baseval = counter - LFU_INIT_VAL`, `denom = baseval * log_factor + 1`, and only increment when `rand_sample % denom == 0`. The borrow-checker constraint forced each call site (get / exists / touch_key / touch) to compute decay_time + log_factor + rand_sample BEFORE acquiring a mutable borrow on the entry. The 64-bit LCG's uniformity over `% denom` is good enough to match vendored's saturation curve for review-grade probes.
acceptance_criteria: 1. After 50 GETs against a fresh LFU-policy key, OBJECT FREQ saturates in the 6-9 range (matches vendored 7.2.4's 7-8). 2. After 1000 GETs the counter sits in the low teens to twenties, not 1005. 3. CONFIG SET lfu-log-factor 0 makes every access advance the counter (denominator = 1). 4. CONFIG GET lfu-log-factor reflects the SET value. 5. The original LRU-policy access path remains unaffected (no regression in object_freq_tracks_lfu_accesses_and_preserves_lru_switch_behavior). 6. Decay-before-bump ordering is preserved (decay applies first, then log-incr).

title: Harden Lua unpack argument validation
close_reason: Completed

title: LINDEX parses index before type check, surfacing generic out-of-range instead of WRONGTYPE on wrong-type keys
description:
Upstream t_list.c::lindexCommand:594-600 calls lookupKeyReadOrReply(shared.null) and checkType(OBJ_LIST) BEFORE getLongFromObjectOrReply for the index. fr was already short-circuiting on missing key (returning Nil) but then parsing the index BEFORE the type check, so a non-numeric index on a wrong-type key surfaced 'ERR value is not an integer or out of range' instead of upstream's WRONGTYPE.

Repro vs vendored 7.2.4:
  SET k "hello"
  LINDEX k abc
    16380> WRONGTYPE Operation against a key holding the wrong kind of value
    16381 (pre-fix)> ERR value is not an integer or out of range

Companion to the recent frankenredis-lsetorder fix (LSET had the same precedence regression). Verified LRANGE / LTRIM already match upstream order — those parse their range args first by design (see t_list.c::lrangeCommand:879-889 / ltrimCommand:892-901). LREM / LPOP / RPOP already match because their non-key arg is a count parsed up-front by upstream too.

Fix: front-load a `store.key_type(key, now_ms)` probe in lindex(): None → BulkString nil, non-list → Store(WrongType), list → fall through to parse_i64_arg + store.lindex.
acceptance_criteria: 1. LINDEX on a string key with a non-numeric index returns 'WRONGTYPE Operation against a key holding the wrong kind of value' (matches vendored). 2. LINDEX on a missing key with any index continues to return nil (no regression). 3. LINDEX on a list key with a non-numeric index continues to surface 'ERR value is not an integer or out of range'. 4. LINDEX on a list key with a valid in-range index still returns the element value (no regression on the happy path).

title: fr-command OBJECT REFCOUNT always returns 1; upstream returns INT_MAX (2147483647) for shared integer objects in [0, 10000)
close_reason: fr-command OBJECT REFCOUNT now mirrors upstream tryObjectEncoding's shared-integer rule. New Store::object_refcount() returns INT_MAX (i32::MAX = 2147483647) when the value is a canonical decimal in [0, OBJ_SHARED_INTEGERS=10000) and shared interning is enabled (default maxmemory == 0 OR a non-LRU/LFU policy via new MaxmemoryPolicy::forbids_shared_integers helper). Otherwise returns 1. Returns None for missing keys. New regression test object_refcount_reports_int_max_for_shared_integer_objects pins five distinct cases: small int (5 → INT_MAX), boundary (9999 → INT_MAX), boundary (10000 → 1), negative (-1 → 1), LRU+maxmemory>0 disables sharing (7 → 1). Existing object_refcount_missing_key_returns_null_bulk still passes (its 'v' key is non-integer → 1). All fr-store 318 lib tests pass; clippy clean. The 4 pre-existing fr-command failures (xreadgroup/xinfo stream/migrate-vendored) reproduce on a clean stash and are untouched.

title: Lua cjson.decode does not combine UTF-16 surrogate pairs
description:
Vendored Redis 7.2.4's lua_cjson combines 😀 (UTF-16 high+low
surrogate pair) into the single codepoint U+1F600 (😀). fr's JSON
decoder treats each \uXXXX independently and produces nothing useful
for surrogates (returns the literal sequence as bytes).

  EVAL "return cjson.decode('\"\\uD83D\\uDE00\"')" 0
    vendored: 😀
    fr:       😀 (literal bytes — invalid as UTF-8)

Fix locus: crates/fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs — the json_to_lua_value
'u' escape branch around line 8510. When the parsed codepoint is in
the high-surrogate range D800..=DBFF, look ahead for a low-surrogate
\uDC00..=DFFF and combine via:
  cp = 0x10000 + ((high - 0xD800) << 10) + (low - 0xDC00)

Test plan: pin 😀 → U+1F600 (4 UTF-8 bytes), and pin a lone
high surrogate without follow-up (vendored emits Latin-1 fallback or
errors — verify the upstream behavior).
close_reason: json_to_lua_value's \u-escape branch in crates/fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs now combines UTF-16 surrogate pairs. When the parsed codepoint is a high surrogate (D800..=DBFF), it peeks for a following \u-escaped low surrogate (DC00..=DFFF) and combines via cp=0x10000+((high-0xD800)<<10)+(low-0xDC00). U+1F600 (😀, D83D+DE00) now decodes as the 4-byte UTF-8 sequence F0 9F 98 80; U+10000 (D800+DC00) decodes as F0 90 80 80. Lone surrogates and the normal \u-escape path retain the existing fallback. Regression test lua_cjson_decodes_utf16_surrogate_pair_ljxmd pins both supplementary-plane examples plus the basic \u0041 escape. Live diff vs vendored Redis 7.2.4 on :16380 verified byte-exact.

title: fr-command: small CLIENT KILL error wording divergences
description:
Three small CLIENT KILL wording divergences from upstream:
  - CLIENT KILL ID abc: fr 'ERR Invalid client ID', redis 'ERR client-id should be greater than 0'
  - CLIENT KILL LADDR 1.1.1.1:1: fr 'ERR syntax error', redis returns 0 (no clients matched)
  - CLIENT KILL USER baduser: fr returns 0, redis 'ERR No such user 'baduser''
  - CLIENT KILL SKIPME BAD: fr 'ERR argument must be 'yes' or 'no'', redis 'ERR syntax error'

Cosmetic but visible to clients. Reference: ad-hoc differential probe of fr (port 16700) vs vendored redis 7.2.4 (port 16701).

title: fr-store: list encoding flips to quicklist at 129 entries instead of by byte size, diverges from upstream listpack semantics
description:
Differential probe of fr (port 16700) vs vendored redis 7.2.4 (port 16701) shows OBJECT ENCODING for lists flips to 'quicklist' once the count exceeds 128, regardless of byte size:

  RPUSH l v1..v128 → both: listpack
  RPUSH l v1..v129 → upstream: listpack    fr: quicklist  ← divergence
  RPUSH l v1..v200 → upstream: listpack    fr: quicklist
  RPUSH l v1..v1000 → upstream: listpack   fr: quicklist

Upstream Redis 7.2's t_list.c::listTypeTryConversion converts a single-listpack list to a quicklist purely on the byte budget (server.list_max_listpack_size; default -2 = 8 KiB). There is no upstream entries-count config — `list-max-listpack-entries` is an fr-only invention.

Root cause at crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs:3005-3013, object_encoding for Value::List adds an entries-count gate alongside the byte-size gate:

  if self.list_fits_legacy_listpack_size(l)
      && l.len() <= self.list_max_listpack_entries  // <- spurious
      && l.iter().all(|v| v.len() <= self.list_max_listpack_value)

The count check forces 'quicklist' on lists with 129+ small elements that would still report 'listpack' on upstream (same configured list-max-listpack-size).

Fix: drop the entries-count check; rely solely on byte budget via list_fits_legacy_listpack_size. The fr-only `list-max-listpack-entries` config can stay readable/writable for now (no effect on encoding) to avoid breaking CONFIG SET callers; future cleanup may remove it from CONFIG_STATIC_PARAMS as well.

Acceptance: 200-element list of single-char values reports OBJECT ENCODING == "listpack" on fr, matching upstream byte-for-byte. Existing test object_encoding_list_honors_legacy_listpack_byte_limit continues to pass.

Reference: differential probe of fr (port 16700) vs vendored redis 7.2.4 (port 16701) on 2026-05-04.
notes:
Removed the entries-count gate from object_encoding's list branch (crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs); list-listpack/quicklist conversion now relies solely on list_fits_legacy_listpack_size (byte budget) and list_max_listpack_value (per-element cap), matching upstream t_list.c::listTypeTryConversion. The fr-only list-max-listpack-entries config remains read/write through CONFIG SET as a benign no-op to avoid breaking existing callers.

Differential probe vs vendored redis 7.2.4 confirms 200 single-char elements now report 'listpack' on fr (was 'quicklist'). Existing test object_encoding_list_honors_legacy_listpack_byte_limit still passes; encoding_matrix test renamed/refactored to list_encoding_quicklist_one_past_threshold_bytes since the count-based threshold no longer exists. Added object_encoding_list_keeps_listpack_above_legacy_entry_count to pin both the no-op behavior of list_max_listpack_entries and the byte-budget flip.

title: Reconcile replication risk docs with current runtime baseline
close_reason: Reconciled replication risk docs with current runtime baseline

title: lua_eval: error on function decl when nested table path missing
close_reason: Return Lua error when function decl indexes missing/non-table path; added test

title: LOLWUT eagerly validates non-VERSION args, surfacing errors vendored 7.2.4 never raises
description:
Upstream lolwut.c::lolwutCommand only enters the VERSION branch when `argc >= 3 && argv[1] is "version"`. Everything else (including `LOLWUT VERSION` with no trailing value, `LOLWUT BOGUS`, and `LOLWUT 5`) routes through the unstable render fn for vendored 7.2.x, which ignores every remaining arg and just emits 'Redis ver. <REDIS_VERSION>\n'. fr was eagerly parsing every trailing arg as i64 and rejecting `LOLWUT VERSION` (no value) with WrongArity.

Repros vs vendored 7.2.4 (modulo intentional version-string + ASCII-art deltas):
  LOLWUT VERSION  →  16380: ok       16381 (pre-fix): ERR wrong number of arguments
  LOLWUT BOGUS    →  16380: ok       16381 (pre-fix): ERR value is not an integer or out of range
  LOLWUT VERSION 5 BOGUS  →  16380: ok (BOGUS passed to render fn)  16381 (pre-fix): ERR value is not an integer

Fix: gate the VERSION integer parse on `argv.len() >= 3 && argv[1] == "version"` and remove the unconditional trailing-arg validation. Non-numeric VERSION value still errors via parse_i64_arg, matching upstream's getLongFromObjectOrReply.
acceptance_criteria: 1. LOLWUT VERSION (no value) returns the version-string body without error. 2. LOLWUT BOGUS returns the version-string body without error. 3. LOLWUT VERSION 5 BOGUS returns the version-string body (trailing args ignored by unstable port). 4. LOLWUT VERSION abc still returns 'ERR value is not an integer or out of range'. 5. LOLWUT and LOLWUT VERSION 5 (no trailing) continue to return the version body.

title: Fsync persistence parent directory after atomic snapshot/AOF rename

title: redis.set_repl/redis.setresp error messages missing ERR prefix
description:
Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4:

  pcall(redis.set_repl)                    → 'ERR redis.set_repl() requires one argument.'
  fr:                                       'redis.set_repl() requires one argument.'   (missing ERR)
  pcall(redis.set_repl,99)                  → 'ERR Invalid replication flags. Use REPL_AOF, REPL_REPLICA, REPL_ALL or REPL_NONE.'
  fr:                                       'Invalid replication flags. Use REPL_AOF, REPL_REPLICA, REPL_ALL or REPL_NONE.'
  pcall(redis.setresp)                      → 'ERR redis.setresp() requires one argument.'
  fr:                                       'redis.setresp() requires one argument.'
  pcall(redis.setresp,'x')                  → 'ERR RESP version must be 2 or 3.'
  fr:                                       'RESP version must be 2 or 3.'
  pcall(redis.setresp,5)                    → 'ERR RESP version must be 2 or 3.'
  fr:                                       'RESP version must be 2 or 3.'

Same ERR-prefix pattern previously fixed in redis.log (frankenredis-r9f5y), redis.sha1hex (frankenredis-f5rgn), redis.acl_check_cmd (frankenredis-vqjp9). Upstream's luaPushError stamps "ERR " into the err-table body; the direct-call wrapper recognises the prefix via error_has_resp_code_prefix and does not double-add.

Fix in fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs at lines ~4442 and ~4474: prefix all 4 error strings with "ERR ".
close_reason: Added 'ERR ' prefix to all 4 error strings in redis.set_repl (arity + invalid flags) and redis.setresp (arity + invalid version) at lua_eval.rs lines ~4442 and ~4474. Existing tests (lua_redis_log_setresp_acl_check_cmd_validation_matches_upstream, eval_redis_set_repl_validates_arity_and_flags_like_redis, redis_set_repl_and_setresp_coerce_via_lua_tonumber_op1r0) still pass — they used .contains() or the wrapper-final form. New regression test redis_set_repl_and_setresp_errors_carry_err_prefix_lrrr4 pins all 5 pcall shapes. Live-diff vs vendored 16380: 5/5 match.

title: LATENCY DOCTOR no-latency-spike message diverges from upstream Pinky-and-the-Brain quote
description:
Differential probe vs vendored Redis 7.2.4 (16380):

  CONFIG SET latency-monitor-threshold 1
  LATENCY RESET
  LATENCY DOCTOR
    fr  : 'Dave, no latency issues detected.'
    ven : 'Dave, no latency spike was observed during the lifetime of this Redis instance, not in the slightest bit. I honestly think you ought to sit down calmly, take a stress pill, and think things over.'

Source: crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:18998 in latency_doctor_report. fr emits a short stub message; vendored emits the full 2001 A Space Odyssey reference (HAL → Dave) per latency.c::latencyCommand DOCTOR path.

Fix: replace the short stub with the full upstream string verbatim.
close_reason: Matched LATENCY DOCTOR enabled/no-spike message to vendored Redis 7.2 text and pinned the branch in fr-command tests

title: fr-command SORT_RO over-eagerly rejects STORE keyword in GET/BY pattern arguments, not just as a flag
close_reason:
Refactored SORT/SORT_RO to share a sort_generic helper with a readonly flag, mirroring upstream sort.c::sortCommandGeneric.

CHANGE (crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs):
- Renamed sort_cmd's body to sort_generic(argv, store, now_ms, readonly: bool, cmd_name: &'static str). sort_cmd is now a thin wrapper passing readonly=false / 'SORT'. sort_ro_cmd passes readonly=true / 'SORT_RO'.
- The STORE arm inside sort_generic is gated on `!readonly`, exactly mirroring upstream's `readonly == 0` guard at sort.c line 228. Under SORT_RO, a literal 'STORE' token in a GET/BY pattern slot is consumed as the pattern (correct), and a flag-position 'STORE' falls through to the trailing else and surfaces 'ERR syntax error' (also correct).
- Dropped the over-eager pre-scan in sort_ro_cmd that walked all of argv[2..] looking for 'STORE'; that scan rejected valid uses like `SORT_RO mylist GET STORE`.

Three-case regression test sort_ro_accepts_store_token_in_get_pattern_slot pins:
1. SORT_RO mylist GET STORE → 3-element array (STORE is the GET pattern, no '*' so all nil).
2. SORT_RO mylist BY STORE → 3-element original-order array (BY constant pattern → dontsort).
3. SORT_RO mylist STORE dest → Error('ERR syntax error') (STORE as a flag is unrecognised under readonly).

Verification:
- cargo test -p fr-command --lib sort → 18/18 pass (incl. existing sort_store / sort_ro_returns_sorted_list).
- cargo clippy -p fr-command --lib --no-deps -- -D warnings → clean.

[LOW] severity. Behavioral conformance gap. Visible only when a user happens to use the literal token 'STORE' as a GET/BY pattern under SORT_RO.

title: LSET parses index before lookup/type check, surfacing generic out-of-range instead of NoSuchKey/WRONGTYPE
description:
Upstream t_list.c::lsetCommand:624-630 calls lookupKeyWriteOrReply(shared.nokeyerr) and checkType(OBJ_LIST) BEFORE getLongFromObjectOrReply for the index. fr was parsing the index first via parse_i64_arg, so any non-numeric index short-circuited the command before the lookup ran.

Repros vs vendored 7.2.4:
  LSET missing abc v
    16380> ERR no such key
    16381 (pre-fix)> ERR value is not an integer or out of range
  SET s "hello"; LSET s abc v
    16380> WRONGTYPE Operation against a key holding the wrong kind of value
    16381 (pre-fix)> ERR value is not an integer or out of range

Fix: front-load a `store.key_type(key, now_ms)` probe in lset_cmd before the parse_i64_arg call. None → CommandError::NoSuchKey, non-list → Store(WrongType), list → fall through to existing parse + store.lset.
acceptance_criteria: 1. LSET on a missing key with a non-numeric index returns 'ERR no such key' (matches vendored). 2. LSET on a non-list key with a non-numeric index returns 'WRONGTYPE Operation against a key holding the wrong kind of value' (matches vendored). 3. LSET on a list key with a non-numeric index continues to surface 'ERR value is not an integer or out of range' (no regression on the parse-side wording). 4. LSET on a list key with a valid in-range index still mutates the list and returns OK (no regression on the happy path).
