title: fr-command: XPENDING IDLE-without-value + extra-args wording divergences
description:
Upstream t_stream.c::xpendingCommand accepts argc ∈ {3, 6, 7, 8, 9} and emits SyntaxError for any other shape. Trailing args past the canonical positions (consumer at startidx+3) are silently ignored.

fr-command:
- Strictly required argc ∈ {3, 6, 7, 8, 9} but emitted WrongArity for argc=4 (e.g. XPENDING st g IDLE) instead of SyntaxError.
- Required argc ∈ {6, 7} (no IDLE) / {8, 9} (with IDLE) so trailing args (XPENDING st g 0 + 10 c1 EXTRA, argc=8 no IDLE) emitted WrongArity instead of being silently ignored.

Differential probe vs vendored redis 7.2.4:
  XPENDING st g IDLE        → fr WrongArity, redis SyntaxError
  XPENDING st g 0 + 10 c1 EXTRA → fr WrongArity, redis empty
  XPENDING st g 0 + 10 c1 c2    → fr WrongArity, redis empty

Fix:
- Keep the WrongArity branch for argc < 3 (commands.def arity = -3).
- Allow any argc ∈ {3, 6, 7, 8, 9}; SyntaxError otherwise.
- Drop the 'argc != 6 && argc != 7' / 'argc != 8 && argc != 9' branches; treat anything past the canonical positions as silently-ignored.

title: COMMAND INFO MIGRATE missing 2nd keyspec (KEYS keyword) + delete flag on 1st
description: fr emits one keyspec for MIGRATE (RW + access at @3) but upstream commands.def declares 2: (1) index@3 RW|ACCESS|DELETE, KSPEC_FK_RANGE{0,1,0}; (2) keyword 'KEYS' startfrom=-2, RW|ACCESS|DELETE|INCOMPLETE, KSPEC_FK_RANGE{-1,1,0}. fr is missing the 'delete' flag on the first keyspec, and the entire second keyspec for the KEYS keyword form of MIGRATE. Fix: add 'migrate' to command_info_multi_keyspec using make_keyspec_index_range(['RW','access','delete'], 3, 0, 1) + make_keyspec_keyword_range(['RW','access','delete','incomplete'], 'KEYS', -2, -1, 1, 0). The 'incomplete' flag is novel for fr but should pass through as a SimpleString flag via the existing flag-array emission path. Acceptance: COMMAND INFO MIGRATE byte-exact with vendored.
close_reason: COMMAND INFO MIGRATE now emits both keyspecs matching upstream commands.def. Added 'migrate' to command_info_multi_keyspec at fr-command/src/lib.rs with: (1) make_keyspec_index_range(['RW','access','delete'], 3, 0, 1) -- the single positional key at argv[3] is read, sent over the wire, then dropped from the local instance on success; (2) make_keyspec_keyword_range(['RW','access','delete','incomplete'], 'KEYS', -2, -1, 1, 0) -- covers the multi-key form 'MIGRATE host port "" db timeout KEYS k1 k2 ...'. The 'incomplete' flag is novel for fr (mirrors upstream's CMD_KEY_INCOMPLETE bit) and passes through the existing flag-array emission path as a SimpleString. Live differential probe: MIGRATE now byte-exact with vendored 7.2.4 (zero-line diff). Tests: fr-command 910 pass.

title: GEORADIUS/GEOSEARCH FROMLONLAT silently accepts out-of-range lon/lat (no validation error)
description:
Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4:

  GEORADIUS geo -200 38 100 km
    vendored: ERR invalid longitude,latitude pair -200.000000,38.000000
    fr:       (empty array — silently no match)

  GEORADIUS geo 0 86 100 km
    vendored: ERR invalid longitude,latitude pair 0.000000,86.000000
    fr:       (empty array)

  GEOSEARCH/GEOSEARCHSTORE FROMLONLAT -200 38 BYRADIUS 100 km — same divergence
  GEORADIUS_RO — same divergence

GEOADD already rejects these correctly (probe confirmed). GEORADIUS/GEOSEARCH/GEOSEARCHSTORE use a different code path that skips the validation.

Boundaries observed in upstream:
  longitude:  |lon| <= 180.0
  latitude:   |lat| <= 85.05112878   (Redis's geohash polar bound)

Boundary inclusivity (vendored):
  lon=-180, lon=180 → accepted
  lon=-181, lon=181 → rejected
  lat=-85.05112878, lat=85.05112878 → accepted
  lat=-86, lat=86 → rejected

Wording: ERR invalid longitude,latitude pair %.6f,%.6f (six-decimal printf-%f format on both values).

Root cause: GEORADIUS / GEOSEARCH / GEOSEARCHSTORE skip lon/lat range validation when parsing FROMLONLAT or the GEORADIUS center, while GEOADD's path does validate via geo.c::extractLongLatOrReply. Upstream's helpers in src/geo.c call extractLongLatOrReply on every center, including GEORADIUS's positional lon/lat and GEOSEARCH's FROMLONLAT lon/lat.

Fix: add lon/lat range checks (in fr's parse_geo_f64 callsite for GEORADIUS, and in geosearchstore/geosearch FROMLONLAT parsing) with the exact ERR wording.
close_reason: Added validate_geo_center helper in fr-command/src/lib.rs (after geo_invalid_pair_error, ~line 3923) that checks |lon|<=180 and |lat|<=85.05112878 (using existing GEO_LONG_MIN/MAX, GEO_LAT_MIN/MAX constants) and returns the dedicated 'ERR invalid longitude,latitude pair %.6f,%.6f' error. Applied at three callsites: GEORADIUS center parse (line ~5152), GEOSEARCH FROMLONLAT (line ~5353), GEOSEARCHSTORE FROMLONLAT (line ~5599). GEORADIUS_RO inherits via the same georadius() dispatch. GEOADD already had its own check at line 4686. Regression test georadius_geosearch_reject_out_of_range_lonlat_nugoc covers 8 bad pairs × 4 commands (GEORADIUS, GEORADIUS_RO, GEOSEARCH FROMLONLAT, GEOSEARCHSTORE FROMLONLAT) plus 5 in-range boundary pairs as accept controls. Live diff vs vendored 7.2.4 matches on all 21 probed variants.

title: Move SLOWLOG state to Store so dispatch_argv path returns real data
description:
# Move SLOWLOG state to Store for Lua/dispatch_argv compatibility

## Problem
SLOWLOG GET/LEN/RESET in fr-command (the dispatch_argv path) returns empty arrays/0/OK because the slow log lives in Runtime's ServerState. Lua scripts calling redis.call(\"SLOWLOG\", \"GET\") get nothing.

## Design
Move the slowlog data structure from ServerState to Store:
- pub slowlog: VecDeque<SlowlogEntry>
- pub slowlog_id_counter: u64
- pub slowlog_log_slower_than_us: i64
- pub slowlog_max_len: usize

## Implementation
1. Define SlowlogEntry in fr-store (or move from fr-runtime):
   ```rust
   pub struct SlowlogEntry {
       pub id: u64,
       pub timestamp_sec: u64,
       pub duration_us: u64,
       pub argv: Vec<Vec<u8>>,
   }
   ```
2. Add the fields to Store
3. Add Store methods: record_slowlog(), get_slowlog(), slowlog_len(), reset_slowlog()
4. Update fr-command's slowlog_cmd to accept &mut Store and call these methods
5. Update fr-runtime to call store.record_slowlog() instead of server.record_slowlog()
6. Remove duplicate SlowlogEntry from runtime (or re-export from store)

## Why Store instead of Runtime
- Store is accessible from both dispatch_argv (Lua scripts, AOF replay) and the runtime
- The slow log is essentially per-database state, making Store the natural home
- This follows the same pattern we used for CONFIG GET/SET

## Migration path
- The runtime already has record_slowlog wired into execute_frame — just redirect to store
- slowlog_log_slower_than_us and slowlog_max_len are already CONFIG SET-able in runtime — redirect to store fields

## Testing
- Conformance: Lua script calls redis.call(\"SLOWLOG\", \"GET\") and gets real entries
- Unit test: record a slow command, verify SLOWLOG LEN returns 1

## Files to modify
- crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs (SlowlogEntry struct, Store fields, methods)
- crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs (slowlog_cmd accepts Store, reads real data)
- crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs (redirect slowlog recording to store)
close_reason: Completed

title: Lua math.random: wrong arg index, missing user_script prefix, accepts 3+ args
description:
Three semantic divergences in fr's math.random vs vendored Redis 7.2.4 (lmathlib.c::math_random):

1. math.random with m > n: vendored reports the empty interval on arg #2 (since the upper bound is what's wrong); fr reports #1.
     EVAL "return math.random(5, 1)" 0
       vendored: ERR user_script:1: bad argument #2 to 'random' (interval is empty)
       fr:       ERR bad argument #1 to 'random' (interval is empty)

2. math.random with 1 arg <= 0: vendored prefixes the error with the source location; fr emits the bare wording.
     EVAL "return math.random(0)" 0
       vendored: ERR user_script:1: bad argument #1 to 'random' (interval is empty)
       fr:       ERR bad argument #1 to 'random' (interval is empty)

3. math.random with 3+ args: vendored rejects via "wrong number of arguments"; fr silently ignores extras.
     EVAL "return math.random(1, 2, 3)" 0
       vendored: ERR user_script:1: wrong number of arguments
       fr:       1

Upstream behavior (lmathlib.c::math_random):
  switch (lua_gettop(L)) {
    case 0: float [0,1);
    case 1: int [1,u]; luaL_argcheck(L, 1<=u, 1, "interval is empty");
    case 2: int [l,u]; luaL_argcheck(L, l<=u, 2, "interval is empty");
    default: return luaL_error(L, "wrong number of arguments");
  }

Test plan:
  - Pin EVAL "return math.random(0)"      -> error with user_script:1: prefix, arg #1
  - Pin EVAL "return math.random(5, 1)"   -> error with user_script:1: prefix, arg #2
  - Pin EVAL "return math.random(1,2,3)"  -> error "user_script:1: wrong number of arguments"
  - Pin EVAL "return math.random(1, 10)" returns a number in [1,10]

title: [parity-gap] RESP3 null parser accepts non-empty payload
description:
Finding: fr-protocol accepts RESP3 Null frames with non-empty line payloads when ParserConfig.allow_resp3=true, e.g. `_payload\r\n` is parsed as BulkString(None). RESP3 Null is exactly `_\r\n`; accepting extra bytes weakens the fail-closed parser contract and lets malformed oracle/fuzz inputs pass as valid frames.

Repro steps before fix:
1. In crates/fr-protocol, call parse_frame_with_config(b"_payload\r\n", &ParserConfig { allow_resp3: true, ..ParserConfig::default() }).
2. Observe Ok(ParseResult { frame: BulkString(None), .. }) instead of an error.

Acceptance: reject non-empty RESP3 Null payloads, keep `_\r\n` accepted, and add regression coverage through the fr-protocol fuzz/parser test lane.
close_reason: Rejected malformed RESP3 Null lines with non-empty payloads and pinned regression in fr-protocol parser tests; validated with scoped fr-protocol test/check/clippy/fmt via rch.

title: fr-command: GEOPOS coordinate format diverges from upstream %.17Lg (16 vs 17 digits)
description:
GEOPOS emits coordinate doubles using Rust's f64 Display (Ryu shortest round-trip, typically 16 significant digits) where upstream Redis emits via addReplyHumanLongDouble formatted with %.17Lg (17 significant digits). Result: fr returns 0.9999999403953552 / 2.000000185646549 where Redis returns 0.99999994039535522 / 2.0000001856465488 for the same encoded geohash score. The actual lat/lng resolution is identical (same bit pattern decodes to the same point on the geoid), but the wire format differs in the trailing digit count.

Impact: low. Geo precision is dominated by the geohash bit budget (52 bits, ~0.6m at the equator), so the 17th digit is below noise. But this is a real wire-format divergence that breaks byte-equal diff tests.

Acceptance: GEOPOS / GEOSEARCH WITHCOORD format coordinate doubles with 17 significant digits in shortest-form (matches upstream %.17Lg). One reasonable Rust path: add a fr-command::format_redis_double(value: f64) -> String helper using a custom snprintf-style formatter, or use the printf crate. Conformance core_geo cases that currently XFAIL on GEOPOS lose the XFAIL.

title: fr-command: CLUSTER SETSLOT and FAILOVER FORCE+TAKEOVER not handled
description:
Two CLUSTER subcommand divergences from upstream Redis 7.2:

1. CLUSTER SETSLOT: any form returns 'ERR unknown subcommand SETSLOT'. Upstream accepts SETSLOT and (with cluster off) returns 'ERR This instance has cluster support disabled'. The four upstream forms are: 'SETSLOT <slot> STABLE', 'SETSLOT <slot> NODE <node-id>', 'SETSLOT <slot> MIGRATING <node-id>', 'SETSLOT <slot> IMPORTING <node-id>'.

2. CLUSTER FAILOVER FORCE TAKEOVER: fr-command::cluster_cmd::FAILOVER branch only accepts a single optional flag (FORCE or TAKEOVER, not both). Upstream accepts both modifiers together.

Acceptance: both cases return 'ERR This instance has cluster support disabled' on a non-cluster instance, matching vendored redis 7.2.4 byte-for-byte. Fix in fr-command::cluster_cmd: add a SETSLOT branch that validates argv shape then returns cluster_disabled_error when cluster is off; relax the FAILOVER branch arity check to allow FORCE+TAKEOVER together.
close_reason: Existing CLUSTER SETSLOT and FAILOVER handling verified; added integration coverage for standalone cluster-disabled parity.

title: math.random + 'attempt to call' errors keep user_script:1: in pcall(C-builtin) shape
description:
Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4:

  pcall(math.random,0)         v=bad argument #1 to '?' (interval is empty)
                                fr=user_script:1: bad argument #1 to 'random' (interval is empty)
  pcall(math.random,5,1)       v=bad argument #2 to '?' (interval is empty)
                                fr=user_script:1: bad argument #2 to 'random' (interval is empty)
  pcall(math.random,'x')       v=bad argument #1 to '?' (number expected, got string)
                                fr=user_script:1: bad argument #1 to 'random' (number expected)
  pcall(math.random,1,2,3)     v=wrong number of arguments
                                fr=user_script:1: wrong number of arguments
  pcall(math.pi)               v=attempt to call a number value
                                fr=user_script:1: attempt to call a number value

Three sites to fix in fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs:

1. `math.random` handler (~line 5629): 4 hardcoded errors (interval-empty ×2, number-expected ×2, wrong-arity ×1). Route the first 4 through lua_format_argerror with name "random"; the wrong-arity is luaL_error wording and needs a conditional-prefix helper. Also: number-expected error must include "got <type>" suffix.

2. `call_function` fallback errors (~line 4317): "attempt to call a TYPE value" for Nil/Thread/other types — keep the prefix only when caller is named (current_invocation_name is Some).

Same dual-shape pattern as previous iterations: pcall directly invoking the target erases the Lua-source frame in luaL_where, so `'?'` name and no prefix.
close_reason: Three sites converted to dual direct/pcall shape: math.random's 4 luaL_argerror calls now route through lua_format_argerror (also added 'got <type>' suffix to number-expected); math.random wrong-arity uses conditional-prefix helper; call_function fallback 'attempt to call a TYPE value' for Nil/Thread/other only adds user_script:1: prefix when current_invocation_name is Some. Regression test math_random_and_attempt_to_call_errors_pcall_shape_o3epl pins 10 pcall cases (4 math.random + 5 attempt-to-call + 1 wrong-arity) and 3 direct-call regressions. Live-diff vs vendored 16380: 9/9 scripted cases match. Existing tests math_random_arity_and_error_reporting_nwmly + math_random_does_not_panic_on_max_range + lua_attempt_to_call_errors_carry_accessor_context still pass.

title: Pre-existing fr-command stream test failures: xinfo_stream/xreadgroup/migrate_stream — 'entries-added' counter divergence and group-cursor advance
description:
Discovered while running cargo test --workspace during frankenredis-ja8yu shipping (2026-05-09). Four fr-command lib tests fail on main without any local edits:

  test tests::xinfo_stream_reports_live_deleted_id_and_radix_metrics ... FAILED
    crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:28858 - entries-added: actual Integer(3) vs expected Integer(2)
  test tests::xreadgroup_reads_new_entries_and_advances_group_cursor ... FAILED
    crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:27280
  test tests::xreadgroup_pending_replay_and_noack_behavior ... FAILED
    crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:27902
  test tests::migrate_stream_dump_payload_loads_in_vendored_redis_and_restores_back ... FAILED
    crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:54829

Root cause TBD — possibly a recent stream-side change (entries-added counter drifted to count XDEL'd entries) or test pin needs updating. The xinfo case shows fr emits entries-added=3 (matches XADD count) where the test expects 2 (XADD count minus deleted). Need to verify against vendored 7.2.4 XINFO STREAM whether the counter excludes XDEL'd entries.

If vendored matches Integer(3): just update the test pin.
If vendored matches Integer(2): fix the entries-added counter to decrement on XDEL.
close_reason:
Realigned 4 stale stream test pins to match vendored Redis 7.2.4 behavior:

(1) xinfo_stream_reports_live_deleted_id_and_radix_metrics — entries-added: 2 → 3. Vendored t_stream.c::streamCreateID() bumps s->entries_added unconditionally; XDEL only sets the SAMEID-tombstone bit and updates max_deleted_entry_id. fr was already emitting 3 (matching vendored); test pin asserted 2, which never matched either.

(2) xreadgroup_reads_new_entries_and_advances_group_cursor — entries-read: BulkString(None) → Integer(3), lag: BulkString(None) → Integer(0). When a stream has no XDEL'd entries that would invalidate the running counter, vendored t_stream.c::xinfoCommand emits both as integers tracking the consumed entries. Confirmed against live oracle at port 16380.

(3) xreadgroup_pending_replay_and_noack_behavior — same entries-read/lag fix for the no-XDEL scenario.

(4) migrate_stream_dump_payload_loads_in_vendored_redis_and_restores_back — xinfo_groups 5-tuple last field: None → Some(2). Vendored DUMP payload carries the group's entries_read counter; fr now reconstructs it during RESTORE instead of leaving it null.

All three fr behaviors that the stale pins were masking are correct — the test assertions were authored before the entries-added/entries-read counters were wired up and never updated when fr's implementation came into compliance. fr-command suite now passes 883/883 (was 879/883). Workspace clippy clean under -D warnings.

Discovered as side-issue: radix-tree-keys/radix-tree-nodes counters in XINFO STREAM differ by exactly 1 vs vendored after XDEL — fr emits 2/3 where vendored emits 1/2. Same length=2 so data is fine, but listpack-merging isn't aligned. Filed separately as frankenredis-2nl2f.

title: COMMAND INFO subcommand-container key_specs (xinfo/xgroup/object) and sort/sort_ro
description:
After frankenredis-imkpg + frankenredis-b0wme fixed multi-keyspec and movablekeys-empty cases, three subcommand-container commands and sort/sort_ro still emit empty (or under-specified) key_specs:

Subcommand-container fan-out — upstream emits one key_spec per subcommand:
- xinfo: ven=[RO,access,RO,access,RO,access] (3 specs: STREAM, CONSUMERS, GROUPS)
- xgroup: ven=[RW,delete,RW,delete,RW,update,RW,insert,RW,insert] (5 specs: DESTROY, DELCONSUMER, SETID, CREATE, CREATECONSUMER)
- object: ven=[RO,RO,RO,RO] (4 specs: ENCODING, FREQ, IDLETIME, REFCOUNT)

sort/sort_ro have begin_search type=unknown for the optional BY/GET/STORE keywords:
- sort: 3 specs ([RO,access] for input + 2 'unknown' specs for BY/GET, STORE notes)
- sort_ro: 2 specs ([RO,access] for input + 1 'unknown' spec for BY/GET notes)

These need:
1. A new helper to emit 'unknown' begin_search/find_keys (empty spec arrays)
2. Subcommand-aware key_specs emission for xinfo/xgroup/object that walks the parent's subcommand list and emits one keyspec per subcommand
close_reason: Added subcommand fan-out for xinfo/xgroup/object via SUBCOMMAND_TABLE filter, plus 3-spec SORT and 2-spec SORT_RO multi-keyspec with new make_keyspec_unknown_with_notes helper. Per-subcommand keyspec overrides for object|*/xgroup|* added. Two pin tests added; existing command|* test updated for new alphabetical order. Sort-block diff vs ven shows zero content divergence. Container-parent ACL category leak tracked in ywh2b. Commit b02a7a5.

title: fr-command WAIT silently accepts extra args (upstream arity is exact 3) and lacks the noscript guard (CMD_NOSCRIPT) so Lua scripts can call WAIT
description:
## Symptom
\`WAIT 0 0 extra\` via dispatch_argv accepts the extra arg silently and returns 0; upstream rejects with the table-level wrong-arity error (commands.def declares WAIT arity=3 exact).

\`redis.call('WAIT', 0, 0)\` from Lua via dispatch_argv returns 0; upstream blocks the call with 'This Redis command is not allowed from script' (CMD_NOSCRIPT flag set in commands.def line 10777-equivalent for WAIT — see legacy_redis_code/redis/src/commands.def).

## Differential probe
Upstream commands.def declares WAIT with arity=3 (exact, positive value) and the CMD_NOSCRIPT flag. Upstream replication.c::waitCommand at line 3529 doesn't need to re-check arity because the table-level arity check fires first; CMD_NOSCRIPT is enforced in the script context before the handler runs.

fr-command's wait_cmd at fr-command/src/lib.rs:15468-15486:
- Uses 'argv.len() < 3' (allows extras silently).
- Has no script_nesting_level guard (other admin/replication-adjacent handlers like WAITAOF, FAILOVER, CLUSTER subcommands all guard via 'if store.script_nesting_level >= 1 { return Err(script_noscript_command_error()); }').

## Fix
1. Change wait_cmd signature: '(argv: &[Vec<u8>]) → (argv, store: &Store)'.
2. Update dispatch_argv call site at lib.rs:1383 to pass store.
3. Add 'if store.script_nesting_level >= 1' guard mirroring WAITAOF's pattern at lib.rs:7843.
4. Tighten arity to 'argv.len() != 3' so trailing args trigger WrongArity.
5. Existing test wait_clamps_negative_numreplicas_and_uses_timeout_specific_errors continues to pass; add a new test wait_rejects_script_context_and_extra_args covering both new branches.

## Out of scope
- WAIT-on-replica check ('WAIT cannot be used with replica instances...') requires replication state which Store doesn't expose; fr-runtime would need its own check (and already returns 0 in standalone).
- The 0-replicas always-returns-0 stub is tracked separately under project_wait_commands_stub.
close_reason: fr-command WAIT now enforces argv.len() == 3 (was < 3, silently accepting extras) and guards on store.script_nesting_level >= 1 to mirror upstream's CMD_NOSCRIPT flag — same noscript-guard pattern WAITAOF and the cluster admin handlers already use. wait_cmd takes &Store now; dispatch_argv call site updated. New test wait_rejects_script_context_and_extra_args pins both branches: extra args → WrongArity('WAIT'), Lua context → SCRIPT_NOSCRIPT_ERROR.

title: fr-protocol parse_frame_internal: chained RESP3 attribute wrappers can stack-overflow the parser — xfxtd fix removed depth budget without adding a per-chain cap
description:
REVIEW MODE finding (cross-reviewing peer commit b658bf8 / xfxtd):

  --- a/crates/fr-protocol/src/lib.rs (before xfxtd)
  +++ b/crates/fr-protocol/src/lib.rs (after xfxtd)
       b'|' => {
  -        let (_attr, consumed) = parse_resp3_map(input, next, depth + 1, config)?;
  -        parse_frame_internal(input, consumed, depth + 1, config)
  +        let (_attr, consumed) = parse_resp3_map(input, next, depth, config)?;
  +        parse_frame_internal(input, consumed, depth, config)
       }

The motive was correct (attribute is a transparent prefix; double-bumping the depth was over-conservative — the test xfxtd shipped pins parsing 1-deep arrays under max_recursion_depth=1). But removing the bump entirely leaves NO upper bound on chained attribute prefixes.

Pathological input:
  '|1\\r\\n+k\\r\\n+v\\r\\n' × N + real-frame
Each '|' triggers a recursive parse_frame_internal call at the SAME depth (no budget consumed). The Rust call stack grows linearly with N. parse_frame_internal is a hot path on the server side handling untrusted client bytes.

Severity: MEDIUM as remote-DoS. With max_recursion_depth=128 (default) the depth check at line 205 fires for nested CONTAINERS but not for sequential attribute wrappers. Default 8 MiB Rust thread stack ÷ ~100 bytes/frame ≈ 80K nested-attr depth before stack overflow. With small frames (15-byte attribute prefix above) ~80K bytes of attacker input suffices to crash the parser.

Reachability: any wire-protocol entry point that parses RESP3 client input with allow_resp3=true. The default ParserConfig has allow_resp3=false, so the impact is gated on TLS/HELLO 3 paths; still reachable via HELLO 3 from any authenticated client.

Fix: introduce a separate attribute-chain counter that's bounded independently of the recursion depth. Bump it each time the '|' branch is taken; reject when exceeding a tight cap (8 — well above any legit pattern). Preserve the existing test (xfxtd's max_recursion_depth=1 case still passes since chain_depth bumps by 1 and starts at 0).
close_reason: Added RESP3_ATTRIBUTE_CHAIN_LIMIT = 8 — separate cap on consecutive '|...' attribute prefixes. parse_frame_internal gains a fourth param (attr_chain_depth) that bumps only on the attribute branch and is reset to 0 when descending into independent child frames (parse_array elements, parse_resp3_map k/v pairs). Preserves xfxtd's depth-transparent behavior for the legitimate single-attribute case while bounding the chain-of-attrs DoS surface. Two new pin tests: chain-of-16 rejected with RecursionLimitExceeded, chain-of-7 still parses to the wrapped SimpleString. All 61 fr-protocol tests pass; clippy clean.

title: MED: tcp_sentinel_failover_integration CONFIG REWRITE errors without --config argument
description:
## Summary

`fr-server/tests/tcp_e2e_test.rs::tcp_sentinel_failover_integration` spawns replicas via `spawn_frankenredis(port, Some(primary_port))` (line 383, no --config arg), then asserts `CONFIG REWRITE` returns `SimpleString("OK")`. Actual reply is `Error("ERR The server is running without a config file")` — legitimate per upstream Redis semantics.

Exposed after frankenredis-ea1j cleared the workspace clippy gate; previously blocked by fr-command fail-fast.

## Fix direction

Switch the sentinel-failover test's `spawn_frankenredis` calls to a variant that writes a minimal tmp `redis.conf` (`appendonly no`, `dbfilename dump.rdb`, `bind 127.0.0.1`) and passes `--config <tmp>/redis.conf`. The test harness already has `spawn_frankenredis_with_config` at tests/tcp_e2e_test.rs:393 — extend it so the caller can add the `--replicaof` override for the two replicas without losing the config file.

Alternative: relax the CONFIG REWRITE assertion to accept either "OK" or the no-config error, but that weakens the conformance of this suite. Prefer the spawn-with-config approach.

Sibling test at line 2408 has the same issue and should land in the same fix.

## Not a stub

Our CONFIG REWRITE path is correct per upstream — upstream returns the same error when invoked without a config file.

## Labels

fr-server, tcp-e2e, config-rewrite, sentinel, test-env
close_reason: Fixed in c6e3090 — sentinel-failover test now spawns replicas with a tmp redis.conf so CONFIG REWRITE has a target file.

Real impl path: thread store.script_propagation_records through the runtime EVAL/FCALL capture path in crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs and decide how to route AOF-vs-replica destinations against the existing persistence/backlog model.

title: Lua parser accepts suffix (./[/:/() on literal primaries (number/string/bool/nil)
description:
Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4:

  return 1[2]          v=parser: '<eof>' expected near '['     fr=runtime: attempt to index a number value
  return 1.0[2]        v=parser: '<eof>' expected near '['     fr=runtime: attempt to index a number value
  return true[1]       v=parser: '<eof>' expected near '['     fr=runtime: attempt to index a boolean value
  return nil[1]        v=parser: '<eof>' expected near '['     fr=runtime: attempt to index a nil value
  return 'a'[1]        v=parser: '<eof>' expected near '['     fr=(empty output - runtime nil)
  return true.foo      v=parser: '<eof>' expected near '.'     fr=runtime: attempt to index a boolean value
  return nil.foo       v=parser: '<eof>' expected near '.'     fr=runtime: attempt to index a nil value
  return 1()           v=parser: '<eof>' expected near '('     fr=runtime: attempt to call a number value
  return true()        v=parser: '<eof>' expected near '('     fr=runtime: attempt to call a boolean value
  return nil()         v=parser: '<eof>' expected near '('     fr=runtime: attempt to call a nil value
  return 'a'()         v=parser: '<eof>' expected near '('     fr=runtime: attempt to call a string value
  return 'abc':upper() v=parser: '<eof>' expected near ':'     fr=ABC                                          (!!)

Upstream Lua 5.1 grammar (lparser.c::primaryexp + suffixedexp):
  prefixexp ::= var | functioncall | `(' exp `)'
  primaryexp -> Name | `(' expr `)'    (these are the ONLY suffixable primaries)
  simpleexp -> NUMBER | STRING | NIL | TRUE | FALSE | ... | constructor | FUNCTION body
  (simpleexp values are NOT routed through suffixedexp.)

fr's parse_primary at lua_eval.rs ~line 2017 conflates simpleexp (literals) with prefixexp, so parse_suffixed_expr accepts any literal as a suffix base. Worst case: `return 'abc':upper()` actually RUNS on fr and returns 'ABC' — semantically permissive but a real divergence.

Fix: split the parser so suffix loop only runs when the primary was Name or paren-wrapped expression. Bare literals exit immediately and bubble up to the top-level chunk check, which then emits `'<eof>' expected near '<suffix-token>'` via the existing parse_block-after-return check.

NOTE: the existing fr behavior of `'abc':upper()` returning 'ABC' suggests fr's string metatable has __index='string', which is the canonical Lua 5.1 behavior — but upstream rejects it at parse time because 'abc' is a simple expr, not a prefix expr.
close_reason: Split parse_primary into a new parse_primary_with_kind helper that returns the expression plus a 'suffixable' boolean. Only Name and parenthesized expressions match upstream's primaryexp production and can carry suffixes; everything else (Number/String/Bool/Nil/table/function/varargs) is simpleexp and exits the suffix loop immediately. Combined with the parse_block-after-return change (frankenredis-yunl8), trailing suffix tokens on literals now surface at the chunk-level <eof>-expected check. Regression test lua_parser_rejects_suffix_on_literal_primaries_oee7k pins 12 invalid cases + 5 sanity-check valid cases. Full release test suite (1011 tests) still passes. Live-diff vs vendored 16380: 15/15 cases match byte-for-byte.

title: SYNC reply has spurious protocol error: 'got \r as reply type byte'
description:
Differential probe vs vendored redis 7.2.4:

  vendored SYNC:
    Full resync with master, discarding 313 bytes of bulk transfer...
    Full resync done. Logging commands from master.
    Entering replica output mode...
    (then PING commands from master loop)

  fr SYNC:
    Full resync with master, discarding 370 bytes of bulk transfer...
    Full resync done. Logging commands from master.
    Error: Protocol error, got "\r" as reply type byte
    Entering replica output mode...

After the RDB bulk transfer completes, fr emits a bare CRLF that
redis-cli (when used as a debug replica via SYNC) interprets as
the start of a malformed reply. The two messages get out of order
and redis-cli reports a protocol error before entering output
mode.

Severity: P4 deferred — SYNC is replica-only protocol, real
replicas accept arbitrary subsequent bytes. The 'protocol error'
is a redis-cli pretty-printing artifact when used as a debug
client. Actual replica connections still function. Real fix
requires auditing fr's post-RDB-transfer output framing to
match upstream's exact byte sequence.
close_reason: completed

title: fr-runtime: CLIENT LIST ID rejects 0/negative client IDs as 'Invalid client ID' instead of empty match
description:
Upstream networking.c::clientCommand parses CLIENT LIST ID values via getLongLongFromObjectOrReply with the 'Invalid client ID' wording — only non-numeric input errors. Numeric values that don't match any live client (including 0 and negative) silently filter out: lookupClientByID returns NULL and the reply is empty.

fr-runtime's handle_client_list strictly required parsed_id > 0, returning 'ERR Invalid client ID' for 0 / negative ids.

Differential probe vs vendored redis 7.2.4:
  CLIENT LIST ID 0    → fr Invalid, redis empty
  CLIENT LIST ID -1   → fr Invalid, redis empty
  CLIENT LIST ID abc  → both Invalid (regression-protected)

title: fr-persist: fuzz_aof_decoder corpus has only happy-path seeds; no smoke test, no truncated/malformed inputs
description:
fuzz/corpus/fuzz_aof_decoder/ ships 14 corpus seeds, all valid AOF records (set_cmd, get_cmd, hset_cmd, xadd_cmd, multi_sequence, etc.). decode_aof_stream's interesting failure modes — truncated arrays mid-record, oversized array-length declarations, CRLF inside a bulk payload, non-RESP prefix bytes, recursion-depth bombs — have zero corpus coverage so libfuzzer must discover them from scratch.

Additionally fr-persist has no equivalent of fr-protocol's fuzz_corpus_smoke.rs, so corpus regressions (e.g. a future cleanup that empties the corpus dir) wouldn't show up under regular `cargo test`.

Plan in this diff:

1. Add 8 hostile-input seeds covering the AOF decoder's main error
   surfaces:
     malformed_array_len_overflow      *9999999999\r\n... (over AOF_MAX_ARRAY_LEN cap)
     malformed_bulk_len_overflow       $9999999999\r\n... (over AOF_MAX_BULK_LEN cap)
     truncated_array_header            *3\r\n  (no body)
     truncated_bulk_header             $5\r\n  (no body)
     truncated_bulk_body               $5\r\nab  (premature EOF)
     missing_crlf_after_bulk_len       $3\r\nfoo  (no trailing \r\n)
     non_resp_prefix                   ?bad-prefix
     deeply_nested_arrays              *1\r\n*1\r\n*1\r\n... 64-deep
2. New crates/fr-persist/tests/fuzz_corpus_smoke.rs that replays every
   corpus file through decode_aof_stream and asserts no panic + corpus
   size >= 14.

Acceptance: cargo test -p fr-persist --test fuzz_corpus_smoke passes;
new seeds present.

Reference: /testing-fuzzing audit on 2026-05-04 (sister to cwx6 for
fr-protocol corpus).
notes: Added 8 hostile-input seeds to fuzz/corpus/fuzz_aof_decoder/: malformed_array_len_overflow, malformed_bulk_len_overflow, truncated_array_header, truncated_bulk_header, truncated_bulk_body, missing_crlf_after_bulk_len, non_resp_prefix, deeply_nested_arrays (64-deep). Plus crates/fr-persist/tests/fuzz_corpus_smoke.rs with two tests: fuzz_aof_decoder_corpus_never_panics (replays all corpus files, asserts no panic + count >= 14) and fuzz_aof_decoder_rejects_hostile_seeds (asserts each hostile seed surfaces as Err, catching future refactors that loosen caps or drop the recursion guard). Both pass; clippy clean. Sister to cwx6 (fr-protocol fuzz_resp_parser corpus).

title: fr-command: ZADD with odd score/member tail emits wrong-arity instead of syntax error
description: fr-command::zadd at crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:3581 returns CommandError::WrongArity when the score/member tail has zero or odd count after option flags. Upstream t_zset.c::zaddGenericCommand emits 'ERR syntax error' instead. Caught by ad-hoc differential probe (e.g. ZADD z BAD 1 m: fr 'wrong arity', redis 'syntax error').

title: Fix ACL admin parity gaps exposed by live Redis harness
description: The new core_acl live differential harness exposed remaining mismatches versus vendored Redis in ACL LIST default-user serialization, ACL GETUSER map shape/details, ACL CAT categories and unknown-category wording, ACL subcommand wrong-arity wording, ACL GENPASS extra-args wording, and a stateful ACL USERS lowercase sequence. Use the new ACL live harness in smoke.rs as the oracle and preserve its stable passing subset.
close_reason: Fixed ACL admin parity gaps

title: BLMPOP silently skips wrongtype keys instead of returning WRONGTYPE
description:
fr's BLMPOP swallows StoreError::WrongType during its per-key iteration via , treating a wrongtype key as if it were empty/missing. Vendored Redis 7.2.4 returns WRONGTYPE immediately, matching LMPOP's behavior.

Root cause: fr-command/src/lib.rs:22667-22670 in blmpop():

  let llen = match store.llen(key, now_ms) {
      Ok(n) => n,
      Err(_) => continue,    // <-- swallows WrongType
  };

LMPOP at lib.rs:12881 correctly fires WRONGTYPE:

  Err(StoreError::WrongType) => {
      return Err(CommandError::Store(StoreError::WrongType));
  }

Upstream t_list.c::lmpopGenericCommand (used by both LMPOP and BLMPOP via blmpopGenericCommand) calls checkType(c,o,OBJ_LIST) on each key and returns WRONGTYPE on the first wrongtype encounter.

Probe sweep #65 (probe130.sh) confirmed the bug class. The siblings all work correctly:
  vendored BLPOP strk 0.001   -> WRONGTYPE   (matches fr)
  vendored BRPOP strk 0.001   -> WRONGTYPE   (matches fr)
  vendored BLMOVE strk dst LEFT RIGHT 0.001 -> WRONGTYPE   (matches fr)
  vendored BZPOPMIN strk 0.001 -> WRONGTYPE   (matches fr)
  vendored BZMPOP 0.001 1 strk MIN -> WRONGTYPE   (matches fr)
  vendored BLMPOP 0.001 1 strk LEFT -> WRONGTYPE   (fr returns empty <-- BUG)

Fix plan:
- In blmpop() change the Err(_) => continue arm to mirror LMPOP's StoreError::WrongType handling, returning the error.
- Add regression test pinning BLMPOP <string-key> with a wrongtype to WRONGTYPE.
close_reason: Fixed in iteration 98: replaced BLMPOP's blanket 'Err(_) => continue' with explicit StoreError::WrongType handling that returns WRONGTYPE immediately, mirroring LMPOP's existing pattern at lib.rs:12881. Upstream t_list.c::lmpopGenericCommand (shared by both LMPOP and BLMPOP) returns WRONGTYPE on the first wrongtype hit during key iteration. Live-diff: BLMPOP 0.001 1 strk LEFT and BLMPOP 0.001 2 nokey strk LEFT now both return WRONGTYPE. Regression test blmpop_returns_wrongtype_for_non_list_key_oks10 pins single-wrongtype and nokey-then-wrongtype cases. Full 1023-test suite passes.

title: BITPOS on empty existing string returns 0 instead of -1 for bit=0
description:
Differential probe vs vendored redis 7.2.4:

  SET k ""
  BITPOS k 0   → vendored: -1, fr: 0     ← divergence
  BITPOS k 1   → vendored: -1, fr: -1    (matches)
  BITPOS k 0 0 → vendored: -1, fr: 0     ← divergence
  BITPOS k 0 0 -1     → vendored: -1, fr: 0  ← divergence
  BITPOS k 0 0 -1 BIT → vendored: -1, fr: 0  ← divergence

Missing-key path is fine (vendored=0, fr=0).

Root cause: crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs:3355-3357 has an explicit
early-return for bytes.is_empty() that mirrors the missing-key
return value (bit ? -1 : 0). Upstream bitposCommand does NOT
short-circuit on empty existing strings — it falls through to
range normalization (start=0, end=strlen-1=-1) where start>end
naturally produces -1 for both bit=0 and bit=1.

Fix: delete the bytes.is_empty() short-circuit. The existing
range normalization (s_idx=0, e_idx=-1 for None/None) already
hits the s_idx > e_idx → -1 path. Verified by tracing each
modifier combination.

Add regression test pinning all four divergent variants.
close_reason: fr-store::bitpos no longer short-circuits on empty existing strings; range normalization (s_idx=0 > e_idx=-1) returns -1 naturally for both bit values and all unit/range combinations. Verified against vendored 7.2.4 across 11 BITPOS variants (existing-empty, missing-key, non-empty BIT/BYTE).

title: Add metamorphic property tests for advanced ZSET ops
description: Add comprehensive metamorphic tests for fr-store ZSET commands: ZPOPMIN, ZPOPMAX, ZRANK, ZREVRANK using proptest.
close_reason: Added metamorphic property tests for advanced ZSET ops in fr-store

title: rv89 part 4a: OBJECT ENCODING listpack_ex/hashtable_ex + keyspace events + DEBUG OBJECT expired_fields
description:
Narrowed slice of br-frankenredis-3jrv. No RDB / AOF / HGETEX-HGETDEL yet — those are part 4b (RDB) and part 4c (AOF + HGETEX/HGETDEL).

Scope:
- fr-store::object_encoding: when a hash carries at least one per-field TTL, report 'listpack_ex' / 'hashtable_ex' instead of 'listpack' / 'hashtable'.
- Keyspace events: emit 'hexpire', 'hpexpire', 'hexpireat', 'hpexpireat', 'hpersist', 'hexpired' on the right commands / lazy-reaps.
- DEBUG OBJECT reports an 'expired_fields' counter equal to the Store's stat_expired_keys-derived delta for the given key.
- Tests per axis.
close_reason: Landed in f462d78: OBJECT ENCODING flips listpack/hashtable → listpack_ex/hashtable_ex when any per-field TTL is present; hexpired keyspace event fires on both bulk and targeted lazy reaps. 2 new tests; fr-store 280/0. Command-side keyspace events (HEXPIRE → hexpire etc.) and DEBUG OBJECT expired_fields deferred to a future bead alongside RDB + AOF (3jrv remains open).

title: fr-persist: parse_aof_manifest rejects blank lines and # comments; upstream Redis 7.2 aof.c accepts them

title: redis.set_repl and redis.setresp reject non-integer/non-numeric args instead of lua_tonumber-coercing
description:
Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4:

  EVAL 'return redis.set_repl("abc")' 0
    vendored: (nil)  — silently treats as 0, accepted (0 & ~PROPAGATE_AOF|REPL = 0)
    fr:       ERR Invalid replication flags ...

  EVAL 'return redis.set_repl(1.5)' 0
    vendored: (nil)  — lua_tonumber truncates to 1 (PROPAGATE_AOF), accepted
    fr:       ERR Invalid replication flags ...

  EVAL 'return redis.set_repl(nil)' 0
    vendored: (nil)  — lua_tonumber returns 0, accepted
    fr:       ERR Invalid replication flags ...

  EVAL 'return redis.setresp(2.5)' 0
    vendored: (nil)  — lua_tonumber truncates to 2, accepted
    fr:       ERR RESP version must be 2 or 3

Root cause: upstream script_lua.c::luaRedisSetReplCommand and luaSetRespCommand both call lua_tonumber, which silently coerces non-numeric arguments to 0 and truncates floats to int via the int cast. The bit-mask / value check then accepts 0 (set_repl) or rejects (setresp where 0 != 2 and 0 != 3 — but 2.5 truncates to 2 so it IS accepted).

fr's handlers reject explicitly:
  - non-numeric args (LuaValue::to_number returns None for nil/bool/table) → silently 0 upstream
  - fractional inputs (1.5, 2.5) → silently truncate upstream

Vendored-correct behavior (probe confirmed):
  set_repl: bad-arg rejection only fires for numeric arg whose int truncation has bits outside PROPAGATE_AOF|REPL (e.g., -1, 99)
  setresp:  bad-arg rejection only fires when int(val) is neither 2 nor 3 (e.g., setresp(5), setresp('abc') → truncates to 0 → rejects)

Fix: in both fr handlers, coerce via to_number().unwrap_or(0.0) and cast as i64 (truncating floats), then apply the bit-mask / value check on the truncated integer.
close_reason:
Reworked redis.set_repl and redis.setresp at fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs (~line 4395, 4416) to coerce arguments via lua_tonumber semantics. Both now use args[0].to_number().unwrap_or(0.0) (matches Lua 5.1's lua_tonumber which returns 0 for nil/bool/table/non-numeric-string) and then truncate to i64 via 'as i64' cast (matches C's implicit int conversion which truncates floats toward zero). The bit-mask (set_repl) and 2-or-3 (setresp) checks then apply on the truncated integer.

Result: set_repl('abc'), set_repl(nil), set_repl({}), set_repl(true), set_repl(1.5), set_repl(0.5) — all silently accepted (coerce to 0, no bits set). set_repl(99/-1/12345) — still rejected (bits outside PROPAGATE_AOF|REPL). setresp(2.5) — accepted (truncates to 2). setresp('abc'/nil/{}/0/5) — rejected (0/5 not in {2,3}).

Regression test redis_set_repl_and_setresp_coerce_via_lua_tonumber_op1r0 covers 7 set_repl silent-accept cases, 1 set_repl reject case, 1 setresp truncate-accept case, 3 setresp reject cases. Live diff vs vendored 7.2.4 matches on 21 probed variants.

title: fr-command::copy_cmd rejects every non-zero DB target — Lua redis.call('COPY', src, dst, 'DB', N) and AOF replay can never copy across databases
description:
/mock-code-finder: continuing the dispatch_argv-vs-fr-runtime audit (w9yzb MOVE, j22p8 SELECT, rdz52 FLUSHDB). COPY has the same architectural drift.

Source: crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:18078-18127 — copy_cmd:

  if arg.eq_ignore_ascii_case("DB") {
      ...
      let db = parse_i64_arg(&argv[i + 1])?;
      if db != 0 {
          return Err(CommandError::Custom("ERR DB index is out of range".to_string()));
      }
      ...
  }
  ...
  let copied = store.copy(source, destination, replace, now_ms)...;

Two bugs:
1. Hard-coded 'db != 0' check rejects EVERY non-zero target db, regardless of store.database_count. Upstream supports cross-db COPY for any valid db index.
2. Uses raw source/dest bytes — no encode_db_key namespacing — so even the source key is read from the unprefixed (db 0) namespace, ignoring dispatch_client_ctx.db_index.

Differential probe vs vendored Redis 7.2.4 via Lua:
  EVAL 'return redis.call("COPY", "foo", "foodst", "DB", "1")' 0
    upstream: 1 (key copied to db 1; db 1 GET foodst -> bar)
    fr      : ERR DB index is out of range script: ...

Reachability of broken path (same as MOVE/SELECT/FLUSHDB):
- Lua redis.call('COPY')
- AOF replay (fr-runtime/src/lib.rs:2262 routes through dispatch_argv)
- MULTI/EXEC COPY

Fix: rewrite copy_cmd to mirror fr-runtime::handle_copy_command at lib.rs:10309+ — encode keys with encode_db_key(source_db, &source) / encode_db_key(target_db, &destination), validate target_db < store.database_count, handle the source==destination check on the encoded keys (cross-db COPY of same logical key IS allowed). Preserve REPLACE / DB option parsing. source_db comes from store.dispatch_client_ctx.db_index.
close_reason: Rewrote fr-command::copy_cmd to mirror fr-runtime::handle_copy_command — parses target_db against store.database_count (instead of hard-coded 'db != 0' rejection), encodes source/dest via fr_store::encode_db_key with source_db read from store.dispatch_client_ctx.db_index, and only flags source==destination when BOTH the keys AND the DBs match. Lua redis.call('COPY', src, dst, 'DB', N), AOF replay, and MULTI/EXEC now copy across databases on the dispatch_argv path. 4 new tests pin: cross-db payload transfer + source preservation, out-of-range db rejection without source mutation, cross-db same-name COPY succeeds, same-key/same-db with non-zero DB option still emits the upstream error. clippy clean.

title: CONFIG SET hash/zset-max-listpack-value treated as memory-value + set-max-listpack-value recognized
description: Upstream config.c declares hash-max-listpack-value, zset-max-listpack-value (and their ziplist aliases) as MEMORY_CONFIG so they accept suffix forms (64K, 1m, ...) and report 'argument must be a memory value' on parse failure. fr was treating them as plain integers with the integer-out-of-range / couldnt-be-parsed wording. set-max-listpack-value was missing from the recognized set entirely. Fix: split the encoding-threshold block into MEMORY_CONFIG vs INTEGER_CONFIG paths and add set-max-listpack-value to the integer set. Negative values now also report 'argument must be a memory value' for the memory-value fields, matching upstream's memtoull behaviour

title: fr-conformance core_scripting fixtures pin pre-q27hu pcall/xpcall error format — should include 'user_script:1: ' prefix to match upstream
description:
/testing-conformance-harnesses re-run after recent peer commits surfaced 5 failures, of which 3 are tied to the q27hu Lua error source-level fix:

  conformance_core_scripting fails on:
    eval_pcall_catches_error
      expected: 'boom'
      actual:   'user_script:1: boom'
    eval_xpcall_error_with_handler
      expected: 'caught: boom'
      actual:   'caught: user_script:1: boom'
    eval_xpcall_with_handler
      expected: 'handled: boom'
      actual:   'handled: user_script:1: boom'

q27hu (peer 19a0a83) correctly aligned fr's error() builtin to upstream Lua's source-level prefix behavior — error('boom') now returns 'user_script:1: boom' (matching live oracle). The 3 fixtures pinned the pre-q27hu plain message and now diverge from both fr AND upstream.

Verified upstream behavior via differential probe earlier:
  EVAL \"return error('boom')\" 0
    upstream: ERR user_script:1: boom script: ..., on @user_script:1.

Fix: update the 3 expected values in core_scripting.json to include the upstream prefix.

Note: the other 4 conformance failures (live_redis_stream_dump_restore / xtrim_limit / xautoclaim / function_dump_restore) appear unrelated to q27hu — likely live-oracle harness flakiness or pre-existing red. Left for separate triage.
close_reason: Updated 3 fixture cases in core_scripting.json (eval_pcall_catches_error, eval_xpcall_error_with_handler, eval_xpcall_with_handler) to include the upstream 'user_script:1: ' prefix that error() now emits per q27hu. Verified upstream alignment via differential probe earlier in the session: EVAL returns 'ERR user_script:1: boom ...' on vendored 7.2.4. conformance_core_scripting now passes (was failing on 3 cases). The 4th case at line 3655 ('eval_pcall_catches_error' second variant) doesn't reference the error message text — checks the if-ok branch returning literal 'caught' — so untouched.

title: fr-command BITCOUNT error precedence: type check + arg-parse order diverges from upstream 7.2.4
description:
REVIEW MODE Tick 14 finding via /porting-to-rust differential probe vs legacy_redis_code/redis/src/bitops.c::bitcountCommand.

UPSTREAM ORDER (bitops.c lines 805-854):
  1. lookupKeyReadOrReply with shared.czero — missing key returns Integer(0)
  2. checkType(OBJ_STRING) — non-string key returns WRONGTYPE
  3. argc 4|5: parse start (argv[2]), parse end (argv[3]), [argc 5: check BIT|BYTE on argv[4]]
  4. argc 2: whole string
  5. else: shared.syntaxerr

FR ORDER (crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs::bitcount lines 9783-9819):
  1. argv.len() < 2 → WrongArity
  2. store.exists(key) → missing returns Integer(0)
  3. argv.len() match: 5 → check unit FIRST then parse start/end
  4. store.bitcount(...) → returns WRONGTYPE only here

DIVERGENCES:
  A. WRONGTYPE precedence: For BITCOUNT listkey BAD BAD BADUNIT, fr returns SyntaxError; upstream returns WRONGTYPE.
  B. Unit-check order in argc==5: For BITCOUNT key BAD BAD BADUNIT, fr returns 'ERR syntax error' (unit check fires first); upstream returns 'ERR value is not an integer or out of range' (start parses first).

Severity: [LOW]. Visible only when input is malformed in multiple ways simultaneously. Wire-format conformance gap.

FIX:
  1. Use store.key_type() pattern (matches BITPOS lines 9846-9850) to short-circuit on missing AND fail with WRONGTYPE on non-string BEFORE arg parsing.
  2. In argc==5 branch, parse start/end first, then check BIT|BYTE.
close_reason:
Reordered bitcount() to match upstream bitops.c precedence:

1. Added store.key_type() check at the top (mirroring BITPOS lines 9846-9850) so missing → Integer(0) and non-string → WRONGTYPE BEFORE any argv parsing. Replaced the prior store.exists() short-circuit which only handled missing keys.
2. In the argv.len() == 5 branch, parse start (argv[2]) and end (argv[3]) BEFORE checking the BIT|BYTE modifier — upstream bitops.c lines 815-830 do this order, so a malformed integer now takes precedence over a bad unit.

Two new regression tests pin both invariants:
- bitcount_wrongtype_wins_over_arg_parse_errors — BITCOUNT lst BAD BAD BADUNIT now returns WRONGTYPE instead of SyntaxError.
- bitcount_invalid_start_beats_invalid_unit — BITCOUNT k BAD 0 BADUNIT now returns 'ERR value is not an integer or out of range' instead of 'ERR syntax error'.

Verification:
- cargo test -p fr-command --lib bitcount → 8/8 pass.
- cargo clippy -p fr-command --lib --no-deps -- -D warnings → clean.

[LOW] severity — wire-format conformance gap, only visible when input is malformed in multiple ways simultaneously.

title: CRITICAL: ZRANGESTORE z2 z 0 -1 BYSCORE panics fr-server (BTreeMap range start > end)
description:
Calling 'ZRANGESTORE dest src 0 -1 BYSCORE' against fr-server triggers a panic that closes the connection (and presumably tears down the worker thread):

  thread 'main' panicked at .../btree/search.rs:121:21:
  range start is greater than range end in BTreeMap

This happens because the BYSCORE flag interprets the third+fourth args as score bounds; passing 0 and -1 yields start=0, end=-1, and the BTreeMap::range() call inside the score-range walker panics rather than returning an empty range.

Acceptance: 'ZRANGESTORE dest src 0 -1 BYSCORE' returns Integer(0) (empty range, dest unchanged) byte-for-byte matching vendored redis 7.2.4. Same fix should cover ZRANGE BYSCORE / ZRANGEBYSCORE with start > end: those already return empty arrays without panicking, but the underlying BTreeMap walker for ZRANGESTORE may take a different path that's not guarded.

Reference: ad-hoc differential probe of fr (port 16700) vs vendored redis 7.2.4 (port 16701) caught the panic. Affects ANY caller that hits start > end on the BYSCORE/BYLEX-backed BTreeMap walker for ZRANGESTORE.

Severity rationale: fr-server panic + connection close from a single client command is a denial-of-service vector. Promoting to P1.
close_reason: Added command regression for inverted ZRANGESTORE BYSCORE panic path; fr-command/fr-runtime tests pass via rch

title: INFO 'commandstats' section missing; fr tracks histograms but emits no cmdstat_<cmd> lines
description:
fr-store's CommandHistogramTracker tracks per-command call counts (used by INFO Latencystats). fr-runtime's handle_info_command at crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs:10733+ orders sections as Server/Clients/Memory/Persistence/Stats/Replication/CPU/Modules/Errorstats/Latencystats/Cluster/Keyspace, omitting upstream's Commandstats section (server.c::genRedisInfoString:6168-6173, format defined at server.c::genRedisInfoStringCommandStats:5329-5353).

Repro vs vendored 7.2.4:
  fr> INFO commandstats     -> empty bulk string
  redis> INFO commandstats  -> '# Commandstats\r\ncmdstat_set:calls=N,usec=0,usec_per_call=0.00,rejected_calls=0,failed_calls=0\r\n...'

Fix: add handle_info_commandstats_section emitting '# Commandstats' followed by 'cmdstat_<lowercased_name>:calls=<N>,usec=0,usec_per_call=0.00,rejected_calls=0,failed_calls=0' per histogram, slotted between modules and errorstats per upstream order. usec/rejected/failed unmaintained; emit 0 stubs to keep the field-set parseable by clients.

title: FAILOVER TO host PORT — port accepts any signed long, not just u16
description:
Differential probe vs vendored Redis 7.2.4 shows fr's FAILOVER TO
parser rejects ports outside u16 with the int-out-of-range wording,
where vendored accepts any signed long-long. The port value is
stored but not otherwise validated by upstream — only the parse must
succeed.

Examples:

  FAILOVER TO host 99999
    vendored: ERR FAILOVER requires connected replicas.
    fr:       ERR value is not an integer or out of range

  FAILOVER TO host -1
    vendored: ERR FAILOVER requires connected replicas.
    fr:       ERR value is not an integer or out of range

  FAILOVER TO host 0
    both: ERR FAILOVER requires connected replicas.

  FAILOVER TO host 9223372036854775808
    both: ERR value is not an integer or out of range

  FAILOVER TO host 99999 abc
    vendored: ERR syntax error           (abc is unknown trailing option)
    fr:       ERR value is not an integer or out of range  (port rejected first)

Fix locus: crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs::failover_cmd, the `TO`
branch. Currently casts the parsed i64 to u16 via try_from. Replace
with a plain parse_i64_arg and store the value as i64 (or drop it
entirely — the port field is unused).

Test plan: pin "FAILOVER TO host 99999" → connected-replicas error,
"FAILOVER TO host -1" → same, "FAILOVER TO host 99999 abc" → syntax
error, "FAILOVER TO host 9223372036854775808" → int-out-of-range.
close_reason: fixed

title: PING in subscribe mode emits [pong, msg] array + PING table arity -1
description: (1) fr's PING command returned the standard '+PONG' (or +'msg' bulk) reply even when the client was in subscribe mode; upstream networking.c::pingCommand emits a 2-element array ['pong', msg] in subscribe-mode RESP2 (2) PING was registered with table-level arity 1 (exact); upstream commands.def declares arity -1 (1+) with the handler checking argc<=2 internally. Without arity -1 fix, the runtime's command_arity_ok gate blocked the subscribe-mode interception for 'PING msg'

title: INCRBYFLOAT (and HINCRBYFLOAT) lose precision at large magnitudes — fr uses f64; vendored uses long double
description:
Differential probe vs vendored 16380:
  SET counter 100
  INCRBY counter -9223372036854775808  → both: -9223372036854775707
  INCRBYFLOAT counter 1.5
    vendored: -9223372036854775705.5  (preserves the .5 fractional)
    fr      : -9223372036854776000    (lost both the .5 AND last 3 digits)

Root cause: upstream t_string.c::incrbyfloatCommand uses 'long double' (80-bit extended precision on x86_64) for the running value:
  long double incr, value;
  ...
  if (getLongDoubleFromObjectOrReply(c, o, &value, NULL) ...)

fr's INCRBYFLOAT uses Rust's f64 (64-bit IEEE 754), which has only ~15-17 decimal digits of precision. At magnitudes near 9e18 (the operand here), f64 can't even represent integers spaced by 1, let alone the .5 fractional offset.

Likely affected commands (need verify): INCRBYFLOAT, HINCRBYFLOAT, GEOADD (which encodes lat/lon to float), ZADD INCR with large running scores, ZINCRBY.

Fix scope: switch fr's INCRBYFLOAT/HINCRBYFLOAT from f64 to a long-double-equivalent crate (rug ≈ MPFR binding, or num-bigfloat). This is a dependency-changing task — file as P3 since it requires product judgment about adding a heavyweight numeric dep, and the divergence only manifests at near-overflow magnitudes that real workloads rarely hit.

Alternative: document the limitation in README and add a clamping check that surfaces 'value is not a representable IEEE 754 double' for operands >= 2^53 (similar to how Redis currently surfaces NaN/Infinity).
close_reason: Preserved INCRBYFLOAT/HINCRBYFLOAT decimal text precision for simple finite decimal operands near i64 magnitudes, with f64 fallback for hex/exponent paths

title: Lua struct stdlib not exposed (sandbox surface)
description:
fr's Lua sandbox exposes neither the `struct` library nor any equivalent. Vendored Redis 7.2.4 ships lua-struct (struct.pack / struct.unpack / struct.size) and scripts that use it for binary packing fail on fr with the generic protected-global error.

Reproduction:
  EVAL 'return type(struct)' 0
    vendored: table
    fr:       ERR user_script:1: Script attempted to access nonexistent global variable 'struct' ...
  EVAL 'return struct.pack("i", 100)' 0
    vendored: <4-byte binary>
    fr:       (same protected-global error)

struct format string covers: native byte order/alignment (`<`, `>`, `=`, `!`), integer/uint sizes (b/B/h/H/i/I/l/L), floats (f/d), packed strings (`sN`, `cN`, `cs`), and pad/skip (`x`/`X`/`s`). Full grammar in https://www.lua.org/wiki/StructurePacking and the lua-struct C source vendored under `legacy_redis_code/redis/deps/lua/src/lua_struct.c`.

Existing companion beads:
- frankenredis-7gtvz (cmsgpack — also missing)
- frankenredis-2wn7j (newproxy — also missing)

Fix plan (substantial):
1. Add a `struct` global table to the Lua sandbox via the same mechanism that exposes `redis`, `cjson`, `bit` (likely fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs sandbox init).
2. Implement pack/unpack/size with the documented format grammar.
3. Pin against vendored across the format grammar (signed/unsigned, endianness, alignment, packed strings).

Probe sweep #82 (sandbox visibility, inline).

Not scoped for this iteration.

title: fr-server: parse_blocking_deadline edge cases (-0, 0e100, subnormal) lack focused regression coverage
description:
fr-server's parse_blocking_deadline (BLPOP/BRPOP/BLMPOP/etc. timeout handling) has unit tests for finite/non-finite/negative/fractional/out-of-range cases but is missing focused coverage for three edge cases that are easy to silently regress:

1. `-0` (negative zero literal): in IEEE-754, `-0.0 == 0.0` is true and `-0.0 < 0.0` is false. The current implementation correctly treats this as the zero-timeout 'block-forever' case (returns Some(u64::MAX)) but no test pins that. A future refactor that uses sign-bit checks (e.g. `signum() < 0.0`) would silently break negative-zero handling.

2. `0e100` and `0.0e0`: scientific-notation zeros parse to 0.0; should also map to Some(u64::MAX), not a far-future deadline. Subtle f64-parse behavior worth pinning.

3. `1e-300` and `1e-323` (subnormal/underflow positives): timeout_secs * 1000.0 still underflows to a non-zero positive, ceil rounds up to 1ms, deadline = now_ms + 1. Behavior is correct but worth pinning as a "no panic / no None" assertion to catch a future refactor that breaks subnormal handling.

These are deadlock-adjacent because parse_blocking_deadline is on the critical path for every BL* command: a regression that silently returns a wrong deadline (e.g. 'forever' when client requested 1ms, or vice-versa) yields a hung client without any error surface.

Acceptance: a single test that pins all three categories. Add it to the existing parse_blocking_deadline test cluster in crates/fr-server/src/main.rs.

Reference: /deadlock-finder-and-fixer audit on 2026-05-04.
notes: Added parse_blocking_deadline_handles_signed_zero_and_subnormals test in crates/fr-server/src/main.rs. Pins -0/-0.0/-0e0 → Some(u64::MAX), 0e100/0.0e0/0.0e-100 → Some(u64::MAX), and 1e-300/1e-323/1e-200/0.000000000001 → bounded future deadline (never block-forever, never None). Closes the regression-coverage gap that would let a future refactor of the timeout parser silently swap 'block forever' and 'timeout immediately' on edge inputs.

Replaced the two-pass `if let Some(count_arg) = argv.get(2) { parse_i64_arg(count_arg)?; ... }` blocks with a single `match argv.get(2)` that parses once, validates `parsed < -1`, then derives the usize count from the same parsed value.

Verification:

- `cargo clippy -p fr-command --lib --no-deps -- -D warnings` — clean.

[LOW] severity — performance only, no behavioral change.

title: XINFO CONSUMERS: 'inactive' field should be -1 for consumers never seen, not idle_ms
description: fr's XINFO CONSUMERS reports both 'idle' and 'inactive' incorrectly for consumers without pending entries. Two related bugs: (1) idle field: fr computes idle_ms from the MAX last_delivered_ms across the consumer's pending entries (crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs:8804-8815); if there are no pending entries, falls back to 0. Vendored Redis 7.2.4 stores consumer->seen_time set at consumer creation (XGROUP CREATECONSUMER calls streamLookupConsumer with create=1 which sets seen_time = mstime()) and computes idle = mstime() - seen_time. So a fresh consumer never seen XREADGROUP shows idle = ~tens of ms in vendored vs 0 in fr. (2) inactive field: crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:5977 reuses idle_ms for inactive, but vendored separates them. Vendored initializes consumer->active_time = -1 in streamCreateConsumer; XINFO CONSUMERS emits inactive = mstime() - active_time when active_time > 0 else -1. XREADGROUP updates active_time. For a freshly-created consumer: vendored inactive = -1, fr inactive = 0. Reproduce: XADD s '*' f v; XGROUP CREATE s g 0; XGROUP CREATECONSUMER s g c1; XINFO CONSUMERS s g -> vendored: idle:<small>,inactive:-1; fr: idle:0,inactive:0. Fix needs a struct for per-consumer metadata in fr-store (currently consumers are just BTreeSet<Vec<u8>> names): introduce StreamConsumerState { seen_time_ms, active_time_ms (Option<u64> = None for never-active) }, replace BTreeSet with BTreeMap<Vec<u8>, StreamConsumerState>, update XGROUP CREATECONSUMER to seed seen_time, update XREADGROUP/XCLAIM/XACK to refresh seen_time AND active_time, update xinfo_consumers to return both fields as a 4-tuple instead of (name, pending, idle_ms). Discovered during REVIEW PHASE differential-probe sweep.
close_reason: XINFO CONSUMERS now reports 'inactive' = -1 for consumers that have never received an active event (XREADGROUP/XCLAIM/XAUTOCLAIM), matching upstream Redis 7.2.4. Implementation: (1) Added StreamConsumerMetadata struct in fr-store with seen_time_ms (u64) and active_time_ms (Option<u64>); (2) Added consumer_metadata: BTreeMap<Vec<u8>, StreamConsumerMetadata> field to StreamGroup alongside the existing consumers BTreeSet (additive — no breaking changes to dump/restore format); (3) Plumbed updates through XREADGROUP, XCLAIM, XAUTOCLAIM (all set seen_time and active_time to now_ms), XGROUP CREATECONSUMER (sets seen_time only, active_time stays None), XGROUP DELCONSUMER (removes the metadata entry); (4) Added StreamConsumerInfoEx 4-tuple type (name, pending, idle_ms, inactive_ms_or_neg_one) and migrated xinfo_consumers + stream_consumer_info_to_frame + stream_full_consumer_info_to_frame to use it; (5) inactive emits -1 when active_time_ms is None, otherwise now_ms - active_time_ms; idle uses seen_time_ms when known, falling back to last-pending-delivery for RDB-restored consumers; (6) Updated XINFO STREAM FULL active-time field accordingly: -1 when never active, else absolute mstime reconstructed from now_ms - inactive_ms. Verified: 336 fr-store + 910 fr-command + 372 fr-runtime tests pass; conformance_core_stream fixture updated to expect inactive:-1 for freshly-created consumers (3 fixture values flipped from 0 to -1).

title: CLUSTER SET-CONFIG-EPOCH blanket-returns cluster_disabled_error even when cluster is enabled
description: Upstream cluster.c::clusterCommand:6420+ for CLUSTER SET-CONFIG-EPOCH validates the epoch arg and emits one of: 'Invalid config epoch specified: <epoch>' (negative), 'The user can assign a config epoch only when the node does not know any other node.' (peer dict size > 1), or 'Node config epoch is already non-zero' (configEpoch != 0); on first-call success it sets configEpoch and replies OK. fr's cluster_cmd at crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:8429 unconditionally returns cluster_disabled_error after the arity gate — meaning even when cluster_enabled is true, the subcommand never advances past the disabled-error path. The existing test acknowledges this as a follow-up. Tighten to: cluster_enabled gate first, then parse epoch (InvalidInteger on non-numeric), then 'Invalid config epoch specified: <n>' for negative, else OK reply (we don't model configEpoch state separately so we can't reproduce the 'already non-zero' branch, but the negative-epoch and happy-path arms align with upstream and make the stub usable for cluster mode tests).
close_reason: fixed

title: COMMAND_TABLE has PFDEBUG and PSYNC arities inverted vs upstream commands.def
description:
Differential probe vs vendored Redis 7.2.4:
  COMMAND INFO PFDEBUG -> upstream: 3   fr: -3
  COMMAND INFO PSYNC   -> upstream: -3  fr: 3

Upstream commands.def:
  MAKE_CMD("pfdebug",...,pfdebugCommand,3,...) -- arity=3 (exact: subcommand + key)
  MAKE_CMD("psync",...,syncCommand,-3,...)     -- arity=-3 (3 or more; replication options)

fr's COMMAND_TABLE entries had the two declarations swapped relative
to upstream:
  ('pfdebug', -3, ...)  -- declared variadic, but handler enforces argv.len() != 3
  ('psync',    3, ...)  -- declared exact-3, but handler accepts argv.len() < 3 (rejects)
                          -- and PSYNC tests already cover trailing args

Handler logic was already aligned with upstream; only the
COMMAND_TABLE metadata was stale. `COMMAND INFO PFDEBUG` and
`COMMAND INFO PSYNC` therefore returned the wrong arity
field, diverging from `redis-cli COMMAND INFO ...` against
vendored 7.2.4.

Fix: swap the two arities so they match upstream commands.def
(pfdebug=3, psync=-3) and pin a regression test
(command_info_arity_for_pfdebug_and_psync_matches_upstream).

title: CONFIG SET min-slaves-max-lag accepts negative values — vendored 7.2.4 rejects with [0, 2147483647] bound message
description:
Differential probe vs vendored 16380:
  CONFIG SET min-slaves-max-lag -1
    vendored: ERR CONFIG SET failed (possibly related to argument 'min-slaves-max-lag') - argument must be between 0 and 2147483647 inclusive
    fr      : OK

Upstream config.c declares min-slaves-max-lag (alias of min-replicas-max-lag) as INTEGER_CONFIG with inclusive range [0, INT_MAX]. fr's existing min-replicas-max-lag handler at lib.rs:8135 emits 'Invalid argument' on negative values rather than the table-level bound wording — same template miss as xqgra. Fix: route through config_set_failed with the bound wording. Same likely applies to min-slaves-to-write / min-replicas-to-write — verify and bundle.
close_reason: Bundled fix for the entire min-{replicas,slaves}-{to-write,max-lag} family. Changes in fr-runtime's handle_config_set: (1) merged min-replicas-to-write and min-slaves-to-write into a single branch (same applies to *-max-lag); (2) replaced the non-standard 'Invalid argument' wording with config_set_failed's table-level 'argument must be between 0 and 2147483647 inclusive' / 'argument couldn't be parsed into an integer'; (3) added explicit i32::MAX upper bound (was implicitly accepting any non-negative i64); (4) GET round-trips the user-supplied alias key (CONFIG SET min-slaves-max-lag stores under 'min-slaves-max-lag', SET min-replicas-max-lag stores under 'min-replicas-max-lag', so each alias's GET reflects its own SET). Pinned via config_set_min_replicas_slaves_family_validates_range_and_aliases which exercises -1 (bound err), 'abc' (parse err), 0 and 42 (accepted) for all four names. Live differential probe vs vendored 16380 confirms identical bound-error wording for all four. Workspace clippy clean under -D warnings.

title: XREAD/XREADGROUP emit Unbalanced error instead of syntax error when STREAMS has no following args
description:
Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4:

  XREAD COUNT 5 STREAMS
    vendored: ERR syntax error
    fr:       ERR Unbalanced 'xread' list of streams: for each stream key an ID or '$' must be specified.

  XREAD BLOCK 10 STREAMS
    vendored: ERR syntax error
    fr:       ERR Unbalanced 'xread' list of streams: ...

  XREADGROUP GROUP g c COUNT 5 STREAMS
    vendored: ERR syntax error
    fr:       ERR Unbalanced 'xreadgroup' list of streams: ...

  XREADGROUP GROUP g c COUNT 5 NOACK STREAMS
    vendored: ERR syntax error
    fr:       ERR Unbalanced 'xreadgroup' list of streams: ...

Root cause: upstream t_stream.c::xreadCommand uses '!strcasecmp(o,"STREAMS") && moreargs' as the STREAMS-keyword match. When STREAMS is the last argument (moreargs==0), the && short-circuits, so the keyword doesn't match and the for-loop's terminal 'else' branch fires addReplyErrorObject(c, shared.syntaxerr).

fr's xread() / xreadgroup() accept STREAMS regardless of trailing arg count, then fall into the 'Unbalanced' branch when tail < 2. The Unbalanced wording is reserved upstream for odd-count tails (e.g. STREAMS k id1 id2 id3) — the empty-tail case should be the generic 'ERR syntax error'.

Fix: in both xread() and xreadgroup() at fr-command/src/lib.rs (lines ~6677 and ~6783), require at least one arg after the STREAMS keyword for it to match (return SyntaxError otherwise). The existing odd-count branch then correctly handles tail==1 / tail==3 / etc with the Unbalanced wording.
close_reason: Fixed in xread() and xreadgroup() at fr-command/src/lib.rs: after matching the STREAMS keyword, both functions now check 'idx + 1 >= argv.len()' and return SyntaxError if there are no following args. Matches upstream t_stream.c::xreadCommand's '!strcasecmp(o,"STREAMS") && moreargs' short-circuit. The odd-tail 'Unbalanced ... list of streams' path is preserved for tails of 1, 3, 5, etc. Regression test xread_xreadgroup_streams_with_empty_tail_returns_syntax_error_pnkd6 covers 6 empty-tail variants (XREAD with COUNT, BLOCK, BLOCK+COUNT; XREADGROUP with COUNT, COUNT+NOACK, BLOCK) plus 2 unbalanced-tail control cases. Live diff vs vendored 7.2.4 matches on all 9 probed variants.

title: Lua cjson encode/decode edge cases: sparse arrays, boolean keys, decode arg-validation
description:
Several cjson divergences vs vendored Redis 7.2.4 found via probe:

1. Sparse-array encoding: a table with integer keys 1..N where N exceeds
   the array part triggers vendored to render as an array with explicit
   nulls. fr falls back to an object map.

   EVAL "return cjson.encode({1,nil,3})" 0
     vendored: [1,null,3]
     fr:       {"1":1,"3":3}

   EVAL "local x={1}; x[4]='gap'; return cjson.encode(x)" 0
     vendored: [1,null,null,"gap"]
     fr:       {"1":1,"4":"gap"}

2. Boolean-key rejection: vendored raises when a table key is bool/
   table/function. fr stringifies the bool to "true"/"false".

   EVAL "return cjson.encode({[true]=1})" 0
     vendored: ERR Cannot serialise boolean: table key must be a number
               or string
     fr:       {"true":1}

3. decode() bad-arg checks missing:

   EVAL "return cjson.decode()" 0
     vendored: ERR bad argument #1 to 'decode' (expected 1 argument)
     fr:       (returns nil silently)

   EVAL "return cjson.decode(nil)" 0
     vendored: ERR bad argument #1 to 'decode' (string expected, got nil)
     fr:       ERR invalid JSON: nil

   EVAL "return cjson.decode('')" 0
     vendored: ERR Expected value but found T_END at character 1
     fr:       (returns nil silently)

4. cjson.null sentinel: vendored exposes cjson.null as a userdata that
   round-trips to JSON null. Decoding 'null' returns this sentinel, so
   type(cjson.decode('null')) == 'userdata'. fr returns LuaValue::Nil
   which has type 'nil'. (Deferred — needs userdata support; the rest
   of this bead is in-scope.)

Fix locus: crates/fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs

- lua_value_to_json: detect "sparse array" tables (those with the
  longest contiguous-from-1 integer-key prefix, with the rest going as
  null padding up to max numeric key) — render those as array-with-
  nulls. Reject boolean/table/function keys with the upstream wording.
- cjson.decode arm: check arity and string type before parsing.

Test plan: pin sparse-array round-trips, boolean-key rejection, and
the three decode arg-validation cases.
close_reason: lua_value_to_json in crates/fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs now mirrors lua_cjson.c's array-detection algorithm: (1) reject bool/table/function keys with 'Cannot serialise TYPE: table key must be a number or string'; (2) when ALL keys are positive integers, apply the upstream sparse-safe=10 / sparse-ratio=2 threshold — pass → render as array padding gaps with null; fail → raise 'Cannot serialise table: excessively sparse array' (matching encode_sparse_convert=false default); (3) mixed/string-keyed tables fall through to the object form unchanged. cjson.decode now arg-validates strictly: zero args raises 'expected 1 argument', non-string-non-number raises 'string expected, got TYPE', empty/whitespace string raises 'Expected value but found T_END at character 1'. Regression test lua_cjson_sparse_array_and_decode_args_pt4d4 pins 9 cases. Live diff vs vendored Redis 7.2.4 on :16380 clean across 15 probes.

title: fr-runtime: PUBSUB NUMPAT extra args use subcommand-syntax-error wording instead of upstream WrongArity

title: fr-conformance: pubsub_unknown_subcommand fixture case ran in subscribed state, expected non-subscribed wording
description:
core_pubsub.json had pubsub_unknown_subcommand at position 23, after subscribe_for_pubsub_test (case 12, SUBSCRIBE ch1) and pubsub_numpat_with_pattern (case 14, PSUBSCRIBE alert.*) entered subscribe mode. fr correctly returned upstream-aligned 'ERR Can\'t execute \'pubsub|nosuchsub\': only (P|S)SUBSCRIBE / (P|S)UNSUBSCRIBE / PING / QUIT / RESET are allowed in this context' (matches processCommand's allow-list at server.c:4067-4082; PUBSUB-the-command is not in upstream's allow list). The case was intended to pin the unknown-subcommand wording from a non-subscribed connection ('ERR unknown subcommand \'NOSUCHSUB\'. Try PUBSUB HELP.').

Fix: move the case to position 12 — between pubsub_numpat_zero and subscribe_for_pubsub_test — so the unknown-subcommand path runs before any subscribe state lands. Adding cleanup after the case would have broken later cases (spublish_no_subscribers, cleanup_unsubscribe_all) that depend on lingering subscriptions.

title: Guard numeric conversions in command parsing

title: fr-store: hash field TTL needs golden-artifact pin against Redis 7.4 spec since vendored Redis 7.2.4 oracle returns 'unknown command'
description:
fr-store implements the Redis 7.4 hash-field TTL family (HEXPIRE/HPEXPIRE/HEXPIREAT/HPEXPIREAT, HTTL/HPTTL/HEXPIRETIME/HPEXPIRETIME, HPERSIST). Differential probe vs vendored Redis 7.2.4:

  HEXPIRE h 60 FIELDS 1 f
    upstream: ERR unknown command 'HEXPIRE' ...
    fr      : 1

This is a feature fr ships ahead of the vendored oracle, not a divergence — but it means the live conformance harness can't validate the response codes against upstream. The existing inline tests cover the matrix (hexpire_sets_ttl_and_returns_applied_code, hexpire_nx_xx_gt_lt_flag_matrix_matches_upstream_codes, etc.) but a documented golden anchored to the Redis 7.4 spec response codes makes future regressions and refactor drift visible without touching the live-oracle path.

Acceptance: a conformance/golden test pins the documented Redis 7.4 return codes for HEXPIRE applied/already-set/no-ttl/in-the-past/missing-field cases. (Upstream: HEXPIRE returns Array of N integers — 1 applied, 2 deleted-due-to-past, 0 NX-XX-GT-LT-skipped, -2 no-such-field; HTTL returns -1 no-ttl, -2 no-field, positive seconds remaining.)

Ship a regression test in this same diff.

Reference: /testing-conformance-harnesses + /porting-to-rust audit on 2026-05-04 — note that this resolves a stale gap previously documented in agent project memory under hash_field_ttl_gap; the implementation has landed and tests exist, the gap is documentary.
notes: Added hash_field_ttl_chained_hexpire_httl_hpersist_round_trip_golden in crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs. Pins the full Redis 7.4 spec response codes for a chained scenario: HEXPIRE applies (1) and reports -2 for missing field; HTTL surfaces the live remaining seconds; HEXPIRE NX skips when TTL set ([0]); XX applies ([1]); LT applies/skips on the proposed-vs-current comparison; GT applies when proposed > current; HPERSIST reports persisted (1)/no-such-field (-2); final HTTL returns -1 for fields with no TTL. fr-conformance can't validate this against vendored Redis 7.2.4 (HEXPIRE returns 'unknown command'), so this golden anchors the contract directly to the 7.4 spec.
