title: fr-command: HINCRBYFLOAT non-numeric field uses generic wording instead of upstream 'hash value is not a float'
description:
Upstream t_hash.c::hincrbyfloatCommand emits 'hash value is not a float' (not the generic 'value is not a valid float') when the existing hash field's value can't be parsed as a float — paralleling HINCRBY which uses 'hash value is not an integer'.

fr-command's hincrbyfloat surfaced StoreError::ValueNotFloat through the dispatch-boundary mapper, which produces the generic 'value is not a valid float' wording.

Differential probe vs vendored redis 7.2.4 (HSET h f abc; HINCRBYFLOAT h f 1):
  fr:    ERR value is not a valid float
  redis: ERR hash value is not a float

Fix: map StoreError::ValueNotFloat to the dedicated wording inside hincrbyfloat's error-mapping closure.

title: Fix O(N^2) DoS vulnerability in BITOP
description: The BITOP command evaluated the operation type (AND, OR, XOR) inside the inner byte-processing loop. With string lengths up to 512 MiB, this caused a massive CPU bottleneck. Hoisted the operation check outside the loop to ensure O(N) time complexity.
close_reason: done

title: CLIENT INFO/LIST: tot-mem/qbuf/qbuf-free/argv-mem/obl/oll/omem still report 0
description: Follow-up to frankenredis-lxccd. After the fd=N fix, the remaining socket-metric fields (tot-mem, qbuf, qbuf-free, argv-mem, multi-mem, obl, oll, omem) are still hardcoded to 0 in fr's CLIENT INFO / CLIENT LIST emission while vendored reports real per-client buffer accounting (e.g. qbuf=26 tot-mem=37786 on an idle connection). Fix scope: thread per-client buffer state from fr-server's ClientConnection (read_buf.len, write_buf.len, in-progress argv size) into ClientSession on every read/write turn, then expose through client_info_line. ClientConnection already has read_buf/write_buf -- the missing piece is wiring it into the session for the runtime emitter to read. Surfaced during REVIEW PHASE after closing lxccd's fd half.
close_reason: CLIENT INFO/LIST now emit live qbuf/qbuf-free/obl/tot-mem via per-session buffer accounting. Added pub qbuf_bytes/qbuf_free_bytes/output_buffer_bytes to ClientSession; fr-server's handle_readable refreshes them from conn.read_buf.len()/.capacity() and conn.write_buf.len() both before swap-in and after swap-back; handle_writable refreshes output_buffer_bytes after each flush. fr-runtime's dispatch_client_context_for_session mirrors them into DispatchClientContext so fr-command's client_info_line emits them too. tot-mem computed as 432 (per-client struct overhead) + qbuf + qbuf-free + 16384 (PROTO_REPLY_CHUNK_BYTES baseline rbs is pinned to) + obl. Live differential probe: fr 'qbuf=26 qbuf-free=4070 tot-mem=20912' vs vendored 'qbuf=26 qbuf-free=20448 argv-mem=10 tot-mem=37786'. qbuf byte-exact; qbuf-free differs because fr's read_buf Vec starts with cap 4096 vs upstream querybuf SDS at 20480 (allocator-baseline divergence). argv-mem/multi-mem remain 0 (not load-bearing for dashboards). Tests pass: fr-store 336, fr-command 910, fr-runtime 369 (3 pre-existing bgsave/rdb failures also fail on main HEAD stash-tested), fr-conformance 94 (3 pre-existing auth/acl/scripting failures also fail on main).

title: string.gsub silently accepts nil/boolean repl when pattern doesn't match
description:
Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4:

  string.gsub('a','b')         -- nil repl, pattern doesn't match
    vendored: user_script:1: bad argument #3 to 'gsub' (string/function/table expected)   (direct)
              bad argument #3 to '?' (string/function/table expected)                     (pcall)
    fr:       'a'                                                                          (no error)

  string.gsub('a','b',true)    -- boolean repl, pattern doesn't match
    vendored: bad argument #3 to '?' (string/function/table expected)                      (pcall)
    fr:       'a'                                                                          (no error)

Upstream lstrlib.c:str_gsub validates the repl type via luaL_argcheck at function entry:

    luaL_argcheck(L, tr == LUA_TNUMBER || tr == LUA_TSTRING ||
                     tr == LUA_TFUNCTION || tr == LUA_TTABLE, 3,
                        "string/function/table expected");

fr's handler at fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs line ~6197 only checks the repl type inside the per-match dispatch (line ~6220), so when the pattern doesn't match, the bad-type error never fires.

Two-part fix:
1. Validate the repl type upfront (after lua_check_string for arg 1/2 but before the match loop), using lua_format_argerror with "string/function/table expected" (no "got TYPE" suffix — upstream's luaL_argcheck wording).
2. Update the per-match catch-all error (line ~6246) to route through lua_format_argerror too so pcall(C-builtin) emits the anonymous shape — but in practice the upfront check makes the catch-all unreachable.

Also: validate that the new wording matches BOTH the direct shape (user_script:1: bad argument #3 to 'gsub' …) and the pcall shape (bad argument #3 to '?' …).
close_reason: Added upfront repl-type validation at the top of the string.gsub handler (routed through lua_format_argerror so both direct and pcall shapes match upstream). The previously-only per-match catch-all is now unreachable but updated to use lua_format_argerror as well. Regression test string_gsub_validates_repl_type_upfront_tfob7 pins 4 pcall cases + direct-call top-level + valid string/number repls + multi-return. Live-diff vs vendored 16380: 7 scripted cases + direct-call error all match byte-for-byte.

title: ACL DELUSER deletes earlier users before rejecting on 'default' — upstream pre-validates
description: Upstream acl.c::aclCommand DELUSER (lines 2789-2810) runs TWO loops: first scans argv for 'default' and rejects the whole call if found ('The default user cannot be removed'), then a second loop performs deletions. fr's handle_acl_deluser at crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs:6342 fuses the two into a single loop that deletes each user as it iterates and only rejects on default when it reaches that arg. Result: 'ACL DELUSER alice default bob' deletes alice (side effect persists), then returns the default-rejection error, then never reaches bob. Upstream rejects atomically — no deletions occur. Split into pre-validation pass + delete pass to match upstream atomicity.

title: fr-command XINFO HELP emits BulkString frames + diverges from vendored 7.2.4 wording (paraphrased descriptions, missing upstream typo)
description:
REVIEW MODE Tick 14 finding via /modes-of-reasoning-project-analysis, fifth and final in the HELP-divergence cluster (after 0o9vo CLIENT, vtege COMMAND, kuthf CONFIG, s574p XGROUP).

LOCATION (crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs lines 7240-7250):
  return Ok(RespFrame::Array(Some(vec![
      hello_bulk('XINFO <subcommand> [<arg> [value] [opt] ...]. Subcommands are:'),
      hello_bulk('CONSUMERS <key> <groupname>'),
      hello_bulk('    Show consumers of <groupname>.'),
      hello_bulk('GROUPS <key>'),
      hello_bulk('    Show groups of stream <key>.'),
      hello_bulk('STREAM <key> [FULL [COUNT <count>]]'),
      hello_bulk('    Show information about stream <key>.'),
      hello_bulk('HELP'),
      hello_bulk('    Print this help.'),
  ])));

UPSTREAM (legacy_redis_code/redis/src/t_stream.c::xinfoCommand line 3850, vendored Redis 7.2.4):

  if (!strcasecmp(c->argv[1]->ptr, 'HELP')) {
      const char *help[] = {
        'CONSUMERS <key> <groupname>',
        '    Show consumers of <groupname>.',
        'GROUPS <key>',
        '    Show the stream consumer groups.',
        'STREAM <key> [FULL [COUNT <count>]',     // <-- upstream typo: missing closing bracket
        '    Show information about the stream.',
        NULL
      };
      addReplyHelp(c, help);
      return;
  }

Two divergences:

1. Frame type: upstream addReplyStatus (SimpleString); fr emits BulkString.

2. Content: fr's GROUPS description ('Show groups of stream <key>.') and STREAM description ('Show information about stream <key>.') are paraphrased from upstream's terser wording ('Show the stream consumer groups.' and 'Show information about the stream.'). Also, fr's STREAM line has '[FULL [COUNT <count>]]' with two closing brackets — upstream has '[FULL [COUNT <count>]' (one closing bracket; this is an upstream typo but parity demands matching it byte-for-byte).

Severity: [LOW]. Affects HELP-scraping tools.

Reproducer:
  redis-cli> XINFO HELP
  upstream 7.2.4: 9 status-string lines with terser GROUPS/STREAM descriptions and the upstream bracket-typo
  fr (current): 9 bulk-string lines, paraphrased GROUPS/STREAM descriptions, both brackets closed

Fix:
1. Replace hello_bulk with hello_simple.
2. Use upstream 7.2.4 wording verbatim, including the [FULL [COUNT <count>] bracket typo (preserving wire-byte parity per project convention).
3. Add regression test.
close_reason: fr-command XINFO HELP rewritten to mirror vendored Redis 7.2.4 t_stream.c::xinfoCommand HELP body line-for-line. Two divergences fixed: (1) frame type — every entry now SimpleString via hello_simple() instead of BulkString; (2) content — fr's GROUPS description ('Show groups of stream <key>.') and STREAM description ('Show information about stream <key>.') were paraphrased; restored upstream's terser wording ('Show the stream consumer groups.' / 'Show information about the stream.'). Crucially, the STREAM line now preserves upstream's missing-closing-bracket typo: '[FULL [COUNT <count>]' (one ']' instead of two) — fr was 'fixing' the typo, but byte-for-byte parity demands matching upstream verbatim. New regression test xinfo_help_matches_vendored_724_wording_with_simple_string_frames pins SimpleString throughout, header verbatim, upstream's terser descriptions present, the upstream bracket-typo present, the OLD paraphrased wording NOT present (negative assertions). All fr-command lib tests pass; clippy clean. Twenty-fourth concrete fix this session, fifth and final HELP fix in cluster (after 0o9vo / vtege / kuthf / s574p).

title: table.insert/concat luaL_error wording keeps user_script:1: in pcall(C-builtin) shape
description:
Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4:

  pcall(table.insert,{1,2,3})
    vendored: wrong number of arguments to 'insert'
    fr:       user_script:1: wrong number of arguments to 'insert'

  pcall(table.concat,{},'a',1,2,3,4)   -- empty table, 'concat' invalid-value path
    vendored: invalid value (nil) at index 1 in table for 'concat'
    fr:       user_script:1: invalid value (nil) at index 1 in table for 'concat'

Both are luaL_error wording (no function name in the message itself — the name is already embedded in the message body), so the dual-shape rule is just toggling the user_script:1: prefix.

Two sites in fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs:
1. table.insert wrong-arity at line ~6378
2. table.concat invalid-value at lines ~6501 and ~6508

Fix: use a conditional-prefix helper that prepends 'user_script:1: ' only when current_invocation_name is Some, matching how math.random's wrong-arity was handled in frankenredis-o3epl.
close_reason: Made table.insert wrong-arity (~line 6378) and table.concat invalid-value (~lines 6502/6508) drop the user_script:1: prefix when current_invocation_name is None. Used inline conditional for insert (single call site) and a closure for concat (two arms). Regression test table_insert_concat_luaerror_pcall_shape_toecv pins 5 pcall cases + 2 direct-call regressions. Existing 5 table_concat_* + 4 table_insert_* tests still pass. Live-diff vs vendored 16380: 5/5 pcall + 2/2 direct match.

title: FCALL error formatting differs from vendored (Error running function prefix, missing script: suffix, user_script vs user_function label)
description:
Vendored Redis 7.0+ functions emit a specific error format for runtime
failures inside FCALL/FCALL_RO bodies. fr currently wraps the inner
EVAL-style error with 'ERR Error running function: ' prefix while
vendored uses upstream Lua's user_function:<line> label and appends
'script: <funcname>, on @user_function:<line>.' as suffix.

Repro (vendored at :16380, fr at :16480):
  FUNCTION LOAD '#!lua name=m1
  redis.register_function("e", function(keys, args) return keys[1]..":"..args[1] end)'

  127.0.0.1:16380> FCALL e 0
  vendored: (error) ERR user_function:2: attempt to concatenate field '?' (a nil value) script: e, on @user_function:2.
  fr:       (error) ERR Error running function: user_script:1: attempt to concatenate field '?' (a nil value)

  FUNCTION LOAD '#!lua name=m2
  redis.register_function("er", function() error("boom") end)'

  127.0.0.1:16380> FCALL er 0
  vendored: (error) ERR user_function:2: boom script: er, on @user_function:2.
  fr:       (error) ERR Error running function: user_script:1: boom

  redis.call to unknown command:
  vendored: (error) ERR Unknown Redis command called from script script: er, on @user_function:2.
  fr:       (error) ERR Error running function: ERR Unknown Redis command called from script

  redis.error_reply: both 'ERR custom' (parity, no decoration).
  EVAL: parity (uses 'user_script:N' label and 'script: <sha>, on @user_script:N.' suffix).

Root cause: crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:10367-10375 builds a wrapper
that strips the shebang (line 1) and transforms the register_function
call (line 2) into a 'function NAME(...) end' definition placed on
line 1, then calls eval_script which uses the default 'user_script'
source label. On error, fr prepends 'ERR Error running function: '
to whatever eval_script returned.

Fix plan:
1. Preserve original line numbers: replace shebang line with blank
   space-padded line (matching the trick eval_script already uses
   for EVAL shebangs at line 9982-9989) so the function definition
   stays on its original line.
2. Plumb an optional source_label parameter into eval_script (or a
   sibling eval_script_with_label) defaulting to 'user_script';
   FCALL passes 'user_function'.
3. Drop the 'ERR Error running function: ' wrapper. Instead, if the
   inner error starts with 'user_function:<line>:' (or contains 'ERR'
   from a redis.call), append the vendored suffix
   ' script: <funcname>, on @user_function:<line>.' — matching
   how vendored's f_lua_error_call rewrites the error.
4. For 'ERR ...' style inner errors (from redis.call), insert
   the script suffix between the inner ERR text and the trailing
   newline, mirroring vendored.

Test plan: pin all four FCALL error shapes above to be byte-identical
to vendored using the differential probe pattern.
close_reason: completed

title: REVIEW MODE Tick 14 audit: /testing-fuzzing count-validation sweep complete; 3 defects fixed (z1h45/wsqf2/xiome), no further sites found in current pass
description:
REVIEW MODE Tick 14 audit summary via /testing-fuzzing skill. Across multiple ticks I shipped 3 defect-class fixes via fuzz-style differential review against the vendored 7.2.4 source:

  - z1h45 [MEDIUM]: fr-server inline parser accepted closing quote followed by non-whitespace; upstream sds.c::sdssplitargs errors.
  - wsqf2 [HIGH]: SRANDMEMBER/HRANDFIELD/ZRANDMEMBER negative count i64::MIN caused 9.2-quintillion-iteration DoS; upstream getRangeLongFromObjectOrReply restricts to [-LONG_MAX, LONG_MAX].
  - xiome [LOW]: LPOS RANK accepted i64::MIN; same upstream guard.

This bead documents what's been swept so the next agent doesn't re-traverse:

COVERED (count/rank/range parameter validation):
  - SRANDMEMBER count          (wsqf2)
  - HRANDFIELD count           (wsqf2)
  - ZRANDMEMBER count          (wsqf2)
  - LPOS RANK                  (xiome)
  - SPOP count                 (already non-negative gate)
  - LPOP/RPOP count            (parse_list_pop_count_arg gates)
  - LMPOP/BLMPOP COUNT         (gates >0)
  - ZMPOP/BZMPOP COUNT         (gates >0)
  - XPENDING count             (gates >=0)
  - XRANGE/XREVRANGE COUNT     (clamps negatives to 0, returns null array)
  - XADD MAXLEN/MINID          (gates >=0)
  - XCLAIM min-idle-time       (clamps negatives to 0)
  - SETEX/PSETEX/GETEX seconds (gates >0)
  - WAIT timeout               (gates >=0)
  - WAITAOF timeout            (gates >=0)
  - BLPOP/BRPOP/BL*POP timeout (parse_blocking_deadline_*)
  - SETBIT offset              (gates [0, 2^32))
  - GETBIT offset              (parse_bit_offset_or_reply)
  - BITFIELD #N hash form      (checked_mul guard)
  - BITCOUNT/BITPOS start/end  (saturating arithmetic on i64::MIN)
  - GETRANGE/SUBSTR start/end  (saturating arithmetic; len + i64::MIN doesn't overflow since result still in i64)
  - LRANGE start/stop          (normalize_index uses saturating_add)
  - SCAN-family cursor         (u64 with checked arithmetic)
  - LCS MINMATCHLEN            (clamps negatives to 0)
  - GEOSEARCH BYRADIUS/BYBOX   (gates negative; infinity tolerated like upstream)
  - CLIENT KILL ID             (gates >0)
  - CLIENT TRACKING REDIRECT   (gates >0)
  - CLIENT PAUSE timeout       (gates >=0)
  - SLOWLOG GET count          (gates >=-1)
  - DEBUG SLEEP seconds        (f64 NaN/inf gates)
  - COPY DB index              (range-checked)
  - parse_i64_arg               (audited extensively — rejects all overflow cases including 19+ digit overflow via incremental u64 multiply check; correctly accepts i64::MIN=-9223372036854775808 and rejects i64::MIN-1; rejects '-0', '+5', leading-zero, leading/trailing whitespace, NUL bytes)
  - parse_f64_arg               (rejects NaN, infinity-saturation from non-explicit literals)
  - parse_geo_f64               (rejects NaN, accepts infinity)

STILL POTENTIALLY UNAUDITED (worth a future fuzzing pass):
  - HRANDFIELD/ZRANDMEMBER WITHSCORES extra range check (upstream restricts to [-LONG_MAX/2, LONG_MAX/2] when WITHSCORES is set; fr accepts any non-i64::MIN)
  - HEXPIRE family (Redis 7.4 — vendored 7.2.4 has no reference)
  - XAUTOCLAIM count argument
  - DEBUG STRINGMATCH-LEN
  - PFCOUNT/PFMERGE with very long key lists
  - inline parser hex-escape edge cases beyond `\x` truncation

Per REVIEW-MODE.md cycle: rotated to /testing-fuzzing for this round, found 3 fixes. Next tick should rotate to a fresh skill (e.g. /profiling-software-performance, /deadlock-finder-and-fixer, /testing-conformance-harnesses, /testing-golden-artifacts) — /testing-fuzzing has been the active skill for the last 3 ticks and the tight wins are exhausted.
close_reason: Informational audit summary already represents completed work — closing to drop from the ready queue. The actionable defects from each cycle were filed as separate beads and have been shipped or remain on the backlog as discrete issues.

title: fr-server: avoid recursive EINTR in replication read

title: CONFIG GET wildcard drops replica-priority and replica-serve-stale-data aliases
description:
Differential probe vs vendored redis 7.2.4:

  CONFIG GET '*' diff (replica/slave keys only):
  vendored has both replica-priority and slave-priority
  fr has only slave-priority

  CONFIG GET '*priority*':
    vendored: replica-priority|100|slave-priority|100
    fr:        slave-priority|50

  CONFIG GET '*serve-stale*':
    vendored: slave-serve-stale-data|yes|replica-serve-stale-data|yes
    fr:        slave-serve-stale-data|yes

CONFIG GET <exact_name> works (so direct query 'replica-priority'
returns the value), but wildcard-pattern matching emits only one
alias. Same bug pattern at fr-runtime/src/lib.rs:7693-7722 for both
configs:

  if pattern_matches("replica-X") || pattern_matches("slave-X") {
    let key = if pattern_matches("slave-X") { "slave-X" }
              else { "replica-X" };
    emit(key, value);
  }

When pattern is '*', it matches both aliases but the if/else
picks just slave-X. Upstream emits BOTH names independently
when a pattern matches both (each is a separate config entry).

Fix: replace the if/else with two independent emits:
  if pattern_matches("slave-X") { emit("slave-X", value); }
  if pattern_matches("replica-X") { emit("replica-X", value); }
The duplicate value emission is correct — upstream treats the two
names as distinct entries pointing at the same value.

Severity: minor — direct CONFIG GET still works for both names;
only wildcard expansion is affected. Most clients query specific
names.
close_reason: Replaced the collapsing if/else with two independent pattern checks for each alias pair. Now CONFIG GET '*' emits all 16 replica-*/slave-* keys (was 14). Pattern '*priority*' returns both slave-priority and replica-priority; '*serve-stale*' returns both. Direct CONFIG GET for either name still works and CONFIG SET on one updates both (true alias). Live diff matches vendored 7.2.4 exactly.

title: fr-command: 76 push_str(&format!()) sites in INFO renderer + LATENCY HISTORY allocate intermediate Strings — convert to write!() to halve renderer allocations
description:
/profiling-software-performance audit:

  grep -nE 'push_str\(&format!' crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs | wc -l
  → 76

Each call allocates a heap String, then push_str copies it into 'info'/'report' and drops the temporary. The INFO renderer is run on every INFO command (frequent path: monitoring, dashboards, every Sentinel ping). Mirrors the mec2q follow-up that already cleaned the fr-runtime side (24 sites, commit ee884f8).

Fix: introduce 'use std::fmt::Write as _' and convert each call to 'write!(buf, ...)'. The trailing-\\n LATENCY HISTORY case becomes writeln!(...) (without explicit \\n) to satisfy clippy::write_with_newline.

Verification: cargo clippy -p fr-command --tests -D warnings clean; INFO test suite (19 tests) green. Differential probe vs upstream untouched (output is byte-identical, only allocation pattern changes).
close_reason: Converted all 76 sites in fr-command/src/lib.rs to write!()/writeln!(). Added use std::fmt::Write as _ import. Clippy clean under -D warnings; 19 INFO unit tests green; live_pubsub_help_and_arity_follow_redis_syntax also passes (sanity).

title: ZADD checks pair-arity before flag conflicts + SUBSCRIBE-mode lowercases command name
description: (1) ZADD with no score/member pairs (e.g. 'ZADD z NX XX') returned conflict-specific wording ('ERR XX and NX options at the same time are not compatible'); upstream t_zset.c::zaddGenericCommand validates the score/member pair-arity BEFORE checking flag conflicts so the missing-pairs case yields plain 'syntax error' (2) fr-server's SUBSCRIBE-mode rejection echoed the user-typed command bytes verbatim, so 'GET' came back uppercase; upstream networking.c uses c->cmd->name (lowercase canonical from command table) when formatting the rejection

title: Wire CONFIG SET client-query-buffer-limit and proto-max-bulk-len to fr-server
description:
## Problem

fr-server/src/main.rs line 36 defines `const QUERY_BUFFER_LIMIT: usize = 1024 * 1024` (1 MiB). Redis defaults are:
- client-query-buffer-limit: 1 GiB
- proto-max-bulk-len: 512 MiB

The current 1 MiB limit is 1000x too restrictive. Any client sending a bulk string > 1 MiB (e.g., a large SET value) gets disconnected. This breaks real-world Redis usage patterns.

Additionally, fr-config/src/lib.rs line 66-67 has `CompatibilityGate` defaults of max_bulk_len=8MiB which also contradicts the config table's proto-max-bulk-len=512000000.

## What Redis Does

Both parameters are configurable via CONFIG SET. The query buffer limit controls the maximum size of the client input buffer (protecting against memory abuse). The proto-max-bulk-len controls the maximum size of a single bulk string in the RESP protocol.

## Implementation Plan

1. Add `query_buffer_limit` and `proto_max_bulk_len` to ServerState
2. Handle CONFIG SET for both parameters
3. In fr-server, read the limit from runtime instead of the const
4. Ensure fr-protocol's parse_frame respects the bulk length limit
5. Update CompatibilityGate defaults to match Redis (512 MiB for proto-max-bulk-len)

## Impact

This is critical for real-world usage — 1 MiB query buffer silently breaks any application storing values > 1 MiB, which is extremely common (images, serialized objects, large JSON documents).

## Files to Modify

- `crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs`: Add fields, CONFIG SET handler
- `crates/fr-server/src/main.rs`: Replace const with runtime value
- `crates/fr-config/src/lib.rs`: Fix CompatibilityGate defaults
close_reason: query_buffer_limit (1 GiB default) and proto_max_bulk_len (512 MiB default) now on ServerState, CONFIG SET/GET wired, fr-server reads runtime values

The optimized classifier at crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs:1209 disagrees with classify_cluster_subcommand_linear at :1254 for at least one sampled subcommand. Repair the optimized CLUSTER subcommand classifier so it exactly matches the linear reference again.

title: Fix RESP parser read_line off-by-one at MAX_LINE_LENGTH boundary
description:
## Problem

fr-protocol/src/lib.rs line 260: The read_line loop condition \"while i + 1 < max_search\" means the last position checked for \\r is max_search-2. If \\r falls at exactly max_search-1 (offset start + MAX_LINE_LENGTH - 1) with \\n at max_search, the valid \\r\\n pair is missed and the line is incorrectly rejected as LineTooLong.

## Impact

Low — this only affects RESP lines whose content (excluding \\r\\n) is exactly MAX_LINE_LENGTH-1 bytes long (64 KiB - 1). This is a rare edge case but violates the principle that any valid RESP frame should parse correctly.

## Fix

Change the loop condition from \"i + 1 < max_search\" to \"i + 1 <= max_search\" (or equivalently, \"i < max_search\"). Ensure the i+1 bounds check for \\n doesn't read past input.len().

## Files to Modify

- crates/fr-protocol/src/lib.rs: read_line loop condition
close_reason: Fixed read_line loop to check full MAX_LINE_LENGTH range before declaring LineTooLong

title: RDB snapshot persistence pipeline

title: fr-runtime: ACL GENPASS hex conversion allocates O(N) small Strings then collects, then re-wraps to bytes
description:
crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs:6448 builds the ACL GENPASS hex output as

  let hex: String = buf.iter().map(|b| format!("{b:02x}")).collect();
  let truncated = &hex[..hex_chars];
  RespFrame::BulkString(Some(truncated.as_bytes().to_vec()))

For the default 256-bit password that's 32 separate `format!` allocations, one Vec<u8>→str→Vec<u8> round-trip, and an unnecessary truncation slice. Each call allocates 32 small Strings + the collected String + the new Vec<u8> for the bulk frame.

Direct constant-factor cleanup: write each byte's two hex nibbles into a pre-allocated Vec<u8> via a 16-byte hex lookup table, then surface it as the BulkString without going through String at all. Mirrors upstream acl.c::ACLGenerateRandomBytesAsHex which writes directly into the reply buffer.

Acceptance: ACL GENPASS continues to byte-match upstream (lowercase hex, length = ceil(bits/4)); zero allocations for the conversion itself beyond the single output Vec.

Reference: /profiling-software-performance audit on 2026-05-04.
notes: Replaced the per-byte format!()+collect with a 16-byte hex lookup table writing directly into the output Vec<u8>. Avoids 32 small String allocations + one intermediate String + one as_bytes().to_vec() round-trip per ACL GENPASS call (default 256-bit). Existing 47 ACL tests pass; clippy clean. Mirrors upstream acl.c::ACLGenerateRandomBytesAsHex.

title: fr-protocol RESP3 double ',' and big-number '(' parsers accept any UTF-8 — empty payloads and malformed numeric content slip through as BulkString
description:
REVIEW MODE — extending peer commit ee97bea / ny5fu's RESP3 null tightening to the sibling laxity in the same parse_frame_internal arm.

Source: crates/fr-protocol/src/lib.rs:259-265

  b',' | b'(' => {
      let (line, consumed) = read_line(input, next)?;
      let s = std::str::from_utf8(line)
          .map_err(|_| RespParseError::InvalidUtf8)?
          .to_string();
      Ok((RespFrame::BulkString(Some(s.into_bytes())), consumed))
  }

Currently both prefixes accept any UTF-8 line as the value:
  ',\\r\\n'         → BulkString(Some(b''))         empty double
  ',abc\\r\\n'      → BulkString(Some(b'abc'))      garbage double
  '(\\r\\n'         → BulkString(Some(b''))         empty big number
  '(not_a_num\\r\\n' → BulkString(Some(b'not_a_num')) garbage big number

Per RESP3 spec, both prefix types require a parseable numeric body — double must parse as f64 (allowing 'inf', '-inf', 'nan'), big number must be a base-10 integer (optionally signed). Accepting empty / non-numeric content weakens the fail-closed parser contract the same way ny5fu's ',' (null) flaw did.

Reachable from any HELLO 3 client; the parser is exposed to authenticated client bytes when allow_resp3 = true. Malformed oracle/fuzz inputs currently pass as 'valid' frames, which can mask real protocol divergences.

Fix: reject empty payloads + validate parseability for both prefixes. Double: try line.parse::<f64>(); on failure return InvalidInteger. Big number: require all digits with an optional leading +/-; on failure return InvalidInteger. Add regression tests pinning the rejection wording and confirming valid forms still parse to BulkString(Some(...)).
close_reason: Tightened the ',' (double) and '(' (big number) RESP3 prefix arms in parse_frame_internal. Double now requires line.parse::<f64>() to succeed (allowing inf/-inf/nan but rejecting empty / 'abc' / '1.2.3'). Big number now requires all decimal digits with an optional leading +/- (rejecting empty / 'abc' / '+' / '1.5' / '123abc'). Both reject with InvalidInteger — matching ny5fu's hardening pattern for the '_' (null) prefix. Two new pin tests: resp3_double_rejects_empty_and_non_numeric_payloads (3 reject + 6 valid forms) and resp3_big_number_rejects_empty_and_non_numeric_payloads (5 reject + 4 valid forms). All 109 fr-protocol tests pass; clippy clean.

title: Lua _G global not exposed (sandbox surface)
description: fr's Lua sandbox doesn't expose _G as a global, but vendored Redis 7.2.4 has type(_G)=='table'. _G is the globals table itself; scripts use it to read/write globals dynamically (e.g. _G[name]=value pattern). Mirroring vendored requires creating a synthetic LuaTable that proxies reads/writes to/from LuaState::globals, plus respecting globals_locked for the readonly-table errors. Currently fr's globals are a HashMap<String, LuaValue> rather than a LuaTable. Discovered while running REVIEW PHASE.
close_reason: Implemented _G as a snapshot LuaTable installed at globals-lock time (right before exec_block in execute()). The snapshot mirrors every registered global plus a _G→_G self-reference. The table's metatable carries two RustFunction handlers: __fr_g_protected_index emits the standard 'Script attempted to access nonexistent global variable' error when an unknown key is queried under globals_locked (and forwards to the live globals map for known names that somehow weren't in the snapshot); __fr_g_readonly_newindex emits the 'Attempt to modify a readonly table' error for any write. Reads of known names like _G.tostring resolve directly from the snapshot since fr's globals are immutable after locking. pairs(_G) iterates the snapshot, so scripts can introspect the sandbox surface. Verified live diff vs vendored 7.2.4 (port 16380): 10/10 cases match including type, tostring, undef error, known-name lookup, self-ref, readonly write, pairs iteration (>20 entries), reference equality, bracket lookup, and that bare undef name access still errors separately. Pinned by lua_g_global_table_exposes_sandboxed_globals covering 6 sub-cases. All 924 fr-command lib tests pass.

title: cjson.encode accepts 0 args + cjson.encode/decode argerror lack pcall shape
description:
Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4:

  pcall(cjson.encode)
    vendored: bad argument #1 to '?' (expected 1 argument)
    fr:       null                                        (silently encodes nil)

  pcall(cjson.encode, function() end)
    vendored: Cannot serialise function: type not supported
    fr:       user_script:1: Cannot serialise function: type not supported

  pcall(cjson.decode)
    vendored: bad argument #1 to '?' (expected 1 argument)
    fr:       user_script:1: bad argument #1 to 'decode' (expected 1 argument)

  pcall(cjson.decode, nil)
    vendored: bad argument #1 to '?' (string expected, got nil)
    fr:       user_script:1: bad argument #1 to 'decode' (string expected, got nil)

Three fixes in fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs lines ~6857-6900:

1. cjson.encode at line ~6858: validate args.len() == 1 and error with "bad argument #1 to <name> (expected 1 argument)" via lua_format_argerror. Currently fr falls back to encoding nil → "null".

2. cjson.encode at line ~6864: the luaL_error-style wording from lua_value_to_json ("Cannot serialise ...") embeds no function name — drop user_script:1: prefix when invoked via pcall(C-builtin).

3. cjson.decode at lines ~6873 and ~6888: route the two bad-argument errors through lua_format_argerror so the name resolves to '?' under pcall and the prefix drops.

NOTE: separate, deeper JSON parser error wording divergences ("invalid JSON: X" vs "Expected value but found invalid token at character N") are tracked in P4 deferred beads frankenredis-9pvke (wording differences) and frankenredis-qatse (strict parser rewrite). This bead only handles the argerror dual-shape and the empty-args validation.
close_reason: Three fixes in cjson.encode/decode handlers at lua_eval.rs lines ~6857-6900: (1) cjson.encode now validates args.len() >= 1 (was silently encoding nil → 'null'); (2) cjson.encode 'Cannot serialise X' error drops user_script:1: prefix when invoked via pcall(C-builtin); (3) cjson.decode's two bad-argument errors route through lua_format_argerror. Regression test cjson_encode_decode_arg_validation_and_pcall_shape_u4mn6 pins 6 pcall cases + 3 direct-call regressions + 2 sanity-check valid usages. Live-diff vs vendored 16380: 6/6 pcall + 2/2 direct cases match. Existing 7 cjson tests still pass.

title: CLUSTER HELP/INFO/SLOTS/NODES blanket-return cluster_disabled even when cluster_enabled=true

Other arms (LINKS, MYID, SHARDS, MYSHARDID, etc) already handle cluster_enabled=true correctly. Start by porting CLUSTER HELP (pure static array) and CLUSTER SLOTS (empty array since fr has no peers); INFO and NODES (synthesized state text) are larger follow-ups.

title: Lua tostring(number) precision and scientific-notation divergence from %.14g
description: fr's LuaValue::to_display_string for Number uses Rust's default f64 Display, which yields 17 significant digits and never switches to scientific notation. Lua 5.1 (and Redis 7.2.4) uses LUAI_NUMFMT = '%.14g' which limits to 14 significant digits and switches to scientific when |exponent| > some threshold. Live divergences: tostring(1/3) -> fr '0.3333333333333333' vs vendored '0.33333333333333'; tostring(1e100) -> fr emits a 101-character decimal vs vendored '1e+100'; tostring(math.pi) -> fr '3.141592653589793' vs vendored '3.1415926535898'. This affects EVERY tostring(number), string.format('%g'), table.concat with numbers, and indirect concatenation paths. Fix: replace the default Display in fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs:430 with a hand-written %.14g formatter (integer-valued fast path stays). Should pick fixed vs scientific based on the decimal exponent (-4 <= E < 14 => fixed; otherwise => scientific), use 14 significant digits, strip trailing zeros after the decimal point (and the decimal point itself if no fractional remains). Acceptance: tostring(1/3)/(math.pi)/(1e100) and string.format('%g', ...) all match vendored byte-exact.
close_reason: Lua tostring(number) now mirrors C's %.14g format exactly, matching Redis 7.2.4 LUAI_NUMFMT. Implementation: replaced fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs:430 fallback 'format!({n})' with a hand-rolled lua_number_to_string helper. The helper: (1) handles nan/inf/-inf/0 special cases; (2) computes the decimal exponent via log10().floor(); (3) if exponent < -4 or >= 14, emits scientific notation via format!('{:.13e}',n) post-processed by rust_e_to_c_g to switch 'e5' -> 'e+05' and 'e-5' -> 'e-05' (C printf %g pads exponent to 2 digits with sign); (4) otherwise emits fixed-precision format!('{:.<frac>}',n) with frac = max(0, 14-1-exponent); (5) strip_trailing_zeros removes trailing '0's and a trailing '.' from the fractional portion. Live differential probe: 14 number-formatting cases byte-exact (1/3, 0.1, 1.0, 1e100, 1/3*3, math.pi, math.huge, 1e-5, 1e-4, 9.999999999999e13, 1234567890, -1.5, 0.0001, 0.00009). Tests: fr-command 910 pass.

title: INCRBYFLOAT result encoding is embstr/raw, never int
description: Upstream t_string.c::incrbyfloatCommand stores the result via createStringObject which always produces embstr (≤44 chars) or raw, never int. fr was running the int-encoding shortcut on the result string, so 'INCRBYFLOAT n 0' on an int-encoded key with value 1 produced 'int' encoding (string '1' parses as integer). Added a per-entry force_string_encoding flag distinct from force_raw_encoding (which forces raw); INCRBYFLOAT sets it so the embstr-vs-raw threshold still applies but the int-detection shortcut is skipped

title: fr-runtime: CONFIG GET * missing 63 upstream config keys (TLS, slave/replica aliases, cluster-announce-*, syslog-*, oom-score-adj, etc.)
description:
Differential probe of fr (port 16700) vs vendored redis 7.2.4 (port 16701) shows fr's CONFIG GET '*' returns 143 keys; upstream returns 195. After deduplicating fr's static + dynamic config sources against the upstream live set, 63 upstream keys are absent from fr entirely:

  TLS (~17): tls-auth-clients, tls-ca-cert-dir, tls-ca-cert-file, tls-cert-file, tls-ciphers, tls-ciphersuites, tls-client-cert-file, tls-client-key-file, tls-client-key-file-pass, tls-cluster, tls-dh-params-file, tls-key-file, tls-key-file-pass, tls-port, tls-prefer-server-ciphers, tls-replication, tls-session-cache-size, tls-session-cache-timeout, tls-session-caching
  cluster-announce-* (~5): bus-port, ip, port, tls-port + cluster-port, cluster-allow-replica-migration, cluster-replica-validity-factor, cluster-slave-no-failover, cluster-slave-validity-factor
  syslog (~3): syslog-enabled, syslog-facility, syslog-ident
  slave-* aliases (~7): slave-announce-ip, slave-announce-port, slave-ignore-maxmemory, slave-lazy-flush, slave-read-only, slaveof, slave-* aliases for replica-*
  replica-announce/ignore (~5): replica-announce-ip, replica-announce-port, replica-ignore-disk-write-errors, replica-ignore-maxmemory, replica-priority
  min-slaves-* aliases (2): min-slaves-max-lag, min-slaves-to-write
  cpu/oom (~6): aof_rewrite_cpulist, bgsave_cpulist, bio_cpulist, server_cpulist, oom-score-adj, oom-score-adj-values
  misc: ignore-warnings, locale-collate, maxmemory-clients, propagation-error-behavior, repl-disable-tcp-nodelay, repl-diskless-sync-max-replicas, repl-ping-slave-period, set-max-listpack-value, shutdown-timeout, socket-mark-id, supervised, tracking-table-max-keys, crash-log-enabled, crash-memcheck-enabled

Upstream emits these via createBoolConfig/createStringConfig/createIntConfig in config.c with their declared defaults regardless of whether the underlying subsystem is wired up (e.g., TLS keys appear even with TLS disabled). fr should mirror at least the shape — emitting upstream defaults for CONFIG GET so monitoring tools that grep these names see consistent output. CONFIG SET wiring is out-of-scope for this bead; just CONFIG GET parity.

Acceptance: CONFIG GET '*' returns ≥195 keys, all 63 listed above present with their upstream Redis 7.2.4 default values. Existing CONFIG SET passthrough behavior unchanged.
notes:
Added 65 missing static config entries (one tuple each in CONFIG_STATIC_PARAMS) with their upstream Redis 7.2.4 defaults from config.c create*Config calls. Covers TLS (tls-*, ~17), slave-* legacy aliases (~7), cluster-announce-* + cluster-port + cluster-allow-replica-migration + cluster-replica/slave-validity-factor (~9), syslog-* (3), oom-score-adj + oom-score-adj-values, *-cpulist (4), repl-* and replica-ignore-* aliases, and miscellaneous (supervised, shutdown-timeout, propagation-error-behavior, tracking-table-max-keys, replica-priority, set-max-listpack-value, crash-log-enabled, crash-memcheck-enabled, ignore-warnings, locale-collate, maxmemory-clients, min-slaves-max-lag/to-write, repl-disable-tcp-nodelay, repl-diskless-sync-max-replicas, socket-mark-id).

CONFIG SET wiring for these is intentionally out-of-scope; the goal is shape parity for monitoring tools (Sentinel/redis_exporter/RedisInsight). Test config_get_returns_upstream_static_keys_for_tls_and_aliases pins the upstream defaults for the highest-impact subset (29 keys spanning every category).

title: fr-store: GETRANGE with fully-negative range returns empty instead of clamped slice
description:
fr-store::getrange at crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs:2290 only clamps the START offset to 0 after the negative-offset normalization, leaving END negative when both inputs are negative AND large in magnitude. The 's > e' guard then wrongly returns empty. Upstream t_string.c::getrangeCommand clamps both.

Repro:
  SET k 'Xbcdef' (len 6)
  GETRANGE k -100 -90:
    fr returns ''
    upstream returns 'X' (clamped to [0..0])

Fix: clamp  to 0 the same way  is clamped.
close_reason: GETRANGE with fully-negative offsets now clamps both start and end to 0, returning the upstream-aligned clamped slice instead of empty. Verified across 10 negative-offset edge cases against vendored redis 7.2.4. Shipped in f69d42e.

title: fr-command: small zset error wording divergences (ZRANGEBYSCORE BAD-BOUND, ZUNIONSTORE WEIGHTS BAD, ZINTERCARD LIMIT BAD)
description:
Three small wording divergences from upstream Redis 7.2:
  - ZRANGEBYSCORE BAD-BOUND 5: fr 'value is not a valid float', redis 'min or max is not a float'
  - ZUNIONSTORE dst 1 z WEIGHTS BAD: fr 'value is not an integer or out of range', redis 'weight value is not a float'
  - ZINTERCARD 1 z LIMIT -1 / BAD: fr 'value is not an integer or out of range', redis 'LIMIT can't be negative'

All cosmetic but clients that match on error wording will trip. Fix at the parser: replace the generic float/int error with the upstream wording for these specific contexts.

title: fr-command: NOGROUP error wording differs from upstream (XPENDING/XAUTOCLAIM)
description:
XPENDING / XAUTOCLAIM emit 'NOGROUP No such consumer group <g> for key name <k>' when the consumer group is missing. Upstream Redis 7.2 emits 'NOGROUP No such key <k> or consumer group <g>' (note the 'No such KEY ... or consumer group' wording, used for both missing-key and missing-group cases — see legacy_redis_code/redis/src/t_stream.c).

Acceptance: error text byte-equal to upstream. Both 'XPENDING nokey g' and 'XAUTOCLAIM s g c 0 0-0' should emit the new wording. Reference: ad-hoc differential probe of fr (port 16700) vs vendored Redis 7.2.4 (port 16701). Fix: search-replace the old error wording in fr-command (likely a const).
close_reason: Added XPENDING/XAUTOCLAIM missing-group NOGROUP wording regression; fr-command tests pass via rch

title: fr-command MEMORY STATS returns flat Array even in RESP3 — same shape divergence as i40x2/e4njz
description:
/mock-code-finder follow-up to i40x2 (CLIENT TRACKINGINFO) and e4njz (ACL GETUSER):

  MEMORY STATS (RESP3, with HELLO 3 / -3)
    upstream: Map shape — 'peak.allocated 3105808 / total.allocated 3105784 / ...'
    fr      : flat Array — every key and value on its own RESP element

Source: crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:15165-15253 — memory_cmd's STATS branch unconditionally builds RespFrame::Array. The TODO comment at line 15172 already documents the expected dual-shape behavior:

  // Upstream object.c::memoryCommand emits a RESP map (or
  // RESP2 alternating-key/value array) with 27 fixed fields
  // plus per-db sub-maps.

But the implementation never branches on store.dispatch_client_ctx.resp_protocol_version.

The pattern fr already uses correctly elsewhere (HGETALL, CLIENT TRACKINGINFO via i40x2, ACL GETUSER via e4njz) is to emit RespFrame::Map under RESP3 with (key, value) pairs and the existing flat Array under RESP2.

Fix: walk the accumulated items in 2-strides, pair them up into (key, value) tuples for RESP3 Map, keep the flat Array path for RESP2. The db.0 sub-map (a single key with an Array of its own) becomes one Map entry whose value is the inner Array.

Severity P1 because MEMORY STATS is a frequently-polled introspection command for monitoring stacks; under RESP3 the wire shape is wrong.
close_reason: fr-command MEMORY STATS now branches on store.dispatch_client_ctx.resp_protocol_version: emits RespFrame::Map under RESP3 by walking the accumulated items vector in 2-strides into (key, value) pairs; RESP2 stays the flat Array. Two new pin tests: memory_stats_under_resp3_returns_map_shape (asserts Map with first 3 keys = peak.allocated/total.allocated/startup.allocated) and memory_stats_under_resp2_stays_flat_array (asserts >=40-element flat array, every-other-element key). Existing 13 memory subcommand tests still pass; clippy clean. Same pattern as i40x2 (TRACKINGINFO) and e4njz (ACL GETUSER).

title: fr-store: list_max_listpack_value forces quicklist for any 65+ byte element, diverges from upstream byte-budget-only logic
description:
Sister bug to frankenredis-llry. After removing the spurious entries-count gate, fr-store's object_encoding for Value::List still has a per-value size cap:

  if self.list_fits_legacy_listpack_size(l)
      && l.iter().all(|v| v.len() <= self.list_max_listpack_value)

`list_max_listpack_value` defaults to 64; the field is wired through CONFIG GET/SET as `list-max-listpack-value`, but this config is not present in upstream Redis 7.2 (it's an fr invention, mirroring `list-max-listpack-entries`).

Differential probe vs vendored redis 7.2.4:

  RPUSH l <65-byte value>   → upstream: listpack    fr: quicklist
  RPUSH l <100-byte value>  → upstream: listpack    fr: quicklist
  RPUSH l <8000-byte value> → upstream: listpack    fr: quicklist  (still under 8 KiB budget)
  RPUSH l <9000-byte value> → upstream: quicklist  fr: quicklist  (byte budget exceeded)

Upstream t_list.c::listTypeTryConversion uses only the byte-budget check; there is no separate per-value cap.

Fix: drop the `l.iter().all(|v| v.len() <= self.list_max_listpack_value)` check from object_encoding. Keep the field/config as a benign no-op for compatibility with existing CONFIG SET callers (mirrors the llry treatment of list-max-listpack-entries).

Acceptance: a list with one 8000-byte element reports OBJECT ENCODING == "listpack" matching vendored redis 7.2.4. Existing test list_encoding_quicklist_when_any_value_exceeds_64_bytes is updated to match upstream byte-budget semantics.

Reference: differential probe of fr (port 16700) vs vendored redis 7.2.4 (port 16701) on 2026-05-04 while extending llry coverage.
notes:
Removed the per-element value-size cap from object_encoding's list branch. Now relies solely on the byte-budget check via list_fits_legacy_listpack_size, matching upstream t_list.c::listTypeTryConversion. The fr-only list-max-listpack-value config remains read/write through CONFIG SET as a benign no-op (mirrors llry's handling of list-max-listpack-entries).

Existing test list_encoding_quicklist_when_any_value_exceeds_64_bytes was renamed to list_encoding_listpack_for_values_above_legacy_per_value_cap and inverted to assert listpack — pinning the new upstream-aligned behavior. Differential probe vs vendored redis 7.2.4 confirmed: 65/100/8000-byte single-element lists report listpack on both fr and upstream.

Covers: GEOADD/GEODIST/GEORADIUS/GEOSEARCH/GEOPOS/GEOHASH. For every divergence file a [HIGH] review bead and mark the case XFAIL. Acceptance: test passes under STRICT with all divergences explicitly XFAIL'd.

title: EPIC: Implement real cross-process replication (PSYNC full-resync + partial-resync + live streaming)
description:
# Replication Epic

## Why This Is Critical
FEATURE_PARITY.md claims "parity_green" for replication, but this is false. The state machine logic (665 lines in fr-repl) is tested in isolation, but:
- REPLICAOF returns "OK" without actually connecting to a primary
- No bytes ever flow between two FrankenRedis processes
- Cannot replicate from a real Redis primary
- A real Redis replica cannot connect to FrankenRedis as primary
- Zero cross-process replication tests exist

The PSYNC decision logic, backlog window math, and handshake FSM are correct in isolation — the gap is the actual TCP client that connects, negotiates, receives snapshots, and streams commands.

## What Success Looks Like
1. `frankenredis --replicaof host port` connects to a primary (Redis or FrankenRedis), completes PSYNC handshake, receives RDB snapshot, applies it, and enters live streaming mode
2. A second FrankenRedis instance can connect as a replica to a FrankenRedis primary
3. Writes to the primary appear on the replica within bounded latency
4. Partial resync works after brief disconnection (backlog covers the gap)
5. An integration test demonstrates this end-to-end

## Architecture
The existing fr-repl state machine and fr-server replica sync code form the skeleton. The missing pieces are:
1. Real TCP client connection in REPLICAOF handler (currently a no-op)
2. Backlog buffer that actually stores propagated commands
3. Command propagation: after each write command, serialize and push to replica write buffers
4. Snapshot streaming: encode current store as RDB and send to new replicas
5. Live command streaming: send write commands to connected replicas in real-time

## Non-Goals for V1
- Multi-master / cluster replication
- Sentinel integration
- Diskless replication (can always go through RDB file)
close_reason: All sub-tasks complete. Verified: REPLICAOF TCP client works (1v8b), RDB snapshot streaming works (de0t), backlog buffer exists via aof_records (iz1j), command propagation works (1ng7), cross-process E2E test proves FR-to-FR replication end-to-end (34b8), FEATURE_PARITY updated (8xmq). Replication status remains in_progress pending REPLICAOF runtime command fix and replica-of-replica chains.

title: [HIGH] BITCOUNT wrong-arity + BITFIELD OVERFLOW wording drift
description: Surfaced by live_redis_core_bitmap_matches_runtime: bitcount_wrong_arity_too_many — upstream quietly returns Integer(0) for BITCOUNT with too-many args (undocumented leniency), ours returns 'ERR syntax error'. bitfield_overflow_invalid — ours: 'ERR Invalid OVERFLOW type (should be one of WRAP, SAT, FAIL)' vs upstream 'ERR Invalid OVERFLOW type specified'. Fix in fr-command BITCOUNT + BITFIELD OVERFLOW parsers.
close_reason: Validated existing BITCOUNT precedence and BITFIELD OVERFLOW wording parity; core_bitmap live oracle passes

title: CLUSTER subcommand wrong-arity check fires before cluster_enabled gate; upstream gates first
description: Upstream cluster.c::clusterCommand starts with 'if (server.cluster_enabled == 0) { addReplyError(c,"This instance has cluster support disabled"); return; }' — meaning the cluster_enabled gate fires BEFORE any subcommand-level arity / option validation. fr's cluster_cmd runs per-arm checks like 'if argv.len() != X { return wrong_arity }; if !cluster_enabled { return cluster_disabled }', so calls like 'CLUSTER MYID extra' against a non-cluster instance return wrong-arity instead of cluster-disabled. Add a top-of-function cluster_enabled gate after the basic argv.len() < 2 check (which mirrors upstream's table-level CLUSTER arity = -2 check), so every subcommand path consistently emits cluster-disabled when cluster mode is off. Last tick (frankenredis-53ati) plugged the unknown-subcommand fallthrough; this generalises to all per-arm paths.
close_reason: wontfix

title: Implement COMMAND LIST FILTERBY MODULE/ACLCAT/PATTERN
close_reason: Implemented COMMAND LIST FILTERBY MODULE/ACLCAT/PATTERN. MODULE returns empty (no modules), ACLCAT uses commands_in_acl_category, PATTERN uses glob_match

title: XACK looks up key/group before validating IDs
description: fr-command xack_cmd parsed all IDs first (rejecting 'abc' with 'Invalid stream ID specified as stream command argument'). Upstream t_stream.c::xackCommand calls lookupKeyRead and streamLookupCG before validating IDs and short-circuits with 0 when either is missing - so 'XACK missing-key group abc' returns 0 not an error. Now uses xpending_summary as a non-mutating existence probe before parsing IDs

title: XADD: invalid ID validated before field-count and after fall-through LIMIT
description: fr-command xadd: (1) LIMIT consumed token even without trailing value, returning generic syntax error; upstream t_stream.c::streamParseAddOrTrimArgsOrReply only consumes LIMIT when (i+1) < remaining, otherwise the token falls through and becomes the candidate stream ID (2) explicit-ID validation happened after field-count check, so an invalid ID like 'LIMIT' yielded WrongArity instead of upstream's 'Invalid stream ID specified as stream command argument' from streamParseStrictIDOrReply

title: fr-command: CLUSTER ADDSLOTSRANGE odd-args + SET-CONFIG-EPOCH missing-args reverse upstream's check ordering
description:
Upstream cluster.c::clusterCommand checks server.cluster_enabled FIRST (replying 'This instance has cluster support disabled'), then dispatches to subcommand handlers which can do further validation. The table-level commands.def arity check fires BEFORE clusterCommand though.

Two ordering issues:
1. CLUSTER ADDSLOTSRANGE 1 2 3 (argc=5): commands.def arity = -4 passes; in standalone mode upstream emits 'cluster support disabled' (the multiple-of-2 pair check is inside the subcommand handler, after the cluster gate). fr was running the pair check at the top and emitting wrong-arity instead.
2. CLUSTER SET-CONFIG-EPOCH (argc=2): commands.def arity = 3 fails table-level; upstream emits 'wrong number of arguments for cluster|set-config-epoch command'. fr emitted 'cluster support disabled' regardless.

Fix: split the ADDSLOTSRANGE/DELSLOTSRANGE arity check (only argc<4 triggers wrong-arity early; the multiple-of-2 check now runs after the cluster_enabled gate). For SET-CONFIG-EPOCH, validate argc==3 before the cluster-disabled error.

title: Lua string.char error lacks 'user_script:1:' source prefix
description:
Vendored Redis 7.2.4 emits Lua argument errors via luaL_argerror, which prepends "user_script:1: " (the source-location prefix) before the argument template. fr's string.char handler returned the bare wording.

Reproduction:
  EVAL "return string.char(256)" 0
    vendored: ERR user_script:1: bad argument #1 to 'char' (invalid value)
    fr:       ERR bad argument #1 to 'char' (invalid value)
  EVAL "return string.char(-1)" 0
    vendored: ERR user_script:1: bad argument #1 to 'char' (invalid value)
    fr:       ERR bad argument #1 to 'char' (invalid value)

Impact: low — wording-only, but client error pattern matching that checks for the source-location prefix would mis-treat fr's reply. Same shape of fix already applied to other builtins (string.format, etc.).

Test plan: pin EVAL "return string.char(256)" / "return string.char(-1)" returning the user_script-prefixed error.

title: fr-conformance fixtures: 3 cases pin behaviors that don't exist in upstream Redis 7.2.4 (swapdb_non_integer, zscan_novalues_*) — fr matches upstream, fixtures are wrong
description:
/testing-conformance-harnesses audit:

  rch exec -- cargo test -p fr-conformance --lib conformance_core_scan conformance_core_connection
  → 3 failures

Differential probe vs vendored Redis 7.2.4 (port 16701):

  SWAPDB abc 0
    upstream: ERR invalid first DB index
    fr      : ERR invalid first DB index   (matches)
    fixture : ERR value is not an integer or out of range  (WRONG)

  ZSCAN myz 0 NOVALUES
    upstream: ERR syntax error  (NOVALUES is a 7.4 HSCAN-only flag)
    fr      : ERR syntax error  (matches)
    fixture : expected NOVALUES output  (WRONG — pins nonexistent feature)

  ZSCAN myz 0 MATCH member1 NOVALUES
    upstream: ERR syntax error
    fr      : ERR syntax error
    fixture : expected NOVALUES output  (WRONG)

Fix: update the three core_scan.json + core_connection.json fixture entries to reflect actual upstream 7.2.4 behavior. fr's runtime is correct; the fixtures drifted.
close_reason: Updated 3 fixture cases to match upstream Redis 7.2.4 actual behavior: swapdb_non_integer expects 'ERR invalid first DB index' (the bespoke db.c wording); zscan_novalues_returns_members_only and zscan_novalues_with_match renamed to *_rejected_as_syntax_error and expect 'ERR syntax error' since NOVALUES is HSCAN-only and 7.4-only. Conformance scan + connection tests now green; fr's runtime was correct, the fixtures had drifted.

title: Lua pattern errors keep user_script:1: prefix in pcall(string.find/match) shape
description:
Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4 — pcall(string.find, …) with bad pattern:

  pcall(string.find,'abc','(')
    vendored: unfinished capture
    fr:       user_script:1: unfinished capture

  pcall(string.find,'abc','[')
    vendored: malformed pattern (missing ']')
    fr:       user_script:1: malformed pattern (missing ']')

  pcall(string.find,'abc','%')
    vendored: malformed pattern (ends with '%')
    fr:       user_script:1: malformed pattern (ends with '%')

  pcall(string.match,'abc','(')
    vendored: unfinished capture
    fr:       user_script:1: unfinished capture

Same dual-shape pattern as frankenredis-fllxr (string.format) and frankenredis-ymb2q (string.char + math.min/max). Pattern errors in `lua_pattern_validate` (fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs ~line 7370) hardcode the "user_script:1: " prefix and must drop it when the host C-builtin is invoked directly by pcall.

Direct calls already match — vendored emits "ERR user_script:1: unfinished capture script: ... on @user_script:1." and fr matches byte-for-byte because the wrapper sees the bare "user_script:1: …" and prepends "ERR ". Only the pcall shape diverges.

Fix: thread `inv_name: Option<&str>` through lua_pattern_validate and route the prefix through a conditional helper. Callers (string.find/match/gmatch/gsub) already have access to self.current_invocation_name.as_deref().

NOTE: orthogonal vendored quirk discovered while probing — `gsub('abc','(','x')` returns 'xaxbxcx' (lazy validation) and gmatch returns a function without raising. fr eagerly errors. Deferring as a separate behavioral divergence (track later if it becomes load-bearing).
close_reason: Threaded inv_name (Option<&str>) through lua_pattern_validate by renaming to lua_pattern_validate_named and removing the old wrapper. All 4 callers (string.find/match/gmatch/gsub) now pass self.current_invocation_name.as_deref() so pattern errors drop the user_script:1: prefix when invoked via pcall(C-builtin). Regression test lua_pattern_errors_pcall_shape_drops_prefix_uqnq6 pins 5 pcall shapes + direct-call regression + lua-wrapped-pcall regression. Existing test lua_pattern_validate_catches_malformed_patterns_vfv8s still passes. Live-diff vs vendored 16380: 5/5 pcall + 2/2 direct cases match.

title: conformance core_replication: psync_returns_fullresync expects all-zero replication ID
description: Pre-existing fr-conformance failure in tests::conformance_core_replication (visible on main pre-40v6f session). All 6 PSYNC variants (psync_returns_fullresync, psync_with_replid_returns_fullresync, psync_with_full_replid, psync_with_zero_offset, psync_case_insensitive, psync_wrong_arity_too_many) expect 'FULLRESYNC 0000000000000000000000000000000000000000 0' but fr returns 'FULLRESYNC <real-40-char-hex-replid> 0'. The fixture assumes an unset/zero replication ID at fresh-boot; fr's TCP server initializes a random replid eagerly at startup. Two options: (a) update fixtures to allow any 40-char hex replid (preferred — vendored Redis behavior is also to generate a random replid on first start, the fixture is overly strict); (b) defer replid generation until first PSYNC handshake. Recommend (a) — extend conformance harness to support replid placeholder match like '{ANY_HEX:40}'. Reproduce: cargo test -p fr-conformance --lib conformance_core_replication. Surfaced during REVIEW PHASE.
close_reason: Added new ExpectKind 'SimplePattern { value }' to fr-conformance harness supporting {hex40} and {int} placeholders. Updated all 7 FULLRESYNC fixture entries in crates/fr-conformance/fixtures/core_replication.json from 'simple: FULLRESYNC 0000...0000 0' to 'simple_pattern: FULLRESYNC {hex40} 0' (and similar for the 0...0001 / sync_alias variant). This lets fr's randomly-generated 40-char replid (per upstream replication.c::initServerConfig -> getRandomHexChars(server.replid, CONFIG_RUN_ID_SIZE)) satisfy the fixture while still validating the FULLRESYNC structure and offset. Implementation: simple_pattern_matches(pattern, actual) walks pattern and actual in lockstep, consuming exactly 40 hex chars at each {hex40} marker and an optionally-signed integer at each {int} marker. Verified: cargo test -p fr-conformance --lib conformance_core_replication passes; full fr-conformance suite drops to 1 remaining failure (conformance_core_geo, separately tracked as frankenredis-15x10).

title: redis parity: SAVE should reject script context
description: Vendored Redis marks SAVE as NOSCRIPT in legacy_redis_code/redis/src/commands/save.json, but save_cmd in crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:11906 records a save without any script_nesting_level guard. Add Redis-compatible script rejection coverage so SAVE errors from Lua instead of returning OK.

title: Investigate flaky E2E test: tcp_multiple_commands_pipelined

The test sends pipelined SET + GET commands and the mock server exits before responses are fully delivered. Likely a timing/buffering issue in the test harness, not the server.

title: RESTORE option/TTL validation order matches upstream cluster.c::restoreCommand
description: fr-command restore_cmd: (1) IDLETIME/FREQ option values were not validated (silent skip), so 'IDLETIME -1', 'FREQ 256', 'FREQ -1', 'FREQ abc' all returned 'DUMP payload version or checksum are wrong' instead of upstream's specific wording (2) negative TTL was clamped to 0 silently, masking the upstream 'Invalid TTL value, must be >= 0' wording (3) IDLETIME and FREQ are mutually exclusive in upstream. Correct order: parse options with full validation -> BUSYKEY check -> TTL >= 0 -> verifyDumpPayload

title: [code-review] CONFIG SET int fields accept values above INT_MAX
description: Review of recent CONFIG validation commits found the shared static integer branch validates list-compress-depth, lfu-log-factor, and tcp-keepalive as >=0 but does not enforce the upstream INT_MAX upper bound. Repro with vendored Redis 7.2.4: CONFIG SET list-compress-depth 2147483648, CONFIG SET lfu-log-factor 2147483648, and CONFIG SET tcp-keepalive 2147483648 each return ERR CONFIG SET failed ... argument must be between 0 and 2147483647 inclusive. Current fr-runtime accepts these values and CONFIG GET echoes them. Acceptance: enforce 0..=2147483647 for the shared branch, add runtime/conformance coverage for over-max cases, and keep live Redis differential cases in core_config.
